Good. And I hope you were careful in that lumber mill. Those splinters and rusty nails can be quite nasty.
Your payment, for a job well done.
Hmph. Well, that's sure to throw a branch into Falkreath's well-oiled political machine.
Here's your payment. Well earned, as always.
Don't worry, my friend, I'll spare you the facetious quip about killing a defenseless old woman.
Infiltrating an occupied fort and making it out in one piece is nothing to joke about. Well done.
Your payment for services rendered.
Well, what happens now is you start your new life in the Dark Brotherhood. You're part of the Family, after all.
This, as you can see, is our Sanctuary. You won't find a safer place in all of Skyrim. So get comfortable.
Of course she is. I hear the mining business is extremely cutthroat. And those hours... they're murder.
I could do this all day. Here's your payment.
Keerava, Bersi Honey-Hand and Haelga.
Do this right, and I can promise you a permanent place in our organization.
Honestly, the debt is secondary here. What's more important is that you get the message across that we aren't to be ignored.
A word of warning though... I don't want any of them killed. Bad for business.
Well, well... color me impressed, lad. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
Reliable and headstrong? You're turning out to be quite the prize!
So... now that I've whetted your appetite with our little scheme at the market, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Have anything for us yet?
I'll be here when you're done.
Come back when you grow a backbone.
Keerava's stubborn, but she's got a soft spot for family.
Talk to Talen-Jei at the Bee and Barb and see if you can get something out of him. They're... well acquainted if you catch my meaning.
Hmph. Yes. Yes, I believe you are. Very well, then.
You need to find and kill Safia, the captain of the pirate ship Red Wave.
The ship travels the northern coast, and sometimes isn't even in Skyrim at all. So you'll have to be patient. Good luck.
Well then, your timing couldn't be better.
As it turns out, I've got two new contracts. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge.
Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern.
Happy hunting.
Well aren't we the eager one? Sorry to disappoint, but there's nothing. Try me again later.
Well, yes. Two of them. But you're reaching the end of what I have available.
Your targets are a dark elf wizard named Maluril, in the dwarven ruin of Mzinchaleft. And Helvard, the housecarl of Falkreath.
Kill these two, and I'll have one last contract to give you. I'm just finalizing some details now.
Just one right now, actually.
I need you to eliminate an old woman named Agnis, a servant in Fort Greymoor.
Enjoy this while you can. Soon you will all be slaves.
Someone get that thing out of here and burn it.
And you. A Stormcloak? I'd thought better of you. You'll all come to regret this day.
I presume you're talking to me?
Yield. I yield, Stormcloak! The city of Markarth is yours.
I said, on your knees, Elf!
Don't worry. We'll take good care of your city when you're gone.
When Ulfric Stormcloak becomes High King, Skyrim will finally be the way my ancestors intended. It's a new day in Markarth.
Raagh!
A bit melodramatic, even for you Laila.
Pack your things and go.
No, Igmund. It's too dangerous.
I will not surrender Markarth without raising my own sword in her defense.
The Empire will return. Count on it.
Not so fast Elf!
He wants the city this badly, he can fight me for it.
He's as pig-headed a man as you'll ever find.
The key is that ugly dwarven urn in his shop. Smash that thing to bits and he'll change his attitude.
Haelga is a devout follower of Dibella and dotes over the statue to the Divine that she keeps at the Bunkhouse. Use it as leverage and she'll cave.
She wants the city this badly, she can fight me for it.
Please, please! This is exactly what I'm talking about. Eagerness must be tempered with caution, or else disaster is inevitable.
Sir, I think we all understand that fairly well. We wouldn't be here if we couldn't control magic!
Kill them all. Protect the Jarl at all costs!
So, as I was saying, the first thing to understand is that magic is, by its very nature, volatile and dangerous.
Unless you can control it, it can and will destroy you.
Then what are we waiting for? Let's get started!
Of course, my dear. Of course. You all certainly possess some inherent natural ability. That much is not being questioned.
What I'm talking about is true control, mastery of magic. It takes years, if not decades, of practice and study.
But we've only just arrived here -- you've no idea what any of us are capable of. Why not give us a chance to show you what we can do?
Welcome, welcome! We were just beginning. Please, stay and listen.
Ready to listen to the Silver-Blood family now, Igmund?
Halt in the name of the Jarl Hrongar.
That's close enough.
Thongvor. I should have executed your whole treacherous clan when I had the chance.
On your knees!
Come Galmar, we have much to do.
Are you two quite done?
You, go pack your things.
And you, go sit on that damn chair and start acting like a Jarl.
Get over to Windhelm. Tell Ulfric of our victory here.
They will sing stories of this day.
I'd be a lot warmer and a lot happier with a bellyful of mead...
Just a few more hours and I can crawl under some furs...
They say Ulfric Stormcloak murdered the High King... with his voice! Shouted him apart!
Stay alert. Never know when those Stormcloak dogs might charge.
Keep an eye out for Stormcloak spies.
Gotta keep my eyes open. Damned dragons could swoop down at any time.
Can you do it? Can you devour a dragon's soul?
I used to be an adventurer like you. Then I took an arrow in the knee...
The Imperials think we need their laws. Pfft.
I long to be out there, with my brothers, waging war against the Empire.
The Imperials think us all lawless beasts. I'm proof of their ignorance.
Legion soldiers gleam like fresh-fallen snow and clank like a kitchen. They head this way, we'll know it.
I'll fight the entire Legion myself if they dare show their faces here.
'Tis a wicked axe you wield there, friend. That blade looks sharp enough to cut through a god.
Vile! None of that - you made a deal, and the mortal stood by it honestly. Now it's your turn to come through.
Oh, fine. Have my boon and be done with it. I've got more interesting deals to make, anyway.
A lack of ambition like that really ought to be punished. Perhaps by turning you into a worm, or maybe a few decades of...
I'll make sure this war ends, all right. Just as soon as the dragons swarm across Skyrim and wipe out all of you puny...
You can be a part of a tremendous power - all you have to do is let your soul be absorbed by this axe. I never said it would be your power...
In Rimerock Burrow, there's an artifact called the Rueful Axe. Bring it to me, and I'll take Barbas back. Simple as that.
Ah, that feels so much better! You forget how nice supreme power feels until you've been stuck in a cave for a few years.
It's a shame you wished for something so dull as me taking back the mutt. Quite the lack of imagination on your part.
Now, as for your wish... An end to the war, was it?
Now, as for your wish... Power, was it?
Well, your classmates certainly seem to disagree with you.
Is that so?
Not going to weigh in either way?
I admire your restraint, but there are times when you must make decisions, even without all the facts. Rely on instinct.
See? He agrees with us too! Why don't you actually show us something?

It lick the panes and smokes the glass...
Yes, you're innocent. As innocent as anyone can be in this city.
I guess we also owe you something for locking you up. Here, my family's ring. And everything the guards confiscated from you.
Now if you'll excuse me, I have a mine that needs new workers to fill it.
My eyes inside Cidhna Mine tell me that Madanach is dead.
You've done a great service to the Silver-Blood family. I've had the Jarl officially pardon you, and taken care of a few other loose ends.
The Dark Brotherhood? Yeah, yeah, yeah. You think this is the first contract I've had on my life?
I'm a pirate, sweetie. Assassination attempts are an occupational hazard. Now, shall we?
And why would you be looking for me, hmm? I'm not sure I appreciate your presence here. Friend.
Death? Oh, there's death to be found here, to be sure.
But not my own!
Ah, I see. The old Dark Brotherhood "silent intimidation" treatment.
Oh, I know who you are. Don't be so surprised. You ain't the first assassin sent to kill me. Won't be the last. Now let's get this over with.
Go away! I don't know who you are, but this is my spot! Whatever's on that ship is mine!
Who are you? Why are you here? Leave this place. I have much work to conduct....
Yes... quite. But if that power could be harnessed. Controlled. The possibilities would be...
But... who exactly are you? And why are you in here? I think it best you leave Mzinchaleft. These ruins can be quite... dangerous.
You'll have to catch me first!
Sithis? Let me guess: "In the Void!" By the gods, you Dark Brotherhood are a predictable lot, aren't you?
Okay, here's how this works. You try to assassinate me, and I kill you. Horribly. I have done this dance before, you know.
What? Why? I don't understand... I have done nothing to wrong you. Please, mercy!
What? You dare challenge Ma'randru-jo? You will suffer for your insolence!
Fiend! You will not leave here alive! Guards! Guards!
Now aren't you a strange one. Well, if you're here to shop, shop. If not, find some other caravan to stalk.
I've been expecting you.
Should have figured the little lunatic would completely snap.
Still here? What's the matter, can't handle what I'm throwing your way?
And why would I... Oh. Oh! Guards! Guards, I need assistance!
Right. Well. If you have some kind of official business with the jarl, I'm afraid now's not the time. Go on then. Off with you.
Onmund, please search that area over there. See if you notice any... residual energies. Alive or undead.
Now, let's see. What shall we have the rest of you do?
J'zargo... what shall we have you do... Ah! Why don't you verify that we're the first ones here? Look for any amount of tampering with the tombs.
Brelyna, my dear, why don't you search for warding magics. Anything designed to keep people out. Don't interact with them, just identify them.
Well, are there any questions before we begin?
And here we all are. Shall we step inside?
All right, everyone. Let's be careful, but have fun!
Ah, yes. Hmm. Well, why don't you see if you can assist Arniel Gane? He's one of our scholars, here working on cataloging our finds.
I expect he'd appreciate some help in locating any additional magical artifacts here in the ruins.
Any enchanted items will do; the usefulness of the enchantment is irrelevant. If you find anything, the class can look it over.
Find Arniel Gane. He's somewhere in the northern section of the ruins. He'll show you what to do.
No? I daresay we don't have all day.
Still? If I have to wait much longer, you may need to just bury me here.
Help! Get that beast out of my house!
It's still here! Get it out!
Get that thing out of here!
Thank you! I thought that beast would be the end of me!
What about them? They seem loyal enough.
No, no girl. It's the pickled spider tongue and then the decayed taproot. Do it the other way, and your cure turns into a poison.
Me? I like to talk to my targets before the kill. Taunt them, scare them... even challenge them.
That can have interesting results. Some victims may flee, others may cower in fear.
If you can goad a target into attacking you first, you may even find any local guards coming to your aid.
Just be yourself. Eliminate the targets any way you see fit. Quiet, loud, quick, slow - whatever you feel is best.
Occasionally I'll give you a contract with certain parameters. Stay within them, and you'll earn yourself a bonus.
The Emissary? What's this about?
Wait... who are you? Intruder!
Ha. I'd like to see those arrogant bastards taken down a notch. Always looking down their noses at us lowly footsloggers.
If a dragon does show up, maybe we'll get lucky and it will eat the mages first. Might give us enough time to kill it.
You gave those mongrels a real whipping, eh? Good work. We'll be sure to send a garrison to reinforce the area and keep the peace.
We hit 'em where it hurts. As soon as we can, we'll take over - make the needed repairs. In the meantime, the people no longer slave for the Empire.
Good work. Knowing more about the enemy's position makes it that much easier to take it from him.
And of course, they'll be caught off guard thinking we're off doing something altogether different.
Ah, good. I've been wondering how we were supposed to defend this place from a dragon.
No. They're high mages, just in from Alinor. I guess Herself is finally getting worried about all the dragon attacks.
Did you see those robes march in this morning? Who're they with? More of the Emissary's treaty enforcers?
Not a day goes by I don't ask that question myself, my dear.
Don't be. We all make mistakes.
Give us a coin, Cosnach. You'll just spend it on cheap ale, anyway.
Ah, Yngvar, here to sing? You know an employee of the Silver-Blood family is always a guest of honor here.
Reburrus, I need you to draft a letter to Ulfric requesting more men.
I miss those days, before the Forsworn took up arms again, when you could walk the roads in peace.
Forgive me for worrying about the future of Markarth. The lifeblood of a city is gold, Igmund, and it doesn't stop bleeding because there's a war on.
Are you sure?
I... I'm sorry. I just, well, the forge gets so hot and I thought....
Mmmhmm.
How did you manage to cripple yourself, Degaine?
Oh, Talos's breath, Reberrus. Skip ahead to the good part.
What can I get for you, sir?
What can I get for you, ma'am?
Yes? What do you need?
No.... Not again!
Do your worst, monster!
I won't bend. Never!
Right away, sir!
I don't know, my darling wife. I assume you're going to tell me?
I don't buy that story, either.
And what do Legion soldiers know about the hills the Reach? And the caves that make up those hills? The Forsworn can hide and ambush every assault.
Shouldn't you be helping me then, wife of mine? Sharing my problems? Being my better half?
We sold all those lovely little necklaces you brought me today. Did you make them yourself?
I see. Well, let's continue this discussion later then.
Endon, we talked about this... Are you still disappointed in Cade's choice?
Bothela, I have a question....
You ready to pay the toll? One shiv.
I'll let that comment go because you're new here. Ask things out of turn again, and we have a problem. Now, are we clear on all of this?
Then find one. That dung heap Grisvar's been known to make a few.
This better not be another waste of the Guild's resources, Brynjolf.
Good. Then I think it's time we put your expertise to the test.
If you're not sure maybe you don't belong here. I'll ask again, are we clear on all of this?
Goldenglow Estate brought in a mountain of gold for the Guild. You could almost call it our sweetest deal.
Then out of the clear blue, Aringoth stops sending us our cut. Mercer was... well, angry to put it kindly.
So we send in Vex and find out he's hired a bunch of mercenaries to guard the place.
The catch is that you can't burn the whole place to the ground.
That important client Mercer mentioned would be furious if you did.
Mercer? This is the one I was talking about... our new recruit.
You've made a mess of things and Maven's furious. I told you not to burn more than three of the hives!
I've smoothed things over with her for now, but you can forget your cut.
Ah, now that's a good idea. Head on in. But don't try anything in there. Madanach is smarter than you think.
They're built like small fortresses to resist the weather, but their one weakness is flame.
Besides, nothing tells the people of Riften we mean business better than a huge column of smoke.
Maven prefers that Aringoth remains alive, but if he tries to stop you from getting the job done, kill him.
The Guild has a lot riding on this. Don't make me look foolish by mucking it up.
That's not how things work around here. The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
Goldenglow Estate is a bee farm; they raise the wretched little things for honey. It's owned by some smart-mouth wood elf named Aringoth.
We need you to teach him a lesson by burning down three of the estate's hives and clearing out the safe in the main house.
Ah, Borkul muscle one out of you on your way in? Fine. Take this one.
Maven wants to see you right away. I suggest you head right over.
Before we continue, I want to make one thing perfectly clear. If you play by the rules, you walk away rich.
You break the rules and you lose your share. No debates, no discussions... you do what we say, when we say.
So tell me, did Aringoth enjoy your visit?
Was there something else?
All eyes are on you, lad. Don't disappoint us.
I suggest you listen to what Mercer has to say... if you want in.
It's just this way. Keep following me.
Keep following, it isn't far.
I wasn't aware that Lisbet was having trouble, Cosnach. Is Arnleif and Sons not doing a good turn of business these days?
Well yes, of course you had to go and mention that.
Jarl Black-Briar. I must admit, I do like the sound of that.
And don't worry about any rioting, Legate. I have it under control.
We will soon begin publicly executing captured men. That should send a clear message to the people.
Same as the others. Looks like she was stabbed, and then cut apart.
It's like an animal got to her, but these are the wounds of a blade.
Look, friend, if you think you can do better than the legion of guards, be my guest.
You'll need to talk to Jorleif, though. We can't just let anyone go around claiming to be on official business.
If he's willing, then we'll talk.
Look, there's a war going on, if you hadn't heard. We barely have enough soldiers to walk the streets, much less patrol every corner.
I don't want to believe it. Wuunferth has been a trusted friend to Ulfric for many years.
It pains me to see that the whispers had truth to them.
Wuunferth shall be apprehended. I thank you for your diligence in getting to the bottom of this matter.
The streets of Windhelm are now safe.
Well... all right. What exactly do you need to know?
Well... all right. What exactly do you need to know?
I've never seen anything like it. I would take this to Calixto at the House of Curiosities. He has a good eye for strange trinkets.
Might even give you a bit of gold for it.
Eehh.. Calixto and his books are often confused about such matters. It happens to the best of us.
Suit yourself. It's only of value to collectors, though. Good luck finding anyone else who will appraise it that highly!
They say they're too busy with the war -- I say what good is winning a war if we're still terrorized by one of our own?
I've had enough troubles recently. What is it that you're after?
Susanna's the third. It's always the same: young girl, killed at night, body torn up.
Wuunferth. There have been rumors swirling about him for years. As long as I can remember.
But he's a dangerous man. It's why they call him "the Unliving." I wouldn't approach him directly.
This information needs to go straight to the steward. He'll listen to you.
Wuunferth? Bah. It's purely ceremonial, and he has no use for it.
Besides, I wouldn't want to be the one to give it to him. Gives me the creeps. They say he dabbles in necromancy.
Necromancy? I am a member of the College of Winterhold, in good standing!
They haven't allowed necromancy for hundreds of years!
If you want to help, ask some of these gawkers if they saw anything useful.
I'm going to examine the body before the rats can get to it.
I've been noting a pattern to when the killings happen.
Now that we know they're tied in to some sort of necromantic ritual, I think I know when the next might occur.
Let's see. From a Loredas of Last Seed until a Middas of Heartfire... it will happen soon. Very soon.
Keep watch in the Stone Quarter tomorrow night. That's almost certainly where the killer will strike next.
What made you think I was to blame in the first place? I've actually been after the killer myself.
My what, now? I've never kept a journal, I can assure you.
What exactly did this amulet look like?
I've been trying to put those up around the city, to warn people. Have them keep their eyes open.
But someone keeps taking them down.
You say you found it in Hjerim? Friga's old place. Let's go have a look around there.
I've got a feeling about this one.
Follow me.
I'll meet you over there.
Good day.
Oh, they care all right. Just none of them thinks to do anything about it.
They say I'm just snooping around bothering people, but I'm trying to save lives!
Wonderful. This will be a splendid addition to my private collection.
I know it well. Or at least, I've heard of it. I would wager that carving once depicted a skull.
That is the Necromancer's Amulet, of legend.
It appears you were at least half-right. There is necromancy at the heart of this.
Let's go. I'd rather not stay out here alone any longer than I have to.
I'm sorry -- she was very dear to me, and it's rather painful to think about.
Let me see... ah, yes. This is the Wheelstone. It's an heirloom symbol of the power of Windhelm.
Traditionally it's carried by the court mage.
I would... eh... be interested in acquiring it. If you're willing to part with it, that is.
For a piece like that, I could pay... 500 gold?
The Wheelstone? Have you decided to sell it, yet?
Hjerim? Well, I'm not sure what you think you'll find there, but you're welcome to have a look.
There's just so much to memorize. How am I supposed to remember what goes where for every single herb?
But mother says you're a terrible innkeeper!
You touch her, and I swear it'll be your fingers that go missing, you stupid Breton!
The excavation, Aicantar! Right after I finish my book. We'll start excavating Nchuand-Zel again.
Playing tonight, Ogmund? Everyone here is anxious to hear the old ballads performed by the True Skald of the Reach.
My Jarl, we need to discuss the state of Markarth's collapsing trade with the other holds.
Sometimes I think we deserve these miserable lives of ours.
Tacitus? What is this?
I'm sorry. You don't have to tell me if you don't....
We don't fight, son. We, um, talk. Loudly.
What is it nephew? Can't you see I'm trying to think here?
Very well. I'll send a request for additional Legionnaires to protect trade to the other Imperial holds. Hopefully Solitude can spare more men.
Endon, dear, please don't.... Save your frustrations for work.
Yes, my Jarl.
Shut it, Kleppr. Thongvor isn't here, so save your kissing up for a Nord who cares.
Like you won't do different?
I've been cleaning up spills and there is fresh linen for the rooms.
You thought what? You would skip a step? Not heat the metal all the way through? Useless! Now do it again.
Frabbi, my dear loving wife, could you please clean out our guest rooms?
Ha! About time we got some more muscle in this city.
Another foul day at the smelter.
That's right! Worst innkeeper in all of Skyrim!
Loyal? Thongvor supports Ulfric and his Stormcloaks! We should imprison the entire family as a precaution.
What are you, blind? That store is empty. Forsworn get almost every shipment that comes in.
The safety of the city and our settlements must be our only concern, Igmund. The rest of the Reach is on its own.
Kleppr, you idiot! Can't you do anything right?
It's rotten because the wood is cheap and its soaked with ale! Now we'll have to replace all the furniture before bugs set in.
Oh, right. Oh, well, things are going well enough. At least, as well as we can hope.
Mining accident, back when I was in prison for being a pickpocket.
No. I think if I was a young man, I would have done the same.
You will. Until then, you'll taste every potion you make before you sell it.
All the wood furniture in this inn is rotting to the core. Do you know why that is, Kleppr?
I am your better half. And your better half is telling you to stop being an idiot and serve the customers their drinks!
That's right! Papa helped me with the clasps, but I did all the links on my own.
Son, I know thousands of the old ballads. If I played all of them, everyone in this inn would be as grey as I am before I finished.
How goes the trade in and out of the city?
Stop bothering me Aicanter!
Always another big meal to cook. Figures.
Too bad. That one's my favorite.
Could I maybe, skip the excavation, Uncle Calcelmo? I, uh, I just think you won't need me.
What was that?
If you ladies are finished bickering, there's much to be done.
Jarl Black-Briar put your government together. Do it now, or there will be rioting in the streets.
We must move quickly to prevent further violence.
Hroggar's house? It burned down not too long ago.
It's a real pity about his wife and kid. The screams woke half the town.
Most folk won't go near it now for fear it's cursed.
That's what they say.
See, he's living with Alva now. That started the day after the fire.
It ain't right, movin' in with a new love the day after your kin die like that.
Aye. Our Jarl would sure like to know if he did though. Might even pay to find out.
Hroggar claims it was a hearth fire. Some folks say Hroggar started it himself.
So enters Maven Black-Briar, the glint of Imperial coin in her eyes. So tell me, what's the price for a woman's integrity these days?
One day when you can see past your own interests, you will come to see that we were right.
And this... This is all wrong.
You never were able to see the forest for the trees, were you?
Oh? I don't expect anyone to miss my meaning.
Come, Legate. There's much to do.
I knew you looked like a clever one...
Of course not! Thieves take what is precious. Thieves steal.
We just take what will not be missed. What no one needs anymore. Maybe we take some weapons and stop a few deaths. Who would call that thievery?
Don't worry about them. We'll make sure they wash up safe. Maybe they'll even pay us for the heroic rescue from their sad crash.
Turn me in? For what? What crime's been committed? I'm just making idle talk about what would most undoubtedly be a tragic event...
A tragic, and very, very profitable event.
Yes, but not likely the one you intend.
With the war, many more ships come through these docks. Loaded with weapons and pay, but few people. And they pass through dangerous waters.
We have an interest in one of those boats, the Icerunner.
The Solitude Lighthouse will be guiding it in... but if its fire were to go out, the Icerunner would run aground.
Where's Anuriel?
The effects have already been felt. The Icerunner has failed to dock on time.
Without the Lighthouse, the Icerunner will have swung wide of the western shoal and run aground on the eastern coast.
My sister Deeja will be at the wreck with our Blackblood Marauders. She knows to expect you.
We Nords were proud warriors once. And we still could be. Evidenced by the men and women who fought and died bravely today.
You could see that if you had any faith left in that black heart of yours.
But no, you're content to snatch scraps falling off the Emperor's table. Fine. Take my home. Take my city. May it burn down around you.
Hey! What're you doing in here?
Get out of here or I'll call the guards!
That's it! You're leaving, right now.
You look new. I'm new, too. I think we could be friends.
Stop right there! Were I can see you.
I think I'll like living here.
As you wish.
Protect the Jarl!
Riften denies the Emperor. You are here illegally. Stand down and you will be arrested and tried in the morning. Resist and you will die.
She's watching over the others. Look alive now.
This warning is passed to you because the Psijic Order believes in you.
Can you tell me more about what you saw?
Hold, mage, and listen well...
I... I swear I felt something rather strange just then.
What just happened?
Judgment will be passed on your actions to come, and how you deal with the dangers ahead of you.
You, mage, and you alone, have the potential to prevent disaster.
How in the world did that happen?
Judgment has not been passed, as you had no way of knowing.
Know that you have set in motion a chain of events that cannot be stopped.
I'm afraid I didn't see anything...
Really? Perhaps the amulet is important somehow. Is there some way you can use it?
Take great care, and know that the Order is watching.
I have no idea! This is amazing. Absolutely amazing. The Arch-Mage needs to be informed immediately. He needs to see this for himself.
I don't dare leave this unattended. Can you return to the College and inform Savos Aren of this discovery? Please, hurry.
The Psijics have no connection to these ruins. And no one's seen any of their order in a long time.
Perhaps we should take a look inside these coffins...
Now please do be careful. Who knows what we're going to find.
The Psijic Order? Are you quite sure about that? That's very odd.
And danger ahead? Why that doesn't make any sense at all.
Well let's not put it like that. Wouldn't want the Thieves Guild catching wind of us, eh?
Nah, just creep in and have a go at taking it out. I'll be here, and pay you half of what my man in Riften promised for it.
You'll be helping out an old beggar, and the Divines smile on that sort of thing.
Aye, a whole heap of it. You see that Temple? Way up there?
All them women, keeping to themselves. Won't let poor Degaine in, no.
Not even for a look.
But they've got a treasure in there. Gold statue, size of a child. Who knows what they use it for, but I know what I'll use it for.
Didn't you hear me? I said the keep is under attack!
Don't you even know what's going on? A dragon is attacking Helgen!
Come with us. We need to get out of here.
There's no way out that way, you know...
Come on!
Forget the old man. I'll come with you.
An old storeroom. See if you can find some potions. Might come in handy.
Look out!
A dragon? Please. Don't make up nonsense.
Hold on, now, we only want to...
Damn, that dragon doesn't give up easy.
You have no authority over me, boy.
You fellows happened along just in time. These boys seemed a bit upset at how I've been entertaining their comrades.
Grab what you can, and let's go.
Although, come to think of it, I did hear some odd noises coming from over there.
Hear that?
Then it is true what they said? I am touched by the gods?
You... you found her? I can't believe it. Let the girl come with me and we can begin her preparation.
I will send word to her family that she is in good hands.
You've truly earned the Blessing of Dibella, child. Prostrate yourself before her altar, and she will bestow her gift.
Damn it, man, are you trying to taunt us?
Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
Tolfdir normally looks after your little group, yes?
Since he's apparently occupied, and I will need to see this discovery for myself, I think perhaps you should begin researching the subject.
Speak with Urag in the Arcanaeum. See if he is aware of anything that matches your discovery.
And... good work. The next time you find yourself exploring Nordic ruins, perhaps this will be helpful.
Please don't tell me that another one of the apprentices has been incinerated. I have enough to deal with right now.
I... see. I trust that Tolfdir will provide a more... specific explanation.
Very well, I trust that you wouldn't be here were it not significant.
Clan Battle-Born's got a proud lineage in Whiterun, but unlike the Gray-Manes, we've also got wealth.
My bloodin' days might be over, but my son Idolaf is out there fightin' for Skyrim! Fightin' for the Empire! Ha!
Aiiiiiiieeeeeeee!!
It's Grelod!
My Alembic. I need it for some research, and can't seem to find it.
Well, yes. This is rather embarrassing, but it seems I've misplaced something.
Ah, yes! There it is! Thank you so much.
I can't find my Alembic. I suppose I could use another one, but... well, it sounds silly, but mine has sentimental value.
If you happen to see it, would you mind bringing it to me?
Paarthurnax has made the decision to help you. This is the road we have to walk.
Even the Greybeards must bend to the winds of change, it seems.
So be it. Tell Ulfric and General Tullius that the Greybeards wish to speak to them.
We will see if they still remember us.
Yes, of course! But how to force a dragon to talk to you...
There is one possibility. But... no, the Jarl would never agree.
I see. The dragon will lead you to Alduin, but without the Jarl's help...
Do you know Dragonsreach? The Jarl of Whiterun's palace?
Its name is no accident. The palace was originally built to hold a captive dragon.
The old tales say that he is able to travel into Sovngarde to devour the souls of the dead.
But they don't say how he does this.
You misunderstand our authority. The Greybeards have never involved themselves in political affairs.
You might be able to trap him there, exactly.
You'd require the Jarl's cooperation, of course. That might not be easy to come by.
You must trust in the rightness of your cause, and the strength of your Voice.
Oh, this was back in the days of Olaf One-Eye, thousands of years ago.
In those days, dragons could still be found among the remote mountain peaks.
Before the Blades wiped them all out.
This is High Hrothgar, our temple, sanctuary, and home. We ask that you respect our solitude while you are here.
We are not warriors. What is overlooked in the Dragonborn is not permitted to any other followers of the Way of the Voice.
Ah! Dragonsreach! There are many difficulties, but I cannot think of a better plan.
Although I fear the Jarl may not be easy to persuade.
Remember, the Way of the Voice trains you for more than battle. Indeed, we believe that battle is the least of its uses.
May the strength and truth of your Voice serve you well. Breath and focus.
Alduin... we heard the Dragonrend Shout from here... you defeated him?
I feared as much. I thought it was him we saw flying east after your battle.
Ah, an excellent question. Mehrunes Dagon is the Daedric Lord of Change, Destruction, and Ambition.
Dagon's Mythic Dawn cult killed the Septim Dynasty and opened the Oblivion Gates into Tamriel. They called it the Oblivion Crisis.
The Razor is Mehrunes Dagon's personal artifact. It has always heralded bloody change and carnage.
It's held many names: Dagger of the Final Wounds, Bane of the Righteous, the Kingslayer.
The Mythic Dawn worshipped Dagon as a god. Having his Razor would be invaluable to my collection.
Get moving. I'll see if I can't delay any patrols out there.
The "problem" is your disrespect for the laws of this land. It's time to face the Jarl's justice.
Smart man. Now come along with us. We'll take any stolen goods, and you'll be free to go. After you pay the fine, of course.
I guess you're smarter than you look.
Then pay with your blood.
We got everything in hand here. You aren't needed 'round here.
We chop trees, we mill the wood. What more would you want to know?
It's a hard way to make a living, but we find ways to make ends meet.
We don't take kindly to people snooping around our business.
These axes can chop a lot more than wood, you know.
Hey, welcome to the mill!
My brother and me bought this place when we left the army. Boy, did we lose a bundle, but at least we're not fighting the war!
We're making our money back, bit by bit. Every extra bit of work helps...
We served our time in the Legion. Now we're just trying to make it day to day.
I used to serve with Trilf and Steirod. Good guys, but don't get on their bad side, you know?
I once saw Steirod tear a whole tree out the ground with his bare hands. Glad he's on our side!
These boys do good work for the Empire!
Nice to be here on the home-front. Wouldn't mind being down in Cyrodiil, though.
We prefer not to announce that the Sybil has died until we can introduce the new one. People like to know that we're connected to the goddess.
The Sybil of Dibella spends her entire life in direct communion with the goddess.
From the time she's a little girl to the moment she expires, she is a direct connection with the heavenly mother.
From her reverie, we know the mind of our god.
We have had a vision of the next Sybil, living in a village to the north. She must be brought to the temple for her reverie to begin.
Damn it, woman, are you trying to taunt us?
It was the Forsworn from Broken Tower. Never been much trouble before. What's it to you?
You, my dear, can earn the Blessing of Dibella. You'll find men to be more pliable in the future... or more vulnerable.
Have you heard of the Blessing of Dibella? It can help you with the wooing of women, if that's your aim.
Or give you strength if you happen to offend one.
Typically we avoid bestowing it upon men, but for the Sybil's escort, we could make an exception.
She... really? That's... I wouldn't have dreamed. Of course, we need her in Markarth, then. I'll come with you.
That's... that's good of you. If you were to save her, I would be very grateful. I don't think there's anything I could give you, though...
Is that an Amulet of Mara? I'm surprised someone like you isn't spoken for.
You should arrange the ceremony at the Temple of Mara right away. I can't wait.
Here you are. Now remember, people in Skyrim respond to deeds. You will find more people interested in your love if you help them.
Good luck. Mara bless you.
Lost the last one I gave you? Very well, here you are. Mara keep you.
I'm sorry, but even Mara's Temple needs coin. We will have a symbol ready for you when you come back with enough gold.
Mara will protect you.
You wish to be married? It's good to see love blossoming, even during these difficult times.
Your wedding will be held tomorrow, from dawn until dusk.
Don't be late, you don't want to miss your own wedding!
Use the gloves to clear the focal points. Return to me when you have finished. I thought I made this rather clear.
I see.
Very well. These specially enchanted gloves will allow you to delve directly into the focal points for these energies.
Go to each focal point and remove whatever impurities you may find there.
Return the gloves to me when you have finished.
Please exercise caution. This is not a procedure without possible... consequences.
As you may be aware, the College has gone to great lengths to purify and enhance the natural energies of the world.
This both enhances our connection to the realm of magic, and eliminates possible interference from outside sources.
These energies, on occasion, become... polluted. Effort must be made to see that they are kept pure, clean.
Do you wish to aid in this cleansing?
There may be a matter in which your... skills... could be useful.
The various points of focus for magic energies around the College have become polluted, and need cleansing.
The magical energies underneath the College still require purification. Your assistance would be most useful.
A book of mine. I was at least halfway through, and wanted to finish it, but apparently I put it down somewhere.
Yes, they have. I can feel the difference already. But of course, I'm far more in tune with these sorts of things than most.
Thank you. I appreciate your assistance.
I seem to have misplaced the book I was reading.
There have been reports of weird happenings near Wolfskull Cave. Travelers disappearing, odd lights. I suspect wild animals or perhaps bandits.
I don't think it's worth our time with the war going on, but if you want to clear out the cave, I'll make sure you're repaid for your work.
You mean the Dragon Bridge issue? I'll be honest with you, I was planning to let that go. Varnius is a bit jumpy at the best of times.
Ho, now... Keep me out of this...
And... you'll take care of it?
Wait, let me guess. You'll get to it later.
Don't I always?
The corn stores are getting low again, Orgnar.
You promised me you'd sweep the entranceway.
Ha ha ha ha ha ha ha! That's my boy!
Ha! I knew it! Had your name written all over it, that one did!
See, now that's what I'm talking about. Working hard, making your mark on this world. Good job, son.
Here we go...
I don't suppose I could convince you to take care of it now, could I?
You do realize these simpletons wouldn't know real music if it bit them in the britches, hmm? Very well. Your wish is my command.
Well. There's something to be said for honesty, I suppose.
I should have guessed.
It's just that papa promised I could make something at the forge. So maybe we could do something together, hmmm... next week? Is... that okay?
Just stop trying to change me, mother! I'm not a baby anymore! I'm almost a woman! I... I can't be who I want to be!
I'm sorry, mother. I can't help it. All that stuff you like to do, it's just so... boring.
Oh. Um. Well... You see...
Wow. Yeah. That sounds great, mother. Only, um... it's just that...
Oooh. Ahhh... Well... See, the thing is...
Dorthe, dear, I've been growin' some really lovely vegetables. Maybe tomorrow you could help me plant a new patch? What do you say?
Dorthe... I was thinkin' I'd knit a couple of blankets, sell them to Lucan. Would you like to make one? Might be fun, workin' together.
You know, Dorthe, with all those vegetables I've been growing lately, I was thinkin' we could make a right lovely stew. Together. How about it?
It's about your singing. Well, not your singing, your songs. But you know what I'm talking about.
Shor's crown! There are things ladies like us are supposed to do. We've talked about this...
Let me guess. You'd rather play "Daedra and Battlemages" with Ordnar. Or go fishing, or catch frogs...
Dorthe, listen to me. Your father and your friend may treat you like a boy, but you're not. Do you understand? So please. Be a girl. For me.
He he he he he.
You just wait. My new pranks are going to the best ever! You'll see! He he he he.
Ha! That ain't nothing. Wait till you see what I got planned for next week.
Hmm. All right. Do you think... maybe we should?
Well, I was thinking... we should. We could stock all kinds of specialty items. You know, to cater to some of our more.... "eclectic" customers.
Hmmm... Well, maybe we could go on a sort of trip. Take the wares on the road. Get some new customers.
Don't worry, I am.
All right. But I am getting a bit tired of waiting.
Yes, sir.
Sven, we need to talk...
Yep.
That's really not at the top of my list, no. Please, Camilla, I need you to focus.
Dear sister, we talked about this sort of thing before. We sell dry goods, nothing more, nothing less.
Camilla, really? You don't have better things to do? Because I could certainly find quite a few tasks...
Of course, Lucan. Of course. I was just, well... Never mind.
Okay, okay. Just trying make this place a little less... boring.
I know, I know. My head's in the clouds again. Just... forget I said anything.
No.
Fine. You play in the mud, or play with your father's forge, or get into sword fights with your little friend. But this is not over.
Oh, Dorthe. You're breaking your mother's heart, you know that?
Well. I can see your father and I are going to have to talk about this. And when we do, things are going to change.
Sure I did. And I'd do it again.
Yeah. So?
Ha ha ha ha! Yeah, I sure did. It was one of my best pranks yet, father.
Hey boy, I heard you nailed a Septim into Lucan's doorstep. Damn near drove him crazy. That true?
Tell me, son. Any truth to these stories I been hearing. About you gluing a cow's udders shut? You really do something like that?
Heard me a rumor, boy. Something about you putting bugs in the stew over at the Sleeping Giant. You do this?
Wonderful. And let's not have this chat again next week. Agreed?
Later, little one. I need to finish what I'm working on. But keep watching. Watch, and learn.
He he. Not just yet, Dorthe. I need to keep forging, and you need to keep watching.
Patience, Dorthe. You'll get some time at the forge, I promise you. But not right now. You need to keep watching me. That's how you'll learn.
Lucan, we don't sell silver buckles, do we? One of the customers was asking.
We don't sell any imports from Summerset Isle, do we, Lucan?
Lucan, I was thinking we should have a grand sale. Lower the prices by half, maybe get some jugglers. We could attract lots of new customers.
Can I make something now, papa?
Is it time yet? Time for me to make my sword? Or maybe... an axe?
I'm bored standing around, papa. I could take over for you...
No, dear sister. We do not.
I'm afraid not.
A sale? To attract new customers? Camilla, everyone in Riverwood already shops here. There are no more customers.
The dawn surely opens upon you, child. You must bear its light that all may see.
Mara has reflected an image to me. At the foot of the throat and young woman... almost a girl... her fickle love must resolve itself.
The village of Ivarstead. The woman, Fastred.
This is the prayer heard by the goddess and relayed to her servants. Return when she has seen her path.
I will entreat Mara on your behalf.
So I hear. Come here, you.
I don't want you to leave Ivarstead.
Not all can hear the broad echoes of deepest dawn.
To receive the touch of Mara, you must first act as her hands in the world.
Explore the facets of the infinite jewel.
Bassianus, sweetie, what is it?
The goddess smiles at your efforts, child. The dawn shines bright upon you.
You have seen the young, fickle love of youth, and helped a more seasoned love find its way.
There is one final aspect we wish for you to explore. For a strong love can withstand storms and even survive death.
Take this symbol of Mara. She will guide you to the wandering souls of two whose love was so great that their entwinement binds them to this world.
Oh my. Probably something about the men. We all wish we had her problems.
Now, don't tell my husband, but I don't have any problem with Bassianus.
Even if it means her leaving Ivarstead, I want Fastred to be happy.
If they just snuck out of town together, I could manage my husband.
I'm not sure what this is all about.
Faleen... I...
We are devoted to the Goddess Mara, who gave mortals the gift of love that they might have a hint of eternity.
Her blessings are many. To love is to know the true nature of the gods.
Oh, and to be with you!
What did you call me?
Your efforts have helped illuminate the entire province. I trust by now you've achieved a higher comprehension of love.
Carry the blessings of Mara with you, so the rest of the province may shine with her glories.
What good are fish if you have to eat them alone?
This is... lovely. Calcelmo wrote this... about me? I never knew he had such feeling in him.
The trouble is that I can't seem to speak around her.
My mouth goes dry, and I start to shake.
I could never hope to approach her.
We must leave. Your father will never approve of us, and I grow weary of this place.
Why not?
I always had a feeling she liked me. More than that brute Jofthor, at least.
Jofthor... what about him? He'll come after us and... it will be unpleasant.
Come back when you've got the gold, then.
Clearly. I've never even thought of him in that manner. I wish that I was as skilled with words as he.
Here, take him this letter. I may not be as eloquent as he is, but I hope he understands.
Are you prepared to receive my golden words?
Wonderful. I'll write it out so you won't forget it.
Fastred, I have something to say to you.
Sssh. It's all right. You don't need to say anymore. I had no idea you were so... nuanced.
Oh, Klimmek...
Look around here. There's not much left.
Used to be a good-sized town, but folks have been moving to Riften for a while now.
If all the young people leave, what happens to Ivarstead?
This is... more than I could have hoped for. Have you really done this for me?
I have to see her. Please, excuse me.
Pardon me?
Where will we go?
Oh, my love. How I long to take her away from all of this.
Why did Boti send you to me?
May you carry the warmth of Mara to all corners of the frozen wastes.
Because... I would miss you.
Are you the one sent by Mara?
I love you.
Oh, thank you so much! It's wonderful to have someone around who understands.
You know how children are. Her fancies change with the moon.
And like always, the boy lacks any kind of spine. He'll need some convincing.
A little push and this would all be simpler.
Klimmek? What is it?
Oh, Fastred. I'm not sure what happened. One moment she's smiling at me, the next I can't even meet her eyes.
I'll bet Bassianus would know what's going on. He understands women.
Fastred, we must speak.
I prayed to Mara and she spoke to me in a dream. She said a stranger would appear to help me.
Is that you?
My parents are being impossible.
Bassianus wants to marry me. And I think I love him. But he wants to move to Riften afterwards, and my father won't allow it.
He only cares about this stupid town, but Bassianus makes me happier than anyone ever has.
My mother's not any help, either.
Then you know what a beauty she is.
I was beginning to lose faith that any help would come.
Eh... You see... I've been thinking about Faleen quite a bit. You know Faleen?
To Riften, my pet. How you'll adore the city.
Well, suffice to say she is resplendent, but not without resolve. Striking, in all senses.
I'm sure I know what this is about.
She thinks she's in love with Bassianus and wants to leave town. With him.
Let me tell you how I see it.
Until a few months ago, the girl was head over heels for Klimmek. Wouldn't stop talking about him.
Now if I thought she was really in love with Bassianus, that would be one thing. But she's a child.
It will pass.
But I thought you only cared about your fishing!
Okay, got it.
(bark)
You a fighting man? How about a little bet?
What do you need?
The final tempering of the Phial was performed with a type of old magic that's been lost since the Dragon War.
The nearest modern incarnation of it is the strange rituals practiced by the Forsworn.
With the heart of one of their Briar Warriors, I can lock the Phial's magic into its physical form.
It's difficult to explain to the layman -- forgive me if I begin waxing alchemic.
On top of the Throat of the World is a patch of snow that's never melted.
They say the Greybeards taught it to ignore the sun, and heat simply washes over it.
The original Phial was made from this snow. We'll only need a small bit for the repairs.
The ivory produced by those beasts is as hard as iron. But the giants have found ways to make it yield.
They're able to grind it down so fine that it can be infused into the lattice of packed snow.
The finished material has the delicate nature of fresh powder snow, but the strength of the hardest steel.
Figures -- I doubt you have sufficient knowledge to harm the Phial even if you wanted to.
Either way, this is the end of it.
Now if you'll excuse me, I'm not quite in the mood to entertain guests. I trust you can show yourself out.
Here's for your trouble.
Anything else?
Can't do that. Sorry.
(woof)
Take my advice. Never gamble. Damn Irnskar has me in debt up to the eyes.
I might just be a servant among lords, but don't think for a moment I don't take my duties as seriously as the jarl himself takes his!
Got of lot of steel to shape.
Hmm?
I ain't much for talkin'.
Why you still starin' at me?
I'm... Well, who I am isn't important. Let's leave it at that, hmm?
Forget Solitude or Windhelm. Whiterun was the crown jewel of Skyrim before... Well, before... Never mind.
This was such a glorious city. But then everything had to change. The Empire came... and my brother was lost...
Ah, but Skyrim! Skyrim is supreme among all nations! Our people, our native people, are unmatched in all Tamriel!
One day the war will end and the true heirs of Skyrim will inherit this great realm.
(panting)
(bark)
Tired of bread and stew? Had enough of fish and fowl? Treat yourself and your kin to a choice cut of steak or a tender rack of ribs!
(growl)
I can't stand those braggart Battle-Borns. They think they're the gods' gift to Whiterun.
Eorlund and I... we both miss our son terribly. Thorald... he went missing, fighting the Imperials.
Everyone thinks Thorald is dead. But not me. I know my boy is alive out there. Somewhere...
What's the matter? You can't stand the sight of a strong Nord woman?
When the Stormcloaks took control of Whiterun, it was a victory for all true Nords.
There's nothing a man can do that I can't do better, whether it's serving drinks or slaying trolls.
I miss the days when the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns got along. Ever since the war started, we've been at each others' throats.
You wouldn't know it now, but the Gray-Manes and Battle-Borns were once fast friends.
Halt in the name of the Jarl!
How kind of you. And I've brought an army to give you a going away parade.
If you two are finished with the touching homecoming, there's a city in chaos out there.
Vignar Gray-Mane! Your family was noticeably absent from the walls. Now I know why. Wouldn't a dagger in the back have sufficed?
Protect the Jarl with your lives!
Balgruuf!
Indeed. Come, there is much to be done.
If they want my city, they're going to have to take it from me personally.
Stay back, lord!
Stop! Enough! Stand down!
The city is yours.
It's over! Everyone stop!
I surrender... I surrender...
Somehow I doubt that will persuade them.
Somewhere up north, in the ice fields.
Said he found some old Dwemer artifact, but... well, that was years ago.
Haven't heard from him since.
Oh no. I hope not. But even I haven't seen him in years, and we were close.
Became obsessed with the Dwemer. Took off north saying he had found some old artifact. Haven't seen him since.
Somewhere in the ice fields, if you want to try to find him.
Here you go. Try not to spill anything on them.
Know about them? Lad, you're looking at them.
They call themselves the "Blood Horkers."
They've been around as long as I can remember, but only recently focused on our ships.
A dastardly group, but they're more than simple brigands. Shrewd. Clever. Not to be trifled with.
You know him?
He made my favorite dolly, but I can't find her.
Are you sure you aren't a stranger?
Helgi. But father says I'm not supposed to talk to strangers.
Are you a stranger?
She's mine! She's my reward. You can't have her!
The smoke woke me up. It was hot and I was scared, so I hid.
Then it got cold and dark. I'm not scared anymore.
But I'm lonely. Will you play with me?
Okay! Let's play hide and seek. You find me and I'll tell you.
We have to wait for nighttime though. The other one is playing too, and she can't come out until then.
<humming>
I certainly do.
Since you went so far as seek out the Augur for advice, I thought you'd be more enthusiastic.
No one here does.
The Synod seemed convinced it was somewhere in Skyrim. They inquired about the ruins of Mzulft, but that's all I remember.
It sounded like they were heading there, though they were rather secretive about why.
I suppose if you're intent on looking for the staff, there's a chance they might be in Mzulft yet. Just don't expect them to be cooperative.
A most impressive attitude. Keep this up, and you'll do quite well for yourself.
Really? And what might that be?
Something as specific and ancient as the Staff of Magnus... I'm not sure we'd ever find something like that...
I seem to recall Mirabelle mentioning the staff somewhat recently. Why don't you see if she can tell you anything?
I'm quite pleased with your progress, you know. You've certainly proven yourself to be more than a mere Apprentice. Well done.
This circlet once proved invaluable to me. I hope it can be of use to you now.
The Augur?
Just what have you gotten yourself involved in?
Whatever is going on, whatever you're up to... Be very careful.
I see. Well yes, I suppose I did mention it, though I'm not sure what he expects me to tell you.
I only brought it to his attention a few months back when the Synod showed up here looking for it.
They were apparently under the impression we were keeping it in a closet somewhere.
And how do you know this?
The "Eye of Magnus"?
I can appreciate that this... thing, this Orb... It's very impressive. Very unique, and definitely worth studying.
But let's not jump to any conclusions, or assign it importance beyond what we're certain of.
Well now that's an odd question. Why in the world would you be asking?
Mages based out of Cyrodiil. They fancy themselves the Imperial Authority on magic these last few hundred years.
My understanding is that all they really do is make noise in an attempt to curry favor from the Emperor. Lots of politics, little magic.
I was quite surprised to find them on our doorstep. They seemed amiable enough, but their line of questioning made me... uneasy.
It became clear they're trying to hoard powerful artifacts, looking to consolidate power.
Did you really?
And he specifically mentioned the Staff of Magnus?
I... I'm impressed with your initiative. Of course, someone will need to follow up on this.
I'm sorry, what?
Well... I'd certainly love to have such a powerful staff, but I'm not really sure that any of us need it.
Well, it's said to be very powerful. Has the capacity to store an incredible amount of magical power, as the story goes.
But it's more myth than anything at this point. I've no doubt that it actually exists, but no one has seen it in what, decades? Longer? I'm not sure.
The only time I've heard it mentioned was when those Synod characters showed up some months ago looking for it.
Here you go. This should cover it.
I think you'll find this payment to be more than fair.
Well, well. I was looking for this little beauty.
If you happen across any other unusual trinkets like this, be sure and bring them to me. I promise it'll be worth the effort.
Leave that dog alone!
Hey, that's a valuable animal!
Okay, okay!
All I know is that Idolaf made some effort to find out what happened to him, but was told to stop asking questions.
He got some letter from the Legion and has left it alone since then.
I'll prove it to you, all right? I'll get you the letter. Just... meet me at the statue of Talos, and don't tell anyone about this.
I'll round up what men I can, and meet you outside Northwatch Keep. We'll get in and out as fast as possible.
I'll see you there.
No? So you don't know anything about my brother?
Then what are you doing here?
Brother? Is... Is that you?
What in the world?
What makes you think I know anything? Everyone knows he's dead and gone!
Thorald... Thorald Gray-Mane? Fighting for the Stormcloaks, disappeared and believed dead?
You really don't know anything about this, do you?
I used some contacts in the Legion, asked some questions. Was told I should mind my own business.
You what?
Oh no. No, no, no. This can't be happening.
Please, you can't tell anyone. It must remain a secret! I'll do whatever you want.
That's privileged information. Now move along.
Thorald? Thorald!
Last I'd heard, he'd been carted off to Northwatch Keep. Ever heard of it?
If you go in there, you don't come out. Ever.
Here, see for yourself. This is the last word I received. I'm telling you, he's gone for good.
A likely story. They won't even do their own dirty work now, eh? Well, you won't get the better of me!
Now tell me where my brother is!
Who are you? What are you doing here?
Are you working with the Battle-Borns? Speak now before I put you in the ground!
I'm here! I knew I'd find you!
Because they've got Thorald locked away somewhere, and if they find me here I'll disappear too!
I am not going to let that happen!
There's no time. We've got to get you out of here! Come on, let's go!
I can't do this all on my own. Are you... are you offering to help me?
Do you have news? Did you find something?
Please, anything you can do to help find my Thorald...
What's there to say? You go against the Empire, you wind up dead. Thorald got what was coming to him.
Best not to ask too many questions about it. People might get the wrong idea about you.
Between the two of us, his family is better off thinking he's dead.
Thorald and I have history, all right? We used to be friends. So I looked into it when he disappeared.
I found out the Thalmor have him. And they don't let anyone go.
So I've kept my mouth shut. Like I said, his family is better off believing he's gone, because he's never coming back.
He fought on the wrong side, and he paid for it. That's all I have to say.
Oh come on, leave it alone. He's gone. There's no use in talking about it anymore.
I wish everyone could just get past that and get on with their lives.
It's been a while now. I miss him, but it's clear he's not coming back.
He knew what he was getting into. I don't... I don't blame anyone.
Unngh...  Uhhh...
Tell me, Vignar. Was all this worth it?
How many of those corpses lining our streets wear the faces of men who once called you friend? What about their families?
Enough! That's enough. I surrender... I surrender.
Peace! Everyone stand down. That's an order. Stand down!
A convenient position to hold now. But mark my words, old man, in the days to come, Ulfric will spread his rebellion thin. And what then?
We need the Empire, as much as it needs us. We Nords are the Empire! Our blood built it. Our blood sustains it! You of all people should know that.
If this was my Empire, I'd be able to worship whoever I damned well pleased. You wish to see an Empire without Talos? Without its soul?
We should be fighting those witch-elves, not bending knee to them. The Emperor is nothing more than a puppet of the Thalmor.
Skyrim needs a High King who will fight for her, and Whiterun needs a Jarl who will do the same.
You think this is personal?
The Empire has no place in Skyrim... not any more. And you? You have no place in Whiterun anymore.
Need something?
Yeah? What?
Yeah?
Good afternoon.
Good evening.
These Stormcloaks are ridiculous. They won't shop here because they think I'll sell them poisoned tonics. I should just go back to Cyrodiil.
You look rather pale. Could be Ataxia. It's quite a problem back home in Cyrodiil.
Maybe a hardy Nord like you doesn't need a cure-all. Hmm? What about an invisibility potion?
I have several elixirs for sale.
I enjoy this work well enough, but I'm ready to retire. I've been thinking of selling the inn to Ysolda. Anyway, what do you need?
What can I get you?
Hungry, tired, or just plain thirsty?
I'm just out and about, love. The Bannered Mare can do without me for a bit, I suspect.
I don't get much time time off from the tavern, but I enjoy it when I do.
I'll get back to the Bannered Mare soon enough. Just need to stretch my legs a bit...
If it's work you're looking for, Hulda's the one to talk to.
If you need more food or drink, speak to Hulda.
Mind your manners and pay your bills, and you'll always be welcome.
There was a great battle here in Whiterun. It was quite thrilling! I must compose a song about it someday.
I'm a bard by trade. Perhaps you've heard of me?
I don't have much of a reputation in Skyrim yet, but I'm working on that, one sweet lady at a time. Haha!
No doubt they still tell tales of Mikael and his sweet songs across Cyrodiil, where last I traveled.
I enjoy steady patronage in the Bannered Mare. I do hope you'll watch me perform.
Welcome to Belethor's General Goods. Welcome, indeed.
I know what you're thinking: "This Belethor, how could he part with such magnificent treasures?"
Imperials, Stormcloaks... I don't care who's in charge around here. I just want to make money!
I'd even buy one of your relatives, if you're looking to sell. Ha ha ha ha. That's a little joke.
You just let me know what you need. I am at your beck and call.
Want to hear a little Nord wisdom? You don't really know a woman 'til you've had a strong drink and a fistfight with her.
You look like maybe you need a new weapon. Something big, perhaps? An axe maybe? Or a greatsword?
We've got small weapons as well as big. Maces, daggers, that sort of thing.
Most of our weapons were crafted by Adrianne Avenicci. My woman's an artist, is what she is.
Crush, clobber, slash or slice. We've got a weapon for any occasion.
Need something? Talk to my brother, Elrindir.
Do you hunt? The plains outside Whiterun are ripe with game.
You should try the venison. It's delicious.
Shhh. You'll scare the deer.
Oh! You startled me. I wasn't expecting company out here.
Ah, hello my Nord friend. In the market for some hunting supplies?
Potema herself? Please tell me you stopped them.
Ah Potema. Former queen of Solitude and one of the most dangerous necromancers in recorded history.
She was responsible for the Empire's near collapse almost five hundred years ago. I believe I have a book about her...
Any resident of Solitude can tell you, she was the deadliest person ever to come out of Skyrim.
Queen of Solitude, granddaughter of Emperor Uriel Septim II, five hundred years ago. Her fight for succession nearly tore the empire apart.
After twenty years of fighting, she had raised armies of the dead, led by vampire generals. If she returns to power, nobody could stop her.
A curious figure. Unrepentantly evil and nasty, of course, but also astonishingly brilliant, and obviously quite a necromancer at her end.
She was always so close to being Empress, but despite her machinations, it was never to be hers.
Ironically, if she were alive today, she'd be the only living member of the Septim bloodline. By all rights, she would now be Empress.
The College of Winterhold is an amazing sight. I've never set foot on the grounds, but always wanted to.
There is an unnatural chill in the air. Some evil power is at work in this place.
I do not envy those creatures that make a home in such cold, dark places as this.
Careful. Our footsteps will echo in every chamber and tunnel.
By the Nine, I'd pay good money for a pint of ale to warm my bones!
Curse this chill! I should have dressed more warmly.
If this cold numbs my fingers any worse, I'll scarcely be able to hold a weapon.
Gods, I'd hate to lose my footing in a place like this. A fall on this stony floor will break bones.
Take extra care you don't hit your head on a low-hanging bit of stone.
Mind your step. I think I spied a patch of ice on the floor.
Don't go stopping for a nap. If you fall asleep in this place, you'll never wake up.
Are you sure you can find your way? It would be easy to get lost in here.
This cold is unbearable. It's all I can do not to shiver until my teeth rattle.
Would it be foolish to stop for a while and light a fire? I suppose it would.
Gods willing, we'll find a chest full of gold in one of these chambers.
At times like this, I truly appreciate the joys of a warm fire and a stiff drink.
There are strange echoes in these caves. Keep you eyes open.
If a man can't feel at home in a freezing cave, then he isn't truly a Nord.
It seems warmer down here than it was outside.
I have to admit, I admire your courage. Most folk in Skyrim wouldn't go near a place like this.
When I was a boy, my friends and I used to play "seek the wumpus" in caves much like this.
In a mine, the poor man labors to find precious stones that only the rich man can afford.
I wonder how many poor souls have lost their lives working these tunnels.
These earthen passages do not agree with me. I already miss the wind and sky.
The fewer of these mines we venture into, the happier I'll be.
The cloying air in this place is almost unbearable.
It is strange to think that these chambers were once home to the ancient Nords.
My nose and mouth are so full of dust, I can hardly breath.
Wouldn't it be something if we found a vein of gold?
It would be easy for someone -- or something -- to sneak up on us down here.
Are you sure you wouldn't rather explore, say, a nice tavern?
Let's try not to make too much noise. I've heard that cave-ins can start that way.
I wonder what they were mining down here.
Let's hope the miners didn't unearth anything besides precious stones, if you take my meaning.
Not for all the gold in Skyrim would I spend my life working in these tunnels.
I've heard that miners sometimes die from poisonous gases trapped beneath the ground.
I'm already looking forward to getting back above ground.
You know what I miss the most right now? Fresh air.
It takes uncommon strength to work the mines in Skyrim. The earth here is cold and hard, like the heart of a Nord.
There's an old maid's tale that says our ancestors dug these mines with their bare hands.
I've half a mind to grab a pickaxe and start digging. Never know what you might find.
You've done a great thing, today. It doesn't matter who you support in the war; Potema would have been a blight on the land for both sides.
Without you this would have been a disaster. I should have paid more heed to Varnius' warnings.  I won't make that mistake again.
Your not winnin' points with me with that attitude.
When you find your nerves, then we'll talk.
Fine, but you're not gettin' any wealthier by weaslin' out of everythin' I offer you.
You're not going to get richer with that attitude.
You need to bloody well get your priorities straight if you want to make it around here.
Too bad, looks like I need to find someone else to take care of business.
If you think you'll make your fortune kickin' around the Flagon all day, that's where your wrong.
And here I thought you were a master criminal. Guess I was wrong.
Good... here's your assignment.
Finally, someone with backbone. Here you go.
Now that's what I like to hear. Off you go.
Nice. Here, this is what you'll need to know.
Keep this up and you'll be rich enough retire before you know it. Good luck.
Just remember the rules... keep it clean and keep it quiet. Here's the details.
Just keep your eyes open and your mouth shut and you'll do fine. Let me give you the details.
Good job. Now here's your part of the deal.
Here's your share. Well done.
Nicely done. Here's your share.
And here's the spoils. Plenty more where this came from!
I can pat you on the back all day, but coin's coin, right?
This kind of work suits you... and is going to end up makin' you rich.
Couldn't have done it better myself. Here's your coin.
If you don't watch it, I may actually start to respect you. Job well done.
What's the matter with you? We said keep it clean! Well, you can just forget about your payment.
Sure you finished, but you killed someone to do it. Next time listen to the rules if you want to get paid.
You needed to do this clean and quiet, and you failed. Now we need to use your payment to cover your mistake.
I hope you weren't expectin' to get paid for this blundered job. Next time, the mark stays alive... you hear me?
This ain't the Dark Brotherhood. Next time, you keep the job clean and you'll get your coin.
Hey, you approached me. You want to play coward, that's your problem.
Look, I don't like wasting my time. Come back when you decide to get serious.
Not interested? Fine. Then let me offer it to someone with a little more backbone.
You need to start pulling your own weight around here, otherwise I might "forget" to offer you any more jobs.
Fine. I'll just keep all this coin for myself then.
Look, if you just want to sit around the Flagon all day getting drunk, that's your business. Just quit wasting my time.
The last time I checked, gold coins aren't falling from the heavens. You better take some jobs if you want to earn your share.
Keep turning down jobs like this and the Guild will begin to lose respect for you.
Good. Here's what you need to do.
Fine. Here's everything you need to know.
All right. Take this and get out of here.
Fine. Just keep it clean. Here you go.
Good, just remember the order: do job first, get drunk second. Now take this and go.
Here you go. Hope you don't muck it up.
Here. Just don't make me look like an ass in front of the Guild and mess it up.
Whatever, just get it done. Here's the job.
Oh, you're back? I suppose you'll be wanting your pay then.
You did the job and you did it clean; that's all we ever ask. Enjoy the gold.
Didn't muck it up I hear. Good. Here's your share.
I suppose you want your share now? Here you go.
And with no strings attached. Not bad. Here, got some coin for you.
Amazing what people leave laying around for us to take, isn't it?
Fine. I guess you'll need your coin then.
Here's your gold. Try not to spend it all in one place.
Are you out of your mind? We said no bloodshed! Well, guess what. You can just forget about your gold.
I thought it was crystal clear... no one dies on these jobs. Remember that next time you want to get paid.
You an assassin or a thief? Next time, keep your blade sheathed if you want to make any gold.
You drew blood, so you aren't getting paid. That's the rules. You don't like it, take it up with Brynjolf.
You idiot! We have to cover your mess up and that costs us gold... which means you don't get any.
I need some more supplies. Can you help out with that?
Some other time? That's not going to help me much, now is it?
If you're feeling adventurous, I'm always looking for someone to procure valuable books from some more... dangerous locations.
If you can bring me three more, that would be helpful.
Well all right. Here, I scribbled down the last place I'd heard about. Check it out, but be careful.
Ah, yes. Thank you. I'll get to work on this right away.
Sure is. I've got another client -- wants me to do some enchanting. You can do the pick-up for me, if you want.
In fact, there is. I need a soul gem filled, if you're up to it.
Ah. Some other time, perhaps?
Excellent. Thank you, I'll see that this gets put to good use.
Yes, a Grand one. Soon, please.
Here, take this Grand Soul Gem, fill it for me, and bring it right back. I'll be waiting.
Yes, yes I did. I've already sent instructions, you just need to make sure this arrives in one piece.
I'm running low on Grand Soul Gems.
All right. Be quick about it, will you? This is a good way for the College to bring in some money.
Here's the name and location of the client.
No! Not again! I shall not be denied again!
You must be the one Falk spoke so highly of. Welcome.
I'll admit, even I'm having a hard time disagreeing with you...
What are you waiting for? My husband is out there somewhere with that lunatic. Find them! Do what you've been tasked to do!
Hush, Veezara. You were very brave. Astrid may well be dead if not for you.
Look, we've got to deal with this situation. You've got to deal with this situation.
Achh... Thank you, dear. You are most kind. The jester's cut feels as bad as it looks, I'm afraid.
It's all up to you now... Listener.
Bested by a fool. Who's the fool now, hmm?
She's right. I'll be forever in your debt, dearest brother. Now be quiet. Just... just rest.
I want you to find that miserable little fool and end his life!
But first... find my husband. Make sure he's all right.
After the attack, Arnbjorn flew into a rage. When Cicero left... Arnbjorn went after him. They disappeared into the wild.
Search Cicero's room. Maybe there's something in there that sheds some light on where he might have gone.
Let me know the minute you find something. I've got to see to Veezara, and calm everyone down.
Damn it, this never should have happened! We knew better. We knew better, and still we let our guards down. Agh!
Just try to relax, Veezara. Let the elixir do its work. You'll feel better, shortly.
Move along, citizen.
I know that he still worships Talos. That he hates the Thalmor almost as much as the Stormcloaks do.
That he worries about being chased from Whiterun.
That he... that I'm...  that I don't have the same mother as my brother and sister.
Regrettably, I cannot reach your plane so directly.
But I forgive you for not knowing who I am. Few hear my whispers anymore.
I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers.
I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal.
The boy was good at sussing out secrets. You, I expect to take a more active role.
This castle is old. Lots of places nobody's been in a long while.
Places where you can overhear things. See things.
And the Whispering Lady.
She won't tell me her name.
I've gotten good at listening to keyholes. At the door in the basement, I hear her talking to me.
I thought I was caught, but she started telling me even more secrets.
But I can't open the door.
In the basement. Trust me, you'll see it.
I bet she'll talk to you, too.
First, you must open this door.
A piece of my power has been locked away behind it, and even my eyes cannot see past the seals.
I'd much rather it be in the hands of an ambitious and talented person such as yourself.
What do you think? Are we going to let Ulfric dictate terms to us before the negotiations even start?
By Ysmir's beard, the nerve of those Imperial bastards, eh?
To think that we would sit down with that... Thalmor bitch. I say she walks or we walk.
Hagraven or horker, it matters not. We've got the arrows to fell any game.
So what can I get you?
Look at you! Betcha think you're something, huh?
All right, you convinced me. Doubt I'd be much good to you, anyway. The little fop cut me pretty deep.
But I slashed him good. Pretty sure I severed an artery. Don't know what you're going to find in there... but you can probably just follow the blood.
Oh, it's ancient. Apparently one of the first Sanctuaries in Skyrim. It hasn't been used by the Brotherhood in close to a hundred years.
If you say Cicero has gone there, it stands to reason that he's familiar with the place. That means you'll be fighting him on his own ground.
Tread carefully.
In there! Through the door. Some old Sanctuary, by the looks of it. I would have followed him, but I don't know the phrase.
Good hunting. I'll see you back at the Sanctuary.
Should have figured Astrid would send you.
Look, the Dark Brotherhood is a family. This Sanctuary is a family. And, we've always welcomed those... shunned by society.
Werewolves, wizards, eternal ten year-old vampires... what does it matter? In truth, I've rarely met a lunatic I haven't liked.
Cicero's problem isn't his madness. It's an adherence to an ancient, outmoded way of life. The Night Mother's ways... simply are not our ways.
He just couldn't accept that. And now he'll have to pay the price.
What gave it away?
Ha ha ha ha ha.
Yeah, got to admit that little jester is good with that butter knife. But don't worry, I gave as good as I got.
Good. I'm spread thin. I could use the help.
We're taking the city. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Good. If you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Now get going.
She began to spend a lot of time with Alva. Yet just a week before, she despised her.
In fact, the night she disappeared, she was supposed to meet Alva.
Alva told me later that she never showed up. I never got to tell her good bye.
You found me! Laelette was trying to find me too, but I'm glad you found me first.
Laelette was told to burn mommy and me, but she didn't want to.
She wanted to play with me forever and ever.
She kissed me on the neck, and I got so cold that the fire didn't even hurt.
Laelette thought she could take me and keep me, but she can't. I'm all burned up.
I'm tired. I'm going to sleep for a while now.
Now there's a mystery.
She ran away when the war started. Her husband, Thonnir, was devastated. No note or nothin'.
I'm pretty sure she up and joined the Stormcloaks.
Laelette? I thought she left to join the Stormcloaks. Ah! My poor Laelette!
Very well. Here. Take this back to Brynjolf and tell him he'll have no more trouble from me.
My wife died. Things won't be the same without her.
My husband died. Things won't be the same without him.
My father died. Things won't be the same without him.
My mother died. Things won't be the same without her.
My son died. Things won't be the same without him.
My sister died. Things won't be the same without her.
My brother died. Things won't be the same without him.
My aunt died. Things won't be the same without her.
