My uncle died. Things won't be the same without him.
My niece died. Things won't be the same without her.
My nephew died. Things won't be the same without him.
My cousin died. Things won't be the same without her.
My cousin died. Things won't be the same without him.
My kinsman died. Things won't be the same without him.
My kinswoman died. Things won't be the same without her.
My friend died. Things won't be the same without her.
My friend died. Things won't be the same without him.
My girlfriend died. Things won't be the same without her.
My boyfriend died. Things won't be the same without him.
All right! I get it. I'll pay on time from now on. Just don't smash anything else.
Here, take your gold and leave me in peace.
Not Lady Dibella? No, please! I can't lose her!
I get the message. Here, take your gold. I hope you choke on it!
How I envy you. I can only imagine the excitement of seeing history through the eyes of another!
Sadly, I am resigned to just reading of its wonders through my research of the Skull.
It... it worked. Mara be praised!
You vanished after drinking the Torpor and materialized on the other side. I have never seen anything quite like it.
I thought I was clear regarding the dangers that awaited you within the Dreamstride.
However, this hardly matters any longer as you appear to have exited unscathed.
After what you just experienced, you are not amazed? I am at a loss for words.
We used to think the pirates had an army of battlemages at their side.
As it turns out, it was just one. Haldyn. I've since learned more about him.
The most dangerous sort of man -- quick to anger, but patient in exacting vengeance.
He also has a fearsome control of Destruction magic.
I wish you the best of luck.
A very powerful family in Windhelm.
They run a private shipping operation, and would love to have the docks to themselves.
Wouldn't surprise me if they were in league with the pirates, somehow.
Not yet, no. But perhaps an intrepid person such as yourself could help with that.
They have a dark elf who oversees their operations. Suvaris Atheron.
She's meticulous to a fault, and keeps a logbook with every transaction she makes.
If it were to somehow end up in my hands, I wouldn't argue.
I know this place looks horrible -- please don't hold it against the Company.
Please excuse the mess.
If you get me the logbook, I'll have proof of Shatter-Shield's treachery.
Let me know when you've learned more about the pirates.
She's probably the one you ought to talk to.
We're so grateful for your help.
You think Alva...but that means...
Ye gods! You think Alva is a vampire?
I don't know much. Only the old tales about the ancient heroes who would use the Voice to defeat the enemies of Skyrim.
They say the Emperors used to be Dragonborn, but that all ended when Martin died during the Oblivion Crisis.
The Greybeards can tell you much more than I can. They're the masters of the Way of the Voice and taught Tiber Septim himself.
The only thing I remember is the old story of Tiber Septim being called Dragonborn by the Nords, before they switched sides and joined the Empire.
No doubt those Greybeards up at High Hrothgar will talk your ear off about it.
They live in seclusion on the slopes of the Throat of the World, the great mountain of Skyrim.
They're masters of the Way of the Voice. If you really are Dragonborn, they're the ones who can teach you how to use your power.
Near as I can tell, a bunch of ancient Nord hermits who live on top of a mountain.
Never heard of them talking to anybody before. If they really do want to talk to you, you'd better go find out what they want. You never know.
In the old tales, Nord heroes would use the power of their Voice to Shout down the gates of cities and strike down their enemies.
But only the Greybeards study the Way of the Voice any more. If you can Shout... you need to talk to them.
Old Nord folk tales always talk about Shouting their enemies to pieces. I always thought it was just a myth.
If you think you might be able to do that kind of thing, the Legion could certainly use your help.
Go talk to the Greybeards, see what they can tell you.
I'm just the Housecarl. If you need something, you should speak to Kraldar.
We may not have as much to offer as Whiterun or Solitude, but we'll do what we can to make your stay a pleasant one.
Winterhold doesn't offer much, but we like living here. Been here all our lives.
If you're here for the College, don't let the folks in town bother you. Just try and keep to yourself.
The Mages at the College keep to themselves for the most part, so we don't see much of them.
Turns out who runs Winterhold doesn't change much. Can't say I'm surprised.
If you've business with the College, you're welcome to stay here. It's where most of our business comes from, in fact.
Unlike some folks here in town, we have nothing against the College or the people that come and go.
Don't worry about Nelacar. He has a permanent room here at the Inn, but he keeps to himself and doesn't cause any trouble.
Let me know if you need anything. We're here to provide.
Without us looking out for Winterhold, it's doomed. Simply doomed.
Korir will be the first to tell you that if it weren't for that College, we'd all be better off. I agree with him.
Winterhold might yet thrive if that College had never been built.
We do the best we can, even though we have next to nothing. But we Nords don't give up, not ever.
Gaius Maro still lives. I am surprised. I had assumed you were more... competent.
Assur is going to grow up to be a great man, because he'll have lived through such hardship.
Driven out of my own home. After all this, I can't believe it.
You know Winterhold used to be the seat of power in Skyrim? Now it's a shell.
Few will admit it, but we know the truth about the Great Collapse.
Those cursed mages... It's their fault Winterhold is gone.
Azura has seen your coming, traveler. It was not curiosity, but fate, that has led you here.
Azura has given me the gift of foresight. I had a vision of you walking up the steps to this altar long before you were born.
You have been chosen to be her champion. I know it is unexpected, but do not worry. It will all unfold as she has predicted.
The College is the worst thing that's ever happened to Winterhold, maybe to Skyrim.
What is it now?
You want something?
Empire's in charge now, huh? Only a matter of time before we start getting bothered by them Thalmoricars... err, Justimors... Y'know, them elves.
I'm just trying to have a quiet drink here, all right?
It's not like there's anything to do here but drink anyway.
You ask me, not enough of Winterhold fell off into the sea.
Nothing to do here but drink till you forget you got nothing to do.
If you're looking for Ranmir, he's either passed out in bed, or working on winding up that way.
My brother's a good-for-nothing drunk. We'd have lost the house by now if it were up to him.
Just because we live in the middle of nowhere doesn't give Ranmir the right to drink himself into oblivion.
Ranmir has no problem drinking himself under the table, so I have to be the one to make ends meet.
I'll sell just about anything, if it's worth my time. Keep that in mind, will you?
If you're here for sight-seeing, well then you've seen the sights. Might as well head somewhere warmer.
Don't worry about those Stormcloaks. You're safe from them here. As if they'd want the place anyway.
Keep clear of the College, and keep yourself out of trouble.
Hard to believe I ever complained about Riften being cold...
Take care if you're venturing too far from town. That's a bitter wind coming off the Sea of Ghosts.
Winterhold belongs to Skyrim, not the Imperials, or the Mages.
By order of the Jarl, stop right there!
You have committed crimes against Skyrim and her people. What say you in your defense?
You're the one who conspired with the Forsworn against Markarth! You're coming with me, murderer!
Don't take too long figuring it out. I've got a war to run here.
Well, soldier? Are you up to the task?
I knew I could count on you, soldier.
Help! Someone's being attacked!
Help! I'm being attacked!
Last mistake.
Hey! Hands off!
Hey, that isn't yours!
Help! Murder!
Murdered! In cold blood!
By the gods, no!
Why you...
Pickpocket! We've got a pickpocket!
That's it. Don't say I didn't warn you.
This is your last warning. Get away from there, or else.
You're trespassing here. You'd better clear out.
You should have listened. Guards! Help! Trespasser!
Guards! Trespasser!
I'm not going to warn you again. Get out, or I'll call the guards!
Last warning. Leave, now.
It's time for you to leave, my friend.
You, of all people. Get out!
Can't a man have a bit of privacy? Get out of here!
Can't a woman get a moment to herself? Leave me!
Just stay out of my way.
Good. I'll be waiting right here.
I'm tired, I haven't slept well in weeks.
You kill the giant, bring me the hammer. We go back to Largashbur, I tell everyone I did it.
You keep your mouth shut, and I pay you. Handsomely.
Excellent work. You have a true fighting spirit. Did that better than I could have.
Of course, I can't let you leave here alive. Wouldn't want anyone finding out.
Yamarz has fallen. It is now up to you to return the giant's hammer to Atub.
The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right.
Yamarz has revealed himself as a coward and a schemer, and has earned the fate of all who follow that path.
The tribe may yet survive, but only if you are willing to do what is right.
Take the giant's hammer to Atub, and tell her what has happened here.
All right, I'll go kill this giant.
Unless, of course, you'd like to make some extra gold.
Fine, then wait here. This should only take a second.
Hmph. Suit yourself.
Stop, outlander. You must pay for your crimes in blood or gold.
You've broken the Code of Malacath. Your choice - pay with your blood or your gold.
Today is a good day to die.
Fight well, and I'll make sure your corpse is not dishonored.
We haven't forgotten your crimes, outlander.
Huh. Okay. We'll test your mettle another day.
Typical outlander weakling. But your gold will strengthen our clan.
Then your poverty is my reward. Kill or be killed!
Whiterun once again belongs to its rightful heirs. It's a glorious day for Skyrim.
For thirty years, I served the Legion as a commander. Fought in the Great War.
We Gray-Manes have been feuding with the Battle-Borns for years. There's no more bitter enemy than an old friend.
Vignar and I have our hands full. It's no small task, running an entire hold.
I had a bad time a few years back, an' I turned to drink. But Vignar, he wouldn't let me stay weak.
Olfrid, patron of the great Clan Battle-Born, a name I'm sure you know well.
I'm as much a Nord as the next man, but I'm no fool. I don't live in the past, like those Gray-Manes.
Eorlund Gray-Mane's a stubborn fool. Our friendship ended when he turned his back on the Emperor.
It's a miracle these Stormcloaks haven't robbed us blind yet. I'm sure it's only a matter of time.
Evgir Unslaad has come at last. The "Season Unending." War... without end.
Can you believe those pathetic Gray-Manes still show their face in public?
There was love between our families. For generations. Now, there is only enmity.
Has the Empire forsaken us? How could Whiterun fall into the hands of these stinking half-wit Nords?
Avulstein Gray-Mane and I were best friends as children. If I see that traitor again, I'll throw him in jail myself!
The cold, frightening truth is that the Empire represents progress. Anyone who fears progress is a coward.
The Stormcloaks are content to live in the past forever. No wonder the Provincials think we're all baseless savages.
Well met, kinsman.
You know what's wrong with Skyrim these days? Everyone is obsessed with death.
People have forgotten humor, poetry and romance. I love a good sword fight as much as the next man, but there's more to life.
It's my desire to enter the Bards College. My parents are hesitant, but I pray they let me attend.
All I want it is to escape from this madness. And if Olfina can come with me, all the better. But I've probably said too much.
Need something? Unlike the rest of my family, I've got work to do.
This war's as stupid as our feud with Clan Gray-Mane.
My brother Jon's got the right of it - join the Bards College and avoid this pointless war.
My boy Lars has been getting beat senseless, and by a girl, no less!
It's no fun getting pushed around all the time...
My friend Olava? The seer? She says things will get better for me, once I start sticking up for myself.
I can't fight. Not even a little bit. My family thinks I'm a little milk drinker.
Come to Olava for a reading, did you? Sorry, pup, but I'm not quite in the mood.
Oh, I tell the future, all right. When I feel up to the task, that is. But I'm afraid my energies haven't quite peaked yet today.
Maybe some other time I'll reveal your destiny, hmm? Tea leaves, palm reading, crystal scrying... Oooh! Maybe trepanning? Ha ha ha ha.
I hope you're looking at me like that because you want to buy something?
I work with my mother, to sell fruits and vegetables. It's fun most days, but hard work.
You'd be surprised how much work it is selling things at a stall. It's not all standing around and chatting.
Did you know there are some fruits that actually grow better in the cold weather?
You have come! You have come to hear the word of Talos!
Praise Talos and all the Divines! Whiterun has been liberated by the true sons of Skyrim!
There are those who would silence the Dragon's truth! But not I! Not Heimskr! His word will be known!
The Imperials silence us because they fear us! They are cowards! Cowards and fools who have forgotten the truth!
The truth, child of Talos, is that the Dragon's children have come! To purge the world in fire and righteousness!
He has returned... Oh, how Talos has returned. Helgen has been purged in his light. Will Whiterun be next?
I spend a lot of time at the market stalls so I can learn the merchant's trade.  I need more experience if I'm going to run an inn someday.
Once I've made enough money trading with the Khajiit caravans, I'm going to buy the Bannered Mare from Hulda.
We Nords are strong, and we're as tough as stone. But I'll bet you didn't know there's some of us with a good head for business, too.
They say there's nothing a Nord woman can't do if she puts her mind to it.
There's some company I certainly prefer over others. Have you met Mikael? The most amazing bard I've ever heard.
You tried mercenary work? It might suit you.
What're you lookin' at? I'm not afraid of you, ya know. Even if you are my elder.
Boys, girls, dogs, elders - there's nobody I won't fight!
Stupid Lars Battle-Born. I fight him almost every day, but it's not much of a fight, 'cause he don't fight back.
If you'll excuse me, I really just want to get home.
Nothing like a good adventure or romance to lose yourself in.
Really, I wish my girl would read more. It would certainly keep her out of all those fights.
Oh, thank Arkay. Please, take this gold for your troubles.
I spend so much time among the dead, I sometimes forget how much I miss the company of the living.
As Whiterun's priest of Arkay, I spread his word whenever I'm able. In the meantime, most of my hours are spent with the city's dearly departed.
With this terrible war, I'm a bit overwhelmed. As are the catacombs.
Well, would you look at that! Argonian Ale! My thanks, friend. Here, take this here healing potion. I, er, "found" it in the temple.
That Heimskr's crazy. Him and his talk o' Talos. Wish he'd just shut up.
What? Why you lookin' at me?
Oho! And what do you want, hmm?
You're in my home. That's right. This here place, Breezehome, it belongs to old Terek.
Now I don't care about no deeds, or laws, or other nonsense like that. This house was empty, so I settled in. And now it's mine.
You can't kick me out of here. You want me gone, you're gonna have to kill me.
I'm a generous man, to those who treat me right. Might be I'll even let you live here with me, if you mind your manners.
Hello, child of Kynareth.
These are trying times for us all, I know. Many come to the Temple of Kynareth to pray, and I assist them as best I'm able.
Lately it seems I am more nurse than priestess, tending to those who have suffered, both in mind and body, from this terrible war.
Come to chat with an old woman, hmm? Do your good deed for the day?
Looking for a horse? I own these stables, but Skulvar can help you with a purchase.
My friend Maven and I - have you met Maven, in Riften? - we know our place in this world. Know the place of others, too.
Do you like horses? You  should visit the stables. I own them, you see. And our stock is excellent.
Azura's Star! I knew the Lady of Twilight had sent you for a reason.
Hand it over to me. I will ask Azura to restore the Star to its original purity.
Do you get to the Cloud District very often? Oh, what am I saying - of course you don't.
Oh, it took years, but I earned my way to the top. I own Chillfurrow Farm, you see. Very successful business. Obviously.
I actually advise the Jarl on political matters. My input is invaluable, of course. But this is all probably a bit over your head.
Looking for my husband Nazeem? Check the Jarl's backside. That's usually where he stuffs himself these days.
Are you married? You look the type. Make sure you treat your wife with the respect she deserves.
Here you stand, talking to a married woman. Why? Am I that interesting? Or perhaps there are other things on your mind.
Men are all alike, from Skyrim to Hammerfell. They care only for war and politics, and treat their women like cattle.
Come to Dragonsreach to discuss the ongoing hostilities, like the rest of the "great warriors"?
It seems this damnable conflict has claimed everyone's passions of late. Me, I prefer my books, and my spells.
There's a tranquility in magic, don't you think? It is simply energy, to be harnessed by those with the will and knowledge to do so.
Got some good pieces out here, if you're looking to buy. More inside.
I don't claim to be the best blacksmith in Whiterun. Eorlund Gray-Mane's got that honor. Man's steel is legendary. All I ask is a fair chance.
Have you met my father? He's the steward, up at Dragonsreach.
I help my father in more ways than people realize.
You think all the council my father provides the Jarl is his alone? Hmph. Let's just say I advise the advisor, and leave it at that.
Exile or no, my duty to Balgruuf remains. Keep that in mind when you approach him, for I will be watching.
It seems our enemies have no end of spies, saboteurs and assassins to send against us.
Make a move toward Balgruuf, and you're dead.
Got the fleetest steeds in all o' Skyrim.
Man like you needs a warhorse, I reckon.
Lookin' to shatter records or bones? My beasts can do both.
Only thing better than ridin' a swift horse is ridin' a swift horse over the enemy.
Need a horse? Talk to my father, Skulvar.
Old lady Maiden-Loom owns the stables. But my father and me, we run things.
So I mostly sweep and shovel. You got a problem with that?
Father says I ain't smart enough to be stablemaster. Some day.
Yes, from what I've heard I expect Tolfdir will be occupied for some time. Hmm, that is a problem, isn't it?
I suppose someone else will need to look after the Apprentices for now.
I'm not sure what to expect here. Please be on your guard.
Well, perhaps it's time for some independent study.
Check with Urag gro-Shub in the Arcanaeum. See if he's aware of any references to this... thing that you and Tolfdir found.
I'd like to know as much as we can about it.
Come to gawk at the dark elf?
Well look at us. Like two gray specks in the snow.
I mean no offense. I am but a humble farmer, not a great wanderer, like you.
You! You'll help me! You help people, right? That's what you do?
So he has been punished? And what of us? What fate shall we suffer?
What? Why would he do such a thing?
You have done admirably, little one.
You have helped rid the Orcs of Yamarz in a way that avoids the destruction of their tribe.
They will become stronger for it. And you... You have proven your strength.
You have earned my favor for now, mortal, and so I give you a little gift. Use it well.
You, place that hammer on the shrine. You're the only one who's proven worthy in all of this...
Ah, that is good to hear, then.
But... What of Yamarz?
I see. He did not survive. He... He died well?
You'll have to prove yourselves, but I'm willing to give you a chance. Gularzob's in charge now. Let's hope he's a better chief.
Yamarz was a coward and a weakling. His deceitful ways have cost you all greatly.
Are you all right? I heard the commotion. Who was Cicero talking to? Where's the accomplice? Reveal yourself, traitor!
Volunruud? Well, that is interesting. I know this place...
Then what in Sithis' name is going on? Cicero spoke to the Night Mother, but she spoke to you? Is this just more of the fool's rambling?
By Sithis.
And... what did she say?
What? The Listener? What are you going on about? What is this lunacy?
It's true, it's true! The Night Mother has spoken! The silence has been broken! The Listener has been chosen!
By the almighty Divines. You've come. You've actually come. This dreadful Black Sacrament thing... it worked.
There are indeed. Three, to be exact.
There's Deekus, a shipwreck scavenger. Ma'randru-jo, a traveling caravaner. And Anoriath, a hunter.
Hmph. How about, instead of giving you another contract, I simply deride you for not finishing the one you still have?
So it would appear. Though I'd suspect she'd want you to complete your other contracts first. Do those, then we'll talk about something new.
Hmm?
No. No! Listen, I don't know what's going on here, but you take your orders from me. Are we clear on that?
The Night Mother may have spoken to you, but I am still the leader of this Family. I will not have my authority so easily dismissed.
I... I need time to think about all this. Go see Nazir - do some work for him. I'll find you when I'm ready to discuss the matter further.
By Sithis, this ends now! Back away, fool! Whatever you've been planning is over!
We need to talk.
Look. Something is happening here. I'm not sure entirely what that something is, but... Well, we need to find out.
If the Night Mother really did give you an order to talk to a contact, we'd be mad to ignore it.
And I think we'd both agree, Cicero's brought quite enough madness to this Sanctuary.
So go. Go to Volunruud. It's a crypt, pretty far to the northeast. Talk to this Amaund Motierre. And let's see where all this leads. Hmm?
I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to him! To the Listener!
I spoke only to the Night Mother! I spoke to the Night Mother, but she didn't speak to me. Oh no. She spoke only to her! To the Listener!
Amaund Motierre? I have no idea who that is.
But Volunruud... that I have heard of. And I know where it is.
As a matter of fact, I do. Two of them. One rather easy assignment, and another that should prove quite a challenge.
Your first target is an Orc bard named Lurbuk. The other is a vampire by the name of Hern.
Happy hunting.
I'm sorry, I don't. Come see me again later.
More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last set of contracts I gave you. Why don't you concentrate on eliminating that final target.
More work? My friend, you still haven't finished the last two contracts I gave you. Let's get those done, hmm?
I beg your pardon?
No! Someone help me! Please!
He was an Apprentice here at the College. Not very skilled, but got involved with a group of mages who took a liking to him.
When they left, he took off after them. Stole supplies and books from the College, I suppose as a way to ingratiate himself.
I know what you want. Word travels fast around here. Discovered some big mystery, huh?
Well you don't even need to ask. No, I don't have anything for you. Not anymore, anyway.
Let's just call it a "difference of opinion" with the College.
They were interested in research that goes outside the bounds of what the College allows, so they were... persuaded to leave.
I said not anymore. Orthorn stole a number of books when he ran off to Fellglow Keep to join those Summoners. Some kind of peace offering.
I think one of those volumes may have had some relevant information. If you want them, you'll have to talk to Orthorn.
And I would deserve it, wouldn't I?
I can't stop you if that's what you want to do. Hircine is too powerful.
But if you spare me, I can be a powerful ally to you. And I would promise to never return to civilized life.
I know now that I can't live among people.
Thank you for your help. I will make my home here, away from anyone I might hurt.
So be it.
A little girl is dead because of me.
Believe me, it wasn't anything I ever intended to do. I just... lost control.
I tried to tell them, but none of them believe me.
It's all on account of this blasted ring.
Thank the gods. Now let's deal with these other hunters. We hunt together!
Move along.
What is the music of life?
Oh ho. The whimpering Nord, eh? Yes, yes, I can see how you'd come to that conclusion. Interesting choice.
Welcome home.
Ah, at last! I hope you found the place all right.
Oh, I couldn't. Goes right through me. Besides, I have so many things to do...
Reeaaaallllyyyy?
Ooh, ooh, what kind of message? A song? A summons? Wait, I know! A death threat written on the back of an Argonian concubine! Those are my favorite.
Well? Spit it out, mortal. I haven't got an eternity!
Actually... I do. Little joke.
But seriously. What's the message?
You are the best Septim that's ever ruled. Well, except for that Martin fellow, but he turned into a dragon god, and that's hardly sporting...
You are far too hard on yourself, my dear, sweet, homicidally insane Pelagius. What would the people do without you? Dance? Sing? Smile? Grow old?
Yes, yes, as you've said, countless times before...
Last I saw him, he was visiting a friend in the Blue Palace. But no one as mundane as the Jarl. No, no... such people are below him.
No, he went into the forbidden wing of the palace, to speak with an old friend. Said it had been ages since they had last had tea.
Oh and you'll need the hip bone... it's very important. No entering Pelagius' Wing without that.
So many undesirables to contend with. Naysayers. Buffoons. Detractors. Why, my headsman hasn't slept in three days!
Hafrumph! Well then, if you're going to be like that... Perhaps it's best I take my leave. A good day to you sir. I said good day!
I have a saying - I'm not going to feel what you have to like. Or something like that.
He is a great man, but one rarely praised! He rules twin empires that span the length and breadth of our minds! All know him, but few can name him!
But... he has forbidden me from saying his name. He says it distracts him, and woe to those who draw his ire.
But you will know him when you see him. He's the one who made me like this!
I'll make an exception for you, but do not bring anything out when you leave. Too many dark deeds transpired in those halls...
If you really want to... Just be careful of the ghost! He snuck up on me once and scared me sick. It took a week for me to feel better...
Well... I suppose in that case, it's okay to let you in. But only briefly. Be careful, and come right back!
More tea, Pelly my dear?
My master has abandoned me! Abandoned his people. And nothing I say can change his mind.
Now he refuses to even see me. He says I interrupt his vacation! It's been so many years... Won't you please help?
Absolutely not. That wing has been sealed for hundreds of years, and for good reason. They say the ghost of Pelagius the Mad still haunts it.
Ghost or not, there are reminders of his dark rule that are best left buried away.
Not on your life. It's dangerous in there, and Falk doesn't even like me and Erdi going in every year to clean out the spiders.
Oh, I'd get in trouble for that. It's not allowed. Besides, it's scary in there!
Well, well. And you seem to be in one piece!
Thank you. I'll look these over, and inform Mirabelle if I find anything relevant.
"Night of Tears", eh? I remember this one. Well, isn't that interesting.
Did you read it yourself? If I recall it correctly, that has some interesting implications. You should mention that to Tolfdir.
And... here. I suppose you've earned these.
You. You must take the Lexicon. Free me of my burden.
Head to the Bannered Mare in Whiterun and look for Mallus Maccius. He'll fill you in on all the details.
No time for idle chatter. Mercer is awaiting your presence.
Don't come back until every one of those things are dead.
You're a firebrand, aren't you?
It's about time Brynjolf sent me someone with business sense. I was beginning to think he was running some sort of beggar's guild over there.
Mercer thinks he knows a way to identify this new thorn in our side. He wants to meet with you right away.
And if I were you I'd hurry. I've never seen him this angry before.
I'm going to keep this short 'cause we've got a lot to do.
Honningbrew's owner, Sabjorn, is about to hold a tasting for Whiterun's Captain of the Guard and we're going to poison the mead.
So you're the one that burned down Goldenglow Estate.
Do you have any idea what that little stunt you pulled is going to cost me?
I'm amazed you even bothered to show your face here.
What are you gawking at? Can't you see I have problems here?
You did this didn't you? I'll get you for this!
I believe Brynjolf wishes to speak with you. We're done here.
Yaaawwwwnn....
Oh, pardon me. Were you saying something? I do apologize, it's just that I find myself suddenly and irrevocably...
Bored!
I mean, really. Here you stand, before Sheogorath himself, Daedric Prince of Madness, and all you deem fit to do is... deliver a message? How sad.
Yes, yes, go. Leave me to my ceaseless responsibilities and burdens...
Were you now?
By whom?
Wait! Don't tell me! I want to guess!
Was it Molag? No, no... Little Tim, the toymaker's son? The ghost of King Lysandus? Or was it... Yes! Stanley, that talking grapefruit from Passwall.
Wrong on all accounts, aren't I?
Ha! No matter! Honestly, I don't want to know. Why ruin the surprise?
But more to the point. Do you - tiny, puny, expendable little mortal - actually think you can convince me to leave?
Because that's... crazy. You do realize who you're dealing with here?
Oh, you just don't understand. Without him, I am not free! Without him, I am doomed! All of his empire shall fall into chaos...
Jolly good guess!
But only half right. I'm a mad god. The Mad God, actually. It's a family title. Gets passed down from me to myself every few thousand years.
Now you. You can call me Ann Marie.
But only if you're partial to being flayed alive and having an angry immortal skip rope with your entrails.
If not... Then call me Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. Charmed.
Oh, no no no! No mistake at all. What you made was a choice. Granted, not a very wise choice, but these things happen. Ah, the folly of youth...
You know, you remind me of myself at a young age. All I cared about was riding narwhales and sleeping in honeycombs and drinking babies' tears...
Word of advice if you ride a Narwhale. Mind the pointy end.
Ah, but there I go, waxing poetic about my misspent youth. Now where were we? Ah yes. You're the mortal messenger. An I am...?
Honestly. Have you any idea?
Wrong!
Actually, you do. Sort of.
I am a part of you, little mortal. I am a shadow in your subconscious, a blemish on your fragile little psyche. You know me. You just don't know it.
Sheogorath, Daedric Prince of Madness. At your service.
He stays in the Pelagius Wing of the Blue Palace. The doors are locked, and only Falk Firebeard can grant entry.
But I hear the maids, Una and Erdi, know how to enter it for cleaning. Surely they can help for such an important matter?
Yes, but don't try to use force! He's far too mighty. He'd crush you like a clay doll, or drop you from the sky, or simply eat your brain right up!
Oh, but he probably won't do those things to you. Probably. Just remind him to come home, and... there'll be no fighting or unpleasantness. Probably.
He's just a little confused at the moment, is all. Knowing my master, he may very well reward you for bringing him to his senses!
How rude! Can't be bothered to host an old friend for a decade or two.
Please, take pity on an old madman!
Who among you can help me?
Without his guidance, our homeland is falling apart. North wages war against south! The holy flame flickers and dies! We need his return.
Oh, but... I'm sure he'll repay you when he comes to his senses! His favor is a powerful, powerful thing. And so very worth any... inconveniences.
Now that's the real question, isn't it? Because honestly, how much time off could a demented Daedra really need?
So here's what I'm going to do. I'm going to leave. That's right. I'm done. Holiday... complete. Time to return to the hum drum day-to-day.
On one condition.
You have to find the way out first. Good luck with that.
Is it?
The Companions? In Jorrvaskr? They're recruiting new members, from what I hear.
Be glad you're here in the city, and not out in Riverwood. Those poor sods don't even have a wall.
You should talk to the Jarl up in Dragonsreach, lend any aid you can.
Fear not. Come dragon or giant, we'll be ready.
Need a blade? You should talk to Adrianne, at the forge.
Need supplies? See Belethor at his store. Sleazy little man, but he has good stock.
If you need potions, you should try Arcadia's Cauldron.
I worry about the other settlements in the hold. Unwalled, lightly manned - prime targets for those damned dragons.
Rorikstead's a nice enough little hamlet, but a boring posting for a guard. If a dragon attacks, well... then it'll be a different story.
You look tired, friend. The Bannered Mare has beds for rent.
No lollygaggin'.
Let me guess - someone stole your sweetroll...
Could sure use a warm bed right about now...
Disrespect the law, and you disrespect me.
At least you remembered one of the things I asked. Let me see that.
Oh my. You would do this for me? Here, take it. I don't want anything to do with this wretched thing anymore.
Seek out the beast. He wanders these woods. Bring him down and... well, the Lord of the Hunt should smile on you.
I wish you luck, but should leave here while I still have my skin.
Should our paths cross again, I will remember your kindness. Farewell.
This is the Ring of Hircine. I was told it could let me control my transformations.
Perhaps it used to. But I'll never know. Hircine didn't care for my taking it, and threw a curse on it.
I put it on... and the changes just came to me. I could never guess when. It would be at the worst times.
Like... with the little girl.
I don't suppose there's a point in keeping the secret if I'm going to die in here anyway.
I'm sure you've heard of men who shift to beasts under the influence of the moons.
I am one of them. A werewolf. It's my secret, and my shame.
That's why I wanted the ring... it was said to give men like me control.
Now I may look like a man, but I still feel the animal inside of me, as strong as ever.
I had just come into Falkreath. They needed some help working the mill, and I thought that would be something safe. Something I could do.
When I saw the little girl, I was just... I could feel it coming on. I could taste the... I needed to hunt.
But this pitiful, limited body wasn't meant for hunting. Slow. No claws. Weak, mashing teeth for chewing cud.
I held in my rage as long as I could. But it boiled inside of me.
She looked so fragile. Helpless prey. And then...
I... I feel terrible about what happened. About what I did. It would probably be best for everyone if I just went away.
Sinding. Came through as a laborer. Seemed like a decent man.
He's stewing in the pit while we figure out what to do with him, if you've got the stomach to look at him.
What could drive a man to do something like this?
She was... he ripped her apart. Like a sabre cat tears a deer. We barely found enough of her to bury.
You take care of yourself, now. And if you ever find yourself in New Sheoth, do look me up. We can share a strawberry torte. Ta ta!
Luggage! Now where did I leave my luggage?
Ha! I do love it when the mortals know they're being manipulated. Makes things infinitely more interesting.
And as for you, my little mortal minion... Feel free to keep the Wabbajack. As a symbol of my... Oh, just take the damn thing.
Pelagius Septim the Third, once the Mad Emperor of Tamriel, now so boringly sane. I always knew he had it in him!
Let's make sure I'm not forgetting anything. Clothes? Check. Beard? Check! Luggage?
Yes, yes, that's quite enough celebration. Let's send you ahead, shall we?
Care to take a look around? This is not, I dare say, the Solitude botanical gardens. Have you any idea where you are? Where you truly are?
Welcome to the deceptively verdant mind of the Emperor Pelagius III. That's right! You're in the head of a dead, homicidally insane monarch.
Now, I know what you're thinking. Can I still rely on my swords and spells and sneaking and all that nonsense? Sure, sure. Or... you could use...
The Wabbajack! Huh? Huh? Didn't see that coming, did you?
Master! You've taken me back! Does this mean we're going home? Oh, happy times! I can't wait to...
Well, I suppose it's back to the Shivering Isles. The trouble Haskill can get into while I'm gone simply boggles the mind...
If I make it back to Solitude, I'll put in a good word for you with General Tullius.
The Legion could sure use someone like you. Good luck.
Stick close. Damn rebels could be anywhere. Don't worry, though. General Tullius will have them on the run soon enough.
The Stormcloaks pay blood for blood!
You dare dishonor the Legion?
Bad idea.
I killed a lot of High Elves in the Great War, and I didn't die. I guess that makes me a war hero.
The "Great War"... there was nothing great about it. Thousands died on both sides, and where did it get us?
Did we really save the Empire? Or did we just plant the seeds for Ulfric's uprising, and another war?
You one of those "Skyrim for the Nords" types?
And you're right, at least as far as I'm concerned.
Don't let Ulfric or some of these other short-sighted Nords bother you. Most of us are happy to welcome newcomers.
That's just what Ulfric wants you to think.
If that's what you really believe, then you're a fool. Never had much patience for fools, myself.
Good. Too many Nords in this town have been listening to Ulfric's narrow-minded words.
He's tough, loyal to his men and a good leader, but if you're not a Nord, Ulfric will never trust you.
Is that so? You ought to join the Stormcloaks, if you haven't already.
Seems to me they're a bunch of narrow-minded bigots, just like you.
Meantime, you best steer clear of me.
I'm sorry to be such a bother. We'll talk another time.
Not a day goes by that I don't think about returning to the ancestral home of my people.
Still, even with the rough treatment we've had at the hands of Ulfric and his lot, I've got ties here.
It's probably more trouble to pack up and leave than it is to stay. Besides, Ambarys can't run the cornerclub on his own.
Fresh fruit and vegetables for sale!
Fruits and vegetables, all ripe and ready for your table!
Take home Windhelm's finest and freshest produce today!
I always have bedlam jobs. Just remember to hit them fast and don't let them see you. Feel like going for it?
Assorted fruits and vegetables, all delicious and affordable!
Come and browse my fine selection! Everything's fresh and delicious!
I've a little of everything for sale, come and see for yourself!
Cookware, knives and other goods for the home, all at reasonable prices!
Of course. Don't forget to keep out of sight, it doesn't count if they catch you red-handed. You ready?
Taking an easy job, eh? Well, go and do some damage. Just stick to the shadows and keep out of sight. Want to go for it?
Assorted wares and tools you just can't live without! Come and see for yourself!
Buying and selling a wide range of assorted goods! The best selection in Windhelm!
Windhelm is divided into four quarters. The Gray Quarter is the one that's home to all the city's Dark Elves.
If it looks to you like an impoverished slum, that's because it is. Ulfric prefers that we live in squalor.
He has nothing but disdain for anyone who isn't a Nord. He tolerates us, but that's the extent of his hospitality.
Help the Dark Elves? Oh, you must have heard me talking to Malthyr.
The Dark Elves live in a run-down slum called the Gray Quarter. Ulfric's content to keep it that way.
I guess they think I can open Ulfric's eyes to their plight, and get him to lift a finger on their behalf.
I'm trying, but Ulfric is set in his ways. For him, there's two kinds of people in this world -- Nords, and the folk beneath them.
I'd be out there fighting the Imperials right now if it wasn't for my war-wound.
I'll find some way to finish my book.
Come slumming to the Gray Quarter, have you?
There's two kinds of Pit fighters -- good ones and dead ones.
I told you, I've got no patience for fools. Keep walking.
There's no glory in war. It's just something they tell soldiers so they'll risk their lives.
It bothers me that fewer people come to Windhelm these days.
I'll never have Master Nurelion's skill with potions.
Infos conditioned on Attack Delta go here.
We are ready to mount our final attack.
Join the forces mustering outside of [CAPITAL]. (Infos conditioned on Capital location go here)
The attack will be launched imminently. Gather your weaponry and get to the front lines... Now, soldier!
Skyrim has no use for your kind.
So be it.
Halt, outlander!
Surrender or die, outlander!
Clanfolk, stop the outlander!
Hold, there!
Lay down your weapons!
Stop, in the name of the Jarl!
Come quietly or taste the Jarl's justice!
By order of the Jarl, I command you to halt!
Sheathe your weapons and come quietly!
Very well. Empty the vessels into the sconce near the throne, then sit in the throne.
When you have done so, I will unbind Hevnoraak. He will awaken in a weakened state, but the lich is still to be reckoned with. Be ready.
Find the three vessels within Valthume, then return to me. With these we can defeat him.
In life, Hevnoraak drained his own blood from his body. His goal was to transfer his power back into himself after death, becoming a powerful lich.
The vessels contain that very blood. Empty them and you remove any chance he has of regaining his former powers.
The cave has a bad history. Long ago, Potema the Wolf Queen used it for necromantic rituals. That's where it got the name.
That was over 500 years ago. Nothing much down there now - but everyone's always convinced the cave is haunted.
More dangerous than you know. Left alone, however, he will awaken when he has grown too powerful to be stopped. This is the only way to be sure.
Yes, old friend, I'm afraid it's not good news.
When you broke up the binding Potema escaped. We've encountered some of her minions.
Styrr says she's still in spirit form or we'd all be dead already.
You've already done us a service in stopping the binding, but I need you to go talk to him, to see if Styrr can tell us what to do next.
This... it matches every description of the Phial that I've found in lore. But if it can't hold liquid, there's no way of knowing.
How did you manage to damage it, then? This is what I get for not retrieving it myself.
Thank the gods. I'll get to work on the Phial straight away.
Even though the Phial was damaged, I still think your efforts deserve reward.
Now, if you'll pardon me, I'm going to tend to Nurelion. I can make his final days a bit less painful.
Trouble? He's one of the most stubborn lizards I've ever met! You have your work cut out for you.
Oh, wait... did you say Goldenglow Estate? My apologies.
I'm sorry to say I know very little about that... bee farm, was it?
So, what do we have here? Hmm. Let me guess.
By your scent, I'd say you were from the Guild. But that can't be true, because I told Mercer I wouldn't deal with them anymore.
Gulum-Ei works in the East Empire Company Warehouse. He helps maintain all of the shipments of goods that goes in and out of Solitude.
That means he has the pick of the litter from some of the finest goods to grace Skyrim's shores.
He isn't exactly in the Guild, but he pays us a cut of all the stuff he lifts from the warehouse.
I can't believe Gulum-Ei's mixed up in all this; that Argonian couldn't find his tail with both hands.
Don't get me wrong, he could scam a beggar out of his last septim... but he's no mastermind.
Gulum-Ei is our inside man at the East Empire Company in Solitude.
I'm betting he acted as a go-between for the sale of Goldenglow Estate and that he can finger our buyer.
Get out there, shake him down and see what you come up with. Talk to Brynjolf before you leave if you have any questions.
There are thieves and then there is Gulum-Ei. No honor, no code at all. He'd shake your hand and stab you in the back at the same time.
The cut he's supposed to provide the Guild has dwindled as of late. He says pickings in the warehouse are slim, but I'm certain he's lying.
Keep your eyes on him, he's quite crafty.
No, no. Mercer wants him alive, for now.
Stubborn or not, he's the only contact we've got at the East Empire Company.
Just keep on his tail and he's bound to step into something he can't scrape off his boot.
A mercantile group that has established ports all over Tamriel. They pretty much dominate the whole shipping industry.
The Emperor himself supposedly backs them, which means they have fairly unlimited resources... so don't get their feathers in a ruffle.
Ah, there you are.
I've consulted my contacts regarding the information you recovered from Goldenglow Estate, but no one can identify that symbol.
What did you need?
Aringoth was a fool to think he could get away with this.
Maybe I did, maybe I didn't. I can't be expected to remember every deal I handle.
Just head right back to the Guild and get the information to Mercer. Nothing else is more important.
If you discover Gulum-Ei's holding out on us and has more loot stashed away than he claims, we'd find that information quite valuable as well.
Shor's bones, what happened to you, boy?
Okay, okay. Come inside, then. Sigrid will get you something to eat and you can tell me all about it.
What's going on? And who's this?
Changed your mind? Come with me. I'm heading to Riverwood. It's just up the road and my uncle Alvor is the blacksmith there.
Uncle Alvor! Hello!
Are you in some kind of trouble?
He's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
She's a friend. Saved my life in fact. Come on, I'll explain everything, but we need to go inside.
Come on. I'll feel better once we're under cover in Riverwood.
I hope that dragon isn't still flying around here somewhere.
I still almost can't believe it... a real, live dragon...
Hadvar? What are you doing here? Are you on leave from...
Shh.. Uncle, please. Keep your voice down. I'm fine. But we should go inside to talk.
She is the Goddess of Dawn and Dusk. Azura sees into the Twilight of the future, and guides her followers through it.
I will feast on you!
Glory to Namira!
Who... who are you? What's going on?
Priest of Arkay. I'm your friend.
Namira grind your bones for my bread!
I will drink your blood and feast on your flesh!
No, I found her horse. Don't worry, I've taken care of it... she won't be using it to escape.
Let's get moving, I want to catch her inside while she's distracted. Take the lead.
I'm sorry, I was under the impression I was in charge.
You're leading and I'm following. Does that seem clear to you?
The last thing I saw was Karliah dumping his body into a opening atop the ruins; an unceremonious end for a remarkable man.
To this day I've regretted allowing her to escape, even if it meant I had died trying. I owed Gallus that much.
She was a stubborn Dunmer... always had to do everything her way.
But she was also the best... bringing in more coin a month than some thieves heist in a year.
Gallus trusted her too much and let her get too close.
Karliah was a master marksman and her greatest weapon was the element of surprise. I was lucky... she missed my heart by mere inches.
I staggered away from the ruins and my vision began to blur. It's then that I realized the bitch had poisoned her arrows.
If you want to call it that, yes. Me? I think she was softening him for the kill.
Gallus would call her his "little nightingale." He was absolutely smitten by her.
Just make certain you keep your eyes open. Karliah is as sharp as a blade.
The last thing I need is you blundering into a trap and warning her that we're here.
Good, you're finally here.
I've scouted the ruins and I'm certain Karliah is still inside.
Twenty-five years ago I was standing outside these very same ruins. Gallus told me to meet here but he wouldn't say why.
When I arrived, Gallus stepped from the shadows. Before he uttered a sound an arrow pierced his throat.
Before I could even draw my blade, her second arrow found its mark in my chest.
You're my... friend....
Greed? Jealousy? Spite? Who can say what drove her to such an iniquitous act.
One thing's certain. I intend to find out before she draws her last breath.
That was just lying around? Good find.
A dragon? That's... ridiculous. You aren't drunk, are you boy?
Hadvar, did you really see a dragon? What did it look like? Did it have big teeth?
Sigrid! We have company!
Not much more to tell. This dragon flew over and just wrecked the whole place. Mass confusion.
I don't know if anyone else got out alive. I doubt I'd have made it out myself if not for my friend here.
I need to get back to Solitude and let them know what's happened.
I thought you could help us out. Food, supplies, a place to stay.
I don't know where to start. You know I was assigned to General Tullius's guard.
We were stopped in Helgen when we were attacked... by a dragon.
Of course. Take what you need.
But what's all this about? What are you two doing here?
Hadvar! We've been so worried about you! Come, you two must be hungry. Sit down and I'll get you something to eat.
Of course! Any friend of Hadvar's is a friend of mine. I'm glad to help however I can.
Hush, child. Don't pester your cousin.
Husband. Let him tell his story.
Now, then, boy. What's the big mystery? What are you doing here, looking like you lost an argument with a cave bear?
Hrrrmm.
You assaulted one of my family members. I've come to accept your apology.
Come again?
I am prepared to deal with your insolence...
Fine. Then it will be an honor to kill you for your offenses against my kin.
I trust this is the last I'll hear of your violent outbursts.
He is gone.
But thanks to you, he died knowing his life's work was not in vain.
It doesn't matter. Look, it's refilling with your tonic as we speak.
Master, look. It's the Phial.
As am I.
You acted very nobly in helping my master realize his life's work. Thank you for that.
As for myself, I've always been content to simply be an alchemist.
I fear keeping the Phial would just remind me of Nurelion's obsession, and how it consumed him.
Here, keep it. You are as much a part of its legend now as Curalmil was. I hope it brings you the happiness that my master desired.
Marvelous.
What? How?
Come on. Let's see what you're made of.
You want a real fight, eh?
Twelve septims on the big one!
Drive that snowback to the ground. Come on!
Fight! Fight!
Get him!
Get her!
Don't let him get away with this!
Get him!
Come on, he's not so tough.
By Sithis, but you are a persistent one. Haven't I told you enough?
Please, leave me to recover my... mental energies.
It's nice to be back in a friendly spot, huh?
Listen, I'm going to lay up here for a while. You can make your own way to Solitude from here.
I'd recommend heading to Whiterun, just down the road from here. From there you can take a carriage to Solitude.
I hope I'll see you in Solitude. Good luck.
Did you really see a dragon?
Make yourself at home.
Take what you need, my friend. Within reason, of course.
See that it doesn't happen again.
See, that wasn't so difficult, was it.
No? Do you care about rotting away for a month in a cell?
Ah, but nobody wants that, right? Painful for you, annoying for me. So, you pay my fine... and you walk away.
Is that so? Well then, I'll have to charge you with disturbing the Jarl's peace.
I still need you to make a batch of those fried potatoes. We can serve them with the ale.
You are going to brew a new batch of ale, right? Like we talked about? What we have's been sitting in that barrel for over a month.
Assur and I are playing a game. Don't tell him where I am!
Assur had to move away because of the Empire. But we get to stay.
Assur says his pa says the College made lots of bad things happen, but the mages I've seen look nice.
There's not many people left in Winterhold, so I only have Assur to play with, and sometimes he's mean.
Assur always wants to play "Hunt the Elf" and he makes me be the Elf.
Pa says the Empire made us leave, but the Empire just does whatever the elves tell them to.
We don't get many people like you visiting Winterhold. Usually it's elves.
Did you know that most of Winterhold fell into the sea? I heard it's because of that College.
Someday I'm gonna grow up and be the Jarl.
I heard the College teaches you how to kill people with magic. Is that true?
I'm very busy, you know. I'm the only one Kraldar can rely on to assist him.
Kraldar has big plans for Winterhold. We'd be better off if he was in charge.
I'm not one to complain, but I'm not sure why Kraldar insists on keeping Malur Seloth around.
My job is to serve the Jarl, not waste time talking with travelers.
Thonjolf is convinced I've got some connections at the College, so he goes easy on me.
I don't really know anyone at the College. I guess everyone just assumes I do because I'm a Dunmer.
People won't say it, but they're afraid of me, so they leave me alone.
It turns out that if people think you're some sort of secret wizard, they'll let you get away with a lot.
I'm trying to do as little as possible. You're getting in the way of that.
Winterhold may not look impressive, but it has a very rich history.
Winterhold was once the capital of Skyrim, you know. Quite an honor.
I'd like nothing more than to see Winterhold regain its former glory.
It's hard to recover from something like the Great Collapse, but I believe Winterhold can do it.
The College of Winterhold is truly an asset to our city... err, town.
Just say the word if you need a drink or something to eat.
If you need a drink, just say the word.
Something I can get for you?
Now, I'm not sure that you're quite as attuned as I am, given my extensive years of experience, but can you feel that?
Find out why he's here, and what he wants.
Really? Well, allow me to clarify the situation.
I'd like to know why there's someone claiming to be from the Psijic Order here in the College.
More importantly, I'd like to know why he's asking for you specifically.
So we're going to go have a little chat with him, and find out exactly what it is he wants.
I need you to come with me immediately. Let's go.
Now, I...
I suppose we'll continue this at some later time, when we can avoid interruptions.
I'm sure you've already noticed the markings. They're quite unlike anything we've seen before.
I'd simply like to talk to you.
I am that which you have been seeking.
Your efforts are in vain. It has already begun.
But those who have sent you have not told you what they seek. What you seek.
Well, I'm quite sure I've never been interrupted like this before... the audacity!...
Now, you are going to speak to this... Monk... and find out why he is here, and then he will be removed from College grounds.
I'm afraid I must intrude. It is urgent that I speak with your associate immediately.
The Augur? Really? Well, it's certainly not my place to say anything about it.
I think you'd better go see Mirabelle Ervine.
The what?
No. No, I'm quite sure I have no idea what you're talking about.
I suggest you mind your own business and return to whatever it is you do here.
Well now, there's a name I haven't heard in some time.
My goodness, it's been years since I've spoken with him. I suppose he's still down in the Midden, but I haven't checked.
Are you going to see him? Do tell him "hello" for me, won't you?
Yes, I've no doubt of its gravity. This, however, is a matter that cannot wait.
Arch-Mage Aren is already hard at work, and hopefully we'll have more information soon.
This is most inappropriate! We are involved in serious research here!
I don't know. When I asked her where she was going she just muttered "Where the end began."
Here, take the Goldenglow Estate Deed as proof. And when you speak to Mercer, tell him I'm worth more to him alive.
Consider it a gift to ensure your silence. That thing seems to be bringing me nothing but trouble anyway.
Karliah didn't even want it, she wanted to keep the sale a secret. I can see how well that went.
Now, there's no need to do anything rash...
This isn't as bad as it seems. I was going to tell Mercer about everything, honestly! Please... he'll have me killed!
Did Gulum-Ei give up any information on our buyer?
No, it.. it can't be. I haven't heard that name in decades.
This is grave news indeed, she's someone I hoped to never cross paths with again.
Karliah and I were like partners. I went with her on every heist. We watched each other's backs.
I know her techniques, her skills. If she kills me, there'll be no one left that could possibly catch her.
If only we knew where she was...
All right, all right. It's Karliah... her name is Karliah.
Yes, I'm going with you and together we're going to kill her.
Prepare yourself and meet me at the ruins as soon as you can. We can't let her slip through our fingers.
Help... ? No, no! Look I didn't even know it was her until after she contacted me.
Please, you have to believe me!
I see you wish to be reasonable, perhaps I misjudged you.
The name of the person you want is Karliah.
Mercer never told you about her?
Karliah is the thief responsible for murdering the previous Guild Master, Gallus. Now she's after Mercer.
I have a long-standing arrangement with the Dark Brotherhood. If I need someone in the Guild taken care of, we do it ourselves.
We both agree it's best to keep these matters in house.
Karliah destroyed everything this Guild stood for. She murdered my predecessor in cold blood and betrayed the Guild.
After we discovered what she'd done, we spent months trying to track her down, but she just vanished.
You needed to know more?
There's only one place that could be. The place where she murdered Gallus... a ruin called Snow Veil Sanctum.
We have to go out there before she disappears again.
They were a secret cabal of the three top rogues in the Thieves Guild. No one knew their identities.
Some people say they watched over the group, others say they were reserved for the most elite jobs. I don't really know.
It wasn't until recently that I heard Mercer Frey and this Dark Elf named Karliah were a part of that group.
I've heard stories about the wonders of the great Temple in Markarth.
But I never dreamed that I would even get to see it.
I am honored to be called for this duty. Please, lead on.
I hope we reach Markarth soon.
Please, open the door. I need to get out of here.
(bark)
(bark)
I'll be fine.
You see, you're not well! Have a seat and I'll fetch you some tonic.
I'll... I can... just...
Master, you're far too old for this sort of journey. We don't know what's inside...
I'm so close to the answer...
You really think I can be a blacksmith someday, papa? As good as Eorlund Gray-Mane? Or maybe... even as good as you?
He he. Thanks, papa.
Oh, I know it! You keep working hard, like you been doing, and you could be the greatest blacksmith Skyrim has ever seen.
Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can. Help yourself to whatever you need, within reason.
In joining the Legion, you'll be taking an oath binding you to the service of the Emperor and thus to every citizen of the Empire.
Are you prepared to make that commitment?
Just head north - you can't miss it.
It's the capital of Whiterun Hold, the biggest and the best of the nine holds of Skyrim. But don't take my word for it.
Cross the river and then head north. You'll see it, just past the falls.
When you get to Whiterun just keep going up. When you get to the top of the hill, you're at Dragonsreach, the Jarl's palace.
An excellent question. Vittoria Vici is the first cousin to our dear Emperor, Titus Mede II.
Vici has obvious Imperial connections. Her husband has ties to the Stormcloaks. Their union is a step toward reconciliation.
So if there's a murder at the wedding... Not only will it stall the peace process - it will send shockwaves throughout the entire Empire.
The Emperor's hand will be forced. He'll have to travel to Skyrim to deal with the aftermath... and he'll find the Dark Brotherhood waiting.
Soon. I'm not exactly sure when. But not to worry - the ceremony itself is being followed up by a public banquet that will last for several days.
Both Vittoria and her husband will be there for the duration, and everyone in Solitude is invited to attend. You'll have plenty of opportunity.
Best put your other tasks aside. The real challenge is about to begin.
Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to.
But by Sithis, I hope I'm making the right choice here. You may be an effective killer, but your command of guile leaves much to be desired.
The sealed letter you delivered to me was mysteriously unsealed upon its arrival. I can only assume you've read its contents. Sloppy...
But moving on to business. As you probably already know from reading the letter... you're going to a wedding.
Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to.
Now then. I hope you have something nice to wear. Because you're going to a wedding.
This is a public kill. How you do it is entirely up to you. Arrow to the throat? Knife in the belly? Your choice, so long as it's loud and messy.
Because of the current political climate, people are going to assume the murder is related to the bad blood between the Legion and Stormcloaks.
In any event, when Vici dies, it's going to be complete pandemonium. Best have your escape route planned out in advance.
Well, more like the public reception. It should be a lovely affair. You'll mingle with the guests, eat some cake... stab the bride.
Oh yes. You've got to kill the bride. At her wedding.
And they say romance is dead.
Hmph. He had a trip to Skyrim planned for months, and canceled at the last minute. Much to his cousin's dismay.
Seems the Emperor realized his presence would necessitate a more direct role in the ongoing hostilities. A role he was, obviously, unwilling to take.
All that will change with his cousin's murder. Everyone will think it was motivated by the conflict between the Imperial Legion and Stormcloaks.
And the Emperor will be forced to come clean up the mess.
If you need any help with Destruction spells, please let me know.
I'm sure when the time for promotions comes, they'll go to the most deserving mage.
Don't you think I'd make a wonderful Master Wizard? Maybe even Arch-Mage someday?
It's a small group of us here at the College. Favors don't go unremembered. Neither do affronts.
Another day, another opportunity to learn!
Someday the College may be fully accepted by the Nords. I hope that my work as Arch-Mage may help.
Like it or not, this College is a direct influence on the public's impressions of magic and those that use it.
It is up to each of us to present ourselves publicly as maturely as possible, and to remain above petty local squabbles.
A legendary bottle, forged in the days when Skyrim was just starting its turn to ice.
A small container, made of the magically infused snow that first fell on the Throat of the World.
It is said that the Phial will replenish whatever fluid is placed inside of it.
For an alchemist like myself, it is the most pure expression of my art. To create from nothingness is... poetry.
Bah. If there was a tonic that could help me, I would have found it by now...
I have spent my entire life searching for it.
It brought me all the way to these frozen reaches from the warm embrace of Summerset Isle.
Entire years spent in libraries. Seeking out tiny villages with local legends that contain but a whisper of a hint of the Phial.
I even named my shop after it, hoping that it might attract anyone who had heard of it.
And now it's within my grasp... but the Eight, it seems, have chosen me for their amusement.
... for in my current condition, I'll never be able to lay my hands on it.
You would do that? It's good to know there are some people out there who are willing to help an old man.
It's buried with its maker, Curalmil, in a long forsaken cave to the west of here.
Curalmil was a crafty one, even in death. You would need the skills of a master alchemist to reach his resting place.
Luckily for you, I've already made the mixture. Here, take it.
Please... don't dally. I've wasted enough time arguing with my useless assistant here.
May the Eight guide you to the Phial...
Please hurry -- I fear my master doesn't have much time left.
I assume you have proof? Can't go making accusations like that without proof.
Alva? Didn't think she had it in her.
So it's true. That traitorous bitch! Morthal owes you a debt.
Here. You were promised a reward for solving the crime, but I need one more favor from you.
Morthal is still in danger. The journal mentions Movarth, a master vampire I thought was destroyed a century ago.
I'll gather together some able-bodied warriors to clean out Movarth's lair. They'll be waiting outside for you to lead them.
What? Were you hoping to claim this house for yourself? Well, the Aretinos always liked me, so I think I'm family enough to take up here.
The milk drinkers in this town wouldn't last five minutes in a real battle.
Rumor holds that the Aretino boy brought an assassin here. Sounds like nonsense to me.
I'm going with you whether you like it or not.
You want in there? Here's my spare key. Take whatever you want.
We were supposed to find a sacrifice. Human sacrifice. I told her I couldn't do it and she attacked.
I've done horrible things living here, but never taken an innocent life. I couldn't just find some person for them to kill.
The Initiation. My mother is going to become one of those things. I can't be a part of that.
Gods, I can't run away from this. I have to stop her.
Let's get up there before mother finds out what we're doing.
Nasty little buggers, aren't they?
The mine will be running better than ever thanks to you.
Sorry, the mine's dry right now.
I hate seeing a good mine go to waste like that, you know?
If you're looking for supplies, I'd advise heading to Riften.
You think you're tough, huh? Wandering Skyrim is a picnic compared to tunnel crawling!
The real work happens in places like this. Blood and sweat is what makes the world turn.
Sure, I used to be a soldier... but that was child's play compared to cracking rock.
Came all the way out here to see us, did you?
Making that trip to Darkwater Crossing was a huge help. Thank you.
I trust my parents are well?
Aye, I see the look you're giving me. Don't worry, I won't bite.
You're looking at the best miner in Skyrim.
I'd wager I could crack rock faster than anyone. Even you.
Ha! You showed those spiders a thing or two!
We're married. I guess we'll need to decide where we're living, won't we? You can always stay with me.
I'm hoping to run my own inn someday.
Please stop looking at me like that.
First the war and now there's talk of dragons. What's next?
You mentioned something like that... when you were running off with my goat!
Tell you what - you bring back my goat, and maybe I'll give a damn about your staff.
You said a whole lot of nonsense about selling Gleda to hire a priestess for some big event.
There was a bit of talk about some staff you had in safe keeping. Never said where, but you sure didn't have it with you!
It's good to see a fresh face. You wouldn't have happen to come from Whiterun would you?
If you're looking for potions you should talk to my aunt, Angeline Morrard.
Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Hey! Wanna play tag?
I bet my papa could beat you up.
I've got weapons for givin', and armor for gettin'!
Seems there's no end to the needs of the Imperials. But what can I help you with?
Why, hello! Here to buy? If you have to ask the price, you may be in the wrong store.
We provide clothing for all of Solitude's most... influential residents. But for you, I'll make an exception.
Oh, what delight. Another charming customer...
Let me just drop everything to help you. I don't mind. Really.
Welcome to the Winking Skeever, friend.
If you need a good rumor or two, I think I can oblige...
Traitors, civil wars, dragons. Bah. Those troubles don't cross my doorway. So sit, relax.
Don't mind me. I'm just running errands for the old man.
Papa says I won't see Roggvir any more, but he wouldn't tell me why not.
Spare some gold for a veteran?
Plenty of fresh fish for ya.
Pardon me.
I don't have anything to say.
Welcome to Solitude. Home of the arts here in Skyrim.
Good evening. I'm Pantea Ateia, the Master Vocalist.
Hello, yes? I'm the Dean of History. And scribe work, when I can find some quiet time.
I wish only to graduate and enroll with the Legion. My drums will lead our troops to victory.
Lady Ateia says my voice is second only to hers. But she's selling me short.
Dean Inge says a bard can never have enough practice. So, would you like a tune?
Unless we have specific business, I'm not interested.
The Imperials are good for business, and business is good for Skyrim.
Welcome to the center of the world. Or at least my brother Erikur's world...
Speak if you must, but mind your tongue. I do not suffer fools gladly.
Spiced wine for ya? It's a family recipe.
I'm too old to work, but that don't make me too old to drink.
Hmm? Sorry, I was lost in thought. I'm planning my wedding, you see. So many details to work out.
You look like you could do with a ripe apple. Or a tomato, cabbage or leek?
Not much work being a jailor. Long as you can ignore the pleading and all that.
With the passing of my dear husband Torygg, the weighty position of Jarl has fallen to me. Please direct questions to my Steward.
I am the Jarl's Steward. If you have court business to tend to, you talk to me.
Please address all questions to the Steward, Falk Firebeard.
Just arrive in the city? Is it true what they say, then? There are dragons about?
Be quick. I have little patience for mundane concerns.
Jarls come and jarls go, but a good cook stays for a lifetime. And I'm a very, very good cook.
Oh, hello! Just tidying up.
Watch your feet. We just cleaned there!
Hello, my child. Arkay watch over you.
Blessings of the Eight Divines upon you. How may I help?
Blessings to you. The Divines are listening.
I have no business with you. Leave me.
You, talk to egg-brothers. Deeja has no words for you.
Dock work is not good work. Better than Black Marsh though. Much better.
Oh my, old Ma'zaka has a visitor!
Ah, yet another new face. I'm from Hammerfell, you see. I can hardly keep track of all the people I've been meeting.
Make it quick. I get enough delays from politicians.
Something to report?
You here for my next interrogation? I got nothing but time...
You got business at the stables?
Is this about the stables? My husband, Geimund, handles the business.
Looking for a horse? You'll want to talk to my father, Geimund.
What do you want?
My duty is simple - oversee the Thalmor Justiciars, and make sure they perform their duties admirably.
Can I help you?
Oh, hello. Have a seat, will you?
Mama said I could help with the goats!
The flame of my master burns low. Without him, we are all lost and forever gray... Please, help us!
Good day to you.
We have to go through town and across the bridge to get to Bleak Falls Barrow.
You can see it from here, though. The mountain just over the buildings.
I handle the fishin', numbers and bedlam jobs... the ones with the more personal touch.
If break-ins are more your thing, go talk to Vex.
Let me make this plain and simple. No more jobs until you pay Vex. No get to it.
You're not getting anything until you pay the Guild back.
I handle the burglary, shill, sweep and heist jobs. Most of them involve breaking and entering.
If you don't like that kind of work, talk to Delvin. He runs the more up-close and personal jobs.
I think you should finish that special assignment Delvin gave you, then we'll talk.
Now you die!
I guess we'd better try this way.
Go ahead. I'll follow your lead and watch your back.
You see what talking to them gets us?
Hmm. That doesn't go anywhere.
I don't know who you are, or what's going on here, but you ought to be ashamed of yourself.
What's happening? Damn it, what are you doing?
Get these things off of me!
Noooo....
Cowards! Stealing a woman from her home! For shame!
By the gods, when I get out of here you're dead! You hear me? Dead!
Aggghh! Re...lease me!
Thank you! Thank you! I won't tell anyone what happened here, I swear it!
Oh, gods, please! Let me live! I'll do anything you want!
I... I can hear you talking out there. Please, let me go. I've done nothing to you.
You cannot escape!
Is this about that raid last week? I told Holgrim there was no honor in killing sleeping men, but he wouldn't listen! It wasn't my fault, I swear!
I'm just a soldier. Killing is my job! Surely you can't fault a man for doing his job?
Please, oh please, I don't want to die...
Ah, sweet freedom. Now that's more like it. You have my thanks, stranger.
Whoever this is, clearly we got off on the wrong foot. Ah, but no worries. This is not the first time I have been bagged and dragged.
Hello? Hello? Ah, I know you're listening.
Come now. Whatever the problem, we can talk about it like civilized folk. Hmm?
A man in my position makes lots of enemies. But what is a life-threatening situation if not an opportunity for mutual benefit? Hmmm?
Die, insect!
You'll let me go. I know you will. This is all just part of the game we play...
I will end you!
Come on, show an old man what you got!
I cannot be defeated!
My power is supreme!
The Old Hroldan Inn is still open, bless poor Eydis's memory.
You'll be looking to rent Tiber Septim's room I take it?
Old Hroldan is the stone that the Empire's history is built on.
Don't underestimate my son, Skuli. He may look young, but he can handle any drunks we get.
Skuli's father is off fighting for the Empire. I'm sure he's just fine.
This is the Old Hroldan Inn. Clean beds and strong drink.
My mother's dead, but I won't close the inn. I've hired Leontius to run things.
If you drink too much, you're out. No drunks.
Changed the linens myself. They're fresh.
Yamarz was a fool. Always trying to scheme his way out of responsibility.
Now, take Shagrol's hammer back to Largashbur, and we'll see about whipping the rest of them into shape.
Well, the giants took care of him, and you took care of the giants. Two problems solved at once.
But, you took care of him and the giants. Two problems solved at once.
Got a shop owner with a very choice item a client wants to get his hands on if you do the dirty work.
If you bring mead, you're welcome here! Even if you don't, you're still welcome!
All this talk of dragons is making people antsy. Nonsense!
I visited the meaderies of Riften once, as a lad. Wish I had their skill.
Working our mine is dangerous work, but Dravynea keeps it going. Well, Dravynea and the promise of more mead.
You've got a fine look about you. Come have a visit at the Braidwood Inn. Better than home!
Welcome to Kynesgrove. If you want to do business here, you'll have to deal with me.
If it's malachite you're after, you've come to the right place. I'll pay for any you dig up out of Steamscorch Mine.
Kjeld speaks for Kynesgrove. Anyone who says otherwise is aching for a fight.
Roggi and I do the work around here. My wife and brats just sit around on their brains wasting time.
My wife is a good woman, but every man needs a little variety, you know?
An outsider? No reason to stop in Kynesgrove. Keep moving.
The mine's always on the verge of collapse. Good thing I'm around to keep it up.
Morrowind used to be a lovely place, but you couldn't pay me to live there these days.
Most people in Skyrim don't care for Dunmer. Roggi's the only one around here even half-decent.
I should get over to Windhelm more. See how the other Morrowind refugees are getting along.
Seen the remnants of the grove? That... well, that was me. Me and my sister.
Came here to start a lumber mill. Who knew the Nords cared about a bunch of old trees?
But... well, they did. Sacred, they said. So we work the mines until we've worked it off. Fair enough, I guess.
Oh, great, another wanderer. Slumming it on your way to Windhelm?
Wouldn't even be here if it weren't for my idiot sister.
I can't believe she dragged me all the way to this snowball. I'd rather be broke in Cyrodiil than broke here.
If these Nords don't let up soon, I might just make a break for it. At least Cyrodiil is warmer than this.
Did you see my husband already? I'm sorry... he can come off a bit rough at times.
I hope you'll stay for a bit. We could certainly use the business.
This is really quite a nice town when you get down to it. Just look at the view!
Got some nice comfy beds at the Braidwood Inn, if you're looking to flop down somewhere.
I've never seen you before. Leave me alone!
My pa runs this town, and you're going to be in big trouble if you mess with me.
You don't look so tough. I'll bet I could take you.
Why are you still here?
Got a good one here involving a shop, a waylaid shipment of goods and an angry client. You fill in the blanks. You want in?
Thank you, hero. Now I may finally rest. Take Hevnoraak's iron mask. It may be of use to you, and serve as some reward for your feat here.
With those three vessels, Hevnoraak can restore himself. Find them before he can.
Kill him now, while we still can!
I have just the place. The owner likes to stash some lovely trinkets in his strongbox like this beauty. Can you get it for us?
The owner of this place just inherited an heirloom from a relative. You want in?
Sure. A client's relative lost this item in a gambling match and they're paying us to get it back. So much for fair play, huh?
The owner of this shop stiffed us on their last protection payment, but we're taking it anyway. You want to help us on this one?
'Jiit caravan brought this shop a nice piece from Elsweyr. Think you can break into their strongbox and get it for us?
Got a client in desperate need for this item. He won't say what it's for, and we don't care. Can you take this one?
... and may we one day rejoin them in eternity.
May the spirit of Lavinia and all those who have left this world and its suffering know the beloved serenity of Aetherius...
But he willingly gave up this existence that we might better understand the vagaries of life and death.
Do you not know the Daedric Lord of the Hunt?
He revels in the chase, and also gave the "gift" of lycanthropy to mortals.
A powerful force, not to be crossed. As I learned too late.
The god Arkay was once like us, bound to winding mortality.
It is through the ebb and flow of this cosmic tide that we find renewal and, in the end, peace.
You like creepin' around in the shadows? Magic'll work just as good there as anywhere else.
