Talk to Babette about potions. She's the creepy little girl - can't miss her.
Yes, yes. I'm your brother and I'm supposed to greet you respectfully. So "hello." There, satisfied?
Don't let my appearance throw you off. I'm older than you. A lot older. Getting bitten by a vampire when you're ten will do that to a girl.
It's been quite a boon, looking like a child. No one ever suspects a thing.
I could brew some potions for you. Or train you in Alchemy.
You looking to buy some potions? Or maybe I could help train you to make your own?
Let me guess - Astrid failed to mention I'm her husband? She has a tendency to do that. Doesn't want the others thinking she plays favorites.
You're part of a family now, hamshank. I trust you... you trust me. Got it?
Don't take offense to the nicknames, morsel. I'm a werewolf. Hard not to think of you as a snack.
Hello, tidbit.
In the Dark Brotherhood, I found acceptance, love, and my life's true purpose.
Does death not come to us all? Is it not a certainty? I am, therefore, honored to be doom's herald. To play some part in a being's inevitable end.
Death is but the time to sleep forever, in the Void.
Sithis' whisper be upon you, brother.
Sithis' whisper be upon you, sister.
Me, I have been part of the Dark Brotherhood from the day I first hatched. I am a Shadowscale, you see. Born under the sign of the Shadow.
I serve Astrid, and this Sanctuary. Life is simple, and good.
The Shadowscales are all but forgotten. An ancient tradition, from another time.
Greetings, brother.
Greetings, sister.
We don't get many visitors through here, unless their headed up to High Hrothgar of course.
By the Eight, I didn't think you could do it!
Now maybe we can put all this behind us.
Take this as a token of our gratitude.
They say a strong drink, a sharp axe and a good woman are all a Nord needs to be happy. Two out of three's not bad.
You want me to smile? What's there to smile about? Soldiers and dragons are going to be trampling all over the crops this year. I just know it.
Oh, stop being such a grouch! Not every visitor to the farm wants to burn it down! Maybe they just want to have a nice supper with us, hmm?
Damn Forsworn. Only a matter of time before they hit here, too.
Left Hand Mine deals with iron, the perfect metal to dig with a war going on.
Kolskeggr, overrun with Forsworn, what next?
Kolskeggr Mine is lost, friend. Forsworn killed everyone.
Don't go to Kolskeggr. It's not safe.
Gat and I are the only survivors.
Pavo and I had to fight our way out of Kolskeggr. Forsworn killed everyone else.
Poor Pavo and Gat. To have lived through a Forsworn attack.
My Erith has her father's eyes. She's all I have left of him.
Why is everyone so upset? If there's Forsworn, the Jarl will take care of them, right?
Why do they call it Left Hand Mine? Can the miners use their right hands too?
Have you met Torom? He's the best dog in the Reach.
Oh, wipe that frown off your face Rogatus. You've been wearing that frown for over 50 years now.
You don't invite monsters into your home to have supper! I won't have them here, and I'm certainly not smiling for them!
I'm been farming this bit of land all my life.
My boy Leontius lives in Old Hroldan. Damn drunk.
Next thing you know there will be some kind of damn blight and undead will walk the land.
You ever have to farm land while there was a battle going on? I have.
Oh, what is this? A visitor? To our farm? Oh, do stay for a while, dear.
Now, you don't let Rogatus cause you any bother.
Rogatus and I have been married over 50 years. He's always been a bit of a grouch.
Our son Leontius moved to Old Hroldan nearly 30 years ago. I still miss him.
Shout for us, Dragonborn. Then we may have time for questions.
I just don't understand what kind of a man does that.
Greetings, fellow hunter.
Lead on.
May Hircine guide our blades.
We follow you, hunt-master.
The Bloodmoon lights the way.
Show us the path to the prey.
Hircine chose you to lead the Great Hunt. So lead.
Yes sir, of course.
All right. Keep up the good work.
Let's make sure we're just as productive this month as we were last. If Bryling's happy, we're happy.
We've got all the miners we need, so I hope you're not here looking for work.
Nothing here but the mine. Probably best you keep moving.
Pactur and I, we make sure the miners aren't stealing, and that the ore makes it to Solitude.
Sorli and I are just here to make sure the mine is run proper.
Wouldn't ever have expected to wind up living in a place like this, but the money's good.
Don't bother the miners. They've got work to do.
I'm in charge now that my parents are in Morthal.
My parents keep a close eye on the miners. They can't be trusted too much.
I hear there's been fighting in Skyrim. Rebellion, maybe. Wonder if our iron is being used for weapons.
If you're here about the mine -- it's already got an owner, and she's not selling. If not, then please stay out of my workers' way.
You get used to the cold after a while. The harder you work, the warmer you stay.
Bryling owns this mine. I wouldn't have much to live for if she hadn't offered me a job.
My job is my life. Whatever happened before doesn't matter.
I'm just here to work the mine. Nothing else matters anymore.
The outside world means little to us, and we'd like it to stay that way.
When you work hard enough, really get into a rhythm, everything else just fades away.
I'm just trying to make a living. I've no interest in trouble.
My business is my own. Why can't others see that?
The mine gives us purpose. Something many of us have wished for.
Stonehills' warmth is a welcome change in this cold, bleak land.
Teeba-Ei the hatchling never imagined working a mine in this cold place. Greater things were dreamt of.
Destroy him!
Yeah. Kind of scary too.
Kill the vampire!
Um...this place looks dangerous.
And it's full of vampires?
Yes!
Kill him!
Yeah!
Cowards! We must kill the vampires. We have to make them pay!
Take us to Movarth's Lair!
Kill him!
I want vengeance for my wife!
Vengeance for Laelette!
We're marching on Movarth's Lair whether you come with us or not.
Take us!
Yeah!
Let's kill him!
Is everything well with you, my scaly friend?
Yes, well... That's all we ask of you. Carry on.
Of course not. I never thought otherwise.
I'm fine, father. Work is fine.
Yes sir, fine sir.
Yes sir.
Good, good. Keep up the hard work. It'll all pay off.
Teeba-Ei is cold, but Teeba-Ei works hard as always.
Good, good. It's honest work, son. No shame in that.
Right. Well, you let me know if you need anything.
How goes it, son?
Everything well, Jesper?
We will see if you truly have the gift.
Show us, Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
Master Einarth is waiting.
But the Night Mother is mother to all! It is her voice we follow! Her will! Would you dare risk disobedience? And surely... punishment?
But make no mistake. I am the leader of this Sanctuary. My word is law. Are we clear on that point?
Understood... husband?
Oh, be quiet you great lumbering lapdog. The man has had a long journey. You can at least be civil.
Oh yes, mistress. You're the "boss." For now.
Oh, what a kind and wise wizard you are. Sure to earn our Lady's favor.
You and the Night Mother are of course welcome here, Cicero. And you will be afforded the respect deserving of your position as Keeper.
Oh yes, mistress. Perfectly! You're the boss.
Oh, yes yes yes! Thank you, thank you, thank you!
Mister Cicero, I for one am delighted you and the Night Mother have arrived. Your presence here signals a welcome return to tradition.
First of all, one of our men was captured by rebels who refuse to surrender him. So we're going to break him out.
Impressive. Your Thu'um is precise. You show great promise, Dragonborn.
Master Einarth will now teach you "Ro," the second Word in Unrelenting Force.
Without training, you have already taken the first steps towards projecting your Voice into a Thu'um, a Shout.
Now let us see if you are willing and able to learn.
We will perform your next trial in the courtyard. Follow Master Borri.
So... a Dragonborn appears, at this moment in the turning of the age.
Try it again. Breath and focus.
Is this your best effort? Again.
Come, Dragonborn. Attend to Master Borri.
Now we will see how quickly you can master a new Shout.
First, let us see if you truly are Dragonborn. Let us taste of your Voice.
Stand next to me. Master Borri will open the gate. Use your Whirlwind Sprint to pass through before it closes.
As high priest of the Eight Divines, I bid you welcome.
You look troubled. Have you come seeking guidance?
The Divines saved me from the streets, and they can save you from your trials, as well.
Go away. I have more important things on my mind right now.
I don't like strangers...
You have no knowledge of who you speak to. Go away.
You want knives? You want them in your belly? No? Then you stop talking to Deeja.
What brings you all the way out here?
Oh, for the salt spray, the creek of the mast, and the clash of sabers. Those were the days...
My papa says I'm greg-gare-ree-yus, just like him! He says it means "smart," but my brother says he's wrong.
Can you spare a septim? Lost an eye during the Great War, or I'd earn it myself...
Them Imperials left me to die in Anvil, and I had to fight my way back home. Then they followed me back here!
I've got some salmon to sell.
I... I don't want to talk.
Are you here to talk about my brother? Do you think his execution was justified?
Feel free to make requests.
Seems there's always more to be done...
I generally don't involve myself in politics. Good tales deal with the issues of the day, but a wise bard remains a neutral observer.
Never forget, the most important job of any bard is to entertain. The day you lose your audience is the day you cut your lute strings.
It's been my honor to perform at the Blue Palace on many occasions. His late lordship was quite the admirer.
If you ask me, this damned rebellion can't end soon enough. I had to end my tour of Skyrim's courts. Too dangerous too travel.
To be honest, I simply can't stand some of my students. Only Aia shows true potential for greatness. The rest are just wasting my time.
I'm Dean of Lute at the Bards College, and no, I don't give private lessons.
Only reason I'm so good with a lute is because I'm old. It takes a lifetime to master.
My position, "Dean of History," actually covers a wide range of subjects. I teach poetry, writing, history, and the finer points of court life.
It's an honor to have Viarmo for instruction on drum, but he's far too reserved for my tastes.
Lisette's a fine lass, but when I join the Legion, she's not the type to wait for my return.
Few can resist my charms. Even fewer are worthy of them.
You're new here, right? Welcome to the Bards College. It's like a... musical museum.
You know, I don't get the impression that I'm very well liked at the College...
Thane Bryling, at your service.
When I was a young rascal, I broke a man's arm with a single punch. Course, I had a horseshoe hidden in my glove...
Bryling's obsession with honor and tradition is quaint, but politically irrelevant.
Half of the businesses in town owe me money, and I own most of the rest. I practically run Solitude already.
My duty is to keep the Jarl safe. Not play nursemaid to you sycophants.
Keep your weapon out of your hands, and we'll get along just fine.
Hello. I'm Gisli. But you can just call me Erikur's sister. Everyone does.
Have you met my dear brother Erikur? The man's as trustworthy as a Khajiit goldmonger.
Working for Erikur may be below my stature, but the compensation is... acceptable.
Sybille Stentor has a grasp of magical theory that I would never have expected from a human. Even a Breton.
Wanderer like you needs a little fire in the veins. Some spiced wine?
If you see my father Octieve around, stay clear. He'll only ask you for money or mead.
You got nothin' better to do than bother an old man?
Some say I got my position because my cousin is the Emperor. Nonsense. The man's given me nothing. He can't even be bothered to attend my wedding.
I don't have time for anyone too lazy or cowardly to make their own way in this world.
An apple to keep you healthy? A tomato to keep you strong? Surely you need one or the other...
You stay out of trouble, now. The Castle Dour dungeon can break even the strongest will.
If you have business, please see Falk Firebeard, my steward.
Do you have business with the court?
Apprentice blacksmith, at your service.
I have so very many ways to deal with people. Very few of them pleasant.
Everyone knows General Tullius wields the real power Solitude. Elisif? Ah, she's a figurehead. A puppet.
Folks at court won't admit it, but Sybille Stentor scares us all to death.
Are you my gallant hero? Have you come to whisk me away, to a life of adventure and romance?
Got lots of things to take care of. What do you need?
May Arkay bless you with a long life, a proud death, and a restful grave.
Wander no more, for you are among friends here, and safe.
Hello and welcome, my friend. How may I help you on this fine day?
Hey, over here!
Did you see the fires from Goldenglow Estate? I was afraid the whole place was going to burn to the ground!
If you're looking to purchase goods, I have none to sell. You should head into Riften instead.
Pretty quiet out here on the farm. My wife and I kind of like the solitude.
Vulwulf has been quite generous to let me take care of their farm considering I am not part of their family.
The Snow-Shod's are very trusting to let me run this place without their supervision.
I'm glad there are guards outside the farm. I don't think I could defend the place alone.
I try to think of it as coaxing the ore from the rock.
I keep hearing stories about Morrowind. Sounds like a wretched place.
Is there something I can help you with?
What's your trouble? Dasturn telling tales again?
We keep hearing that the war could come through here. I try not to worry about it.
It's tough to keep track of Hrefna. If her father wasn't such a snowback, he'd be around to help out.
Hi! I've never seen you around here before. Are you a miner too?
Sondas takes care of me when mom's not around.
Mom says the Empire is mean, but I don't think they're that bad.
The Stormcloaks are named after Ulfric. He's a bad man, but don't tell the soldiers that.
A traveler? I'll be you've seen some sights. We should swap tales sometime.
There's a gorgeous tree that grows right up through a cave to the northeast of here.
If you're heading to Kynesgrove, stick to the road. Some pretty dangerous caves between here and there.
Verner used to love exploring before we got married. Now he's become a bit of a milk drinker. Likes to stay home.
If you head straight west from where the White River splits towards Whiterun, you can get a look at the Cradlecrush giants.
You looking for work? We could use an extra hand in the mine. Just bring me any Corundum you dig up.
Annekke used to help more with the mining, but nowadays she's a bit of a bounder.
I do love my wife. Just wish she'd get her head out of the clouds.
If you want some information about the area, Annekke knows it better than anyone.
Sometimes I feel like Annekke cares more about finding new rocks than being with me.
That's right. College of Winterhold, just north of town. Hard to miss.
How do you think the crops are coming, dear?
Terrible. Only a matter of time before soldiers come and burn all my hard work into dust!
Look at the sky. Be a terrible day tomorrow. I know it.
Are you all right, Gat?
No. I have enough trouble without more mouths to feed! They're the Jarl's men, they can eat supper at the keep.
Still shaken from all the fighting, my friend. It's been too long since I had to kill someone.
Yes it can. I haven't been wrong in 50 years, and I won't be wrong tomorrow.
Maybe we could invite some of those nice guards over. You know, for some supper?
Oh, Rogatus! Maybe tomorrow will be pleasant for once, hmm? It can't be terrible every day!
Dravin! One of the soldiers I spoke to on the way home said you threatened them. What's come over you?
That doesn't matter. All it would take is a stray wolf or a bandit crossing your path... I can't bare to lose you after everything we've done.
All you'll accomplish by threatening them is ending up in the Riften dungeon. You're lucky they didn't take you seriously.
I don't care! Just stay away from the soldiers and keep to your business. It's the only way we'll make it through this.
I can't stand them, they represent everything I hate about Skyrim. They're tearing the place apart!
If it puts your mind at ease, I'll take it with me. I'm sorry.
Now that you've brought Dravin's bow back, he's back to his crabby old self. For better or for worse, I still thank you.
You'll have to forgive my husband's manners. He's not used to pleasantries.
The war's been hard on the farm and hard on Dravin. We don't know what to expect from one day to the next.
Someday, I hope we can get away from here... perhaps move back to Morrowind. This isn't at all what we expected.
Husband, you worry far too much. We are within an hour's walk of Riften.
Synda... you left your dagger behind again yesterday. I told you, the road isn't safe.
Sorry, Marise. I just can't sit back and watch them treat us like a resource. This is our home!
I wish you didn't hate him so much... I wish the whole family could be together again.
There is a copse of elms to the east. We could really use that wood.
Just be careful, Gralnach. That copse doesn't look safe. If you see any trouble, you run right home.
That's because there's nothing to tell. He left us to fend for ourselves and that's all you need to know.
Mother, I finished cutting at the northern stand. Where do I move on to now?
Despite what you discovered it's still comforting to know my husband is at peace.
Men are utter pigs... they only care about one thing.
If I ever catch my husband in bed with another woman, he's going to find himself homeless.
I hope you're not here to start some trouble. I'll fight you if I have to.
It's just me and my mother at the mill here. My father is gone. We don't know what happened to him.
I'm not afraid of anyone. Put a sword in my hand and I could defend this place if I had to!
Millwork sure is boring, but at least I don't have to work in a nasty mine.
I was hoping to head into Riften for supplies, but I can do that after I'm done with the elms.
Gralnach! I told you not to go through your father's things!
I'm sorry mother, I just want to know more about him. You never tell me anything!
True, I may look the part of dashing swordsman, but I prefer the art of wit to that of warfare.
Your typical Solitude wench is like the city itself. Stormy exterior, but ah the charms that lie within...
If you're looking for any archery or fletching supplies, you should see Fihada. I'm just the apprentice.
Fletching suits me. It's difficult, and sometimes tedious. But a well made arrow strikes forth like the fury of a god.
I trust this is something important?
The General and I have our disagreements, but he's the best hope for both the Empire and Skyrim.
Many of my countrymen joined the rebels. They can't see the big picture. Skyrim needs the Empire as much as it needs us.
I stand with the Stormcloaks. You Imperial dogs will never break my will. Never!
Don't think I don't know what's coming.
Work hard for the future and leave the past be. That's my motto.
With a wife like Fridrika and a son like Horm, I'm the luckiest man in Skyrim.
I really don't mean to be rude, but I don't think there's much I can help you with.
My family's weathered its share of problems, but we don't dwell on the past.
I grew up tending some of these horses. They're almost family.
When I was a boy, there was a year we couldn't afford enough feed. So I snuck my dinner out to the stables, and gave it to the horse.
Come in, come in! I was just about to take a break.
Living outside the walls is a risk, aye. But at least the guards don't come nosin' around.
We had some trouble, while back. Stormcloak tried to set the mill on fire. But we sorted her out.
Out here at work, Hjorunn treats me like an equal. Better than most in town.
Any day is a good one, long as I don't have to go into town. But some days, Hjorunn's too drunk...
The Empire, in its wisdom, has come to recognize Talos worship as a barbaric remnant of a bygone age.
Talos was a heroic man, but not a god. It pains the Altmer that we must remind our younger cousins of the difference.
We only want what is best for Tamriel. Surely, the rebels will accept peace when they realize this.
I could swear I've seen you somewhere before...
My husband handles the indoor work. Just as well - he'd only hurt himself out here.
It's hard work keeping Solitude fed, but I wouldn't trust any other family to do it.
I was a much younger man when I married Katla. Now I can hardly keep up with her.
My brothers would have been appalled to see a man outdone by his wife, but not me. Without Katla, this farm would fall apart.
You're a guest. And father says we have to be polite to guests.
Father says I don't have the knack for reading, but mama says I won't need it to work the farm.
Wait, hear my plea! My master, he is lost between worlds and I cannot bring him back!
Why does everyone ignore me? Why do you turn your heads? Why will no one help me?
Do you have business with the East Empire Company?
I will thank you not to interrupt me in my tasks.
Stand next to me, and prepare your Whirlwind Sprint. Master Borri will give the signal.
Follow me. Master Borri will test your Whirlwind Sprint.
You must feel the Word within yourself.
You learn quickly, as only a Dragonborn can.
Master Borri is waiting.
Your quick mastery of a new Thu'um is... astonishing. I'd heard the stories of the abilities of Dragonborn, but to see it for myself...
No. Indeed not. But beware that your skill does not outstrip your wisdom.
You are now ready for your last trial.
Retrieve the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller, our founder, from his tomb in the ancient fane of Ustengrav.
Remain true to the Way of the Voice, and you will return.
Masters Einarth, Borri, and Wulfgar will each demonstrate a Shout. Make your choice.
Don't worry, though. You will find all three Words in Ustengrav.
Dragons have the inborn ability to learn and project their Voice. Dragons also are able to absorb the power of their slain brethren.
A few mortals are born with similar abilities -- whether a gift or a curse has been a matter of debate down through the centuries.
What you have already learned in a few days took even the most gifted of us years to achieve.
Some believe that Dragonborn are sent into the world by the gods, at times of great need. We will speak more of that later, when you are ready.
Thanks for the help. Not many willing to take on the Forsworn these days.
Boring? That food is paying for everything. If we lose even a single crop, we could starve or be forced to beg. I won't have it!
All right, I understand. I'll do it tomorrow, okay? Just get off my back.
Painting won't put food on the table, but our crops will. Be patient Aduri... it will happen someday, I promise.
I'm sorry, Aduri, not this year. Perhaps next year after the harvest, I don't know. There's so much to do.
It's not going to be next year, nor the year after that! You just care about this farm more than you care about me!
I don't suppose you could come back another time? I'm very busy.
I have a lot to do around the farm, so make it quick please. What is it you wanted?
This place is boring. Nothing but the same thing every day. Sigh.
I wish I could head to one of the capital cities... maybe even back to Mournhold. Anywhere but here.
One day I hope to become a painter, but at this rate it looks like it's never going to happen.
Can you imagine how wonderful it would be to set what you see on a canvas so you'd have that memory forever?
My sister keeps telling me that the farm keeps us alive, but I don't really call this living.
Have you decided if I can head up to Solitude yet? I hear it's the best place to meet someone who may be willing to teach me to paint.
Aduri, how come the fields haven't been tilled like I asked?
Because it's boring. The fields are going to do fine... you don't have to dote over them so much.
I'll just prepare the subject now...
Greetings, mother.
I can't let you do this, mother. You have to die now!
I found a willing subject for you. Payment has already been discussed, of course.
Splendid! Please take a seat on the chair behind me, would you?
This is the last hagraven between us and my mother!
But mom, it's so boring with just me and Torom. I was just asking Skaggi some questions.
Yes, ma'am.
Skaggi tells me that you were bothering him today. Can't you and Torom just play by yourselves?
You work hard, Daighre. Glad you have stayed with us.
You leave him alone. I don't want to hear Skaggi telling me you've been bothering him again!
Mining out this old rock is a lot quieter, now. Thank the Divines.
Hard to believe there'd be an old crypt underneath the mine.
It's crazy to still be working here. What if there's more draugr down there?
This mine will be our grave yet. I just know it.
So you're the newest member of our dwindling, dysfunctional little Family. I've heard quite a bit about you.
Stop. Turn around. And go back the way you came. Astrid is waiting for you. She's down near the entrance. You must have passed right by her.
Divines sake, Skald. Who do think we're threatening with our old war wounds? We're not soldiers, anymore.
Alduin's Wall was created by the ancient Akaviri Dragonguard, the forerunners of the Blades.
One of the lost secrets of the Blades. Where they recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy.
Its location was lost for centuries, but I know where it is: Sky Haven Temple, an ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden within the crags of the Reach.
You... Dragonborn? Is it really true?
So Delphine keeps up the fight, after all these years.
I thought she'd have realized it's hopeless by now. I tried to tell her, years ago...
Fine.
Why do I always have to be the Elf? I don't want to!
No. Even Ranmir can only drink so much.
I suppose that's a benefit to having so few customers.
How long are we going to let Ranmir drink himself into a stupor? When will enough be enough?
As long as he keeps paying, I suppose. He keeps to himself, and isn't hurting anyone.
It's not my place to get involved in their family's business, remember? You're the one that told me to stay out of it.
Are we running low on mead?
We're playing Hunt the Elf. You're the Elf.
Let's just keep an eye on the supply. It's such a chore to restock.
Good morning, Assur. What do you want to do today?
Too bad. That's what we're playing, and you're the Elf. Now go hide so I can hunt you!
Are you so sure of that? I think Birna might disagree with you.
Yes, yes, so you said. But so what? The end is upon us. I may as well die here as anywhere else. I'm tired of running.
But, give me... just a moment... I must gather a few things...
Yes! Yes! You see, you know but you refuse to understand!
Oh, yes. It's all been foretold. The end has begun. Alduin has returned.
Only a Dragonborn can stop him. But no Dragonborn has been known for centuries.
It seems that the gods have grown tired of us. They've left us to our fate, as the plaything of Alduin the World-Eater.
I know, I know. I'm hurrying.
Come in, come in. Mustn't speak of these things in the Ratway.
Haven't you figured it out yet? What more needs to happen before you all wake up and see what's going on?
Alduin has returned, just like the prophecy said!
The Dragon from the dawn of time, who devours the souls of the dead!
No one can escape his hunger, here or in the afterlife! Alduin will devour all things and the world will end. Nothing can stop him!
No, certainly not. A small experiment may have gone awry, but clearly with no discernable consequences.
It's just children playing, Haran. I wouldn't fret over it.
Is everything well, Nelacar?
I say, you didn't hear any loud noises last night, did you? No, ahh... explosion sounds?
I'm not "fretting." I don't want Eirid playing those sorts of games!
I... see. And is this something I'll need to worry about in the future?
I understand Korir's been complaining about our customers again.
I believe he was suggesting their presence causes him harm.
Well, isn't this a surprise!
And there haven't been any... problems?
If I wanted to get yelled at, I'd be home. Can't you leave me be?
Please, come home. Just... Just come home.
Why don't you go... sell something.
Ha! Drink ain't gonna kill me. Not today, anyway.
No, I certainly didn't!
Oh, certainly I hope not! That would be quite unfortunate for my research.
Oh yes, quite fine. My accommodations here continue to be just what I need.
Are you referring to your other customers? No, everything is quite all right. I expect a bit of animosity now and then.
Is there a reason you're asking me?
All right, all right. I'll speak to her.
What of it? It's our Inn, and they cause him no harm.
Well, then he's welcome to eat and drink at home, isn't he?
I see. Well, let me know if things get out of hand.
How much of our money have you spent today, Ranmir? Anything left, or is it all gone?
Oh, I'm sorry. Am I bothering you? Am I making your life more difficult?
Can't. Got at least two rounds to go.
I understand Eirid's been playing "Hunt the Elf" again.
Why? Because I'd rather be here trying to keep my only brother from drinking himself to death.
That's because you always lose... Elf.
I hate this game.
I'm gonna find you, Elf!
Ha! Found you!
Thanks!
Fine, Elf-Lover. I'll find her myself.
Eirid and I are playing Hunt the Elf. It's my job to find the Elf, and kill it dead.
Have you seen it?
I'll find that Elf. I always do.
Thanks!
All right!
Indeed. Speak with me when the box has been opened, and all shall be revealed.
Your continuing aid to Septimus renders him increasingly obsolete. He has served me well, but his time is nearing its end.
Once that infernal lockbox is opened, he will have exhausted his usefulness to me.
When that time comes, you shall take his place as my emissary. What say you?
I am Hermaeus Mora. I am the guardian of the unseen, and knower of the unknown. I have been watching you, mortal.
Most impressive.
Come closer. Bask in my presence.
Aye, that's the work of Septimus Signus.
He's the world's master of the nature of Elder Scrolls, but... well.
He's been gone for a long while. Too long.
Come, my champion.
What is this... it's... it's just a book?! I can see. The world beyond burns in my mind. It's marvelous....
Go forth. Do the biddings of Septimus, for he is working my will in your plane.
Now you have my Oghma Infinium. It contains the knowledge of the ages as revealed to Xarses, my loyal servant.
For hundreds of years it's been shut away from the world. Septimus was a useful tool for unleashing it.
Now it is in your hands. Let us work wonders together...
Be warned. Many have thought as you do. I have broken them all. You shall not evade me forever.
Keep an eye out for Imperials. They still hold this entire region. As long as we don't do anything stupid... we should be all right.
There's a pack of werewolf hunters camped nearby, at Gallows Rock.
The Silver Hand. I think you've met them before.
We're going to slaughter them. All of them.
Lead on. Skjor's already scouting ahead.
To reach the heights of the Companions, you must join with us in the shared blood of the wolf.
He was one of the strongest we had, but numbers can overwhelm. He should not have come without a Shield-Brother.
Get out of here. I'm going to make sure we got the last of them, and see if there's any information to be gotten from the bodies.
You and I have work to do. The Silver Hand will tremble at our sight.
I'm glad you came. It's been a long time since we had a heart like yours among our numbers.
That pitiful ceremony behind the hall does not befit warriors like us.
You are due more honor than some calls and feasting.
We do this in secret because Kodlak is too busy trying to throw away this great gift we've been granted.
He thinks we've been cursed. But we've been blessed. How can something that gives this kind of prowess be a curse?
So we take matters into our own hands.
A sad time.
Like I said in my note, I've heard that you might be Dragonborn.
I'll explain what I want when I want, got it? You'd already be dead if I didn't like the look of you when you walked in here.
But I had to know if the rumors about you were true...
Dragons aren't just coming back, they're coming back to life.
They weren't gone somewhere for all these years. They were dead, killed off centuries ago by my predecessors.
Now something's happening to bring them back to life. And I need you to help me stop it.
If you don't trust me, you were a fool to walk in here in the first place.
So you're the Dragonborn I've been hearing so much about.
You'll be back. If you're really Dragonborn, this is your destiny.
Surprised? I guess I'm getting pretty good at my harmless innkeeper act.
You can't be too careful. Thalmor spies are everywhere.
You ready to hear me out?
Ha. A few years ago, I said almost the same thing to a colleague of mine. Well, it turned out he was right and I was wrong.
I'm not your enemy. I already gave you the horn. I'm actually trying to help you. I just need you to hear me out.
I'm part of a group that's been looking for you... well, someone like you, for a very long time.
If you really are Dragonborn, that is. Before I tell you any more, I need to make sure I can trust you.
Kynesgrove. There's an ancient dragon burial near there. If we can get there before it happens, maybe we'll learn how to stop it.
That mound you remembered sounds like what we're looking for. East of Kynesgrove, right?
It was the only way I could make sure it wasn't a Thalmor trap.
Lead on.
Of course. But why not let him go in first?
Of course. But why not let her go in first?
They may be cowards, but I'm not. I'll go with you.
I suppose you're right. I'm not a fighting man.
Go, and avenge my Laelette for me.
All right. If I make it back to Windhelm, I'll make sure Ulfric knows how you helped me.
Skyrim needs people like you, if we ever hope to regain our freedom from the damned Empire. Good luck.
I was hoping you'd change your mind. Come on. I'm heading to Riverwood, just up the road. My sister Gerdur will help us out.
Karliah? Then she's finally found it.
Do you have Gallus's Journal?
Then you were well informed. I am at this very moment on the cusp of completing my magnum opus on the subject.
I'm calling it "Calcelmo's Guide to the Falmer Tongue." It will revolutionize the way we understand those ancient beings.
No. However, I know someone who might.
The court wizard of Markarth, Calcelmo, may have the materials you need to get this journal translated.
A word of warning. Calcelmo is a fierce guardian of his research. Getting the information won't be easy.
A problem? Let me see it.
This is just like Gallus. A dear friend, but always too clever for his own good.
He's written all of the text in the Falmer language.
I'm not certain how many scholars you're accustomed to dealing with, but I can assure you that personal wealth is our lowest priority.
Gallus had his wealth and he had you. All he had to do was look the other way.
You're a clever girl, Karliah. Buying Goldenglow Estate and funding Honningbrew Meadery was inspired.
I'm no fool, Mercer. Crossing blades with you would be a death sentence.
But I can promise the next time we meet, it will be your undoing.
Did you forget the Oath we took as Nightingales? Did you expect him to simply ignore your methods?
How interesting. It appears Gallus's history has repeated itself.
Karliah has provided me with the means to be rid of you, and this ancient tomb becomes your final resting place.
But do you know what intrigues me the most? The fact that this was all possible because of you.
You always were a quick study.
Enough of this mindless banter!
Give me a reason to try.
Not quick enough, otherwise Gallus would still be alive.
Do you honestly think your arrow will reach me before my blade finds your heart?
"To ensure an enemy's defeat, you must first undermine his allies."
It was the first lesson Gallus taught us.
This better be important.
Dragonborn!
I'm going to find whoever did this.
Oh... what happened?
The way you and your mother forge weapons is impressive.
Your muscles ripple when you work the forge. It's pleasing.
I am honored.
Thank you, my chief.
Malacath is pleased with your strength, Larak.
It's simple Orc smithing practice, my chief. Nothing that hasn't been passed down for years.
You are speaking to Larak, chief of Mor Khazgur.
I have poisons for your weapons, and cures for your ills.
Our chief is strong. That is all that matters.
Larak is strong, but he has much to prove as chief.
You need some armor, outlander?
My son, Olur, learns the forge well. His work brings me pride.
I envy you, outlander. You can choose to leave if you wish.
Soon, I will be of age. The taste is bitter.
Perhaps I'll join the Legion one day, as my father had done.
I work in the forge and the mines. Mother says it pleases her and our people.
Even good hammers warp metal if struck without care.
Patience, my son. The hunter days not welcome the blood of his prey, he waits for it.
He is pleased. Ours is the strongest tribe in all the Reach.
Tell me, mother, does Malacath favor our stronghold?
Do they, my chief? Am I now favored enough to replace Shel by your side at the feast? Back your words with action!
You could challenge Burguk, my brother. Yours is the stronger swordarm.
My chief, Gharol threatens to have me work in the mines. You won't allow my pretty hands to dirty, will you?
The air has the smell of beasts. The hunt is good today.
Oh, thank you my chief!
Ah, the air is good today. I welcome the smell of blood from all the kills I will make.
I saw you working the forge today, wife. Your muscles have grown strong from the metal shaping!
This metal will serve the forge well, my brother.
No more talk of Shel. As chief, I will do what I please, so point your fiery gaze to our enemies.
No, my wife. You have been given other gifts to please your chief than mine work. I will talk to Gharol and all will be well.
Yes, mother. I'm eager to prove my skills today.
Malacath rewards the strong and punishes the weak.
Murbul knows many poisons and cures if you need them.
You wish to trade in Orc steel?
As forge-wife, I'm also in charge of our mine.
I am Burguk's favored wife. He knows my beauty should be praised.
Arob and Gharol eye me with envy. They don't know how to please their chief.
Umurn and Nagrub will be held up as weak next to the son I will one day bear for my chief.
I work the forge, with my mother Gharol. We mine our own ore and shape our own metal.
One day I will shape a weapon fit for a chief. Maybe that chief will be me.
I help in the mines as well as the forge.
I help my mother Arob with the hunt. We bring much meat and skins to the stronghold.
Brother Umurn is a strong warrior. I look forward to fighting him for chiefdom one day.
One day, I intend to be chief. I will fight my father and my brother for it, as is tradition.
Sorry, outlander, but this old Orc cannot help you. Burguk is the chief here.
I don't know how Ghorbash has so much spirit. He should act his age.
I serve my chief and my brothers by working in the mines. It's the only place fit for an aging Orc.
I used to wander the corners of Skyrim like you. I miss those days.
We honor the bravest fighters, but I have seen many types of bravery in my travels.
It is a fine day to work the mines.
You have what you came for. Now leave.
Take your books and go, before I change my mind.
There's no mistake. You're a wanted man... and it's time to pay for your crimes.
Wait... I know you.
In the name of the Jarl, I'm ordering you to stand fast!
Well, your bounty is low... All right, get out of my sight. But you're known to me now, scum. Remember that.
You know what? You're not worth the hassle. Go... be some other guard's problem.
Sure I am. We get some furs and tree branches, paint them white, and then tie them to Stump. Instant Frostbite Spider!
Frodnar... nobody is going to believe that your dog is a... Frostbite Spider. And if they do... they'll kill him. That's not much of a prank.
Wait, so... a costume? You're not serious...
How about... we play tag! You're it!
Hey, no fair! Get her, Stump!
Oh yeah, and what would you rather do?
Ah! You've retrieved the Horn of Jurgen Windcaller.
Well done. You have now passed all the trials.
Come with me. It is time for us to recognize you formally as Dragonborn.
I feel the eyes of angry spirits upon us. We are not welcome here.
Few would dare to trespass in these ruins. What you lack in wisdom, you make up for in courage.
Do not ask me to take treasures from this place. I will not risk the anger of the dead.
They say ruins like these are filled with treasure. Of course, they're filled with traps, too.
I could have sworn I heard something right behind us.
I'll bet some of these artifacts would fetch a good price. I hope they're not cursed.
I wonder what the ancient Nords did in these ruins. Unspeakable sacrifices, maybe?
If these ruins frighten you, take comfort in the knowledge that I am here.
Do try not to set off any traps, will you?
I suppose you intend to carry off every item of value in these ruins.
These ruins might be interesting if I wasn't already an expert on Nordic history.
Remember to check behind you every once in a while. Who knows what might try to sneak up on us.
Keep your eyes open for traps. Old ruins like this are usually filled with them.
The ancient Nords were a savage people, but they built some remarkable crypts and temples.
There's a lot of history in a place like that. I'll bet these walls could tell some amazing stories.
Look at this craftsmanship! These old halls fill some folk with dread, but as a Nord, they fill me with pride.
We're bound to find some gold in these ruins, but out of respect, let's leave a few coins for the dead.
It's strange to think that some of my own ancestors might be buried in here.
Plunder these ruins at your peril, friend. The spirits of the dead won't look kindly upon it.
The Dwemer who built this place were sometimes called Dwarves, but history records that they were no shorter than humans.
There are scholars in Cyrodiil who have dedicated their lives to solving the mystery of the Dwemers' disappearance.
It is remarkable that so many of these ancient Dwemer creations still function.
There are those who believe that the entire Dwemer race will one day return just as suddenly as it vanished.
What good is a sword or an axe against an enemy made of brass and steel?
It's unnatural, building all these machines. If you ask me, the Dwemer got what they deserved.
I don't like fighting these Dwemer creations. Where's the fun in killing something that doesn't even bleed?
Watch out for the hot steam vents down here. They'll burn you worse than fire.
Had they placed their faith in magic instead of machinery, the Dwemer might still be around.
A living Dwemer was once discovered in Morrowind. Can you imagine it, being the last surviving member of your race?
I should bring some of these Dwemer artifacts to the Arcane University in Cyrodiil for study.
I wonder what keeps these machines working so long after their masters disappeared.
The Dwemer created so many wondrous machines, and yet nobody ever followed in their footsteps. Why is that, I wonder?
Among other things, the Dwemer were expert trap-makers. Watch the floor for tripwires and false plates.
I've half a mind to dismantle one of these Dwemer machines. I'd like to see how it works.
I suppose the warmth in this place comes from all the hot steam vents.
Scholars say the Dwemer were an advanced culture. Ha! The Nords outlasted their entire race. So much for being advanced.
I wonder why the Dwemer came to Skyrim. Why did they build this place? I suppose we'll never know.
Do these steel walls and strange Dwemer devices make you uneasy? Perhaps it's just me.
I've heard that you can sell Dwemer artifacts on the black market down in Cyrodiil. They fetch a handsome price, too.
So many shadowy corners to strike from! I could learn to like this place.
Silence will serve us well in this place. Let us take our foes by surprise.
Be wary for traps. If you spy one, try to get an enemy to trigger it.
I can see the battle-lust in your eyes. Doom will surely come to any who cross your path.
If it wasn't for all the dangers in here, this place could almost be cozy.
When I was a child, I dreamed about having a fort like this all to myself.
I wonder if we'll come across some bandits who've just finished roasting a rabbit on a spit. Maybe they'll have a keg of ale, too.
If my ma could see me now, she'd scold me good. She'd tell me I should know better than to go poking around in abandoned forts.
This smell of dank earth and worm-eaten wood is going to make me lose my last meal.
How pathetic, being forced to eke out a miserable existence in a place like this.
Just look at this place. Is this what passes for a fort in Skyrim?
The sooner we can get out of this filth-pit, the better.
These timber supports are rotted through. It's a wonder this place is still standing.
Check every crate, chest and barrel you see. Who knows, maybe a few of them have something valuable inside.
Did you bring a torch? These old forts can get pretty dark inside.
There are forts like this all over Skyrim. The Imperials abandoned most of them a long time ago.
Just look at this place. I've seen better workmanship from Nord children only ten winters old.
So this is what passes for Imperial craftsmanship. Try not to sneeze, or you might bring the whole fort down.
What is that stench? Rotting wood, perhaps? That can't be good.
You'd have to be pretty desperate to want to make a home in this dank hole.
I hope that when I die, I don't end up as a shambling corpse.
Outlaws and brigands are the lowest of the low. They prey on their own countrymen for profit.
It isn't just your blood that a vampire wants, it's your mortal soul.
Fighting draugr is as much about controlling your fear as anything else.
These wizards are mad beyond reason. They understand only violence.
You can easily break a blade on the metal skin of a Dwemer construct.
By Shor, what do you want?
Astrid, I've been hearing some, er... interesting things, from the others... about what you did when you left the Sanctuary last week...
I heard about what happened...
Tell me, brother. Are the stories true?
Tell me, sister. Are the stories true?
Your latest exploit certainly has the Sanctuary talking...
But to be outnumbered twenty to one...
You infiltrated the position, eliminated the target, and made it look like an accident. Impressive. I'd say you earned your bonus on that one!
To kill triplets, secretly, and then have the whole thing blamed on the mother. Remarkable.
You're referring to that blacksmith contract?
You must be talking about the caravan job...
Word certainly travels fast, doesn't it?
The temple contract? Well, I certainly won't deny my involvement. He he he he he.
The Redoran contract? Yes, you heard correctly. In and out of Vvardenfell in less than forty eight hours.
It was a stupid plan, and now Rigel is so mad she won't even let us cut him down from there!
I tried to tell him that he wouldn't get past the traps, but he wouldn't listen...
Aye, and she's added more traps now as well... A moment of silence for our fallen friend...
The crucial nature is pure apparent. Mundane sight traded for another sort.
Generally posting information is need to know only.
He's the sorry bastard who's going to be executed.
A gate guard who let Ulfric Stormcloak escape the city, after he killed the High King.
There's no greater honor than to serve. If you're serious about enlisting, speak to Legate Rikke in Castle Dour.
I... I've been trying to find the time and place to let her know.
I'm sorry, I can't help you.
If you've come to Solitude to join the Legion, speak to Rikke. If not, just keep your nose clean while you're here... or you'll end up like Roggvir.
Yes, Sir. Although I'm not sure you'll be happy to know.
That's the way it is.
I told her. I told her the imperial army would kill her. That this was a war of Jarls and Imperial Generals and we had no part in it.
She just wouldn't hear it.
It was brave of you to come to me with this. I can't thank you for it, not for this, but you have done me a service.
You got Aldis to tell you something? I knew you would. What did he say?
Need something for a potion? You're certainly in the right place.
If you want to know that bad then fine.
Do you mind? I'm busy doing the fishstick. It's a very delicate state of mind!
Unscrupulous daring. It's why you fell in love with me, my dear.
It was a suicide mission! Astrid said as much. Yet here you stand, hale and hearty.
I work silver, like my father, and his father before him.
My wife Kerah runs our jewelry stall in the market.
Silver-Bloods tax everything now that they run the city.
Redguard craftsmanship in every piece.
Are you looking for a gift for a friend, perhaps? Or... a lover?
Perhaps a silver necklace or a silver ring? Easy to wear and soft against the skin.
I have a necklace that would look lovely on you, or on a... special someone.
That's it. I'm placing you under arrest.
This is your last warning. Get out, or get arrested.
You're not supposed to be here...
Yeah, why?
But, I've got the key to the Sabre Cage! Look!
Take down that one with your bare hands too?
What's that supposed to prove? Get back on patrol!
Wasn't our last catch a Cave Bear?
Why, you don't think I could do it?
Troll's blood was he a tough one!
Pfft
So, you're saying you brought that cat down alone?
You will be called on to take swift action. Rise to the challenge, and discover what you are capable of.
You are on the right path, and you will prevail.
It is imperative that you return to your College at once.
You have done well thus far, but trying times are ahead.
By Sithis, you slew everyone! Well, except for that one you kept alive, to serve as witness. Does your wickedness, or your skill, know no bounds?
Bah! A child could have accomplished it. Such is the power of the arcane. If you only knew of the spells I didn't use. He he he he he.
What? You think me too old, too feeble? There is more knowledge in me than in two of you pups put together. And knowledge... knowledge is my weapon.
Once again, you have underestimated me. You see the man, brutish and hulking. But I am wolf as well. Deliberate. Cunning.
When I am become wolf, I am treachery incarnate. And then, then... there is no one who can stand between the hunter and his prey.
When I first received the blessing of the wolf, my creator gave me a valuable piece of advice. "Arnbjorn," he said. "Never lose!" And I never do.
He he. Don't forget that I am Shadowscale. I was, quite literally, born to kill.
It was a simple matter, really, when one has mastered the art of camouflage. Scale becomes stone, becomes shadow. Then... death.
My greatest challenge was deciding which weapon to use, for I am prone to indecision. Bow, dagger... axe? In the end, I opted for my claws.
In truth? It was so easy, I feel a bit guilty. Once again, the sweet, innocent child earned more trust than was wise.
You give me too much credit. For how could I fail? I am, after all, just a little girl. What threat could I possibly pose? He he he he he.
I am the perfect weapon. My innocent, childish form grants me nearly unrestricted access. Poison or a good bite usually takes care of the rest.
I am but a humble instrument of Sithis. Our Dread Father guides me. Therefore, failure is an impossibility.
In the end, I relied on a woman's natural weapons - beauty and seduction. The rest was a formality. Though, a rather wet, messy formality...
When one can divine the future, success may not be assured... but it can at least be anticipated. It helps that I've already foretold my own death.
What's he doing poking his nose in there?
What's she doing poking her nose in there?
Maybe he's looking for something to eat?
Maybe she's looking for something to eat?
What's this? It thinks it can just throw refuse wherever it likes?
Skyrim isn't very friendly for Argonians, so we mostly just keep out of the way.

Good honest work staves off the cold.
I like to bring Grimvar down here sometimes. It reminds him how lucky he is.
When Grimvar asks me why women are being killed around the city. I don't even know what to say to him.

Spare a coin for a poor old woman, milord?
Spare a coin for a poor old woman, milady?
Spare a coin? Talos rewards the generous.
I hope the wind dies down a bit before tonight. Hard when you've got to sleep outside.
Nervous being alone at night. Especially these days, with the Butcher prowling the streets.

These lazy Argonians better get their tails moving.

Ugh. I'll need to bathe thoroughly to get this stench off of me.
Looking for something?

I'd love get one of those Imperial weasels under my boot...
