Nothing, you pig-headed oaf!
What was that you said, venomous wife of mine?
I've worked in the Treasury House for almost twenty years.
So the Stormcloaks took the Reach? Maybe Talos really is on their side.
The selection seems small, but we can provide whatever you need. Just ask.
No, I'm not Arnleif. Yes, I know it's called Arnleif and Sons.
Lisbet handles the customers. I just check the shipments.
Feel free to take a look around the store.
I've been at Arnleif and Sons all my life. It's a good store.
Old Arnleif would be proud of Lisbet. She's kept this place running, thick and thin.
I brought in my cousin Cosnach to help me handle shipments.
"Check the shipments" Lisbet says. "There are no shipments" I says.
No breaks for a good worker out here.
If I can't get you something, I'm sure my wife will bellow at me until I can.
Now you just tell me if you need something.
You come right to me if you find an unwashed floor or lumpy bed.
Isn't my son Hreinn such a good worker? Not like his father at all....
Don't mind the yelling. My parents are always going at each other.
I'm just here to clean up.
My father Kleppr is the one you want to talk to. I just clean up.
Poor Banning. Twice as much work here in the stables without him.
You treat a horse well, it'll take you to Oblivion and back.
Stormcloaks took Markarth. It is good to see Talos worshipped openly again.
Songs are just old memories that help the mead go down quicker.
You should see that Orc over in Understone Keep, have him forge you some new armor...
Sorry. You already have an animal with you.
War dogs cost gold, friend. Come back when you have some.
Haven't seen you here before, egg sister. Welcome!
Well... you probably should go collect it before someone else does, shouldn't you?
Oh... well. Never mind then.
Well what did you go dropping it for? Got my hopes up for nothin'...
You dropped it by mistake? Well, if I was you, I'd go grab it now before somebody does.
Aww. I thought you were done with it.
Well... fine. I wasn't really gonna do anything with it anyway. Hmph.
Well, you'd better go pick it up then, before someone snatches it.
Stay away from my family if you value your life.
Good. But know that further dishonor to my family will mean your death.
You stay away from my family. Or you die. Be smart. Stay away.
This had better be the last I hear of you disrespecting my family.
Mark these words carefully, fetcher. Stay away from my family, or I will paint the ground with your blood.
Any further dishonor done to my kin, and I'll put you in the ground.
That wasn't so hard, now was it.
You! Fetcher! I should kill you for assaulting my family. But I will accept your apology...
You know who I am? You attacked one of my clan. I've come to accept your apology... or your death.
You! You hurt my family. I don't like when my family gets hurt. Say you're sorry. Or say goodbye.
You attacked one of my family. What do you have to say for yourself?
You can't attack my family and get away with it, fetcher! I will have an apology, or your head on a spike. Choose.
Dishonorable dog. You attacked one of my kin. So now you're going to apologize... or I'm going to lop off your head.
Here's the situation. You attacked my family. So now you're going to apologize.. or I'm going to kill you. I hope we understand each other...
Really, the nerve of some people, leaving their trash wherever they please.
What kind of filthy barbarian just throws their trash on the ground?
Oh, by all means. Just leave your refuse wherever you see fit.
There's a place for trash, and that's not it.
In my day, we knew how to dispose of trash properly.
What's the matter with you? You think you can just go dropping your junk wherever you like?
Look at this one. Spoiled as a child, I'll bet. Allowed to leave trash just lying around...
I swear, is there no end to these uncouth savages, just throwing their trash wherever they please...
Hrmmm. Pick up your trash, fetcher.
I never did understand the local custom of simply leaving one's trash wherever is convenient...
Who does that? Who just leaves their garbage lying about?
Gah! Quit throwing your garbage everywhere.
One person's trash is another's treasure. Don't be surprised if you find that gone...
Umm... You dropped a piece armor over there. I was kind of hoping that I could, you know, have it?
Excuse me. I'm assuming that armor you got rid of wasn't an accident, but just in case, I wanted to make sure I could take it.
That armor you got rid of. I can have it, yeah?
Pardon. I saw you toss a piece of armor. You wouldn't mind if I take it, then?
Couldn't help but notice, that, um... you dropped some armor. Just wanted to check with you before I took it. Is that all right?
Hey. That armor. You got rid of it, yeah? I can take it, no problem?
If you're not going use that armor you dropped... do you mind if I take it?
Is he... sifting through trash?
Is she... sifting through trash?
What's he rummaging around in there for?
What's she rummaging around in there for?
Look at him, rummaging through refuse. Wonder what he's lost... besides his mind.
Look at her, rummaging through refuse. Wonder what she's lost... besides her mind.
Tsk. What a man will do for a few coins...
Tsk. What a woman will do for a few coins...
Picking through garbage. Has he no shame?
Picking through garbage. Has she no shame?
What's he digging around in there for?
What's she digging around in there for?
What's he doing? Looking for rubies in rubbish? Get a job!
What's she doing? Looking for rubies in rubbish? Get a job!
Are you out of your mind?
If you value your well being, you will offer an apology...
You should be.
You may care nothing of honor, but I do. I'm not leaving without an apology.
You sure you want it to come to this?
In your death may you find the honor you lacked in life.
What? You stupid or something?
Say you're sorry. Say it now.
That's it!
Really?
You're making a fatal mistake. Apologize, and you may yet live...
So be it.
I will not tolerate this offense against my kin.
Are you mad? Apologize, or I will gut you like a horker.
Then you are fool, and will die for it.
Honor may mean nothing to you, but it means much to me. Your apology, or your life.
Only your death or an apology will restore my family's honor. Last chance.
Then I guess we're done talking.
Perhaps I haven't made myself clear. Your best and least painful course of action is to apologize and promise to leave my family in peace.
No? You sure? 'Cause we're reaching the point of no return here...
Annnddd... Point of no return reached.
Oh, well, um... thank you.
Thanks! I'm going to go put it on right now.
Mighty decent of you.
My thanks!
Well great! Let's see anyone try to hurt me now...
Best luck I've had all day.
I'm going to go get it right now and wear it. Many thanks!
What'd you pick up there? Oh, I wish I saw that first. You're so lucky!
Hey, someone had to take it, right?
Lucky for you that was just sitting there for the taking.
Oh, wow! That was just sitting there? I wish I found it first.
Hope nobody misses that...
You just find that lying around? Must be your lucky day.
He might find something good in there. One man's trash is another's treasure.
She might find something good in there. One man's trash is another's treasure.
The poor dear must truly be hungry to comb through rubbish like that. Hope he finds something edible.
The poor dear must truly be hungry to comb through rubbish like that. Hope she finds something edible.
Sometimes it pays to look in every nook and cranny.
Sometimes it pays to look in every nook and cranny.
When you're hungry, you'll look anywhere for food. Can't blame him.
When you're hungry, you'll look anywhere for food. Can't blame her.
Desperate times call for desperate measures. Maybe he found what he was looking for in there.
Desperate times call for desperate measures. Maybe she found what she was looking for in there.
How unfortunate he has to dig through refuse like that. Someone should give him a piece of bread.
How unfortunate she has to dig through refuse like that. Someone should give her a piece of bread.
You never know what you'll find laying around in a crate or barrel. People toss the darnedest things in them.
You never know what you'll find laying around in a crate or barrel. People toss the darnedest things in them.
Oh! Look over there!
That's certainly unusual.
Some folk - they got no shame.
Well now! That's a shamefully absurd way to go around in public.
Ummm... they'd lock me up if I went around like that.
What is that half-naked fetcher trying to prove?
What a vulgar display.
That's not something you see every day...
Gracious! This one is certainly unaccustomed to such sights...
Well now! Aren't you putting on quite the show?
Shameful, really. I blame the parents.
Oh, dear. Would you look at that? How embarrassing.
Look at that fool.
Gah! Look over there. I can't believe it.
It takes a lot of confidence to walk around naked. Trust me on that.
Whoa.
Crazy person... Something must be wrong with its head, to strut around like that.
They're half-naked. That ain't right. Somebody's gotta say somethin'...
Look! Look!
Somebody get that idiot some armor...
A bit much, going around like that...
Why the impropriety!
Never see me strutting around like that!
Crazy, naked n'wah...
Whoa... Not even I'm that drunk...
Positively vulgar.
Somebody get that fool a blanket.
Despite the shame, this one can't help but stare.
By Ysmir's beard! I didn't know it was gonna be a party!
For shame! You should be locked up for strutting around like that.
Oh my. Been a lot of years since I've seen something like that...
Got to admire the chops it takes to walk around like that.
Not my thing, but who am I to judge...
Wait until the boys hear about this.
Now that's something else, right there!
You left this back there. Figured you'd be wantin' it. Here ya go.
I think you accidentally mislaid this, dear. Here you go.
You dropped this. Good thing I found it and not someone else, otherwise you might have lost it for good.
You left this over there. You want it back, right? Here.
I think you dropped this! Good thing I brought it to you, huh?
You should take more care, leaving things lying about. Here.
Excuse me. You misplaced this, I believe? There you go. Take care now.
You left this over there. You're lucky - someone else might have kept it for himself. Here you go.
We've all heard that one before, Bryn! Quit kidding yourself.
Dying breed, eh? Well what do you call that then!
Give it up, Brynjolf... those days are over.
I'm telling you, this one is different...
It's time to face the truth, old friend. You, Vex, Mercer... you're all part of a dying breed. Things are changing!
You need to meet with him and give him whatever you think you can't live without while you're inside the embassy.
The Thalmor are paranoid and thorough. You won't get in the front door with anything but the clothes on your back.
Don't worry, it will all be waiting for you when you get back.
Just make sure you get back out of there alive with the information we need.
Good luck.
Really? You're who she picked? I hope she knows what she's doing.
Good. I have your invitation to the party.
But the only way you're going to get past the guards is if they really believe you're an invited guest.
Which means you need to look the part, and not be armed to the teeth. Here, put this on.
Okay. I'll get this inside the Embassy for you. I've got to go. I'll find you at the party, don't worry.
This is no time for cold feet. I've put everything in your hands here, not least Malborn's life.
Now do whatever it is you need to do, and let's get going before we miss our chance!
You can't go to a party at the Thalmor Embassy dressed like that.
Have you given Malborn the gear you want to smuggle into the embassy?
Your job is to get into the party without being fingered as a spy. I'll take care of the rest.
Here's the deal. I can smuggle some equipment into the Embassy for you.
Don't plan on bringing anything else in with you. The Thalmor take security very seriously.
Give me what you can't live without, and I'll make sure to get it into the Embassy. The rest is up to you.
When you're ready, I'll keep the rest of your gear safe until you get back.
You'll only have what Malborn smuggled in for you, plus whatever you can pick up inside.
I'll give you a few more minutes. I can't wait here all day. It's my neck on the line, remember.
Well, well... color me impressed, lass. I wasn't certain I'd ever see you again!
The Hag's Cure is here for all your discreet needs...
A little bit of old Reach magic can cure whatever ills you....
Oh yes... you'll need one of my special brews...
Not scared of an old woman now, are you?
I'm Bothela's assistant. She's the one you should talk to if you need an herb or a potion.
There's so much to learn about the shop. I'll never get it all straight.
Why can't the men in this city leave me alone?
Alms. I said "alms" you backbiter. Give a crippled worker a helping hand.
They don't like us talking to people when we work.
Got metal to shape.
Busy with the smelter.
Can't talk. Mulush will beat me if I stop working.
Can't you see I'm working? Beat it.
I oversee Markarth Smelter. Worker gets out of line, I get him back in line.
These workers. Always complaining. Always excuses.
Shipment of silver this month is behind. Nepos is to blame, not me.
I tell Thonar I can only smelt what Nepos and his miners dig up.
I'm in charge of the prisoners here.
No funny business. I'm watching you.
You do something out of line, I'll put you down.
Don't reach for a shiv around me, or we'll both regret it.
Whenever I get out of here, I'm going to go kill a Nord.
I was sent here because someone said I was a Forsworn.
I wonder how grandma Bothela is doing. She's probably worried sick about me.
You need to not bother me.
You serve your time by digging. So dig.
I got locked up one too many times. Now I'm in for life.
I swear, if I ever get out of here, I'm giving up thieving. Really.
So far so good. Let's hope nobody saw us slip out.
We need to pass through the kitchen. Your gear is hidden in the larder.
Just stay close and let me do any talking, got it? Follow me.
Tss! Get out of here, I saw nothing.
Rules, is it, Tsavani? I didn't realize that eating Moon Sugar was permitted.
Your gear is in that chest. I'll lock the door behind you. Don't screw this up.
Who comes, Malborn? You know I don't like strange smells in my kitchen.
A guest, feeling ill. Leave the poor wretch be.
Perhaps I should ask the Ambassador...
A guest? In the kitchens? You know this is against the rules...
Of course. Let me see if we have another bottle of that.
I'll be waiting by the door for everyone to be distracted.
Hurry it up. I've got to get back before I'm missed.
Come on. If someone misses me at the party, we're both dead.
I need to lock the door behind you or the patrols might notice something's wrong.
I tried to tell them. They wouldn't listen. Fools. It's all come true... all I could do was watch our doom approach...
Dragons, pah. They can be killed. The Blades killed many in their early days as dragon-slayers.
No, the dragons are merely the final portent of the End of Days.
Oh, yes! The prophecies make clear the signs that will precede the end times. One by one, I have seen them fulfilled.
You're right, I forget myself. I've lived without hope for so long...
The prophecies are clear. Only the Dragonborn can stop Alduin.
What? You're... can it really be true? Dragonborn?
Then... there is hope! The gods have not abandoned us! We must... we must...
Then... there is hope after all. For so long, all I could do was watch our doom approach, helplessly.
The Blades knew, once upon a time. Alduin's Wall is the key. Yes, yes!
Don't move. Don't move. I have it here, somewhere... never thought it would be any use, but now...
We must go, quickly now. Take me to Delphine. We have much to discuss.
Important? Far more than even she realizes! If you are Dragonborn, then...
No! You're wrong. You must be wrong. Laelette may have met her fate out in the marsh.
I refuse to believe Alva had anything to do with this. There is no way you can prove it to the Jarl.
Like I said. It got hot and smoky. Now it's cold and quiet.
I wish someone would find me.
That's cheating. We're playing hide and seek. You have to find me before Laelette does.
Don't you remember? His name is Hroggar.
I can't tell you. She might hear me. She's so close.
If you can find me first, I can tell you.
I thought Laelette left to join the war. I hadn't heard from her since she left.
Perhaps we can go another few decades without a disaster. That would be nice.
The exact nature of my research is a secret, I'm afraid, but it really is quite valuable.
I don't think anyone here truly understands how useful my work will be.
When I've finished what I'm working on, everyone will understand its significance.
We have survived difficult times, and are better mages for it. We owe you that, Arch-Mage. Thank you.
Illusion really is the least appreciated of the schools of magic.
If only I could be free of the mundane, day-to-day activities life so often requires.
I'm not one for commendations, but I might've lost everything that matters. So... thanks.
Hundreds of years have gone into assembling this collection. It's going to stay pristine, understand?
You need a book, you talk to me. Otherwise you're going to find yourself in a lot of pain.
I don't care if you wrote it yourself -- if you want a book from here, you go through me.
The Arcanaeum is always accepting new volumes. I'll take what I can get.
We all have you to thank for saving the College. Your position is well-deserved.
I can't wait to see who succeeds Mirabelle. I've no doubt she'll be promoted to Arch-Mage at some point.
Certainly a few of my colleagues aren't quite as talented as they'd like to believe.
Savos Aren is wise beyond his years. He knows real talent when he sees it.
Nasty business with that Ancano fellow. Pity that I was unable to assist, but I was... otherwise occupied.
There are few places where one can pursue my type of work without fear of persecution.
That 'incident' in Morthal was just a misunderstanding. No matter what the guards claim.
The people of Skyrim have no appreciation for what we do here. Not that I care.
Should you ever fall victim to an unfortunate accident, I promise your remains will be put to good use...
Restoration is a perfectly valid school of magic, and don't let anyone tell you otherwise!
Why are my research materials constantly disappearing?
Once again, I have found very insulting notes left in my personal effects. Simply barbaric.
Just because I don't fit in here is no reason for others to ridicule me behind my back.
I think the College is due for a change in leadership.
If you're working on any experiments, please clean up after yourself.
Just like anywhere else, it's all about who you know. Lucky for you, you know me.
I hope I can count on you if I ever need a favor.
The Arch-Mage triumphant! The College... Skyrim... All of Tamriel owes you a great debt!
There are some experiments the College frowns on. If they're aware of them, of course.
If you ever need something, especially something hard to come by, let me know.
I've spent a long time building up a list of contacts for goods from all over Tamriel.
I am always available should you need me, Arch-Mage.
The College will be here for you, no matter what. We always look after our own.
Education is never appreciated until after the fact. Young people never understand this.
Have you, by chance, seen my research notes? I seem to have misplaced them.
What you learn here will last you a lifetime. Several, if you're talented.
It's no secret that the College's reputation in Skyrim is... tainted.
Please don't do anything to anger the locals. They don't need additional reasons to hate us.
It is not the place of the College to become involved in local politics.
Please don't bother the Arch-Mage unless it's absolutely necessary.
If there are any problems, let me know. It's my job to keep things running smoothly.
Day-to-day operations are my responsibility, which means if you become a problem, you get sent to me.
Mine is not a popular position, but someone needs to keep things running smoothly around here.
Make Laelette go away!
Really? How did we afford that? Besides, I like the gown I already have.
Oh certainly, dear sister. We couldn't afford to have our customers look like the simple pigs they are, now could we?
Miss Stentor, watch your tongue in the Jarl's court!
Svari, Jala said you stole an apple from her cart yesterday. Is this true?
Patience, sister. Patience.
It's the root that looks like an ugly little man.
Steward, I will need the following reagents for my studies. Please see to them.
Good. And while you're at it, perhaps you should sew your mouth shut when our fine customers are within earshot, hrm?
My lady, I'm pleased to inform you that you have a new gown for court, specially tailored by madame Endarie.
Not that I mind the break, of course. But, still...
I suppose in wartime, people aren't looking to part with their old junk...
Silana, you don't seem yourself. Is something the matter?
Of course they do, brother. You keep raising their rent.
And? I've seen jarls come and go. This one only became Jarl because her husband died. She'll be replaced in a matter of months.
She does, my lady. But apparently, she hates you just a little less than the others.
I see you have meditated on this subject quite thoroughly. I shall not stop you, but please, try to attend to your temple duties as well.
Even a fine weapon is only as good as its wielder, sir. But they should fetch a high price from the Imperial army.
Wish I could learn some of that charm. I'm just good with bows, not people.
Possession of the holds change on an almost daily basis, but I can report that Haafingar is still secure. Your people are safe.
Got any work?
I ain't begging. I just need a job. Anything.
You'd better hope the war's over by then. The Stormcloaks would eat you alive!
One more reason to pray for the fighting to end then, my love.
Certainly, acolyte. What troubles you?
Fish? Again?
Potatoes? Again?
I know, my lady. I just needed to see you outside of court, away from all of the politics.
Profits continue to rise. The warehouse continues to bring in good revenues, and rent on your stores in the Well is acceptable.
Listen to you, speaking of sacrifice. You've never gone hungry a day in your life, Erikur!
I'm sorry...
We barely have enough for ourselves...
Here, it's all I can spare...
We took you in, didn't we? We would be glad to extend our welcome to any other who showed your dedication. What more could we do?
Yeah, but can he hammer the anvil for 8 hours straight?
Sure, but I bet he can't hold his mead as well as me!
A word, my love?
Yes, headmaster.
That clumsy maid at the palace spilled wine on my best suit. Sister, I believe it's time for a replacement.
The Stormcloaks only rebel because the Empire uses Skyrim as its personal larder. The more they take, the more support the rebels gain!
Ah, that must be it. So many of the men are serving in battle. I shall pray to Stendarr that they return victorious.
Hmph. Music tames the most savage beasts. Even Nords.
Your concerns are duly noted, Legate, but unfounded. I assure you, this war will be over in another six months.
The same place I learned my charm and quick wits: I used to be an Imperial ranger, my boy!
You there. Have you performed your voice exercises today?
Your solo performance is about a week away. I expect to hear your early rendition tomorrow, understand?
Pardon me, but I trust you've been working on your poem, on the classical heroes of Skyrim, yes?
Eat up, Svari.
Melaran, you lazy milk drinker, have you finished studying the imports from Black Marsh?
I don't see any reason why we'd need them. As long as those dogs stay outside of our borders, we're fine.
Try not to play favorites, Lady Ateia. It does a disservice to the whole class.
Of course not! Complacency is the bane of art. They're in good hands with you, Inge.
I'm sorry to break it to you, Giraud, but students are an unfortunate necessity of a college. Do try to persevere.
Yes, well, see that you keep at it. That is all.
I'll be the judge of that. Now, back to your practice.
Patience, Illdi. We study the heroes of the past so we can have better understanding of the heroes of today... and tomorrow.
I'm sure it will be fine. Just don't be late.
That may well be our only answer. I will empower my Steward to seek out capable individuals to secure Haafingar. Thank you, my Thanes.
I respectfully disagree, sir. Stendarr and Zenithar may demand strength from them, but Mara and Dibella demand compassion from us.
And what would my impetuous Thane Bryling suggest, instead?
Sir, these are missing commands for about half of the commanders.
Yes, Lady Ateia. My voice is strong as a Dragonborn's!
Always! Perfection takes dedication, after all!
Yes, madam. I know I have a lot to work on.
I've converted the classic tale of Eyldi the Bear to be performed with drums. I think you'll be impressed!
At this point, I hardly see how your advice would improve it. It's flawless.
Oh, thank you! I've been working on an original piece. I hope it can stand up to the classics!
Yes sir, Professor Gemane! It's a war epic about Hoag Merkiller's last stand!
Must it be written? Poetry is so dull when it isn't set to music.
Why do we always focus on retelling the old stories? There are so many new stories being made, right now...
At the market, father.
It's okay, dear. More work than ever down at the blacksmith.
We'll make it through, dear. We always do.
Who needs customers and their old junk?
Father oughtta punch them in the mouth! That'd get people in here!
Right you are, elf! Or even from the rebels, if they've got enough money to make a competitive offer.
I'm afraid he can. His authority comes from the emperor. Who you also serve, technically.
I know you and father Rorlund have many duties to attend to in the temple, but sometimes I feel that there are more important matters to attend to.
That is quite enough. Perhaps I will raise these issues when I have an audience with General Tullius.
How's it coming there, Jawanan?
Sure has been quiet around the shop lately.
We hardly ever get new customers anymore.
Yeah, you said that yesterday.
Give him time. In a couple years, he'll be the age you were when you started!
Right you are! Guess I'll keep training you... for now.
How are your students coming along?
Miss Stentor, these are quite expensive. I trust you know how limited the hold's funds are at this time?
I know, and I appreciate it dearly. But I can't help but feel we could be doing more...
The Stormcloaks are far from the only threat to the hold. The roads are beset by bandits and beasts... and now there's even talk of bloody dragons.
Your wish is my command! As long as it doesn't involve much physical activity. I seem to be having trouble staying on my feet at the moment.
Yeah, that was a shame.
But now Alva takes care of me. And I take care of her.
Really? How nice.
That's true, but I'm in a new house now. A better house.
I have Alva to take care of now. I don't need Helgi anymore.
Let me know if you find out anything about the fire.
I hope Alva is not what you think.
He was cruel to her. He would beat her every night.
It's no wonder she fled in the middle of the night.
She came by so Alva could kiss her goodbye.
Then she left to join the Stormcloaks.
Any word on that fire?
I hear the steward asked you to look into the fire.
Have you found out anything about Alva?
I hear you put Laelette out of her misery.
You speak to Igmund. Son of Hrolfdir. Jarl of Markarth.
Markarth is not without its problems, but the Empire is here to maintain order.
Forsworn have troubled the Reach for too long. I intend to deal with them.
I'm Igmund's uncle as well as his steward.
The Forsworn know every stone in the Reach. We have to wait them out.
As his housecarl, Igmund's security is my chief concern.
Igmund's father was killed by Forsworn. I won't rest until they've paid with their blood.
Raerek is far too cautious, but I'll agree with him on any measure that keeps this city safe.
Need a new blade?
I learned to forge when I was a pup, then I joined the Legion and mastered it.
I served in the Legion with Thongvor Silver-Blood. He's a good man and a tough soldier.
I'm the Jarl's personal blacksmith, but that doesn't mean I can't sell you a few pieces for a fair price.
I'm Ghorza's assistant. I help her with the forge.
I'm not very good for anything more than smithing nails right now.
One time, I almost cut a finger off sharpening a blade. I hate blood.
The artifacts I've discovered in Nchuand-Zel will make history one day.
I've only ever lost a few guards to Steam Centurions. Good odds, considering...
I used to travel so much before all this Forsworn nonsense. Such an inconvenience...
I'm Calcelmo's nephew. I help him around the laboratory.
I'm afraid to touch half of the things we keep in the tower.
Don't let my uncle go on about his theories. You'll be here all night.
I try not to think about how my uncle got all the Dwemer Centurion parts.
Before you say anything, no, I am not from the Reach.
I have meals to cook for the entire castle and almost no help.
I have to work, or Anton will rip my head off.
Anton sure looks like he's from the Reach, but don't tell him that.
Only reason Jarl keeps Anton on is because he cooks up a storm.
Don't mind the dogs, they get anxious when they haven't had dinner.
You should talk to Anton if you need anything. He's the master chef.
Even the dead need company. That's what Arkay provides.
We all come into Arkay's light in the end, but there's no need to rush.
Markarth is a city of wealth, but gold doesn't follow you in death.
Arkay's blessing to you.
You have the honor of addressing a member of the Thalmor. Bask in it.
The Aldmeri Dominion is the only thing keeping the Empire on its feet.
This war is such nonsense. All this fuss over their false human god.
I understand. Hopefully I'll be able to save my meager earnings up to buy them one day.
She's an absolute pleasure to work for! In fact, I almost feel guilty accepting pay from her.
Why, just this last week I was commenting to my fellow meadery workers just how lucky we are to be working for such a charitable family.
Sure am! I thank my lucky stars every time I look to the night sky that Maven took me in and gave me this opportunity.
I... I... I don't know what to say. Such kindness is unheard of in these times.
Here. I want you to accept this as payment for your sacrifice and I... well, we both thank you.
Nivenor is my wife. How I was ever able to meet such a gorgeous creature is one of life's mysteries.
We're... at odds about my charity. She thinks the people in this town have become lazy and expecting a handout.
I try to include her in my lessons from Maramal over at the Temple of Mara, but she prefers to shop instead. Different strokes, eh?
Lady Maven's been developing the secret recipe for Black-Briar Mead over the last several decades.
She uses a very secret blend of spices, fresh honey from Goldenglow Estate and specially constructed aging casks to ensure the best flavor.
Even when you get to the last sip in your flagon, it'll taste just as good as the first!
It's going to take a lot more than that.
Damn it! Sorry. I'm not very good at this.
Look, just don't say anything to Maven, will you? I've spent weeks trying to get that right and if she sees how stupid I am, she'll get rid of me.
Yes, actually. I'm partnered with Maven Black-Briar. I put up a chunk of the gold she needed to get the place off the ground.
We've been staggeringly successful so far. In fact, I'm almost surprised how quickly she's generated business for us.
My name may not be on the label, but some of my blood, sweat and tears are in every drop. Uh, figuratively of course.
Yeah... fired. Sure. That's what I meant.
Every Argonian wedding proposal band is supposed to have three flawless amethysts as part of their design.
Two of the gems, the ones on the outside, represent the couple to be married... one for the husband, one for the wife.
The amethyst in the center represents The Hist, the entity that all Argonians consider a sacred part of our lives.
Three in fact. They're my own recipe. Brought them over here from my days as a bartender in Gideon.
First is the "Velvet Lechance" which is a mixture of blackberry, honey, spiced wine and a touch of nightshade... perfectly safe, I assure you.
Second, we have the "White-Gold Tower" which is heavy cream with a layer of blended mead, lavender and dragon's tongue on top.
Last, and only for the bravest of souls, we have the "Cliff Racer" which is Firebrand Wine, Cyrodiilic Brandy, Flin and Sujamma.
Only the best in all of Riften! I own Riften Fishery.
Started out with a single fishing pole and now I have an entire building and two boats to my name. Not bad for a middle-aged Nord, eh?
Too bad I haven't had the same success helping this city shed its poverty. Nivenor thinks I'm wasting my time trying, but I disagree.
I was hoping you'd offer. I didn't think it was my place to ask such a favor of you. I thank you.
That is the truth, my friend! Maven is a saint and don't let anyone tell you otherwise.
Outsiders aren't trusted in Markarth. Best keep that in mind.
Can't believe I'm posted here. Even the beds are made of stone.
Don't mistake my political beliefs as a substitute for my family values. They're still my parents, and I love them dearly.
I just think they've let my sister's death skew their outlook on Ulfric and what he really brings to the table with this war.
I'm not so certain that Ulfric's intentions are as noble as they think. I think Ulfric cares about Ulfric and the rest is a smokescreen.
I'd... appreciate if you kept my opinion between us. The last thing I want to do is have my parents "lose" another child.
Come back and see us again.
If you'll excuse me, I have other things to attend to.
Let's see those Stormcloaks try to break into this canyon.
The Empire didn't think we could take the Reach, but here we are.
Imperial bastard.
Farewell, kinsman.
Prison's not so bad. Less complaints when I kill people.
What do you want? Have digging to do.
Other prisoners get suspicious when we talk to each other, so keep it down.
I'm innocent. The Nords framed me. I was nowhere near that murder weapon.
Jarl decided I was too much of a problem. Threw me in here with the Forsworn.
Support Ulfric or die trying. There's nothing in between.
Talos preserve you.
Talos guide you.
It's been good talking to you.
Good, now maybe I can get back to work.
Well, that was important.
Come back and see us again!
Travel safe.
Remember to give at the temple.
Excuse me.
If you've got the coin, you're welcome here. Otherwise, hit the road.
Welcome to the Bee and Barb, milord. If I can interest you in one of our special drinks, you let me know.
Welcome to the Bee and Barb, milady. If I can interest you in one of our special drinks, you let me know.
It is my personal mission to ensure that the Bards College preserves the great deeds of the past, in both poetry and song.
I'm so glad you got back from Olaf's Tomb okay. They gave you a tougher task then they gave me.
I can always find time to share a word with a warrior.
The Jarl is not to be disturbed with common affairs of court. You direct those concerns to me.
Someday, a gallant hero will ride up and take me away. Someday...
Is that all? My friend, you've come to the right person. You could say that causing a scene is somewhat of a specialty of mine.
Stand back and behold my handiwork.
Wonderful! I can begin to repay your generosity immediately. Say on, friend.
Stand back and watch a true professional wreck a party.
Of course! I would do anything for you, my one and truest friend! What do you need from me?
Ah, the one generous soul amongst a gathering of pinch-pennies and lick-spittles!
If there's anything I can ever do for you, do not hesitate to call upon me!
My friend, you are very perceptive! I have a powerful thirst that cannot be slaked!
And none of the waiters will bring me a drink. Elenwen must have told them to cut me off, the frigid bitch.
Afraid I'll cause another scene, I suppose...
Aye. A gift from the Black-Briars after they kicked the previous family out... place called Riftweald Manor.
He never stays there, just pays for the upkeep on it. Hired some lout by the name of Vald to guard the place.
Then I have an important task for you.
I need you to break into Mercer's home and search for anything that could tell us where he's gone.
Be careful, lad. This is the last place in Skyrim I'd ever want to send you.
Ooh. What a fun trick. Teach me, teach me!
For someone called the "Listener," you do an awful lot of shouting...
Oooh. Time to kill?
Finally!
Ooh. That had to hurt.
What? Why would the stranger attack innocent Cicero?
Ow! What did I do?
What have I done, Listener? Has Cicero offended you?
By Sithis, you will pay for that!
Why you insolent little bastard...
What is this madness? Rexus, help me!
Wait! That's not how this is supposed to work!
What are you doing? I thought we were friends!
You can't be serious.
Uhh.. thank you...
But... we had a deal...
Yes, Listener?
I am yours to command.
How may I serve you, my Listener?
Please...
What is it you want?
Just... just stop. And I'll tell you where I've hidden my treasure.
It's... in a hollowed-out rock. In the tundra. A bit west of Whiterun. Now please... please stop.
Oh gods, you've taken everything I have. Just kill me and be done with it...
Why are you people doing this?
Is it information you want?
This... this is about the inheritance, isn't it? All right! I'll tell you where I've hidden it.
It's in a hollowed-out tree stump. In the marsh, south of Solitude. Now please, you'll let me go now. Right?
Why don't you be a dear and let me go now. All right? I won't say a word to anyone. Our little secret. I promise.
You... you can't do this to me...
Do you have any idea who I am? When my father hears about this, he will gut you.
Ah, now I understand. This is about that my father's hidden cache, isn't it? The dowry for my wedding.
Ha! Well it's safely hidden away, in a hollowed-out tree stump. Just outside Riften, tucked away nice and secure. You'll never find it.
Damn it. I gave it away, didn't I?
I'll tell you anything. Anything! Just please stop... hurting me...
I just...  can't take the pain. Not a second longer.
I'll give you money. Treasure. I have some things hidden away. Take them all. They're yours. Just please... stop.
Just north of Windhelm. There's a hollowed-out rock. I have some things hidden in there. Take them! They're yours.
I've told you everything I can. I don't know what else you could want...
You dare invade my home?
Yeargh!
Raaarrgh!
Grragh!
Arugh!
What do you expect to find? My juggling balls? He he.
Hey, that's my lint! Ha, Cicero is just joking. You can have it.
If I had anything worth giving, I'd have offered it already.
Anything good in there?
See if they have any cheese. I'm getting a bit... peckish.
You're putting your hand... in my pocket? Have you gone complete cracked?
I've hardly anything worth pickpocketing...
This how you get your thrills? Feeling up an old man?
I haven't been touched like that since I visited a certain brothel in the city of Sentinel...
It would seem you're not the accomplished pickpocket you think you are...
What do you hope to find? Sweets and my dolly? Or maybe some of my spare Nightshade?
You're not actually... picking my pocket? Well, you've got guts. I'll give you that.
It's clear that memories are far too short.
And it's clear money matters more to Dagur than honor.
Eirid's a little girl who doesn't understand that they're responsible for destroying our home.
Because they don't care about you or I, cub. They think they're better than all of us, and that we can just be thrown away.
That's my boy.
Aren't they all?
I'm sure you're right.
This is what it's come to. No one seems to care what they've done to our home.
Eirid says the mages at the College are nice people, but you said they're bad.
Why did they do that? Why would they be so evil?
I hate them.
Looks to be another cold day.
Not like this. Feels colder than usual. Bet it's those damned Mages.
That... wizard is still at the Inn. I can't believe Dagur allows him to stay there.
It's... it's like watching an artist create a masterpiece.
And another soul for Sithis.
You are an inspiration. Do you know that?
She's the Master-Wizard here. Arch-Mage Aren may be in charge, but Mirabelle runs the place.
You'd do well to remember that.
Is that... the best... you can do...
Have you... gone... completely... mad?
This... this is... an outrage...
It will... take more than that... to stop me...
Oh, but the Listener jests with poor Cicero. You already have company. You don't need my help at all.
I am honored to be chosen.
But you already have a traveling companion. I fear I would just get in the way.
I am yours, Listener.
Oh, well... You seem to already have assistance. Surely you mean to remain inconspicuous...
And we understand a rebel garrison is close to abandoning the resistance. We need to send them a little encouragement.
And we hear a handful of Imperials are realizing they are Stormcloaks at heart. We need to send them a little encouragement.
First, if we can assassinate their commander, we'll be able to strike them while they're busy tucking their tails between their legs.
First, our intelligence suggests one of the rebel garrisons has become disaffected. If we play our cards right, we should be able to turn them.
What an amazing day this has been. I hope you're enjoying the festivities. I'm... I'm just so overwhelmed. Such kindness... for me.
Ha ha! The news is everywhere! Vittoria Vici, the Emperor's cousin, butchered at her own wedding! Well done!
Let's see his eminence try to ignore this.
Ha ha ha ha! Yes, yes indeed! Hail the Imperial Legion! Hail the Stormcloaks! Hail Solitude and the Eight Divines!
On this glorious day, we honor everyone! Ha ha ha ha!
Oh, no need to be shy! My day is your day! Please - eat, drink, make merry! We're all friends here!
Please, enjoy yourself!
Her name is Vittoria Vici. She oversees the East Empire Company's business holdings in Solitude.
The wedding is being held in that city, at the Temple of the Divines. Her death will cause an uproar, which is exactly what we want.
Vici is likely to address her guests frequently, as is the wedding custom. Kill her when she does that, and I promise you a significant bonus.
Now go. And give my best to the bride.
Oh! Well, you tell your Stormcloak friends "hello" right back at them! This is a day of healing! Legion, Stormcloaks - all are welcome here!
Oh, Asgeir! My husband! My dear, sweet husband! Why would someone do this to you? Why? This was supposed to be the happiest day of my life!
My wife is dead! Dead!
Well aren't you a pretty thing? Should my new bride by worried? He he he he.
With Vici's murder, you've started us down a path the Dark Brotherhood hasn't traveled in centuries. The assassination of an Emperor.
And now, your reward. A unique spell to summon a legend of the Dark Brotherhood. His soul serves us now in death, as his body once did in life.
Ah, and of course, a bonus, for killing Vici while she addressed her guests, as instructed. Gold... simple and pure. Spend it as you will.
Now then. Time to proceed to the next stage of the plan. Go and speak with Gabriella. She's been helping me arrange your next contract.
Orgnar, hold down the bar for a minute, will you?
Sky Haven Temple. An ancient Blades sanctuary, hidden deep in the crags of the Reach. That's where we'll find Alduin's Wall.
This way.
You see, right here. Sky Haven Temple, constructed around one of the main Akaviri military camps in the Reach, during their conquest of Skyrim.
This changes everything, of course. There's no time to lose. We must locate...  let me show you. I know I had it here, somewhere...
Shh!
One moment, I know, time is of the essence, but mustn't leave secrets for the Thalmor... there's one more I must bring...
Esbern. What are you getting at?
Delphine! I... it's good to see you. It's been... a long time.
Oh yes! Dragonborn! Indeed, yes.
This is where they built Alduin's Wall, to set down in stone all their accumulated dragonlore.
A hedge against the forgetfulness of centuries.
Esbern, what...
Well, then. You made it, safe and sound. Good. Come on, I have a place we can talk.
Let's pretend we haven't. What's Alduin's Wall and what does it have to do with stopping the dragons?
Either of you?
Yeah, sure.
Now then. I assume you know about...
Alduin's Wall was where the ancient Blades recorded all they knew of Alduin and his return. Part history, part prophecy.
Its location has been lost for centuries, but I've found it again. Not lost, you see, just forgotten.
The Blades archives held so many secrets... I was only able to save a few scraps...
Do you know what he's talking about?
I don't like being out in the open like this.
Did you hear that?
I'll need this... No, no, useless trash... where'd I put my annotated Anuad?
Well, I guess that's good enough... let's be off...
Give me... just a moment...
A wise and foresighted policy, in the event.
Despite the far-reaching fame of Alduin's Wall at the time - one of the wonders of the ancient world - its location was lost.
Ah! Here it is. Come, let me show you.
It's good to see you, too, Esbern. It's been too long, old friend. Too long.
You mean... you don't mean to say you haven't heard of Alduin's Wall?
Shor's beard! He's going after the Eyes of the Falmer? That was Gallus's pet project.
If he gets his hands on them, you can be certain he'll be gone for good and set up for life.
We've scoured the town and I've spoken to every contact we have left. No sign of Mercer.
Any luck on your end?
Agreed. He's taken everything the Guild has left, and to go after one of the last greatest heists is just an insult.
I've spoken to Karliah, and made amends for how the Guild's treated her. Now she wishes to speak with both of us.
Quickly, we have no time to lose.
Better question would be "what did he leave."
Mercer took everything. Even all of our plans are gone.
Stabbed in the back. It's like the Dark Brotherhood all over again.
If I see Frey, I'll pluck his eyes from his skull with my bare hands!
We can talk after I find out what's going on.
I'm relieved Brynjolf has an open mind or that could have gotten bloody.
I can't believe this is happening, lad.
What's that? Oh yes, yes. Thank you, I will.
Did she, perhaps, mention any dangerous research she might be working on? Anything that could get her in trouble?
Can you believe we're here? Saarthal, of all places.
Are you looking for any specific volumes, Drevis?
I'm surprised that the College is allowed to excavate here.
Can I help you with anything, Nirya?
I'm here to help you with books, Nirya. That's all.
Ah, yes. Of course. No, I don't need anything.
Hmm? Oh, no. No, not really. I... Actually, I've forgotten what I was looking for.
I was wondering. Has Faralda been in recently? Borrowed any books that you can recall?
I bet your family would be amazed, right?
Amazed isn't the right word. They might find this... offensive.
Ah. Well, then. Let me know if you need anything.
Nirya...
You know, this is one of a very few places where you can receive a real education in the schools of magic.
I think my parents reserved a spot for me here at the College on the day I was born.
There's a lot of pressure for me to excel in my studies. I hope I can live up to expectations.
My sweet Leanora, I will never let anything happen to us. I promise.
It brings me joy to hear that, but what possessed you to say that so suddenly?
It will have to do. Besides, if it came to blows, I'm sure the Snow-Shod's would come out here and assist us.
Just worry about keeping the fields tended and let the Jarl worry about the war.
My darling, you've made me the happiest woman in all Skyrim.
I suppose so. I just fear they won't make it out here in time.
I've already lost a family, and we're about to start a new one. I don't want that to happen again.
I'm not so sure the few guards they've assigned to the farm are enough.
My husband and I were just recently married.
I'm originally from Cyrodiil, but I left there after I had a falling out with my family.
This was the first door in Skyrim I knocked upon, and little did I know my future husband would be answering the door.
I like the farm life. It's a huge change from living in a city.
Addvild's made me a happy woman. I wouldn't want to be with anyone else in the world.
I'm dealing with a few new research proposals, evaluating the results of three long-term projects, and keeping an eye on our new Apprentices.
This is why I avoid being social.
So, you don't need anything then.
I... see.
Oh! Well... I... I'm sorry. Yes, we have been working on something together.
Your schedule is always clear, Arch-Mage. I see to that.
I only wish to escape the constant glaring of my "colleagues" and discover an opportunity for repose. Your Arcanaeum is so lovely and quiet...
Forgive me, but at what point does it become our concern?
There were also two items requiring disciplinary action recently.
Don't fret, my fanged acquaintance. I shan't trouble you a bit.
Ah, yes. True. Well, thank you.
Oh, my dear Urag. You're trying so hard to help. I do hope you're not disappointed.
You've been spending a fair bit of time with Sergius lately, I see.
Never. The College cannot become mired in local politics. What happens inside these walls is our worry. What happens outside is theirs.
Everything's under control, then? Right. Keep up the good work.
Calm down! Why are you over-reacting? I was going to ask if the two of you are collaborating on research!
Was there something specific you needed?
Did you need something, Colette?
"Disappointed" is not the word I would choose, Colette. Excuse me.
Ah, my dearest Urag! How are you today, my green-skinned friend?
Sir, the Imperial Legion has again requested assistance. What shall I tell them?
Is there anything new I should be aware of, Mirabelle?
What? Why, the audacity! How dare you imply such a thing!
Yes, thank you Arch-Mage.
I'm positive whatever I require will be revealed to me on its own, in due time.
The same thing we've told them before, and the same thing we've told these Stormcloaks. It's not our concern.
Mirabelle, would you please make sure my schedule is clear for the next few days? I'm working on something important.
Arch-Mage, the Synod have contacted us again, asking if we've any word on their researchers. What shall I tell them?
That hasn't changed, has it? Then just tell them again.
No, I don't think so. Respond to their request, and we'll leave it at that.
Err, excuse me Enthir. Might I have a moment?
Yes, err, apologies for the intrusion. I was wondering, err, if you could possibly, ah, procure a few select items for me.
See that you do.
Oh, no. Err, wouldn't do to say at all. Not, err, not now. Not at all. I have far too many things to do still.
Really, Arniel? Because I seem to recall doing just that for you recently, at which point you assured me that you'd cover my expenses.
What did you tell them last time?
So, Arniel. How's that little project of yours coming along? Any progress worth mentioning?
If you say so.
That they had arrived, asked for information which was provided to them, and went on their way with no suggestion of further interaction.
Should we consider investigating the matter?
Yes, Arch-Mage. Thank you.
What is it?
And that, my nervous little friend, has not happened. Would you care to comment on that, perhaps?
Oh, that. Err, yes. It's... It's quite promising, I believe. Definitely on the right track. Results, err, results should be soon.
Ahh, hmm. Yes. I, err, I was unaware that I had forgotten that. I will, err, I mean I'll take care of that as soon as possible.
Thanks to you, the people of Skyrim will continue to have a place to study magic. Even if most of them resent you for it.
Nords don't trust magic, or those that use it. Made it difficult for me growing up.
They won't say much, but most people have very strong opinions about the College and those that study here.
Don't expect the local Nords to take kindly to you once they find out you're from the College.
J'zargo is pleased, and more than a little jealous, to greet the new Arch-Mage.
J'zargo will go wherever he can to learn more. Oblivion itself, if need be.
Khajiit are not known as mages, so J'zargo has much to prove.
J'zargo can learn magic from these men and elves, but they cannot learn to be as dashing as he. J'zargo comes out on top.
I think we could blow up half of Eastmarch, and people would still come to us for enchantments.
The local Nords may not like us very much, but they don't seem to mind having access to enchanted weapons and armor.
Savos doesn't care for politics. He likes to believe the College is removed from the rest of Skyrim.
If he could, Savos would float the the entire College above Skyrim, for all the Nords to see.
Right. And what did you say the point of this little venture was?
Yes, I'll be ready in a moment, I just need one more thing...
Fine! I'll just... leave the rest of it. Let's go, then, if you're in such a hurry.
Some fool is opening a museum in Dawnstar. They say he's paying people to collect artifacts from that old Mythic Dawn cult.
Silus keeps filling his house with those Mythic Dawn things. Creepy, I tell you.
I can't believe Silus is collecting Mythic Dawn artifacts. Right here in Dawnstar, no less.
Your son chose his side, and he chose poorly. And now he's gone. Such is the way of war. The sooner you accept his loss, the better.
Come on, father. There's nothing more to be said here.
Do you believe this old hag?
Face it, cow! Your stupid son is dead! He died a Stormcloak traitor. And you... you best keep your mouth shut before you suffer the same.
Nothing? And what of my son? Hmm? What of Thorald? Is he nothing? So don't talk to me about suffering!
Foolish old woman! You know nothing! Nothing of our struggles, our suffering!
I will never accept his death! My son still lives. I feel it in my heart. So tell me, Battle-Borns, where is he? Where are you holding my Thorald?
"Holding him"? Why I've got him in my cellar. He's my prisoner.
If she's come this far from home, it must be important. Lead on. I just need to report back to camp by sunrise.
Is Ulfric's armor done yet?
I'm so confused. What's happening?
Ruki? Where is she?
Our fires are visible for miles. We're in a low valley. Sitting ducks.
Of course I am. What brings you here?
Don't even bother with that one. Leave it to the orcs.
I'm searching for my Fenrig. He was marching with Gjukar's men, who they say were wiped out here.
I've turned over every body, though, and I can't find him.
Please help me look. He has a bright red beard and hair.
It doesn't matter. I'm here. We're together now. And we will be forever.
Exactly.
But that battle isn't expected until tomorrow... Ruki, what's going on?
Fenrig! You're alive!
How wonderful. Like the sea, their love roils and swells, but brings life and nourishment to all.
I think I've got a handle on it, but Orichalcum is confusing.
Are you a fool? Look at the bodies around you. Even if you're blind, surely you smell the blood.
We're expected to fight there tomorrow. Gjukar elected to camp here for the night. I don't like it, though.
I had heard that Gjukar's men were wiped out. I came to find you.
Oh, right. That makes sense. Kind of a messy metal, anyway.
Come, help me search for Fenrig.
Have you been learning your ores?
We are done talking about this.
Wh- no... I.... Oh, by the Eight, fine. But only to the edge of town!
I wonder why they only stole Lucan's golden claw. I mean, we have plenty of things in the shop that are worth just as much coin.
Lucan found the claw about a year after he opened the store. He never quite explained where he got it. He's a tricky one.
So this is your plan, Lucan?
Oh really? Well I think your new helper here needs a guide.
Well what are you going to do then, huh? Let's hear it!
I remember you. You're the stranger. Father says I'm not supposed to talk to strangers.
Those thieves must be mad, hiding out there. Those old crypts are filled with nothing but traps, trolls, and who knows what else!
Yes. So now you don't have to go, do you?
Well one of us has to do something!
Have you thought about my invitation?
No, I really don't. It'll be a while before I'm ready for that. I'll be on my way now.
It was an old Nord proverb. It said, the best way to get over the loss of a lover is to find a new one.
Captain Lonely-Gale! I've been looking for you everywhere!
Viola, dark elves sleep just like the rest of us.
Did you notice that the snow settles in a different direction near the murder sites?
You wouldn't think it from the racket that Cornerclub makes.
This is more delicate work than you're used to.
Hello, captain! Can you spare a moment? I heard something the other day that I wanted to share with you.
Can I help with it? Maybe just the cuirass?
I've thought about it, but I'm not sure it's such a good idea.
I did that engraved piece just last month!
Did you look behind the forge? I think I saw it there.
Hammers don't have legs.
Oh... yes. Now that you mention it, I think... sorry about that.
What is it now, Viola?
Do you think those dark elves had anything to do with the murders? The attacks all happened at night, after all.
Did you use my good hammer?
No, I want to take my time with it.
I'm sure the guards have the matter well in hand. Now, if you'll excuse me.
Yes, I think that must be it. My wife hasn't been gone that long, after all. Ask me again on another day.
Captain, captain!
And now you've found me. Can I help you?
Don't you think all your tramping around there investigating might have something to do with it?
What did you hear?
This is the royal armor, lass. Maybe in a few years.
I didn't lose a lover, Viola, I lost my wife. It's not the same thing.
No.
I didn't touch it!
Oh, I see. Is it still too soon?
Well, if it works in the one case, why not the other? Don't you want to at least give it a try?

The dark elves are uneasy. There's a killer on the streets. And Viola Giordano won't leave me alone.
Jorleif, I want you to send a messenger to our western camps.
As you wish, sir.
Don't bother. I don't have much chance of seeing my husband these days. He's always up in the palace with Ulfric.
What about being loyal to his wife?
Yes, sir. I've been trying to quiet her, but she's insistent. She thinks she can find out who's been killing the women...
I'm not seeking advice right now, friend. Just send the message.
Those milk drinkers? Might as well be elves. Think they're better than us.
But most of their supply routes come south, from Cyrodiil.
Be very careful with that. It sounds as if it may be the Death Ring of Dro'farahn. A ring of the most dangerous sort.
Why do you even need to ask? They treat your people as bad as they do mine.
I used to have five good men who worked the mill with me. Then this war started and they all ran off. Idiots with mead in their blood.
There's plenty of good wood around here, but I just can't harvest it all by myself. Everyone able-bodied is fighting.
Of course, without functioning mills, who will make the arrows for our soldiers? Men have no sense of irony.
Welcome, welcome, welcome! Don't see many strangers out here. Come to get your hands dirty on a real farm?
Nothing tastes better than food you've grown yourself. Something magic about dirt and sunshine making food.
The weather's harsh, but I like to think my sunny personality is what helps the grain grow.
Bolfrida is certainly grating, but she pays well. Thankfully nobody from the city can see me out here.
Farm work is hard work. It's good to relax when I can, but I still have to listen to my brothers harping about "injustices."
Of course working for Bolfrida isn't my first choice. But what else can a Dunmer do out here?
My sister and I both work for the Nords. Our brother is ashamed of us, but at least we can affort to eat.
Starting to get too old to work all day, but hopefully Master Hlaalu will let me slow down a bit.
Every now and then someone from the city comes by. Think they aren't used to seeing a Nord working for a Dark Elf.
Better to work in the cold than in the heat, I say. Though some sun would be nice.
Master Cruel-Sea comes and works the fields sometimes. I don't think the Mistress likes it, but she won't come out here to catch him.
Once the sun goes down, it's just me and the dogs. They keep me warm, and don't talk too much.
I wonder if I could put the dogs to work somehow. But I don't really have the heart to force them.
Regardless. It appears Skyrim must stand alone. Again.
You may go now...
Yes, Listener. I won't move unless you give the word.
I'll remain in this location until ordered otherwise.
Very well, Listener.
As you wish, Listener.
Of course.
Yes, Listener.
Off we go.
Lead on, Listener.
Yes, Listener.
Oh, of course. Mother needs me, after all.
I will return to the Dawnstar Sanctuary immediately, Listener.
Yes, Listener. I'll be here if you need me.
Yes, Listener. I'll return to the Dawnstar Sanctuary without delay.
Of course. But if you need me, just give the order.
Yes, Listener? How may I serve you?
Listener. It is an honor to stand before you.
How may I serve you, my Listener?
And what does the Empire do? Nothing! Nay, worse than nothing! The Imperial machine enforces the will of the Thalmor! Against its own people!
For we are the children of man! And we shall inherit both heavens and earth! And we, not the Elves or their toadies, will rule Skyrim! Forever!
So rise up! Rise up, children of the Empire! Rise up, Stormcloaks! Embrace the word of mighty Talos, he who is both man and Divine!
Good enough. I'll just confiscate any stolen goods you're carrying, then you're free to go.
Let me do the talking.
Not now.
Shut up, will you?
As soon as you distract the guards, I'll open the door.
Hmm. I guess I'll defer to your judgment on this one, but I think it's a mistake.
Maybe so, but we shouldn't be making concessions before negotiations even start. Makes us look weak.
I'm glad we see eye to eye on this.
Well said.
What? You, alone, reasoning with the Thalmor? That's madness, friend.
You're serious, aren't you?
Are you sure? I doubt you stand a chance against the Thalmor.
I can have a group of men ready at a moment's notice. Many would be willing to help rescue my brother.
Very well. I'll give you a chance. But if you can't do it, I will.
Exactly. Together we stand more of a chance of making this work.
Make your way to Northwatch Keep in Haafingar. I'll meet you near there, and we'll plan our assault.
Welcome to our home.
So, you're here to help?
We kill every Thalmor between us and Thorald, and we bring him out alive. Let's go.
How do we know he's not spying for the Battle-Born? This was foolish!
How do we know she's not spying for the Battle-Born? This was foolish!
Anything, anything at all would be a start. Then we can figure out how to get to him.
I'm sorry for doubting you, friend. You have my thanks.
Avulstein, put that down! He's here to help us find Thorald!
All right, mother.
I can't take any more of this. No weapons, please. Let's just talk.
Please, I'm desperate. This is my brother's life! I won't let them take it away!
I beg of you, look for proof of Thorald's capture. The Battle-Borns have it, I just know it.
Mother, what's the meaning of this? Who have you brought into our home?
I know that Thorald's not dead. I just know it. The Imperials have him, and are keeping him somewhere. But I don't know where.
Those damned Battle-Borns... they know something. They're hiding it. They practically taunt us with it. They must have some sort of proof!
There's got to be something in that house of theirs. I just know it.
I don't dare leave the house myself. So I need your help.
My brother, Thorald. He was fighting for the Stormcloaks, and went missing. Everyone assumes he's dead.
But he's not. I just know it. The Imperials captured him, and they're holding him someplace. The Battle-Borns know where.
I'd go and search their house for proof, but they want me too. I'd be captured, and then we'll never know what happened to him.
So I need someone to find that proof for me.
I knew it! Let me see, please!
The Thalmor? By the Nine, it's worse than I thought.
So... Northwatch Keep. Then we know where to hit them.
We can't trust anyone! Who knows what they'll do if they find me here.
Anything that confirms that Thorald isn't dead, but is being held captive. I know those Battle-Borns have it.
They'd be hiding it, of course. Wouldn't want it to get out that they've been lying this whole time.
But you might be able to butter them up enough to lower their guard.
I cannot thank you enough for rescuing me from this place. I suspect I'd never again see the light of day otherwise.
But why would you risk your life for me, a stranger?
I never thought I'd see another friendly face again.
We need to get to safety as soon as possible. Let's move.
Talos the mighty! Talos the unerring! Talos the unassailable! To you we give praise!
We are but maggots, writhing in the filth of our own corruption! While you have ascended from the dung of mortality, and now walk among the stars!
Trust in me, Whiterun! Trust in Heimskr! For I am the chosen of Talos! I alone have been anointed by the Ninth to spread his holy word!
Excellent! We've hit 'em where it hurts. Repairs will be needed when we take over, but at least the Stormcloaks won't benefit in the meantime.
I'm one of the last members of the Blades.
A very long time ago, the Blades were dragonslayers, and we served the Dragonborn, the greatest dragonslayer.
For the last two hundred years, since the last Dragonborn emperor, the Blades have been searching for a purpose.
Now that dragons are coming back, our purpose is clear again. We need to stop them.
The first thing we need to do is figure out who's behind the dragons.
The Thalmor are our best lead. If they aren't involved, they'll know who is.
Really? Where?
I'm not sure yet. I have a few ideas, but I'll need some time to pull things together...
Meet me back in Riverwood. If I'm not back when you get there, wait for me. I shouldn't be long.
Keep an eye on the sky. This is only going to get worse.
Interesting. Same dragon...
Damn it, we're blundering around in the dark here! We need to figure out who's behind it all!
Go ahead. Whatever you want to know. Nothing held back.
Not a damn thing. I was just as surprised as you to find that big black dragon here.
Nothing solid. Yet. But my gut tells me it can't be anybody else.
The Empire had captured Ulfric. The war was basically over. Then a dragon attacks, Ulfric escapes, and the war is back on.
And now the dragons are attacking everywhere, indiscriminately. Skyrim is weakened, the Empire is weakened.
Who else gains from that but the Thalmor?
We need to find out if the Thalmor are behind the dragons coming back. We need to get into the Thalmor Embassy, somehow...
If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work.
It's under your feet, lad. Just follow the smell.
The walls have ears and you're being tested, lad. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
The walls have ears and you're being tested, lass. We'll talk in the Ragged Flagon.
Isn't it obvious? As the Night Mother's Keeper, he believes he's entitled to the rule of this Sanctuary.
Cicero will cite our independence as the need to revert to the Old Ways. He'll claim we're undisciplined, unruly. Heretical, even.
Ironically, the Night Mother could prove to be just as much a victim. The queen in a fool's twisted game of chess.
I don't believe so. But I'll be able to instruct you further after you've returned from the chamber. It depends on what you learn.
And make haste! You must be in place before Cicero and his... ally arrive! They could be planning another meeting at any time!
It's Cicero. Ever since he arrived, his behavior's been... Well, erratic would be an understatement. I do believe he is truly mad.
But it's worse than that. He's taken to locking himself in the Night Mother's chamber, and talking. To someone. In hushed, but frantic tones.
Who is he speaking with? What are they planning? I fear treachery.
That's the real question, isn't it? The jester enters, seals the door, and the conversation begins. So someone must be waiting for him inside.
Any one of us could enter that chamber silently. Unnoticed. But who amongst us would dare conspire against the Sanctuary?
The very thought breaks my heart.
Yeah. I bet I know your guy. He's hiding out in the Ratway Warrens. Paying us good coin for nobody to know about it.
Lot of people looking for him. Don't know his name, but he's paid good money for nobody to know he's down here.
He's holed up in the Ratway Warrens. Be careful. You're not the only one looking for him.
Huh. Lot of old guys around. I don't really know how I can help.
Finally, you're back.
What about you? Have you... have you spoken to anyone? No.... No, of course not. I do the talking, the stalking, the seeing and saying!
...sweet Night Mother.
Are we alone? Yes... yes... alone. Sweet solitude. No one will hear us, disturb us. Everything is going according to plan.
The others... I've spoken to them. And they're coming around, I know it. The wizard, Festus Krex... perhaps even the Argonian, and the un-child...
And what do you do? Nothing! Not... not that I'm angry! No, never! Cicero understands. Heh. Cicero always understands! And obeys!
You will talk when you're ready, won't you? Won't you...
Help! Can anyone hear me? I'm stuck down here.
You, you there! Help me, I'm trapped.
Wait, come back!
Noooo!
Aaaaagh!
Cut me down, or you'll never get the claw!
Where are you... get me down from this!
Sorry, but I have a treasure to get to...
So long "friend." Ha ha ha!
Is anyone out there? Anyone?
I don't know what you overheard, but the Riverwood Trader is still open. Feel free to shop.
You're not getting that treasure.
I'm too swift for you!
Too slow!
I heard Lucan yelling the other day about a thief breaking into his store. It wasn't me, honest!
Ironic, right? The old enemies assume that every calamity must be a plot by the other side...
Even so, we've got to find Esbern before they do. He'll know how to stop the dragons if anybody does.
Do they know where he is?
He's holed up in the Ratway Warrens. Hardly ever leaves the place - has someone bring him food and such.
Crazy old coot, from what I've heard. For that to stand out down here, he must really be off his nut.
Riften, eh? Probably down in the Ratway, then. It's where I'd go.
You mean, aside from wanting to kill every Blade they can lay their hands on?
Esbern was one of the Blades archivists, back before the Thalmor smashed us during the Great War.
He knew everything about the ancient dragonlore of the Blades.
Obsessed with it, really. Nobody paid much attention back then. I guess he wasn't as crazy as we all thought.
Really? That seems hard to believe. You're sure about that?
Dangerous-looking elves who didn't give their names. Draw your own conclusions.
You'd better get to Riften. Ask around the Ragged Flagon, in the Ratway. It's at least a good starting point.
You'd better get to Riften. Talk to Brynjolf. He's... well-connected. A good starting point at least.
Well... there is guy like that. He's paid good money for nobody to know he's down here, but I guess I can tell you.
You're right, you're right. I just... I was sure it must have been them. If not the Thalmor, who? Or... what?
Did you learn anything useful?
Esbern? He's alive? I thought the Thalmor must have got him years ago. That crazy old man...
Figures the Thalmor would be on his trail, though, if they were trying to find out what's going on with the dragons.
You think I don't know that? If anyone learned of this meeting, my corpse would swing over Solitude for a decade!
But what I ask of you... it must be done. There is too much at stake.
No, she certainly wouldn't. And... for whatever reason, she chose to relay Motierre's information to you.
I don't know exactly what's going on here, if you're the Listener, or this is some fluke, or what. But what we now have before us...
Yes, um.... So it would seem. Well, I won't waste your time. I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually.
I daresay, the work I'm offering has more significance than anything your organization has experienced in, well, centuries.
In the year 3E 41, Emperor Pelagius Septim was murdered in the Temple of the One in the Imperial City. Cut down by a Dark Brotherhood assassin.
His killing ushered in, shall we say, a necessary change in Imperial policy.
There are those now who wish for a similar change. I am sorry, but that's all I'm at liberty to say.
The logistics of such an endeavor will be staggering, I know. There will be several minor assassinations necessary. Leading up to the... main target.
As I mentioned, the amulet I gave you is exceptionally valuable, and will certainly cover the costs of all these other unpleasantries.
The sealed letter explains everything else, in detail. It should be delivered to your superior. Whomever that may be.
I'm thinking we need that amulet appraised. I want to know where it came from, how much it's worth, and if we can actually get away with selling it.
And, there's only one man who can give us what we need - Delvin Mallory. He's a fence, a private operator. Works out of the Ratway, in Riften.
Give me the letter. Bring Mallory the amulet. Find out everything you can, and sell it if he's willing. He'll offer a letter of credit - that's fine.
Delvin Mallory and the Dark Brotherhood have... history. He can be trusted.
As I said, I want you to kill several people. You'll find the targets, as well as their manners of elimination, quite varied.
I'm sure someone of your disposition will probably even find it enjoyable.
But you should know that these killings are but a means to an end. For they pave the way to the most important target.
The real reason I'm speaking with a cutthroat in the bowels of this detestable crypt. For I seek the assassination of...
...the Emperor.
I performed the Black Sacrament, contacted you people, because I thought you guaranteed discretion.
Is this no longer so?
Oh, I know. I know. But I'm so glad you're here. Please, allow me to state my business. Surely your time is as valuable as my own.
I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
What's this?
By Sithis, you're not joking.
To kill the Emperor of Tamriel... The Dark Brotherhood hasn't done such a thing since the assassination of Pelagius.
As a matter of fact, no one has dared assassinate an Emperor of Tamriel since the murder of Uriel Septim, and that was two hundred years ago...
You're back. Good. All right, so? Did you meet this Motierre? What did he want?
You're joking.
Oh, wonderful. You don't know how happy I am to hear you say that.
So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. It's as if the very stars have finally aligned.
But I digress. Here, take these to your, um... superior.
Rexus. The items.
Oh, my furtive friend. When Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, there will be gold... a fortune in gold. But so much more!
It is said that the Dark Brotherhood, in recent years, has been in decline. That you lack the power, wealth, and respect of days past. Is it not so?
If you do this, if you kill the Emperor... Oh, how the masses will fear and respect you.
Right, then. You prefer to listen, is that it? Well, you must represent the Dark Brotherhood. I certainly wasn't expecting anyone else.
So I'll cut right to the chase.
I would like to arrange a contract. Several, actually. I daresay, the most important work your organization has had in, well... centuries.
It's a shocking request, I know. But it is inside the purview of what you Dark Brotherhood types do. Isn't it? If history is to be believed?
You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now you're here, as if the very stars have finally aligned.
But I digress. Here, let me give you these. They are to be delivered to your, um... superior.
Rexus. The items.
You're damn right we'll accept it. If we pull this off, the Dark Brotherhood will know a fear and respect we haven't seen in centuries.
You think I'd abandon an opportunity to lead my Family to glory?
But this is all so much to take in. I need time to read the letter, and figure out where we go from here. And this amulet. Hmmm...
That is correct. What I ask is no small thing, of course. But you represent the Dark Brotherhood. This is... what you do? No?
You must understand. So much has led to this day. So much planning, and maneuvering. Now, it's as if the very stars have finally aligned.
But I digress. Here, take these. They need to be delivered to your, um... superior.
Rexus. The items.
I need you to deliver some false orders to the Stormcloak commander in Dawnstar.
But first we need to get our hands on some rebel orders to make the forgeries.
What's that you said? Dragonborn? Then... there really is hope after all?
You'd better come inside. Quickly now. Thalmor agents have been seen in the Ratway.
What?! No, that's not me. I'm not Esbern. I don't know what you're talking about.
Go away!
I'm very dangerous! Don't make me come out there and hurt you!
I'm not opening this door, so you might as well leave me alone.
If my friends find out you've been bothering me, you'll be very sorry!
Go away!
So, he says to petition Malacath for relief? Interesting.
Fine, it's your neck.
The kind that curse you, haunt you in the dead of night. There is nothing you can do, nothing anyone can do.
Shagrol, once the leader of this tribe, unhappy with his demise, has dealt with Malacath himself to curse me, curse us all.
Yes, but he offered it as an idea. Perhaps he hopes it will be helpful, but does not wish to risk personal failure.
You should do as he says. Go to Malacath's shrine, northwest of here, and ask for his help. See what happens.
Forgive Ugor's harsh words. She is merely doing as she has been told.
Spirits! Ghosts! I am cursed, haunted, and I can do nothing. Can you fix that?
Of course you cannot. No one can. It is my lot as Chief of this tribe, and so I suffer quietly.
The ears of the wind?
Malacath himself said this to you? But what does it... Ah! Yes! I see! A ritual is necessary!
This message is for me. I understand now. I have work to do. You, I shall need your help.
I need a Daedra's heart, at once! Quickly, quickly!
His shrine lies northwest of Largashbur. I am unsure that he will listen, but it cannot hurt.
Perhaps. But he spoke of this "curse" to you, which means it has deeply affected him.
It may be worth attempting contact with Malacath after all.
Go to Malacath's shrine, southeast of Largashbur. See if perhaps he will aid us after all.
We cannot carry on this way! You know we are doomed if we do not do something!
You? An outsider? I'd sooner slit my own throat than accept help from you.
To even consider it would be such a sign of weakness that I might as well abandon my tribe now.
Yamarz charged me with keeping outsiders away from Largashbur. You would have me disobey him?
Atub! Meddlesome woman... You would help us? Help me?
Tell me, outsider. Can you banish spirits? For that is what I need.
You were charged with keeping us inside the walls. Have faith, Ugor. I only wish the best for our tribe.
Our chief is weakened. He will not rest, will not sleep. He suffers, and so the tribe suffers.
