Yamarz is proud, and refuses to see that we need outside help.
He needs someone to confide in. If it will not be one of us, perhaps it will be you. Go to him.
Halt! You have no business here, outsider. Leave at once.
Ugor, no! This may be the one we need.
We need nothing from outsiders! Yamarz will provide for us.
Speak with Nazir and see if he has any contracts available. I need time to read Motierre's letter.
Your training proceeds well, Dragonborn.
Yes. For matters of such gravity, we need to know where you stand. Or who you stand with.
Where did you learn of that? Who have you been talking to?
Ah, the prophecy. Be wary of prophecy. Some are meant as a guide. Some as a warning. Are you sure you can tell one from the other?
Iron-Breaker Mine is three times the worth of that Quicksilver Mine and its fat-head owner.
My husband Leigelf is dead. I... I never thought I would miss that oaf so much.
My miners are as dumb as rocks are grey.
The Mortar and Pestle has cures for every illness, from Ataxia to Witbane.
A Stamina Potion maybe? Popular with young men.
Frost took my husband years ago. He was a good man.
I will serve Dawnstar as I always have.
Horik and I have lived here ever since I retired. He's a good soldier.
My duty is to guard Brina Merilis.
I've been in the Legion all my life. I'm content to serve.
You should speak to the Jarl if you need something, stranger.
I'm just one of the miners. I don't want any trouble.
I'm no one special, just a miner who swings a pickaxe for his drinking money.
Beitild thinks I drink on the job. Well, what does it matter if I do? Huh?
Quicksilver Mine is the best mine in Dawnstar, and you can tell those Iron-Breaker miners I said so.
Need a dagger? No? How about a claymore?
I trained in Hammerfell during my journeyman years. Redguard smiths are second to none.
I'll forge you a weapon tomorrow. Right now, I'm closed.
I traveled far and wide when I was younger. Glad to have settled down finally.
I help my husband Rustleif run the forge. He's a marvel with steel.
I married Rustleif when he was in Hammerfell, apprenticing under my father.
We discovered recently that I am with child. I'll need to stop working next season.
I work in the mine, just like everyone else.
Used to be twice as many miners working here before all this trouble started.
I mine. Work the smelter. Ain't much else to tell.
Been mining all my life. Quicksilver is as much my home as anywhere.
The nightmares in Dawnstar have lifted, and I hear you helped that happen. You have my thanks.
The Pale will throw those Imperial backbiters up against the wall when they come for us.
I'm Skald's housecarl and head of his guard in Dawnstar.
Skald is very... vocal about his hatred of the Empire.
I served in the Great War against the Thalmor. I remember how bravely a Legionnaire can fight.
Just a servant of Skald the Elder. Not worthy of time or consideration.
My family has served Skald Felgeif's family for generations.
There's no honor in being a Jarl's servant. No Sovngarde waits for dust cleaners.
I'm here to lend my magic to the people of Dawnstar.
I served in the Great War as a Legion battlemage. I've no desire to go back.
I'll work my spells and potions for whoever is willing to leave Dawnstar in peace.
The Windpeak Inn features the finest bard in all of Dawnstar. Me.
I'm sorry. My mind was just somewhere else. Did you need a room? Drink?
Oh, do you need a room?
Dawnstar is the last port of call before Solitude. Damn rebels aren't taking it.
We won't give up this port to the Imperials without a fight, don't you worry.
Dangerous post, Dawnstar. If the bandits don't get you, the animals will.
I'm aspiring to earn that title, yes. However, the road ahead is paved with loose cobbles and deadly pitfalls.
Master Elgrim says I'm a natural; that I have a unique talent. But I fear his approval masks his intolerance for my mistakes.
My errant formulas have cost Master Elgrim a fortune in ingredients; some of which are almost irreplaceable.
Well, that's just about the best news I've heard all week.
I was going to give this to whomever killed her for me, but I suppose you've earned it by bringing me this information.
This key opens one of my drop spots in the basement of the meadery. Take anything inside you like.
Yes, Master Elgrim will be pleased I've restored his stock of these rare ingredients.
I feel I owe a bit more than some meaningless coin for all your diligence. Here, this key unlocks my supply chest at Elgrim's Elixirs.
Feel free to take what you need from time to time. I'll be refilling it once every few days or so.
Nirnroot is the easiest. It grows only by the water and makes a unique chiming noise. The only drawback is it won't regrow after harvesting.
Nightshade is native to the pine forest and marshy tundra regions of Skyrim and has a distinctive starburst-like violet flower.
Last we have deathbell which only grows in marshy tundra terrain. Its inverted bluish-purple flower bunches are unmistakable.
She was a young woman... buxom, with long flowing black hair.
She used to sing the most lovely songs to me when we were courting. I've never heard a voice so beautiful.
All that talent will go to waste when I'm finished with her.
It's exhilarating to observe the effects of my potions on the body. Watching the heart stop... the eyes go blind.
We're made up of thousands of parts with thousands of different functions all working in tandem to keep us alive.
Yet if only a single part of our imperfect machine fails, life fails. It makes one realize how fragile... how flawed we are.
You ask why I'm so fascinated? The irony... the irony that the same world that gave us life provides us the means to die.
To restore Master Elgrim's supply I would need 20 deathbell, 20 nightshade and 20 nirnroot.
Am I sensing that you may be interested in gathering these rare ingredients for me?
I'm sorry, I don't know what you're talking about.
That means more time for my experiments and less time in the field. Much appreciated.
That's the spirit. Find her and I promise you'll be well compensated.
You've done me a great service, my friend. Allow me to compensate you for the information.
This key opens one of my stashes in the basement of the meadery. Help yourself to any of its contents.
Disappointing.
Amusing.
Please, I beg you, don't tell Sibbi where I am. He'll kill me!
Sibbi's been spreading lies about me, you must hear me out.
Cut out the middleman, eh? Shrewd, very shrewd. You sure you don't have some Black-Briar blood in you?
I've been hurt worse before.
You idiot. You came all the way down here for that?
When I find that bitch, I'm going to wring her little neck.
Visit me anytime. I'm not going anywhere soon.
I believe we're done.
Out of my way.
Don't forget to check your supply chest from time to time.
My experiments can't be completed until you've brought me those ingredients. Do hurry.
Now was it two parts giant lichen or three? Hm...
Well, well. Aren't you a sight for sore eyes. I've always got time for lovely ladies.
Any luck gathering those ingredients?
Hm? Sorry, just thinking about my experiments.
Dov wahlaan fah rel. We were made to dominate. The will to power is in our blood. You feel it in yourself, do you not?
I can be trusted. I know this. But they do not. Onikaan ni ov dovah. It is always wise to mistrust a dovah.
I have overcome my nature only through meditation and long study of the Way of the Voice.
No day goes by where I am not tempted to return to my inborn nature. Zin krif horvut se suleyk.
What is better - to be born good, or to overcome your evil nature through great effort?
The Blades are wise not to trust me. Onikaan ni ov. I would not trust another dovah.
(Deep breath)
(Deep breath)
(Deep breath)
(Deep exhale)
(Deep exhale)
(Deep exhale)
Steady now. The Legion has faced down worse than a few dusty old bonewalkers.
We're not leaving here until we get what we came for.
Don't give them a chance to warn the others!
Be careful men. There's bound to be more up ahead.
Is this what killed that Stormcloak over there? Can't be. It looks like it's been dead for a hundred years.
What in the nine holds is that?
Of course you'll get a cut. We take care of our own.
Now if you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Get going.
If you were expecting a palace, then maybe you're not cut out for this line of work. Our methods involve secrecy and discretion.
Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
If I have to hold your hand through everything, maybe you aren't cut out for this kind of work.
It's under your feet, lass. Just follow the smell.
No risk, no reward. That's the way it goes, so you better get used to it.
Now... if you're done bellyaching like a child, how about handling a few deadbeats for me?
Judging from how well you handled those shopkeepers, I'd say you've done more than simply prove yourself. We need people like you in our outfit.
That's the spirit! Larceny's in your blood... the telltale sign of a practiced thief.
I think you'll do more than just fit in around here.
If you're looking for a challenge, you've come to the right place.
Maybe you just aren't up to the challenge...
You made it!
We need to get inside. Follow me!
Come on! We need to get inside before that dragon comes back!
A fight!
Gods! Another fight.
The path to Paarthurnax lies through this gate. I will show you how to open the way.
It's good to hear you say that. I wish my wife was as simple as you.
You'll shut your mouth and get back to work, or you won't be a miner or a soldier by the day's end. Hear me?
I'm just worried about raising a child in the middle of this war of yours.
With you by my side, is that right Horik?
What about your family? There must have been someone waiting for you back at the farm. Wondering if you were ever coming home?
If you don't mind me saying, Leigelf. You're working too hard. Go home.
What is this feud you have with your wife? What started this?
You don't want to talk about it, or you don't know?
Your people's war, I mean. The Nords. What kind of life would we be giving our little one if we stayed here?
So much for old soldiers settling down to a life of luxury, eh?
With respect, Jarl. You can't ask everyone to throw themselves against trained Legionnaires. Let's leave the fighting to the Stormcloaks.
You never talk much about yourself, do you Horik? I swear, these past 30 years you've been at my side, and I think I barely know you.
The forge is hot today.
You weren't. There's just not much to it.
I see. You're a good soldier, Horik. I'm sorry for intruding.
If we win. This war is going to drag on for years. I can feel it. We need to prepare for a long struggle ahead.
We're at war with Leigelf? Isn't he your husband?
Drafted into a war, how could my life get worse?
Hammerfell? I'm not against the idea, but why?
Where else would I go? Dawnstar is my home and working here is my job.
Work hard, now! The pride of Iron-Breaker Mine is on the line.
No. If we must fight, we will, and you will lead us to victory. As you always have.
Pride of the what? This isn't the Legion, boss. We're miners, not soldiers.
Uh, yes ma'am.
What's to say? I was born on a farm, joined the Legion when I came of age, and I've served you ever since.
I'll, uh, just take that as a compliment.
War of mine? It's not my war, Seren!
Of course. I would have it no other way.
Never. Not until we turn this mine around, drive Beitild out of business, and force her to apologize to me for everything she's done!
I don't want to talk about it.
I don't want to talk about it.
Rustleif, I've been wondering. Maybe after the baby is born, we should move to Hammerfell.
I... well, let me think about it.
No. Can't say there was. My parents died when I was young. You and the Legion have been my only family since.
You're right. Why dilute the glory with too many souls fighting over it? We'll drive out the Empire and be celebrated as heroes when we win.
I'm fine, Horik. I just... are you worried about this war?
Not hot enough. Does your Skyrim ever get warm, husband?
Thank you for sticking by me.
We are poised for glory, Jod. The Empire will never take Dawnstar while every last man, woman, and child here draws breathe!
Enough of your dire predictions! We will talk about this another time.
Are you all right? You seem tired.
No he isn't! He's the enemy, and we are going to crush him.
So, feeling loose? How about you run a job for me.
I'm busy. Don't you have something to do?
I'm not here to hold your hand. Figure this one out for yourself.
You need advice? Talk to Brynjolf... I got better things to do.
I'd had Eorlund forge this for Thorald. It was to be a present for his return. I suppose he can't have it now.
Why don't you take it? We can always make another for the day when this war ends and Thorald can come home.
With Eorlund gone, I'm afraid I can't offer you much in the way of thanks. But please, take this. It's the least I can do.
Is he? You've saved him?! I must see him at once!
What? After all this, I can't even see him?
How... How do I know you're telling me the truth, and not just what I want to hear?
"...for it bears aloft next summer's seeds." That's my boy.
So it's true, then. For now, it's enough to know that he's alive. I can find peace in that.
Thank you, dear friend. You've given me back my son.
Thank you, Madena. I appreciate your assistance.
Brings back memories. Do you have any orders for me, Legate?
Glory is meaningless if they have no home to return to. I urge you to lower our commitments to the front until I'm sure we're safe.
Uh, yes sir. That's me. The oafish servant.
Exactly, Bulfrek. You always know your place.
Considering how the Great War ended, I think we need to be cautious about what we rely on.
Skald, if you don't mind sir, I have a request.
Skald, I've been meaning to ask you for a leave of absence. I wish to return to High Rock until this war is over.
You keep pledging more and more of our guard to the frontlines. The way this is going, we won't be able to defend our own borders.
Maybe you're right. I guess the battles I won yesterday won't win today's wars.
So you say. We'll see if that's true when the Stormcloaks make their move.
I, well, no of course not. I would be glad to help.
I won't have you rob our soldiers of their chance to drive out the Empire. I'm done discussing this, Jod.
I'm sorry. I just don't feel up to entertaining anyone just now.
I'm fine, Madena.
Madena, I was wondering if you could help me with something. Your magic would be invaluable.
Why would they be looking at me? Are they upset about the service?
I told you before, Skald, I have no interest in taking sides in this war. I've no desire to be killed.
Madena, can we count on your spells to assist us in this war? The Empire is bound to bring their battlemages against us.
Just like old times, huh Horik? You and me fighting in a war over some forsaken scrap of land.
I know old men sometimes have pains in their legs when they rise from bed. I know a few spells that could help.
What good is a court mage if she refuses to fight? Where's your sense of duty?
You wish to leave now? In Skyrim's moment of triumph, just before we cast off the yoke of Imperial rule?
Well, it's a winding road up the mountain just ahead. You'll know you're in the right place once you spot the old watchtower.
Once you get to the tower, head north. Bleak Falls Barrow should be just around the corner further up.
Actually, I was just wondering if you could make sure people are tended to if they get hurt. I'm sure you wouldn't object to that.
It'll do you good! Maybe you could visit Irgnir or Fruki on your way out? I've seen them looking at you when they come in for drinks....
Bulfrek, I have an order of swords and armor from the local blacksmith coming here. I need every piece polished to a fine shine.
I'll tell you the same thing I told Skald. I'm here to help the people of Dawnstar, not play politics. Point your suspicions of treachery elsewhere.
Why would I do that? There's work to be done here.
Safety? Don't I have you to handle the town's safety? What is this about, Jod?
You, have a request of me, Bulfrek? This should be interesting.
I've seen you walking with a bit of pain sometimes, Horik.
If this is about battle magic, forget it. I've told every Jarl I've served under that I won't....
Yes, Legate. No one is getting through us.
I don't think that's needed, Horik. It's the same strategy we used for every major defense during the Great War. It'll serve just as well now.
I'm sorry, Legate. I didn't want to imply anything about your ability to lead. You're a hero to the Empire, and I....
Yes, my Jarl. We could hardly waste anyone else's precious time on such a tedious task.
Yes, Legate.
I want you to know I don't trust you, Madena. And I'll be watching you.
Nonsense. The best place for our men is driving the Imperials back. I will ensure that men from Dawnstar get every opportunity for glory.
Well, I was thinking sir, with the war going on and all. Maybe, I could enlist? Fight the Imperials?
Yes, that's exactly what I'm asking. I'm not a Nord, I'm a Breton, and I have no wish to fight this war.
Shor's bones, I hope you're right.
We should go over our strategy again in case the Stormcloaks attack.
Oh, never mind.
When I came here, my only duties were to cure crop diseases and occasionally light a bonfire on holidays. I didn't sign up to kill.
I was wondering, father. Maybe you should take a day off and head into town. I could watch the inn while you're away.
No, Karita. I don't think I could.
Still suspicious of me. Well, don't say I never offered.
This is the bridge out of town. The path up the mountain to the northwest leads to Bleak Falls Barrow.
I guess I should get back to my brother. He'll throw a fit if I take too long. Such a child....
I don't think so, Madena. There will be no deserters in this hold, and that's final.
Father, do you want to talk? About mother?
I'm sure she would want you to be happy, father. Entertaining the guests and drinking and making your lewd jokes like before.
My Jarl, I have concerns about the safety of Dawnstar.
I said I'm fine, Madena. I won't have some court mage fixing me up like some magical experiment.
Keep the guard alert. I want to make sure no townspeople get hurt if there's trouble.
Fine, but this isn't over Madena. We'll talk again.
Oh, don't be such an old stick, Horik. I was joking. I can do that, you know. Redo the battle plans. I'll look over them later.
Well, at the first sign of battle I'll be comfortably sitting in my room, waiting for it to end, so don't you worry.
You have the claw. Oh, you have to tell Lucan at once. Hurry!
Hurry up and tell Lucan you have the claw. He'll be so excited!
Please, you can't leave me in here! If you let me out I can show you where they hide their best treasure.
Please! Help me get out of this cage. I don't know why those things locked me up and I don't want to wait around to find out.
Hey! Over here! Get me out of here!
Hey you! Let me out! I can help!
Yours was not an easy transformation. But you're still alive, so congratulations.
We even have a celebration planned for you.
The bastards... somehow they managed to kill Skjor.
Ah, you're from the College? I've been waiting for you.
Please make sure this arrives in a timely manner. And don't scratch it up.
Oh, good. I've been waiting for you. Here you go, it's all ready.
Now, Sergius knows what I want. I'm hoping he can get it done quickly.
I'm glad they didn't find my lucky dagger. It's time to gut these rotten birds. Lead the way!
Actually... on second thought, I'll just kill you!
Now that we've got that out of the way, I'll take everything you have... or your life.
Not now! It's time to get back at these crows.
No! Don't leave me here! I beg you to let me out!
Please! I'll pay you. I'll give you whatever you want. Just don't leave me here!
Hurry! Get me out of here!
Use the key to open the door! We have to be quick!
Whatever you're selling, I'm not buying.
Always a pleasure, boss. What can I do for you?
Ah, you're back.
Something troublin' ya? I'll listen.
So, what can I do for ya today?
Still have quite a good bit of jobs available if you're looking for some extra coin.
Need some gold? I may have some work for you.
Looking for work?
Got lots of jobs available. Interested?
Talk to me again if you prove yourself to Brynjolf.
Come back later and I may have some work for you.
I think Brynjolf can give you all the information you need.
Yeah?
I'm listenin'.
Looking to fix your armor?
Can I interest you in anything? Some fine armor perhaps?
Be glad to repair any damage to your Guild armor.
So, name your poison! Ha ha ha. Oh, I never get tired of that one!
Need some sort of toxin for a special occasion? That's where I come in.
I sell all sorts of poisons for almost every occasion.
Looking for the best smith in Skyrim? You found it.
Need your weapon repaired? Or could I interest you in a new one?
They don't call me the Ember-Master for nothing. What do you need?
Skilled, lethal and good-looking. Yep, it's a curse.
If you're in the market for the finest bows money can buy, you're talking to the right person.
I've got all sorts of weapons for the discerning marksman. What can I get you?
I've been a part of this Guild for as long as Mercer has been Guild Master.
I might not be as big as some of my fellow Nords, but in a fight they can barely lay a hand on me.
My specialty is pickpocketing. All I need is a rich mark, a moon-lit sky and I'm in my element.
I wouldn't mind teaching the ways of the pickpocket... if the gold was right.
I miss the glory days of this place. We had more coin stacked in the vaults than we even knew what to do with.
Come here a second.
Ugh. The wine in Skyrim tastes like urine compared to the fine vintages we had in Valenwood.
I don't miss home one bit. I'm glad to leave that life behind me.
I'd much rather earn a living liberating others of their coin this way than spending my days as a carpenter like my father.
It's not hard to fit in and make friends when you have the coin to make it happen.
I don't bother with silly pickpocketing jobs when I can make far more at the gambling table.
Our Guild Master graces me with a visit. What can I do for you?
Back with good news I trust.
Well? You done or what?
Out with it. Time is money.
Need a few spare septims? I got the cure right here.
I have work if you've got the backbone.
Ready for some work or you just going to wander around all day?
I have no business with you.
This place is getting worse and worse.
Delvin, please tell me you have good news.
You tell this sorry bunch of thieves that they need to put down their flagons and get to work if they want to keep their status!
Yeah, I know what you mean. If things don't start getting better soon, I may look for work elsewhere.
You stupid cow. You don't know what you're missing.
Oh, really? Well then... how can I make that happen?
Thank you, Vex. You've always been the most reliable one in the Guild.
I'll keep that in mind.
Absolutely.
So, what do you think, my dear? Could a beautiful woman like you ever see yourself with an old codger like me?
Well, if you don't want to hear it, that's your loss.
She said that she barely had enough coin to cover it and I should come back later. Can you imagine that?
All right. I have everything you asked for all ready to go.
You seem to be a bit closer to Brynjolf than the rest of us. Is he giving you any idea why this place seems to be falling apart?
So is it true, bandits get... you know, real friendly with the wildlife?
I've picked up some new clients and did a few jobs on my own, but I'm not sure if that will cover our expenses.
No, but I know that you're going to turn up missing if you keep up this kind of talk.
So, Thrynn, you were a bandit, huh?
What about Mercer? Have you asked him?
I think I know, but go ahead.
Your idea to start a brothel, right? Do you want me to tell you exactly what I think?
I trust you've applied the appropriate pressure in order to change their mind?
Yes, I'm afraid you're right. Such a shame...
Do we have anyone reliable to send out there? I don't want to look the fool again in front of her, Brynjolf.
Remind me why we even bother to allow him to be a part of the Guild.
Well, what the hell is Mercer going to do about this mess? Have you even talked to him?
Two reasons. He's rich and because we need every sword we can get right now.
There's no need to be concerned. We've been through more difficult times than this.
That's impossible. I distinctly heard that lout in the tavern say it was a full shipment of furs. They should be worth a fortune.
I'll speak to the Jarl and see what I can do. Just keep them at bay a little longer.
If I told you that, I'd be lying. We haven't had a bite in weeks. Our clients are starting to get angry.
So, the ending of my tale I began earlier...
So, Sapphire. Can I do anything for you, or more to the point... to you?
I tried to fence some goods to Tonilia, and you know what she told me?
Hold a moment, Rune. What did you think about my idea?
If he does, he isn't telling me a thing. I've asked, but all I get from him are more questions.
Yeah, what of it?
No, and I'm not about to either. I value my life you know... you may want to remember that for yourself.
Brynjolf, something stinks around here and it's more than just the Ratway. We all know it. The question is, what's Mercer going to do about it?
I think if Brynjolf catches wind of it, he'll boot you right out of here... so drop it already!
I don't understand it either. They were a sure thing, I swear.
You're an idiot.
If I wanted to hear a tall tale, I'll read a children's storybook. Get some better stories, Vipir.
Well, yes! Please!
Mercer is being Mercer. If he has a plan to get us out of this rut, then he isn't sharing it with me.
What in the name of Oblivion is going on in this place?
Is this your tale of single-handedly fighting off a pack of trolls or my favorite... where you bedded four women on the same night?
Hey, I still have a contact in Valenwood that may have some work for us. You're welcome to join me.
Vipir "the Fleet." The only man who's foolish enough to name himself after his bedroom prowess.
How are you feeling, Vex?
I hope this deal goes through. I'm putting quite a bit of coin on the line... and the last two you set up fell through.
I have. I think we may have to turn this one over to the Dark Brotherhood.
Maven has been nagging us about her request. Any thoughts?
You just better hope this one isn't another waste of my coin.
Yes, I know. That last job was my fault. I obviously thought Niruin was ready, but I was wrong.
You idiot. He said "firs" not "furs." It was a damn logging caravan!
Sorry Mercer, they no longer wish to be involved in our operation.
Delvin, that last shipment we heisted on the Cyrodiil frontier was worthless!
Well, I see your wounds have healed but your pride still needs some time.
I will, I will...
Oh my... umm, you won't let Mercer hear about this will you? Ahem.
When you're in your grave and I'm standing over it. Now get out of my way!
Pretty angry actually. I can't believe I botched the Goldenglow job. That should have been like stealing a sweetroll from a baby.
Brynjolf, have you had a chance to speak to our contact in Whiterun?
I think we need to prepare for the worst. The coffers are dwindling and I can barely keep our contacts happy.
Normally I'd agree, but we've already lost so many contacts across Skyrim. If we lose the ones in Riften, we may as well close up shop.
Thank you so much for taking care of those thieves. The Riverwood Trader is back to the way it used to be!
It means so much to us to have the claw back where it belongs. Thank you!
I'll add it to the list of things I need to take care of. Thank you.
There are more books to be recovered and secured here in the Arcanaeum.
There's more enchanting to be done. You can acquire whatever it is and bring it back here, and I'll take care of it.
It's about time someone arrived. Here, make sure this is handled properly.
Oh yes, that. Here you go. I hope this can be ready soon.
Yes, yes. Let's keep our voices down, all right? Just make sure this gets there.
Let's see here...
I've got a letter and a lot of gold. Something about it being your inheritance? Oh, and... sorry for your loss.
Gah!
Nargh!
Unf!
The Forsworn are as wild as wolves. Practice those old magics, so some folk say.
There is no greater madness than those who seek to become vampires. That's a dark bargain if there ever was one.
I'd rather fight bandits than draugr. At least bandits know when to stay dead.
You can't argue with these mages. They'll attack you on sight.
It probably took some Dwemer craftsman a year to build one of those things.
There may be no enemy more foul than a walking corpse.
These ruins and their mechanical inhabitants are all that remain of the Dwemer.
It's said that the Forsworn make dark pacts with the Hagravens, and that is why they can use magic.
There is no adversary more deadly than a vampire. They are as cruel as they are cunning.
Bandits are desperate men. They have nothing to lose, and they fight like it.
What some call devotion to the dark arts, I call foolishness.
I would find the Dwemers' mechanical guardians fascinating if they didn't try to kill everything on sight.
Keep digging! Ugh... If Lu'ah Al-Skaven can raise an army of Draugr, then why do I have to babysit them?
They burned his body before I could raise him... it should have been returned to me...
Go on in, already.
Grrh!
Nnh!
No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
I hate those damn things. Too many eyes, you know?
What next, giant snakes?
No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
You fighter types are always underfoot. You want me to clean? Then scat!
I'll be sure to tell her.
Just promise me when you get some more in, I'll have the first crack at them.
I doubt Ulfric would let Thalmor into Windhelm without a fight. But please, do continue. How would you help us?
Oh, I see. Yes, yes of course I'll take a look. Just bring them by whenever it's convenient for you.
We've got another dozen ripening on the vine. They should be ready in a few days.
I've been meaning to speak to you, Niranye. Rumor is, you've got ties to the Thieves' Guild.
Well, I've heard awful stories about these Thalmor, and how they're rounding up everyone who still worships Talos.
The interrogations are quite merciless, from what I've heard.
I fear for you and your husband, should the Thamlor come here. But perhaps there's a way I can help.
Well enough, I suppose. Nilsine bought up most of the tomatoes, and was asking if we could get more.
You've done more than enough already. You've been a good friend in a time of need, and we won't forget that.
I'm sorry I lost myself for a moment there. I just need some time to get over the loss.
You know, I'm not sure. My husband's been tending to the vines, so I'll have to ask him.
Is that so? And what were you thinking, exactly?
Yes, that's right. Whether or not I'm interested depends on what you've got, of course.
How's business today?
Oh, I see. Well, I'm sorry to have troubled you about it.
Just... know that your efforts are appreciated.
I... I'm so sorry, Torbjorn. You must be suffering terribly. I wish there was some way I could help.
Is that new batch of tomatoes ready for sale yet, Hillevi?
I understand you purchase used goods, as well as sell them?
I'm not looking for special treatment, Aval. All I did was talk to Ulfric.
For all I know, he's already forgotten the conversation. I wouldn't get your hopes up.
You're such a wet blanket, captain. Can't a girl have a little fun from time to time?
Hello, Jora. I'm glad to see you. In fact, I was just thinking about you and your husband.
But I insist. and with good reason.
Malthyr told me that you've been speaking to Ulfric about our situation. He said you're going to help us.
So few Nords have ever offered to help us, a discount is the least I can do.
My little Friga loved to cook, but she was terrible at it. Then one day, out of nowhere, she made the most amazing leek soup.
She watched while we ate it, and when she saw how much we loved it, she was... so proud...
By the gods! What are you doing here?
If that business proposition includes smuggling, forget it.
The sailors I know are plenty good at finding trouble without any help from you.
I'll be sure to stress your urgency. It seems like every farmer in Skyrim is short on supplies these days.
The eyes of the law are everywhere these days.
The guards are looking for a killer, but who knows what else they might turn up? Good day.
That's not necessary. I'll pay the same price as anyone else.
It was my pleasure, Torbjorn. I enjoy cooking, and after what you and Tova have been through, it was the least I could do.
Tell him I'll pay double the normal price. We've been getting by with one broken pitchfork, but it's falling apart.
Not that I'd know anything about it, of course, but it seems to me that the last thing a thieves' guild would want is to draw attention to itself.
This is Stormcloak territory. If you're one of those craven Imperials, don't expect a warm welcome.
If you're here to join the Stormcloaks, the palace is inside.
Loyal Nords are always welcome here. Anyone else better watch their step.
Hrm. You go find your own wreck. This one's mine.
If you're an Imperial spy, better turn around right now.
Friend Brunwulf, how good to see you!
If you see anything you like, just let me know and I'll glad offer you a discount.
I wanted to thank you again for the mince pie you sent over. It was delicious.
And how may Ma'randru-jo assist you, hmm?
I was wondering if you still had many associates in the shipping profession.
I have a business proposition for any sailors who might be looking to make a few extra septims.
Revyn put the word out to his suppliers but with the war on, tools are scarce. Both sides have been snapping them up.
It's a bad time to be a criminal, what with these murders going on.
In fact, thieves have been known to kill, if there's a profit in it. This war's been hard on all of us.
We accept the risks that come with our faith, but I'll keep your offer in mind. Thank you, Niranye.
A pleasure to see you as well, Niranye. What can I do for you?
Did you have any luck tracking own some more pitchforks?
One should never pay too much attention to rumors, Torbjorn.
You have piqued my curiosity, however. Please, do continue.
Go back to Morrowind, Dark Elf maggots! You're not welcome here!
Get out of our city, gray-skins! This is Nord land!
We don't want your kind here, dark elves!
This place reeks of gray-skin filth!
You like living in this filthy slum, dark elves? Maybe you should go back to Morrowind, where you belong!
Well, let's just say that I know some people who are experts at helping others disappear when they need to.
They are discreet and thorough, though not inexpensive. I hope you'll keep that in mind, should the worst come to pass.
Captain Lonely-Gale, it's always a pleasure to see you.
In fact, I'm glad you're here. There's something I'd like to ask you.
Whoops.
What's the hurry?
Why are you here? Get away from me!
Hist spit on you.
We're used to getting by on scraps, but the Shatter-Shields want everything for nothing.
No. I'm not. I feel like my scales are clawing into me. Maybe you could help an old Argonian out?
I heard some of the sailors talking about how they stashed a bottle of Double-Distilled Skooma in the Gnisis Cornerclub. I just need a taste.
I yield. By the honor of Clan Shatter-Shield, the Argonians will be paid as much as any other worker.
Torbjorn Shatter-Shield. I wish someone would beat the coin out of his fat fists. He clings to every Septim.
He says an Argonian's labor is only worth a tenth of a "proper Nord worker." My people are not slaves!
Thank you. I'll be waiting for that bottle.
Easy. Maybe I was being too harsh on them. By the honor of Clan Shatter-Shield, they will be paid fairly. You have my word.
Thank you so much. Here. Let me show you a thing or two about not attracting attention to yourself.
We will make do somehow. We always have.
Damn boot-lover.
Well, my friend, if you think those boots needs more coin, I'll make it happen. But I'm doing this for you, not them.
Those boots aren't worth the septims I do pay them. I'm not giving them coin I could give to good, Nord workers.
You can try, but we've gotten nowhere talking to him ourselves.
This is Markarth, traveler. Safest city in the Reach.
You here to see the Jarl? No sudden moves, understand?
Whole dwarven city underneath the keep. Nchuand-Zel. Best steer clear if you don't want a Steam Centurion carving you up.
By the Hist, if I don't get something soon, I don't know how I'll get through the week.
That's what I thought.
Bold words, but true. Fine, you win. I'll pay the Argonians more coin.
They don't work now. Why should I waste more money?
You talked Torbjorn down? I'm impressed.
Here, I was saving this for a rainy day. Maybe now I can afford a few more for my people. Take it.
Paying the wages yourself? Well, I won't say no to gold. By my family's honor, this money will go to the Argonian workers.
What future?
Well now. What have we here? A lost little lamb?
Considering most of us are now dead, I assumed as much.
And before you ask, no - I don't think it was you. Well, maybe I did, but you saving my sorry hide just now sort of erased any doubts.
So thanks.
So you are alive. I was starting to wonder.
Listener. I am your only salvation. Come. Embrace me.
You've got that right. Only a matter of time before we're roasted alive. Come on!
Watch out for pressure plates here.
I'll let you live. This time.
You're not worth it.
I didn't think you had the stomach for it.
Point that somewhere else!
Careful there!
The door up ahead will be locked. Klotha, one of the hagravens, will have the key on her.
Who are you? What do you want?
You're making me... nervous. Is there something you need?
Yes, yes. What is it? We're all very busy here...
I'm really not in the mood. State your business, or take your leave.
Let me guess - you have some "business" to discuss...
Is the line for my next performance starting already? Come on, don't be shy. Plenty of room for everyone.
Do not be so sure about that. Beware - the Blades may claim to serve the Dragonborn, but they do not. They never have.
As for me, I kept from you only what you were not yet ready to know. Are still not ready to know, as your question reveals.
If you wish to stand alone, so be it. If you wish for our help, you'll answer my question.
Perhaps not. But this Shout was used once before, was it not? And here we are again.
Have you considered that Alduin was not meant to be defeated?
Those who overthrew him in ancient times only postponed the day of reckoning, they did not stop it.
If the world is meant to end, so be it. Let it end and be reborn.
The Dragonrend Shout is evil. Would you cure one evil with another?
The Blades! Of course. They specialize in meddling in matters they barely understand. Their reckless arrogance knows no bounds.
Keep moving, stay focused on your goal, and you will reach the summit.
The Blades were born of dragon-hating crusaders. They still carry that legacy although they may not remember from whence it came.
They carried out a genocide of dragons when they first came to Tamriel, and now they will do it again, with your help.
I am as my father Akatosh made me. As are you... Dovahkiin.
Drem Yol Lok. Greetings, wunduniik. I am Paarthurnax.
Who are you? What brings you to my strunmah... my mountain?
Yes. Vahzah. You speak true, Dovahkiin. Forgive me.
It has been long since I held tinvaak with a stranger. I gave in to the temptation to prolong our speech.
That may be what you are here for, Dragonborn.
But stopping Alduin is one thing. Helping you wipe out dragons again is quite another.
Dragons are part of the natural order of the world. The Blades thought to change the order to suit themselves, by wiping out the dragons.
But again, we see that the natural order will be restored, one way or another.
Perhaps we would have dragon allies against Alduin if the ancient Blades had not acted so arrogantly.
They see me as master. Wuth. Onik. Old and wise. It is true I am old...
So. You have made your way here, to me. No easy task for a joor... mortal. Even for one of Dovah Sos. Dragonblood.
What would you ask of me?
Drem. Patience. There are formalities which must be observed, at the first meeting of two of the dov.
Where did you learn of a Shout used to defeat Alduin?
No. I cannot teach it to you because I do not know it.
True... But qostiid - prophecy - tells what may be, not what should be.
Qostiid sahlo aak. Just because you can do a thing, does not always mean you should.
Do you have no better reason for acting than destiny? Are you nothing but a plaything of dez... of fate?
Evenaar bahlok. There are many hungers it is better to deny than to feed.
Dreh ni nahkip. Discipline against the lesser aids in qahnaar... denial of the greater.
Not until introductions are complete. Zin. Heyv. Test your Thu'um against mine, Dovahkiin!
You are bold, wunduniik. You dare enter a Dovah's home without permission.
Pruzah. As good a reason as any. There are many who feel as you do, although not all.
Some would say that all things must end, so that the next can come to pass.
Perhaps this world is simply the Egg of the next kalpa? Lein vokiin? Would you stop the next world from being born?
Tell me. Why do you come here, volaan? Why do you intrude on my meditation?
Krosis. No. I know little of what has passed below in the long years I have lived here.
You are likely better informed than I.
This is the most sacred mountain in Skyrim. Zok revak strunmah. The great mountain of the world.
Here the ancient Tongues, the first mortal masters of the Voice, brought Alduin to battle and defeated him.
Do you know why I live here, at the peak of the Monahven - what you name Throat of the World?
Ah. I have expected you. Prodah.
You would not come all this way for tinvaak with an old dovah. No. You seek your weapon against Alduin.
Yes. There were a few of us that rebelled against Alduin's thur... his tyranny. We aided the humans in his overthrow.
But they did not trust us. Ni ov. Their inner councils were kept hidden from us.
I was far from here on the day of Alduin's downfall. But all dov felt the... sundering of Time itself.
Vomindok. I do not know. Perhaps in the very doing they erased the knowing of it from Time itself.
The dov are children of Akatosh. Thus we are specially... attuned to the flow of Time. Perhaps also uniquely vulnerable.
I warned them against such a rash action. Even I could not foresee its consequences. Nust ni hon. They would not listen.
I cannot tell you in detail. I never heard it used. Kogaan. It was the first Thu'um created solely by mortals.
It was said to force a dragon to experience the concept of Mortality. A truly vonmindoraan... incomprehensible idea to the immortal dov.
Yes and no. Viik nuz ni kron. Alduin was not truly defeated, either. If he was, you would not be here today, seeking to... defeat him.
The Nords of those days used the Dragonrend Shout to cripple Alduin. But this was not enough. Ok mulaag unslaad.
It was the Kel - the Elder Scroll. They used it to... cast him adrift on the currents of Time.
Drem. Patience. I am answering, in my way.
True. But few now remember that this was the very spot where Alduin was defeated by the ancient Tongues.
Vahrukt unslaad... perhaps none but me now remember how he was defeated.
Tiid krent. Time was... shattered here because of what the ancient Nords did to Alduin.
If you brought that Kel, that Elder Scroll back here... to the Tiid-Ahraan, the Time-Wound...
With the Elder Scroll that was used to break Time, you may be able to... cast yourself back. To the other end of the break.
You could learn Dragonrend from those who created it.
Save the niceties for now. I have no intention of getting invested in someone who may be dead tomorrow.
If you're still breathing in a few weeks, I'm sure we'll be the best of friends.
Ooh, cocky.
I give you three days before you screw up and someone runs a knife across your throat.
But who knows? I've been proven wrong before.
Ahh, going for the mysterious and brooding thing, hmm?
That may work for your targets, but you'll find I'm not so easily impressed.
You've got people to kill, my friend. Best get to it.
Wind guide you.
Breath and focus.
Morthal used to be a quiet place. But then, the world used to make sense.
You ask me, we'd be better off without that wizard in town.
This isn't the time or place for magic, or whatever it is Falion does.
You're tougher than you look. You got me, fair and square.
I didn't mean nothing by it! Just... leave me alone! What do you want?
I don't have to take this from the likes of you.
So the Blades have survived yet another disaster of their own making, and continue to meddle in things they do not understand.
Looks like the boss is at it again.
Don't say that to loud, you don't want it getting back to him.
Why? What's he doing now?
He's on one of his drunk rants again. Except this time he's challenging everyone to a duel over in the hole.
We've got him locked in there now, but he's still challenging anyone that looks crooked at him.
Someone should let that cat lose.  That'd shut him up real quick.
What in the world was that racket? Is everything all right?
Anything! Anything at all that might be of interest. That's why I adore this location... we have no idea what we're going to find.
And if, along the way, my message about the dangers of magic should happen to sink in for a few students, that would be a happy coincidence.
You found what?
Hmm. Well now. This is all very fascinating, but I don't recall asking to you to touch anything in here.
I suppose you'd better show that to Tolfdir nonetheless. And please, ask before tampering with anything else.
We're particularly interested in the prevalence of magical seals placed on the tombs here. It's rather unlike anything we've encountered.
I'm not exactly built for manual labor. Now come on, you've almost got it.
One more... pull...
Can you get it open?
Yeeaahh! There.
I think so. Just hold on a moment.
Hurry, Nazir! I'm telling you, he's in there!
Hurry, Nazir! I'm telling you, she's in there!
You must speak with Astrid. Here, in the Dark Brotherhood Sanctuary.
I'm going... as fast... as I can, you stupid she-devil. I don't see you... helping...
You're telling me.
Oh, really? So that Dark Elf took my ring. So typical of his kind.
I think the Jarl should hear about this. Maybe double his taxes. No, triple them. That should teach those people a lesson.
You deserve something for sniffing out that thief. Here.
Fair enough.
Of course nothing in here is stolen. Only a careless, shameful, idiotic fetcher would do something as stupid as to buy pilfered goods....
Oh by Azura, I've made a terrible mistake. I bought a gold ring, and Viola Giordano has been missing a ring that looks just like it.
Be that as it may, we have no other choice. You need to remain unseen! Now go! Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
You must understand. If Cicero is turning the others against me... against us... Our Family would not survive such division.
So you understand my fear. If Cicero's planning something, and conspiring with one of our own, it would be disastrous.
Sorry, I thought Skald the Elder sent you. We've got an order to fill, and he's not known for patience.
Look, if you're heading towards Dawnstar anytime soon, can you give the Jarl this note? Let him know he'll get his lumber.
Thank you. Thank you! Azura's Prophecy always guide you to fortune.
Speaking of fortune. Here. Everything I earned from my last shipment. It's yours.
Thanks.
It's not that easy. She'd go to the Jarl if she knew I was even remotely involved. You have to get it to her some other way.
Look, it's dangerous, but if you sneak the ring into her house. Put in a dresser or something. I will make it worth your while.
Ah yes, Adonato's new work. Shorter than I expected, but words must be judged on merit, not length.
I'll look over this later. Why don't you take this, for your trouble?
That wasn't a fair swing.
Don't like it? Too bad. This is our city. Ours!
Don't think I can take you? One hundred septims says I can punch you back where you came from.
My axe is yours, outlander. Just say the word.
You. You a Dark Elf lover? Get out of our city, you filthy piece of trash.
You damn gray-skin. Go back to Morrowind!
All right. Fists only. And none of that magic stuff, either. Let's go!
I said a hundred gold! Damn broke elves and damn broke elf-lovers.
Maybe so, but healthy paranoia has saved this Sanctuary before, and my gut's telling me that demented little fool is up to something.
No objections, then? Good. Go now. Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
They'll never think to check it. Who in their right mind would hide inside? Now go. Before they meet. And report back to me with whatever you learn.
I don't think you were followed. Come on. I have a plan.
Now then. Here's my invitation, I don't have a poisoned dagger strapped to my thigh, et cetera, et cetera.
Ah! A fellow latecomer to Elenwen's little soiree.
So you really are...
You're asking me? She promised that she was sending someone who knew what they were doing...
If you actually want to get out alive, I'd bring whatever you need to move quietly, and kill quickly.
Sounds good. Be careful.
Before the Great War, the Blades helped the Empire against the Thalmor.
Our Grand Master saw them as the greatest threat to Tamriel. At the time, that was true. Maybe it still is.
So we fought them in the shadows, all across Tamriel. We thought we were more than a match for them. We were wrong.
You'd do that for a stranger? I don't know what to say. Lead on, I suppose.
Hello? You! Over here! Please, I need help!
You'd really risk your life for a stranger's honor? I don't even know what to say. Thank you, my friend. I'll wait here.
Much better. Thanks, friend. I wouldn't have lasted much longer without that.
Please, do you have a potion you can spare?
Please! I'm not sure I'll make it on my own.
Step lightly. Who knows if those things can hear us or not.
Be careful. They're faster than you'd think.
That is a lot of blood.
Not sure how much longer I can hold out here.
I really got myself into it this time.
Thank you. Please hurry!
Thank Kynareth for you, stranger. I'd almost given up hope of walking away from here.
I just need a little to get going.
That's when the fun starts. You'll have to slip away from the party without raising the alarm.
Then you'll need to find Elenwen's office and search her files. Malborn should be able to point you in the right direction.
Please. You first. I insist.
I just need to rest a moment, right here on this lovely rock.
Inside, my friend, inside! Where only the women and drinks are cold.
Awful damn cold up here. And only the guests are allowed inside. Damn foreigners and their inhospitable ways.
What are you out here talking to me for? Party's inside.
I'm paid to wait to take you home. Enjoy yourself.
The lady's already paid the fee. We leave when she gives the word.
Let me handle that. You'll have a real invitation, don't worry. As long as you can act the part of a Thalmor toady, you'll get past the guards.
They're the high elves that rule the Aldmeri Dominion. Sworn enemies of the Empire and everything it stands for.
Thirty years ago, they invaded the Empire and came close to destroying it. They smashed the Blades in the process.
Since then, they've been working to weaken the Empire any way they can.
What? Something you're still confused about?
Not here. Downstairs.
Of course. Just bring it to me when you find it.
Don't worry about Malborn. He's not a dangerous character like you, but he hates the Thalmor at least as much as I do.
He's a wood elf - the Thalmor wiped out his family back in Valenwood during one of their purges that we never hear about.
Luckily they don't know who he really is, or he wouldn't be serving drinks at the Ambassador's parties.
They're the rulers of the Aldmeri Dominion - what used to be the Imperial provinces of Summerset Isle and Valenwood.
The Thalmor take the arrogance of high elves to the extreme - they believe they are the rightful rulers of all of Tamriel.
For a century or more, the Thalmor had been picking away at the Empire. Valenwood was the first, then the province of Elsweyr.
But even the Blades didn't see the Great War coming. We underestimated the Thalmor, and they destroyed us.
Welcome. I don't believe we've met. I am Elenwen, the Thalmor Ambassador to Skyrim.
And you are...?
I prefer to arrive early. Often the day before the party. So as not to miss out on any of the drinking.
There's not enough drinking in the world today, wouldn't you agree?
Ahh!
I guess I was just hearing things.
I hope there aren't any more.
Is it safe?
Ahhh!
There you are.
Nobody here anymore.
I will just... rest here a moment. Although it is damnably cold out here. I don't look forward to the trip back down...
Perhaps we might share a carriage after the party?
I know, I know - we've just met. But think, by the time the party is over we'll no doubt be fast friends. I look forward to it.
Please. Take your time.
Don't mind me. I'm perfectly insensible to the cold.
I'll go after you. Although these fellows know me. I never miss one of Elenwen's parties, you see.
Of course not. Purely a formality. Now, I need to see your invitation.
So that's your plan, huh? Even if you could survive, by the time you got inside, whatever documents they had would be long gone.
We're there for information, remember? Trust me, I've been doing this for a long time. My way is better.
The faction that rules the Aldmeri Dominion. The ones who almost destroyed the Empire during the Great War, thirty years back.
There's no worse enemy to humankind in Tamriel. The Empire barely survived the last war. The Thalmor don't intend to lose the next one.
Yes, yes. Now to find myself a drink.
My lateness is due more to getting lost on the way up this gods-forsaken mountain than to any desire to actually arrive late.
I've figured out how we're going to get you into the Thalmor Embassy.
I've been doing this a long time, remember? While the Thalmor's been looking for me, I've been watching them.
Everything's in order. Welcome back, sir.
And arriving by carriage, no less! I salute you, sir!
And arriving by carriage, no less! I salute you, my good lady!
I have told Idgrod of your concerns. She'll look after you all. Please, go back to your business.
What's the Jarl going to do about it?
Morthal has enough problems as it is!
Please, enough already!
We've no need for wizards in our midst!
Ah, you saw that, did you?
Life in Morthal has been troubled lately. The people are uncertain, restless. They merely look to the Jarl for leadership.
There's news of rebellion against the Empire. Strange noises have been heard in the marshes at night.
And then, the tragedy with Hroggar's home.
The men simply seek wisdom. Everything will be fine.
The world's going mad, and our Jarl does what?
She hides inside with her 'visions'. We need a leader, not some mystic!
How can I?
She never speaks with us. She's allowed this wizard, Falion, to live in our midst practicing gods-know-what.
And now there's talk of rebellion, and dragons? What help could Idgrod be against them?
No, we can only rely on ourselves. You'd do well to remember that.
How are we supposed to feel safe in our own homes?
I... I don't know. Ancano is gone, but whatever he's done to the Eye doesn't seem to have stopped.
I have no idea what to do!
I knew you could do it!
Let's hope we succeed.
This is our only chance.
Yes. I had Falk call you back because you were at the summoning, you have a connection to Potema now. You must deal with her.
I can provide you with help for her minions though.  This should help you deal with the Catacombs themselves.
I'll see you back in Riverwood.
I don't know. Beem-Ja has looked after me since I was knee-high to him. I need to rest, and then I think I'm going back to High Rock.
Go on without me. I think I need to be alone for a while.
You've been at it for five minutes. Here, let me try.
I think I almost got it.
Come on, just open.
I can't get it open.
Thank you, sir. Go right in.
Thank you, ma'am. Go right in.
Oh I know. Isn't it wonderful? I bet she squirmed like a skeever when you pulled them out of your pocket.
I think things are going to be a lot different around here from now on and I have you to thank for it.
Here, I want you to have this. It was my father's but I'm certain you'll put it to good use.
Yeah, you're right. Maybe it's not the best idea.
Oh, this is going to be great!
You need to get the Marks of Dibella from Bolli, Hofgrir and Indaryn. Not sure how you're going to do that, but try your best.
Then just confront Haelga with them and the rest works itself out.
I'm not sure what it's made from, but I can tell you that once you've had a taste, you'll never crave anything else.
The Dunmer came up with the stuff a long time ago. Very illegal and very hard to come by.
I got mine off of a merchant ship in Solitude. Worst mistake I ever made.
Good. Now get going.
That would be a bad idea. I'd be too likely to attract the wrong kind of attention. But they don't know you at all, yet.
Bolli is a good man, he pays us well and watches out for us.
I used to work at the Black-Briar Meadery, but Indaryn and I never saw eye-to-eye. That elf has no patience at all.
It's probably for the best anyway; I'm far better at fishing than working in a meadery.
Almost anyone in Riften is in dire need of those missives. Speak to them, child. You will know who will listen and who will sin.
A word of advice, keep away from the Keep and the city guards. They follow their own path and have already forsaken Her.
Now that the Imperial blight has poisoned Riften, the worship of Talos is strictly forbidden by the terms of the White-Gold Concordat.
They can defile our shrine, they can arrest our people and they can silence our voices, but the Empire will never destroy our spirit.
As long as we keep Talos in our heart, his legacy will never die.
Very well. I suppose I'll just curl up somewhere until this passes as usual.
What? How... where did you get these?
No, don't tell me. I don't want to know. Look, we need to keep this quiet... between you and me, okay. No one else needs to know about it.
If word got out that I'm practicing my Dibellan Arts in Riften, they'll run me out of town.
Here, take this and don't mention a word of this to anyone, especially Svana!
What, did you suddenly grow a conscience? Suit yourself.
Look, it was one night, okay? I think she slipped something in my drink. I haven't even been back to the Bunkhouse since.
Just take this damn thing. I never want to see it again.
I don't want a bad reputation in Riften, but Haelga's out of control. She wanted to try it in the stables so I said yes.
If you want the mark that badly then go ahead and take it.
If word gets around that I bedded her, they'll lose all respect for me at the meadery.
Take the mark if you want it. It's not worth all this hassle anyway.
My job at the Riften Fishery is in danger. The owner, Bolli, said that if I show up for work in this condition one more time, then I'm out.
I don't mean to do this to myself, but I can't help it. I tried some skooma a year ago, and ever since then, I can't stop!
If you could give me a healing potion, I could cleanse this poison from my body and get back to my life.
Your kindness will never be forgotten.
Here, take this. It's all I can offer for what you've given me.
Mara is the handmaiden of Kyne, the Goddess of the Storm and the Mother of Men.
She is the bearer of love and the patron of marriage.
If you wish to make a donation to her Benevolence, speak to Maramal and ensure your true love will endure for all eternity.
Thank you, my friend.
Work? I suppose you can call it that. I call it slavery. I work my fingers to the bone keeping this disgusting place clean.
Ever since my parents died and Haelga took me in, it's been a nightmare. How was I to know she was such a wretched woman?
So now I'm stuck living here while those pigs she calls customers grope me and say the most awful things.
I should say so.
Next time think before you speak.
You've got some nerve.
Thank you. I'd almost given up hope.
Here, take this. It's all I can offer for what you've given me.
I have no idea what you're talking about. I'd never sleep with anyone besides my good wife, Drifa.
Huh? What are you talking about? I don't even know what that is.
You're crazy! How dare you make such an accusation!
It's not just the work, it's everything about her. She's disgusting. I think she takes her worship of Dibella a bit too seriously.
Do you know she's slept with three different men in the last month alone? What kind of a woman would do such a thing?
Just for once, I'd like to see her squirm... to rub her nose in it.
Such a shame. Perhaps another time then.
Actually, there is, but I don't think I could get away with doing it. She'd kill me if she found out.
You see, I happen to know after she makes love, she gives her partner a token of her affection called a Mark of Dibella.
If you confront her with three of the Marks, she'd be so embarrassed... well, I don't know what she'd do!
Hand what over? I have no idea what you're talking about.
Look, you must have me confused with someone else.
Welcome to the Thalmor Embassy. Your invitation, please.
Your invitation, please.
You aren't making any sense. Hand what over?
You must present your invitation to be admitted to the party.
Or else what? You don't scare me.
You have a death wish or something?
You're threatening me? Oh, that's rich!
Farewell.
Were the Greybeards any help? Did they know this anything about the Shout used against Alduin?
Ask Esbern. If he doesn't know himself, he'll know where to find it out.
Ah, indeed? There's a pretty puzzle. Not the kind of thing you'll find in your local bookshop. Let me think...
Perhaps the College of Winterhold. What learning there is in Skyrim is gathered there.
One of the mages there may be able to put you on the trail of this Elder Scroll.
An Elder Scroll? That's not going to be easy to come by.
Esbern's your man for that kind of thing. He may be able to at least point you in the right direction.
Did he, now? That doesn't sound like my nephew at all. I doubt he'd be mixed up with the likes of you.
I'd be glad to help a friend of Hadvar. But you didn't answer my question - how do you know him?
Hadvar? How do you know him?
You'd do well to remember who's really in charge around here.
You'd do well to remember who's in charge around here.
May you return to Her Benevolence in safety.
You represent Mara now. Set an example for all.
Good journey, my child!
I don't know why I bother.
Try and get those marks without violence please. I don't want to be responsible for their deaths... or yours.
Sure, leave. Not like you care anyway.
Remain vigilant and don't trust anyone.
You've been kind, I thank you.
Any healing potion will do. Please hurry.
What am I going to do?
A Dreth never forgets a friend.
Remember, keep this to yourself.
Blessings upon you, traveler.
A Dreth never forgets a traitor. You'll pay for this!
I believe you have a delivery to make.
I'm Riften's appointed Steward. How can I help you?
Steward Hemming Black-Briar at your service.
Blessings of Mara upon you!
Has the Warmth of Mara been given to Riften?
Lady Mara bids you welcome to Her Benevolence.
There's just one more thing I need from you. To fully absorb Gathrik's power I require a blood sacrifice.
Your blood should be good enough. This will go easier for us both if you don't bother fighting back.
Fool. I should thank you. I knew the girl wouldn't be strong enough to help me kill Gathrik alone.
New around Riften? Take my advice. Stay at the Bee and Barb... the Bunkhouse isn't for you.
Any luck getting the marks from those men?
Sorry about the mess. Keeping this place clean is quite a chore.
Go right on in. The party has already started.
I'm sorry, sir, I'm on duty.
I'm sorry, ma'am, I'm on duty.
We're not supposed to talk to the guests.
The Thalmor Embassy welcomes you.
Please proceed into the Embassy.
Guests are advised to remain inside the Embassy.
What are you doing here? You're supposed to be meeting Malborn in Solitude.
Not here. I told you to meet me in Riverwood.
I'm just doing my duty, sir.
We're in this together. Stay close.
Let's clear the place out first. Then we'll gather our loot.
Look, I never needed treasure. My father owns land; our family is wealthy. I just wanted adventure. Not this.
Yeah, I don't know how I feel about what goes on down there.
I bet Naris gets a kick out it. Nasty fella he is.
Ah, the sweet smell of burnt flesh. Nothing better really.
They drink all day and night and what do I do?
I work the forge.
They die too quickly. Maybe I'll try and hold off on the paralysis next time. Give me more of a moving target. Good practice.
Why do they need me to do this?  We use magic, not weapons.
You work the forge.
You better quiet down before you end up there. Then I'd have to work the forge.
Oh, it's you again. What in blazes do you want?
What can I get you? Mead? Wine?
Looking for a place to stay?
You have some nerve coming back here after what you did to Keerava.
Is there something I do for you?
Be brief Imperial, there's only so long I can stand being downwind of your stench.
Hm. You're not from around here. Imperial spy perhaps?
If your allegiance lies with the Empire, we haven't much to discuss.
Ah! A fellow Nord! I trust you're bringing the fight to the Empire.
Talos bless you, my child.
How can a humble servant of Talos help you today?
Blessings of Talos upon you.
Sorry, I'm afraid I can't help you much right now... I'm currently in mourning.
Yes? What is it?
I'm the brewmaster around here. What did you want?
I'm quite busy. Need something or can I get back to work?
Yeah? What do you want? If this is about the meadery, we can talk later.
Welcome! Allow me to recommend the Black-Briar Mead... the purest brew in all of Skyrim!
We serve only the best here... Black-Briar Mead. May I get one for you?
I'm off work at the Black-Briar Meadery. I'll be back there in the morning.
Hey, don't look at me that way! Who are you to judge me?
So what if I have a problem? I know how to deal with it!
Maramal thinks he's so smart; tells me that Mara will cleanse the filth from my blood. Ha! What does he know?
If Indaryn finds out, he'll have me thrown out of the Meadery for certain. So don't breathe a word of it to him. Not a word!
Oh what am I going do? I can barely afford to stay at the Bunkhouse.
My n'wah of a father thought he could pull my strings... "continue the Hlaalu tradition." Bah! I left all that nonsense behind.
I'm going to stop. That's what I'm going to do! Then everyone will see that I'm not as useless as they think.
I'm selling the best mead in town for cheap. Interested?
Wilhelm was pleased with the delivery, well done.
Well met. I don't think they're hiring anyone at the moment, so if you're looking for work, you're out of luck.
The trees... they're beautiful here. Reminds me of autumn at home.
The fall forest makes me miss my childhood; playing among the towering trees in Valenwood and living with my brethren.
Seeing the sorry state of this city almost makes me regret my decision to leave my home when my apprenticeship completed.
I was fortunate enough to be guided by one of Silvenar's master armorers until he passed.
On his deathbed, my master urged me to take over his business but I decided against it. I chose to wander Tamriel instead.
Now here I am, working for this dreary city's Fishery. I sometimes wonder why I don't just move on.
Hello. Welcome to the Pawned Prawn.
We both own the Pawned Prawn together, but he's singlehandedly kept the place running despite all our problems.
Bersi's afraid that someone's making a move to take the Pawned Prawn from us. I think he's being paranoid.
All the crime and corruption in Riften is really getting to him... and now that we hear rumors about the dragons, he's been very depressed.
I just care about my husband so much and I hate to see him in this state.
Welcome to the Pawned Prawn. What can I do for you?
I have all sorts of interesting items for sale. Have you looked around?
Pickings may be a bit slim today, but if you see anything you like I'll make you a good deal.
I presume you're bothering me for a good reason?
What in the name of Oblivion do you want?
I don't have time for idle chit-chat. What is it?
Fancy yourself the adventuring type, eh? It's just as well, I doubt your combat prowess would prove valuable in a real battle anyway.
I was trained by some of the finest warriors in all Skyrim to wield a blade as if it was an extension of my own arm.
My mother spares no expense where I'm concerned. I'm heir to the family fortune after all.
Once I'm in charge I have bigger aspirations than just Skyrim. I plan to bring Black-Briar Mead to all of Tamriel.
My children, Sibbi and Ingun, are being groomed to run the family business one day as well.
Sibbi will prove to be a fine leader one day, but Ingun... that girl has her mind somewhere other than business.
Maven's disappointed that Ingun's heart isn't in the family business, but she says "Ingun still has her uses." Not sure what that means.
Have you come to gawk at me or is this a social call?
Well, if it isn't my little problem-solver.
My mind is on my experiments today. What did you need?
What was it you wanted?
Welcome back to the temple, messenger.
How may I help you, my son?
What is it you seek, my child?
New in town, eh? Here for the fishing I'd guess.
Thanks again for the delivery to Markarth. What can I do for you?
If you're looking for a handout, you're not going to get one from me.
If you're looking for a handout, you'll want to speak with my husband. If you're looking for company, I suppose I could do worse.
Bolli's around somewhere, planning to feed the poor with his nonsense about Mara.
With my husband's new found religion, what I do with our coin and what I do all day seem less important to him. Suits me.
At this rate, he'll give the entire Riften Fishery away to charity. Then how will I live?
Such a waste of time preaching to the poor. If they're that hungry, they should just grow some food or something.
I spend every Friday at the fishery, so I know the meaning of hard work. All those forms to fill out and file; it's exhausting!
Mark my words. If Bolli thinks he's giving all of our wealth away to some poor slob, I'll leave him high and dry.
I'm feeling much better thanks to your efforts.
I can see that disapproving look in your eye.
If you're looking for a bed, this is the wrong place.
You looking for something or what?
Hey! Good to see you again.
I'd appreciate it if you'd keep your hands to yourself.
The Bunkhouse isn't an inn. I'd recommend the Bee and Barb.
Who did this to me?
If there's anything you wish to purchase, just let me know.
Have anything you wish to sell? I pay fair prices for all sorts of goods.
I'm beginning to think leaving Mournhold was a mistake.
If Riften is the best Skyrim has to offer, I may as well turn back right now and just head for home.
Working at the Riften Fishery is tough, but it puts some coin in my pocket.
Please help me, I'm going to lose my job at the Riften Fishery.
Oh, my aching head. Why do I do this to myself?
You have no idea what it's like to have everything, then watch it all disappear.
I'm feeling much better now thanks to you.
Did you bring anything? The pain...
Welcome to Riften, traveler. I hope the road fared well for you.
What can I do for you?
I feel so helpless out here. The people of Riften depended on me, and I failed them.
Out of my way. The son of the Jarl has no time for idle conversation with travelers.
If this isn't about official city business, you're wasting my time.
Come to gloat, have we? To poke fun at the jarl's youngest son?
Did Harrald send you to taunt me?
I'm afraid my days as the steward of Riften has come to an end.
As Riften's Steward I'm afraid I can provide only limited assistance, but speak your mind.
Welcome to Mistveil Keep. I'm Jarl Laila's steward, Anuriel.
As Jarl Laila's housecarl, I would ask that you maintain a respectable distance from her at all times.
Did you have some sort of official business here?
I'd request that when speaking to Jarl Laila, you show the proper respect.
Being in exile is the most humiliating thing I've experienced in my life.
I killed scores of the Empire's men before I was forced to surrender.
The only reason I stopped fighting was a plea from Laila, otherwise I'd would have fought to the death.
I know you. Where was it? Hm. Oh! You were getting something for me?
Excuse my disorganization, but I'm in the middle of some delicate experiments.
Nothing like the smell of a white-hot blade, eh?
Looking for some armor? A weapon perhaps?
Here to purchase some armor or a weapon?
I used to live at the orphanage here in Riften. Grelod said my ma and da left me there when I was a baby... that they didn't want me.
Balimund took me in and has been so kind to me. I don't know why, but he thinks I can be a great blacksmith like him.
At first I don't know why he picked me. I never even held a hammer in my life! But the first time I picked it up, it just felt right!
Folks in town say that Balimund is the greatest blacksmith in all of Tamriel. I hope I can live up to that some day and make him proud of me.
Welcome back! Good to see you again.
Visitors like us are rare in this part of Skyrim.
I've never seen Mjoll this upset about anything. The city really gets to her.
Mjoll told me that it isn't this bad everywhere. Maybe one day I'll venture out and see if she's right.
Mjoll's brought me nothing but safety and piece of mind... and some lessons with the blade.
I hope that someday this city will be free from corruption and we can all live in peace.
Do you have any idea how busy I am? My mixture could be burning as we speak!
You here for lessons like Ingun? Or did you wish to buy something?
Take a look at my supplies if you must, but do hurry... I'm not getting any younger you know!
Thanks again for delivering that ore sample.
Where's my head? I never even greeted you properly.
I apologize in advance for my husband's crankiness; I promise you he means well.
Please, please. I need but a septim. A septim is all I ask. Is that so bad?
I can't take this place anymore. The trees... all the colors, but the city, so dark. Why? It makes no sense.
So cold, so hungry, so tired.
No one cares about us! No one! We could die and no one would ever even know! They'd just throw our corpses in the canal!
Are you scared of death? I am. I hear everything just stops... forever. It just all goes away, but you don't realize it. I don't want to die.
Sure, sure. I'm a dirty beggar... why would you want to even speak to me.
To Oblivion with all of you! You rich piles of dung walking by us like we're not there! Pretending we don't exist! How dare you!
Edda... she's crazy as a loon, but at least she doesn't have the mind to realize what a horrible world this is. She's the lucky one.
Just leave Riften. Go far, far away and never return. This place is nothing but a rotten, stinking corpse that's sat in the sun far too long.
Greetings, honored friend. Could I interest you in some fine jewelry?
If anything pleases the eye, don't hesitate to make me an offer.
If you have any jewelry to sell, I'd be glad to pay you a fair sum in gold for it.
You'll never get the claw!
Too slow!
I'll... get you...
This... isn't over...
All right... End it...
Come on. Finish it!
I'll bathe in your blood!
You won't even live to regret this!
I'll gut you like a horker!
I won't hold back!
Your end draws near!
Shor have mercy on you!
The White take you!
You're as good as dead!
I'll carve you into pieces!
You're going to pay!
I'll see you burn!
You're no match for me!
This is your last mistake!
I won't back down!
You're outmatched!
You're already dead.
You're wrong to come here, stranger. No rest in Dawnstar.
By the Divines, I'm tired.
These dreams are real I tell you. Real!
What is it? I haven't had much sleep.
If I get any rest soon, I'll count myself richer than a king.
Gods I'm so tired.
He needs to die. He deserves to die. And it falls to you to kill him.
Until he's dead... well, I'm sorry, but we would dishonor our oaths as Blades if we continued to help you.
That's fine. We needed his help. Now we don't, and it's long past time for him to pay for his crimes.
And he's not just any dragon. He was the right hand of Alduin.
He committed atrocities so infamous they are still remembered, thousands of years later.
Have you decided to do the right thing, Dragonborn?
Make your choice, Dragonborn. You're either with us or against us.
I wish there were another way, Dragonborn, but it falls to you to serve justice upon Paarthurnax.
Justice can be harsh. But it is still justice. Paarthurnax deserves to die.
There's one more thing. We know about Paarthurnax.
I knew we could count on you! Glad to have you back on our side.
At long last. An ancient evil is avenged. The shades of many Blades salute you this day.
Paarthurnax. The dragon, that the Greybeards have been protecting for all these years.
Ah, you're back. I hope you've made some progress on tracking down this Shout we need to defeat Alduin?
I will not lie to you, there is some risk involved. The last time the Torpor was imbibed could have been decades ago.
But I swear upon Lady Mara that I will do everything within my power to prevent any harm from befalling you.
Quite amazing, yes. Alchemy and the blessings of a Divine distilled down into a ingestible liquid.
Sadly, I have yet to see it function in person.
Yes. The Torpor grants an ability the priests of Vaermina called "The Dreamstride"; using dreams to travel distances in the real world.
No, all my research points in the opposite direction. This is the exact liquid the priests would drink.
The only question in my mind is whether you will be granted The Dreamstride. Otherwise, the worst that could happen is a bad aftertaste.
I swear upon Lady Mara that I would never let any harm befall you.
I assure you, the Dreamstride is well known in Vaerminian Lore.
Sadly, I have yet to see it function in person.
As a sworn priest of Mara, the elixir won't work for me. The Torpor will only work for Priests of Vaermina, or the unaffiliated.
The Jarl does little to protect us these days.
All well and good. But did you know that he was Alduin's chief lieutenant in ancient times? Responsible for terrible atrocities?
It's true that his crimes are long in the past, but justice does not count the passage of years.
He's a dragon. Not just any dragon, but the right hand of Alduin, responsible for many atrocities during the ancient Dragon War.
I'm afraid there's a further problem. A serious one. I've discovered who the Greybeards' leader really is.
Here's the big picture. He helped Alduin enslave our ancestors. He may have betrayed Alduin in the end, but that makes him worse, not better.
We can't afford to give Paarthurnax the opportunity to betray us in turn, and return to his old master.
Paarthurnax was the author of many atrocities during the Dragon War - crimes great enough to be remembered for thousands of years.
True, he turned traitor to Alduin and helped overthrow the Dragon Cult, but that does not excuse or expiate his previous deeds.
Whether or not he has truly repented, or merely acted to save himself, justice demands that he pay with his life.
