The Blades have been hunting him for centuries, but he was protected by the Greybeards and then the Emperors.
Justice demands that he die for his crimes. Until he is dead, I'm afraid my oath as a Blade prevents me from offering you aid and comfort.
So I've heard. Thanks to you, this should be the last time they forget to pay.
One small detail though - I need all of the gold they gave you. No one skims around here. Come back when you've got every last coin.
A new face! Such a welcome sight in dreary old Morthal. Welcome friend, welcome!
Don't fret over the gloom, there's nothing to fear! Old Lurbuk will brighten your spirits -- just lend him your ear!
The good people of Morthal have yet to embrace my talent.
Try as you may, they won't say "good day," but will just walk away. Worry not, Morthal! Lurbuk is here to stay!
The fair Jonna does me a great courtesy by allowing me to entertain the guests of her noble establishment.
I, Lurbuk the Great, will win the hearts of the people of Morthal one day, mark my words!
Welcome to the Moorside. If you need anythin', I'll be 'round. Good to have a customer.
There ain't much to offer, but if you wanna place dry to spend the night, I'll rent you a room.
Runnin' an inn weren't my plan. But Falion decided to move here, so I joined him.
I take what little business I can git. I'm grateful for anyone willin' to spend money here.
Ignore the Orc. He pays, so I let him stay.
You'll want to be careful travelin' at night. The marsh is a dangerous place.
So, life has brought you to Morthal, and to me. What purpose this serves, we will no doubt see. Welcome.
Tamriel is full of wisdom and magic, if one is willing to look for it. Few have their eyes open.
I speak, and others hear. It is my hope that instead they listen.
In Morthal, there is little to rule. But there is much to teach. And so, I am Jarl.
All this talk of rebellion and strife. Too many are focused on what might be instead of what is. It tires me to talk about it.
There is too much to learn in the world, too much to know, to fight over "laws" that have no real purpose.
We're safe here now. But what of Morthal?
I do what I can to see that Idgrod's time isn't wasted, and that she only has to deal with important matters.
Too many bother my wife with their petty problems, and then don't even understand the advice she gives them.
The only thing that matters to me is keeping my wife safe. I won't let anyone harm her.
Morthal doesn't need any trouble. You keep your nose clean, understand?
My mind weighs heavy enough. Leave me to my food.
Morthal has enough problems without you bothering me.
I'm under a lot of pressure. If you don't leave me alone, I won't be held responsible.
Please don't mind Joric. He's not mad, really he's not.
I don't... I haven't mother's gift, not quite, but I've seen things.
Someday I'll be Jarl. Mother reminds me often. I hope I can help the people of Morthal the way she has.
It's difficult sometimes... To know whether what I see is real or not.
You're different, aren't you? Not like anyone else. Not in Morthal, or Skyrim.
I don't understand. Why can't we go home?
Gorm thinks I'm mad. He doesn't say it, but I know he thinks it. Is he right?
My mother sees things. She doesn't know why, but she does. Only sometimes, she doesn't see me at all.
It's Falion. He... He did it. I don't know what it was, but he did it.
Keep your wits about you. Morthal's hardly a safe place.
I'm just a poor man trying to make a living. I'd leave Morthal were it in my power.
This damned war has cost us... me... everything.
My boy's to grow up without a mother? What life is that? It's not right, I tell you.
Hasn't been seen for a while. She just vanished without a trace.
Have you seen my mother? I'm still waiting for her to come home.
Papa says that I shouldn't keep waiting. Why not? Mama has to come home some time.
Who are the Stormcloaks, and why would mama want to spend time with them instead of me?
I know you for what you are. This town's blood is ours. Leave it! Now we must return to our roles lest the sheep suspect the wolves.
The people here work so hard, and they never allow themselves a moment of fun. It's such a pity. It won't last forever though.
These people are like cattle. All they do is work, sleep and eat.
Gathering plants, picking mushrooms, brewing potions. It all gets so dreary after a while. This town needs something exciting to happen.
Thank you for saving me from Alva. I was completely under her evil spell. I'll never forgive myself for what happened to Helgi and my wife.
Alva's been good to me ever since... well, since the fire.
I just try and do my job, and not to think about what's happened. Try to find what happiness I can.
Ignore the talk of spirits and creatures and such. It's just superstition.
You are new to Morthal. We have not spoken before. This is interesting.
The changes in Morthal are of little consequence to me. So long as the laws are upheld, I do not care who does the upholding.
Morthal is a troubled place, and it is my duty to see it rest in peace.
Please remain indoors at night. It is dangerous to go outside.
Some day, brother. Some day.
Think the guards will be coming down, soon?
Nearly broke my back out in the mines today. Damn Nords should bleed for putting us through this!
You make one move towards my skooma, and I shiv you.
They keep the doors locked when they come down. You kill them, you're trapping us here with no food.
Think maybe we should jump the guards when they come down next?
Have any skooma? I need to get something from Grisvar.
Ah, it's not been more than a few days since they came down the last time.
When the orcs invaded the temple, I fled. I left my brothers and sisters here to die.
Khajiit.
Breton.
Imperial.
Redguard.
Nord.
Argonian.
Oh, half the field should do.
I told you I don't like you going there. I've seen scavengers picking at the mammoths. They could be dangerous.
I just think pollinating would be easier if I could do a lot of plants at once.
I had a look at that boneyard the other day, Vantus. The one to the Northwest.
That should be all right. How much space were you planning?
You know, I was thinking of expanding the herb garden. Make room for some new plants I'm planning.
Half the field? What could need half the field?
Uh... why don't we talk about this later?
I hadn't asked you because I was afraid you'd say no. I appreciate your help.
Here, I'm entrusting you with the dagger. Bring it to Andurs and he'll take care of the rest.
If you're trying to ask if I tend the hall of the dead alone, then the answer is yes.
You think anyone around here is going to lift a shovel to help me? Guess again. "Leave the dirty work to old Alessandra."
Look, I didn't ask for this job, I got stuck with it thanks to my father.
Yes. Perhaps one day I'll be able to make the journey, but for now I need to tend the dead.
Let's see. Last time I used the Dwemer Spoon was at my dear friend Boti's house in Ivarstead... Fellstar Farm I believe it's called.
The orichalcum ingot should still be in Winterhold at the Frozen Hearth Inn. I don't know why I didn't just take it with me.
And last is the soul gem. I left that one in Windhelm at the White Phial alchemy shop. It was a good trade too. Ah well.
You don't have to, because it's utter nonsense. It's never going to work.
No, no, no. What I need to do is to reverse that. Draw the harmonic energy into the reagent and then apply it with the proper incantation.
What was I thinking? It would never work that first way. Why didn't you tell me?
I felt a caress on my cheek some time ago... I thought it to be a trick of the wind, but I think it was a message from my father.
We've made peace with one another without even uttering a single word and I'm content for the first time in my life.
I thank you. Here, please accept this as a token of my appreciation for your selfless action.
I'll tell you why you won't tell me. Because you were absolutely right not to.
It's a fundamental rule of magic and it would be ridiculous. You can't draw from harmonic energy without a soul siphon.
Since you're so well informed I'm certain you've solved the problem with thermic drift, right?
What's not to get? It's as simple as Traven's First Axiom of Magic... or was it Galerion's Law of Casting. Hmm.
I'll tell you what, let me find out where that's from. I'll need to look through my library... and get you an answer.
Umm... what were we talking about?
Yes, I could use some help with that. An assistant perhaps? No, no, no. Maybe a familiar. No, too messy.
Well, at any rate, organization is not my strong suit. Not at all. I need as much help as I can get.
In fact, if you could retrieve a few things for me, I could start on my next set of experiments.
A shame. Perhaps another time then.
Excellent. I can't wait.
Well... why are you still standing here? Oh, right. You need to know where they are! That would help.
As you can imagine, I tend to forget things often. Leave things around. I really must learn to put these things away.
What I need is my Dwemer Stirring Spoon, my Orichalcum Ingot and my Grand Soul Gem. In fact, I could use them immediately.
Bring those items back to me and I'll be happy to experiment on you. No, no... that isn't right. I'd be happy to reward you.
My father was a Priest of Arkay. All my life was spent around the dead, being forced to learn the proper burial rituals and prayers.
I never had any friends; who'd want to spend time with "the little weird girl who hangs around dead people?"
So I grew up with a miserable trade. This is all I know how to do... prepare and inter the deceased.
Please, come back and speak to me anytime.
Be cautious, the pilgrimage to the Shrine is not an easy one.
I'd ask you maintain civility while you're here.
Good journey to you.
You're welcome in Riften as long as you continue to obey our laws.
I'm keeping my eye on you.
I have to remember... ward first, then summon.
Okay, now where did I put those spiders?
Someone actually interested in what I'm doing? Amazing! Well, allow me to explain.
My experimentation involves a magical construct and a reagent that will allow the construct to maintain a field of permanent harmonic energy!
I'd like to make the journey to the Hall of the Dead in Windhelm and give my ceremonial dagger to Andurs, the caretaker.
I believe once it's interred with my father's remains I'll be at peace with his memory.
Items? What items? Let me see those.
Hmm. I'm not sure how you managed it, but these are exactly what I need to start my next set of experiments. Wonderful!
Well, at least allow me to reward you for your uncanny foresight.
The pilgrimage is complete, I can feel it.
Anything else before you make the journey?
Did we have an appointment? No... it was a delivery? I can't remember.
I understand. But you're being paranoid. I'm inspecting security, not charging off into battle. There's not a lot that can go wrong.
Son, when the Emperor's safety is concerned, anything could go wrong. Off with you, now. And good travels.
Farewell, father. I'll return as soon as I'm able.
Well, this is it, then. Look after yourself, Gaius. You're doing your duty, and I'm proud of you. But you'd better come back to me. You hear?
Oh, Faida. I may travel alone, but you know I carry you always in my heart. I'll see you soon.
No, so your best plan is to follow him after he leaves Dragon Bridge. However, there is one other option...
We know there's an actual written travel schedule, somewhere in the Penitus Oculatus outpost. Securing that would, obviously, be most beneficial...
It's a small town, with one rather unique feature - it houses Skyrim's only Penitus Oculatus outpost.
It was established by Commander Maro when he first arrived in Skyrim, several months ago. When he began preparing for the Emperor's initial visit.
Father, you worry too much. I'll be fine.
Yes. Do not kill Gaius Maro in Dragon Bridge, or on the road. Kill him in one of the other major cities he'll be visiting.
There, the body will be discovered quickly, as will the letter implicating Gaius Maro and the Stormcloaks in the Emperor's assassination.
Do that, and Astrid has authorized me to grant you a rather unique bonus. It is a special token, to be given to Olava the Feeble, in Whiterun.
Olava is an old and dear friend, and a powerful seer. The token entitles you to a reading of your future. It's an opportunity one should not pass up.
I know you will. But all the same, remember everything I said. Stay alert, and when you get to the cities, make your observations and move on.
Let those that watch from Sovngarde envy us this day!
Skyrim will be free!
Hold, Alduin on the Wing! Sister Hawk, grant us your sacred breath to make this contract heard!
It worked... you did it...
He will come. He cannot ignore our defiance. And why should he fear us, even now?
We shall see soon enough. Alduin approaches!
We've bloodied him well. Four of his kin have fallen to my blade alone this day.
So be it.
But none have yet stood against Alduin himself. Galthor, Sorri, Birkir...
No. We will deal with Alduin ourselves, here and now.
You do not understand. Alduin cannot be slain like a lesser dragon. He is beyond our strength.
It's no use! Use the Scroll, Felldir! Now!
Yes, the World-Eater is gone... may the spirits have mercy on our souls.
They did not have Dragonrend. Once we bring him down, I promise I will have his head.
Felldir! We agreed not to use it!
I never agreed. And if you are right, I will not need it.
Why does Alduin hang back? We've staked everything on this plan of yours, old man.
No, I'm sorry. I'll just... I'll know it if I find it. Does that make sense?
Is there something you need, dear?
All right, can you describe it to me?
If it does to you, dear, that's all that matters.
Do you like magic? Falion says I'm good at learning. I like learning about magic.
My mother and father died years ago. Falion takes care of me now.
I asked Falion if someday I could learn from the mages in Winterhold, but he doesn't like that idea.
If you need any alchemy supplies, I'll do my best to provide them. Assuming I haven't used them all up.
Jorgen and I both work hard, all the time. Morthal is important to both of us.
I don't get many customers, but that leaves me time to try things out. Experiment, I guess.
Unless it's about the mill, I don't have time to talk. Too much to do.
We've gone from a Jarl lost in her own head to a Jarl that cares for nothing but herself. Lot of good that does us.
I just want to be left alone to do my job, and to enjoy what little free time I have when I have it.
I... I'm not sure. I think perhaps, but I don't know what it is.
You can have anything except the scroll of course.
Oh alright.  Well I'll be here for a bit if you change your mind.
If Vokun is as powerful as I think, I may need a little help um ... dealing ...with him.
I'm Anska.  Are you here to help?
Great, with the two of us this should be easy.
I've finally located Vokun's crypt! The scroll I'm looking for just has to be here.
That's right. Whole group of the natives called the Forsworn rose up against the Nords. Drove them out.
Then Ulfric Stormcloak came with his militia. That must have been a sight to see, him shouting folks to death.
You'd think that'd be the end of it, but those Forsworn are still in the hills, and they're as wild as wolves.
It's the whole reason Ulfric revolted against the Empire. Well, the first reason anyway.
Forsworn had taken over Markarth, and Ulfric and his men drove them out. Empire promised they'd be free to worship Talos afterwards.
Pity no one told the Elves. Whole group of those Thalmor came and they demanded Ulfric's arrest.
That's right, at least according to them scholars. Stonework's clearly not Nord, that much is for sure.
Old Elven fella named Calcelmo is up in Understone Keep's the one you want to talk to for more about that. Wrote all those books.
Before the Nords came, we worshipped the old gods, had our own kingdom.
Times have been good and bad since then, but some folks couldn't handle not ruling their own land. Those are the Forsworn.
The Forsworn follow the old ways, but some of those were best forgotten. Blood sacrifices, communing with Daedra. It's the road to ruin.
It's an old city. Legends say Dwarves built it first. Then after they vanished, the natives moved in.
Finally, the Nords took over around Tiber Septim's time. That bent a few folks' arms the wrong way.
What's this? Oh, that's right, the timbers from Anga's Mill.
I swear, that Aeri and her men would make fine Stormcloak recruits. Pity we can't spare the lumber to conscript them.
Here. Take this. For your time and all that.
That would be Jarl Igmund. Young for a ruler of a Hold, especially the Reach.
Wasn't surprising he stayed loyal to the Empire. His father helped capture Ulfric Stormcloak after the Markarth Incident.
Since the Stormcloaks took over, Jarl Thongvor is in charge.
It'll be lean times for the Reach with a Silver-Blood ruling it, that's for certain.
Yeah, yeah.
Well, look at you, thanks. Take it to Voada, she handles all the dogs for the Jarl.
My family has been doing it for years. You need one out in the Reach. Don't want to be alone when some Forsworn comes swinging.
I personally trained a whole litter for the Jarl. That reminds me, I was going to deliver some dog food to the Keep. Spiced beef. Their favorite.
You need a reliable horse in the Reach. That's what we have.
Traveling in the Reach is dangerous. You need a good carriage.
City of Markarth sure is a beauty. No doubt about that.
A war dog is good company and good protection.
Don't fall.
The man doing your job?
Whatever happened to Ennodius? He just up and left.
Oh, I've heard this one before. Watch out Divines, poor Kodrir curses you all for giving him life!
Watch your mouth with me you pup! Who do you think you are?
You're all heart, Kodrir. I bet Lady Mara is weeping a special tear for you right now.
Don't know why you're asking me. But I'm glad that damn Imperial is gone. Something wrong about him.
I swear that woman Aeri doesn't appreciate all the work I do for her damn mill.
I run the mill, so I'm a busy woman. What is it?
A good lumberjack is quiet, hard-working, and honest.
I used to work at Anga's Mill, but I... I had to leave.
Someone's after me I tell you. They're watching me. Somewhere.
Strangers are always trouble.
I don't need to talk to you. Get lost.
I work at the mill. Chop trees, carry logs, saw lumber, you know.
Aeri's the boss. Should talk to her if you want work.
My cousin's out fighting dragons, and what do I get? Guard duty.
Been so long since I killed a Stormcloak, my sword arm's getting flabby.
I cannot thank you enough for all of the help.
When I grow up, will I go to the College in Winterhold?
It's just a dream, child. Nothing more.
When I'm known throughout Tamriel, you'll regret this decision.
Nah. Just... don't. Leave me outta it.
Yes, I'm sure that's true.
Yes, sir.
What can I getcha, Alva?
Oh, of course! I'm sorry, I didn't mean to say that it's not.
Oh Hroggar, you know I'm always hungry. Don't worry, I'll eat when I need to.
Hush now. Think no more of it.
No. They ain't. And they aren't gonna. If you weren't payin' for your room, I'd have thrown you out a long time ago.
Well dear, you know where to find me if it ever becomes too much for you.
Yes sir, I have.
Oh, look! We have company!
Ho, Gorm.
But...
Jonna, do you think the townspeople are warming to my serenades?
Still the same. I tell you, something's got to be done.
It was scary. It was like it was real!
I'm thinking of composing a song about you, Jonna. Would you prefer that I proclaim your beauty, your strength, your...
I like your shop, Lami.
I was there only for a short time. Much of what I have learned has been on my own. And I will teach you. You have no reason to go there.
Good. Concentration is paramount when dealing with magical forces.
Morthal will be mine to govern someday. I have a great deal to learn.
I had a bad dream again last night.
But I'm learning about magic, and that's what they do there. Shouldn't I go?
When the time is right, friend. When we're sure.
Yes, but they'll come around eventually. You'll see.
Not right now. Anything new?
Buy you a drink?
Oh, I quite like living in the hall. Mother says it's an honor to live there and serve Morthal.
Have you been working on your concentration?
Because an unfocused mind will almost certainly be obliterated. Destroyed by the forces you attempt to control and yield.
Thank you, dear. It must be nice to get out of the hall every now and then.
The longer we wait, the worse it gets.
That is enough.
Oh?
How are you feeling?
No. I mean it.
Oh no! I couldn't. Mother says they are a burden we must bear for our gift.
Oh, yes. All the time. They come with the visions, as always.
Eat? No, I don't think I'm... hungry. Not now, at least.
No, I don't think that will be necessary.
Let's talk about it some other time.
Have no fear. I believe it is within you to excel in your studies. But you must learn to concentrate!
How are the headaches, dear? You or your mother still having them?
Right. Okay, tell me if you change yer mind.
I dreamt that you went away. You made monsters.
I feel wonderful, just like every night.
Umm... Did you want somethin' to eat?
Why?
But you went there, didn't you? Isn't that where you learned?
Do you need anything? Are you hungry?
Are you sure you don't want something for them? I may be able to brew you something to help.
No interest in being immortalized in song? Remembered and praised for all eternity?
What can't you get me, sweetie?
Oh, I shouldn't be surprised if they come 'round. And if you're never seen or heard from agin.
The College is not the only place to learn about magic.
Oh.
Suleyki mulaag, Paarthurnax. My power has waxed, while yours has waned.
Dovahkiin! Use Dragonrend, if you know it!
So, you're Brynjolf's new protege, eh? Don't look like much to me.
It's one thing to say you've got the skills to be a thief. It's another to actually use them.
It's been a long time since anyone joined us. Seems like people are in a rush to leave.
I hear even the simple jobs are becoming difficult. It never used to be like this.
Less coin in everyone's pocket means less coin in mine.
Something has to change soon or I may as well pack this place up and leave it for the skeevers.
You better have coin to pay for your drinks. There's no handouts here.
Got a lot of nerve coming down here. People tend to get hurt in the Ratway.
I wouldn't stay here long if I were you.
There's a reason the city guard doesn't come down here you know.
Don't make trouble or else I'll ask Dirge to "walk" you out.
Even if you're one of us, you better not make trouble.
I don't care if you're best buddies with the Guild Master. I'll still smash in your skull if you try anything.
The last person that made trouble for Vekel ended up floating in the canal... catch my drift?
Stay outta trouble, or there's gonna be trouble.
Last person who made trouble for Vekel ended up with a smashed head.
Vekel doesn't like strangers snooping around the Flagon.
There's two things to do at the Flagon: spend coin and then get out.
If it isn't the best brawler in Skyrim. Good to see you again!
Too lazy to walk, eh? Well, you've found the right place.
If it's horses you want, you'll want to speak to Hofgrir at the stables.
Hofgrir is a great teacher. I've learned so much about horses from him.
There's a lever, right there in the center. Hurry, before more of them show up!
What? The bo... Oh. Oh dear.
I shouldn't have taken them, I know! It was stupid. I was stupid. It won't happen again.
Help me get out of here, and I'll help you find them. Please!
You have to let me out. They're going to do something awful to me!
Thank you, thank you! I promise I'll help. And then I'll go back to the College and I'll beg them to let me back in.
I don't have them anymore. She took them... The Caller. She's the one who put me in here!
Please, let me out of here!
Please, please let me out of here!
Yes, yes! Did Arch-Mage Aren send you? He sent you to rescue me, didn't he?
Lost funt. You are too late, Alduin!
You may have picked up the weapons of my ancient foes, but you are not their equal!
Unslaad hokoron! Never again!
Bahloki nahkip sillesejoor. My belly is full of the souls of your fellow mortals, Dovahkiin.
Die now and await your fate in Sovngarde!
Meyz mul, Dovahkiin. You have become strong.
But I am Al-du-in, Firstborn of Akatosh! Mulaagi zok lot! I cannot be slain here, by you or anyone else!
I'll tell you one thing, if you don't make peace with them soon you may get a walkout on your hands. Then we're both in trouble.
If only Maven would stop raising the price on it maybe I could afford to stock more cases.
Talen my friend, up for some fishing this week?
Oh come now, what's a dragon or two compared to a day full of fishing!

That's not funny, Bolli. People are scared.
I don't know Bolli. It's getting dangerous outside the walls.
We could always look for another brand.
Don't worry, the time will come when we can walk away from here.
They always try to take advantage of me. Give them an inch and they'll take a mile.
I hope so. I can't stand this city anymore.
Maybe they're afraid of you. Maybe it's your breath. How should I know?
Damn it, Talen. We still don't have enough this year to get out of Riften.
That isn't an option. Maven sees me serve a competitor's brand here and we may as well dig our own graves.

Well guess what... now you've inherited their debt. Don't come back to me until you have every last coin.

They're the worst kind. Even the Dark Brotherhood abides by a strict set of rules and tradition. These thieves are just rabble.
What do you think?
Many years ago I lost my blade, "Grimsever," within a Dwemer ruin. I took it as a sign that I was wasting my days in search of wealth.
You and I are alike. We seek challenge and great fortune. But for me, that's where the similarities end.
You see, Riften is my great beast to be slain and my fortune comes from gratitude and trust.
I was adventuring in the depths of the ruins when I was attacked by a massive construct... like nothing I had ever seen.
When the colossus struck, Grimsever was knocked from my grasp and I was wounded badly.
It was only through blind luck that I was able to crawl away from the Dwemer abomination and make my way to the surface.
I must have collapsed, because the next thing I remember is Aerin standing over me tending to my wounds.
It was lost years ago in a Dwemer ruin.
Without it, I feel almost as defenseless as a newborn. I don't think I'll ever be able to find a replacement.
Hardly! In fact, I don't know how I get anything done with all of the distractions I have to put up with.
My wife Hafjorg, well, she's a necessary evil I suppose. Not a lick of talent when it comes to alchemy, but she keeps me fed.
Then there's Ingun Black-Briar. Bright young thing and a worthy apprentice, when she isn't wasting my rare ingredients.
I couldn't ask you to undertake such a trecherous journey, it would be a fool's errand.
But I see that spark in your eye and I know better than to warn you away.
Grimsever rests with the Dwemer ruin of Mzinchaleft. Tread carefully, friend... those same ruins almost took my life.
I see I still have much to learn. Lead on.
Just stay away from me and I'll forget we had this discussion.
Watch yourself around here, it isn't safe.
Yes, yes... goodbye.
Yes, quite a shame. It was a fine weapon.

Corruption, lies and deceit are the order of the day here.
If it wasn't for Aerin, the young man who saved my life, I think I'd already have given up long ago.
Word is Ulfric Stormcloak escaped from the Imperials. War's only going to get worse, now.
They say a dragon attacked Helgen. Sounds like hogwash to me.

Oh, you wanted the attic room? Why didn't you say so the first time?

Next to the door, the room on the left.
Ain't used to strangers here in Riverwood.
You're pretty, I'll give ya that. Just stay away from my husband, Alvor.
My girl likes to work the forge with her papa.
All of that blacksmithing... It's not good for a little girl. Ain't... ladylike.

It don't matter what I grow - my Alvor, he'll eat anything.
Maybe I'll do turnips next. Hmmm... Or maybe radishes?
New to Riverwood? If you're looking for work, go see Hod at the mill.
We're a small village, but we work hard, and take care of our own.
I own the lumber mill, and pay a fair wage. See my husband Hod if you're looking for work.
Only worry we've got is the war reaching this far.
Years past, the lumber was used for houses or furniture. Now, the soldiers just buy it all.
The thought of my wood being used for weapons... Makes me sick.
No use thinkin' of things I can't change. Too much work to be done...
You look like a traveler. Someone that has seen far away places and heard new stories.
I've been trained as a skald, as was my father, and my father's father before him.
You should come hear me play at the Sleeping Giant Inn. For a small sum you can even pick the song.

I'm composing a new song. A ballad, for Camilla Valerius.
Some day Camilla will know of my affections. Some day...
A new face. And a right fair one at that.
A new face. And a right ugly one at that. No offence.
Got business here in Riverwood, eh? There are worse villages.
At least the damned war hasn't gotten here. Yet.
We work hard, try to do right by our families.
Me, I've got my axe, my wife Gerdur and my boy Frodnar. That's all I need.
Woodcutting's my trade. Best log splitter from here to Riften.

You met Camilla yet? Wouldn't mind getting my hands on those trunks. He he he he.
Now you, lass... You've got some beautiful trunks yourself... Hmm?
Well... I'll let you go about your business.
You're new around here, so I'll go easy on ya. But don't get on my bad side.
Get me mad, and I'll prank ya. And not a little. A lot.
Ah, everybody around here's so serious.
My friend Dorthe doesn't like my pranking. "You're being mean, Frodnar!" So what if I am?
Mother wants me to work at the mill. But I ain't nobody's slave.
You gotta have better things to do than talk to me all day...
Greetings, sister Elf. Good to see a familiar face so far from home.
Greetings, brother Elf. Good to see a familiar face so far from home.
Did I see you talking to Sven? Maybe not. Maybe...nevermind. But I would stay away from him if I were you.
There's beauty here unmatched in all of Skyrim, to be sure.
Maybe you've already met Camilla Valerius?
If only Camilla shared my affections. Or even knew about them.
Are you a hunter, by chance? The hills surrounding the village are ripe with game.
I work at the lumber mill, but I escape into the forest whenever possible.
I miss Valenwood, but the wilds here are... serviceable.
I don't like to brag, but I once took down a bear at three hundred yards. In a blizzard.
We got rooms and food. Drink, too. I cook. Ain't much else to tell.
Need a room, talk to Delphine.
I'm just the cook, lad.
I'm just the cook, lass.
I ain't lookin' to make friends.
You like Skeever liver. No? Tough luck.
Food's hot. Never said it was good.
You're that visitor, been pokin' around.
I'm the innkeeper. It's my business to keep track of strangers.
We don't get a lot of travelers here in Riverwood.
Most of my customers are locals, come to relax after a hard day's toil.
So what's your story? Just here to... relax?
You look like you could use an ale.
If you're staying the night, I've got a free room.
Wanderer like you must have quite an appetite. Some food?
You're a strapping young man. Don't be a stranger.
The men here like my attention, and I like theirs. Best know your place.
You're not from around here. That makes two of us.
I came here from the Imperial Province, to work with my brother Lucan.
It got bad back in Cyrodiil. The war with the Thalmor ruined... everything.
I came to Skyrim looking for a better life. So what did I get? Another war.
I just want to find a good husband, and start a family of my own.
The Riverwood Trader is everything you need in a general store.
My sister Camilla or I will be happy to help you.
Maybe you're looking for something... particular?
We carry just about anything you could need...
I know some of it looks like junk, but everything's a treasure in the right light.
Ain't every day we get visitors in Riverwood.
Whatever you need. By Ysmir, if it's simple and strong, I can forge it.
You lookin' for a new blade?
Iron or steel is what you need. Leave that fancy Elven stuff to the Elves.
Iron and steel mostly. I might even have some of your fancy Elven pieces lying around somewhere...
I could forge you a warhammer that would crush a giant's head like a walnut.
You the sneaky type? Got some nice daggers around here somewhere...
Papa says I'm too friendly with strangers, but you seem all right.
My father is Alvor, the blacksmith. I'm his assistant. I mean, apprentice.
Some day I'm going to forge my own sword!
Papa says I'm not ready for weapons. So I make lots of horseshoes and hobnails.
Mama doesn't think I'm girlish enough. But that's just stupid girl talk.
Papa may have wanted a son, but he got me. He's a lucky man.
Ain't nothin' a boy can do that I can't do better.
Nobody believes me, but I tell you, I saw a dragon!
It's my boy Sven you need to listen to. He sings at the inn, most every night.
Sven has the voice of a snow lark. Only good thing he got from his father.
My boy fancies that Camilla Valerius. She'll come around soon enough.
Camilla, I've seen her talking to that Faendal. Filthy elf needs to keep to his own kind...
What happened to my Skyrim? Everywhere you look, cats, lizards and elves. It's sickening.
I raised my Sven to be strong, and proud. A true Nord.
You're kinda fuzzy. What's wrong with ya?
Outta my way, stranger.
I'm spoken for, you know. So don't get any ideas.
The war keeps most folks away, these days.
So I like to have a little fun, so what?
It is time, Yamarz.
Grrr. Fine, let's get this over with.
In a Daedra, of course.
You bring an outsider here, and now insist I call on Malacath for help, when he has clearly forsaken me? You try my patience, Atub.
Doing nothing will not grant our tribe relief from this curse. We must try.
Hmm, yes. But your su'um is strong. I do not doubt that you can convince him of the need.
Lot krongrah. You truly have the Voice of a dovah. Alduin's allies will think twice after this victory.
Ni liivrah hin moro. True, this is not the final krongrah - victory. But not even the heroes of old were able to defeat Alduin in open battle.
Alduin always was pahlok - arrogant in his power. Uznahgar paar. He took domination as his birthright.
This should shake the loyalty of the dov who serve him.
Yes... one of his allies could tell us. Motmahus... But it will not be so easy to... convince one of them to betray him.
Perhaps the hofkahsejun - the palace in Whiterun... Dragonsreach. It was originally built to house a captive dovah.
A fine place to trap one of Alduin's allies, hmm?
Why, why have you done this to us?
I cannot protect you if you murder us. This is the only warning you will receive.
Then I would be glad to help you with your mad dragon-trapping scheme.
But getting both sides to agree to a truce will be difficult at this point. The bitterness has gone too deep.
Maybe... hmm... what of the Greybeards? They are respected by all Nords. High Hrothgar is neutral territory.
If the Greybeards were willing to host a peace council... then maybe Ulfric and Tullius would have to listen.
No. I can't risk weakening the city while we are under the threat of enemy attack. I'm sorry.
I must have misheard you. I thought you asked me to help you trap a dragon in my palace.
Yes. This was ages ago, you understand. There were more of us then. Before the bruniikke - the Akaviri - came and killed all my zeymah.
I used to visit him from time to time. Nearly crazed by loneliness and captivity. Tiiraz sivaas. He did not even remember his own name.
I do not know how he came to be caught. But the bronjun... the Jarl... was very proud of his pet. Paak!
The hofkahsejun has been known as Dragonsreach ever since.
According to legend. Although I never thought to put the tale to the test.
Jarl Olaf One-Eye it was, who later became High King.
They say he Shouted it into submission in single combat atop Mount Anthor and brought it back to Whiterun.
Numinex was the dragon's name. That's his skull decorating the main hall.
What you're asking for is insane. Impossible!
You want me to let a dragon into the heart of my city, with the threat of war on my doorstep?
What is it? Dragons?
Perhaps... Perhaps it might. If nothing else, you could be an excellent sacrifice to Malacath.
You really want to help? You go offer yourself at Malacath's Shrine. Petition him for help.
He might not devour you whole.
Are you blind, or just stupid? You think the tribe has not seen you talking to me? You think they would not know?
Do not mistake our life for one of stupidity. It would be the last mistake you make.
Hrmph. Breaking a curse is not a simple thing. It is dangerous.
But... To have won against Malacath himself... That would be seen as real power.
You persist? Fine. You go tell Malacath to lift this curse from me. See how well he listens to you.
Offer yourself up as a sacrifice while you're there.
I am the one cursed by Malacath. It is my burden. I bear it, and continue to lead, and the others see how strong I am. That is how I rule.
Yes, because a worthless City Orc is just what we've been waiting for. Finally, we're saved!
Don't make a mess of this.
I must focus now. This ritual is very important.
I will stay right here and wait for you to bring me what I need.
Do you bring word from Malacath?
Do whatever Yamarz asks of you. It cannot make things worse.
Speak to Yamarz. Convince him to let you help him.
You should not be here, outsider.
We have angered Malacath, and so we must be punished.
Our tribe has survived this long without you interfering. We'll be fine.
You've got to tell them! We weren't with you! This is a mistake!
Whoa!
Why are we stopping?
Let's go. Shouldn't keep the gods waiting for us.
No! Wait! We're not rebels!
Why do you think? End of the line.
Steady. I don't know what's happening. Let's watch and wait.
Wait. Something's happening... gods above!
You've been in Riften for quite some time now, Lady Mjoll. I fear that you soon may leave us.
Yes, my lady. I will pass the good news on to her.
I'm relieved to hear you say that. If you were to leave, I'd miss you terribly.
This is the last warning. You do it again, and you'll be sleeping in Beggar's Row with the rest of the riff-raff!
Romlyn, I've been meaning to speak to you. I did a count at the meadery and we're four cases short... again.
That s'wit. One day, he'll regret pushing all of us around.
I'm not accusing you of anything. Yet.
Hemming, I need you to bring a note to Sibbi. I need him to do something for me.
All I'm saying is Indaryn's going to push someone too far one day and they're going to push back... hard.
For your sake, I hope you're right. Because if it is you, and Maven finds out, the Dreth family is going to be one member smaller.
I understand. It just isn't me. I'm not a warrior and I don't think I ever will be.
I know that!
I can promise you when he's ready, he'll find the cell door unlocked.
Please, milady, it was only because he threatened me. He said he'd have me thrown in the prisons if I didn't give him what he wanted!
I can't afford to just give my jewelry away!
The Dark Elves and the Wood Elves have never been the best of friends. Be patient, they'll come around soon enough!
We already have people that do that and they make quite a bit of coin for it.
I have a single one left, quite expensive, but also quite elegant.
You need to be easier on Asgeir. All he wants to do is please you.
I tolerate your son because he'll do anything I ask, without question. I'd expect the same from my own son.
Chin up, Ungrien! Remember, you have a roof over your head and food in your belly thanks to the very same Dark Elves. Be thankful.
Anything you say, Mother.
That's right. He is your son. He has his own hopes and dreams and we have no right to tell him otherwise.
I love when you spin that yarn. Gets better every time you add something new.
What would you have me do, woman? Cross the Guild? Take them all on one by one?
Anuriel seems to think that seeing me on the streets might bolster the city's morale.
Laila is as clueless as she is stupid. No. If anything's to be done about this, we have to deal with it ourselves.
Spices? I'm certain I have no idea what you're talking about.
Begging your pardon milady... I know these have something to do with Dibella, but whatever are they for?
Talen! Another round!
What would you have me do, send him to the gallows for treason? He's your brother, Harrald.
Yes mother? What is it?
So, is this what it's come to? You'd have your own flesh and blood hanged just to solidify your grip as the next Jarl?
Madesi, my friend. How have things been since last week's incident?
Lady Mjoll, if you lost a gold ring anywhere in this city I'm afraid it's likely never to be seen again. I'm sorry.
Outrageous! Who does she think she is?!
Was this before or after the Mythic Dawn attacked the Emperor and Valen single-handedly fought them off? Just shut up and drink, Romlyn.
Oh, I'm so sorry. I'm certain Bolli would be happy to lend you a few septims.
Then put the thumbscrews on him and hit him... hard.
Lady Maven, how may I help you?
Here's another septim, Madesi. How much more do I need?
When are you going to stop by the temple, Madesi?
You stupid lizard! I said give me some more drink, or I'll have your head on a pike!
Begging your pardon my lady, but I strongly recommend against it.
Yes, yes, I know. I just wish to show them there's more to life than drowning your sorrows in a goblet of mead.
It fills me with joy to hear you're with child, Dinya. They said it wouldn't... that it couldn't happen, but Mara has shown them they're mistake.
It only takes but a moment to say a silent prayer to Mara. Please come by soon; not for me, but for yourself.

Ah, my disciples have sent me a fresh soul. Good. I was getting... hungry.
Wait. There's something different about you.
Twilight watch over you, guardian.
Are you ready for me to show Azura her Star and receive her wisdom?
While you were in the Star, Azura gave me a vision. Her last, she said.
I have never been without Azura's foresight since escaping Morrowind. I don't know what to do.
If you need me, I'd be honored to accompany you, Guardian of the Star. It would give me a purpose.
Leave this shrine, blasphemer, before I kill you for defiling Azura's artifact.
She wishes to speak to you herself. Please. Place your hands on the altar, and you will hear her voice.
Just glad all that business with Malyn is over with. What did you need?
I don't like doing business with Azura's faithful. Make it quick.
Are you ready to give me the Star?
Azura. Mother of Roses. Goddess of Dusk and Dawn. Your chosen champion has returned your Star to you.
No. She said my part was over. That my fate had moved beyond the Twilight, and I was on my own.
I will tend Azura's shrine when you do not need me. I still have my duties, but for the first time I feel... alone.
And who are you to challenge me? I have conquered mortality itself. I've spat in the eyes of the Daedric Lords.
This is my realm now. I've sacrificed too much to let you take it from me!
Good. I'm ready for anything.
I've seen better. Well, maybe not.
Get ready. Trouble ahead.
You sure? All right. I'll keep guard here.
She finally decided to return it to her father. Bless her heart.
I will make sure this finds its way to his tomb as soon as possible. Thank you for bringing it, my child.
I had to for honor's sake, but I had waited too long. My father died before he could see my training to completion.
When I finally became a priest, they gave me the new high priest's dagger, but I already owned my father's.
So you see, I have to return it. It breaks tradition, and I need to tell him that I finally did what he wanted.
A Priest of Arkay is entrusted with a ceremonial dagger once they've completed their training.
It usually given by the head priest who sanctified the ritual. In my case, the head priest happened to be my father.
The thing is, I never wanted to follow in his footsteps... I some ways I still don't.
Malborn? Ah... very good. I think we understand each other.
Yes, Madame Ambassador.
Well, we'd better get back to our rounds.
Of course. I've told you before not to bother me with such trifles.
Move on, friend. I have nothing to sell today.
Move on, friend. I have nothing to sell today.
Do I have your permission to uncork the Arenthia red...
Ah yes. I remember your name from the guest list.
I was trying to get to Morrowind. The Dunmer don't care much for the Thalmor and I figured I'd be safe there. As safe as anywhere,that is.
But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me.
I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
It's just that we've run out of the Alto wine.
Sure, fine. Thanks for nothing. You already got what you needed from me.
No, you can't ever be too paranoid where the Thalmor is concerned. Look what happened to the Blades. The Blades!
If the damn Blades are no match for the Thalmor, what chance do I have?
Please, just go check it out. You'll be saving my life.
What is it, Malborn?
You will? Really? Well, okay, here's the situation.
He looks so... sweet. Go ahead. Have the first bite.
You'd be saving my life. Again. I know I probably seemed ungrateful... I've just been so scared. Expecting a knife at my throat any minute.
Madame Ambassador, I'm so sorry to interrupt...
Trying to stay alive. I think time's running out though. I'm pretty sure the Thalmor know where I am.
Maybe you can help me. You know, by way of making amends for getting me into this mess.
The Thalmor never forget.
You'll have a traitor's death.
Dir volaan!
Bolog aaz, mal lir!
Faaz! Paak! Dinok!
Kren sosaal!
Qiilaan us dilon!
Sovngarde saraan!
Aav dilon!
Why struggle? The Thalmor will only send more like me.
Have you? All good, I trust. But you have me at a disadvantage. I'm afraid I know nothing about you...
Well, there's an old sewer tunnel that dumps into the lake on the northwest side of the island.
That's how I slipped in there. Should still be unguarded.
Watch yourself out there. The mercenaries Aringoth hired are trained killers. Just ask our poor little Vex.
The honey they collect on that hunk of rock goes into making Black-Briar Mead; just about the best tasting mead in all of Skyrim.
I wouldn't bother smacking at those hives with a weapon. Set 'em on fire and they'll go up like kindling.
I'd like to know why you're wasting time asking about it when you should already be on your way.
Don't worry, I'm mad at her, too. And at myself for ever agreeing to get involved in her insane scheme.
Indeed. That would be best.
Please, tell me more about yourself. What brings you to this... to Skyrim?
Don't worry, my parties are always well-stocked with the finest food and drink available in this country.
But first, you were introducing yourself.
I count my blessings every day.
Here you go, ma'am. The finest Colovian brandy.
Is there anything else I can get for you?
We seem to have run out of Colovian brandy. I'll check in the wine cellar for more when I get a moment.
Here you go, miss. Colovian brandy.
I'm sorry, but I think you've had enough for the moment.
Trying to stay ahead of the Thalmor assassins. I'm not a hero like you. I don't stand a chance.
My apologies. We'll have to get better acquainted later. Please, enjoy yourself.
That's... great news. I'd... I'd better go. Right now. Before they find me again. This is my chance.
Thank you! Here... I stole a lot from the Thalmor over the years. You may as well have some of it.

But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me.
I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
