But there's a Khajiit hanging around the main gate. I didn't like the way he was looking at me.
I'm sure he's Thalmor. Just waiting for me to leave so he can follow me and kill me in a lonely place.
Oh, it's you. I didn't expect to see you again.
I suppose that's the fairest deal we're likely to get.
It seems I have no choice but to let the Dragonborn decide. Although I'm starting to doubt your fairness.
Don't hand me a mug of sheep's piss and call it mead. These terms are still not acceptable.
Come on, Tullius. You have to give a little ground here. The dragons are a threat to us both.
Thank you for your help.  Did you see my family inside?
He's a rogue mage who defected at the beginning of the war.  I turned him in but apparently he escaped and now this.
Stop! That's priceless!
Please, I don't have anything to pay you with! Don't destroy my urn!
If I had it, I'd pay! There's no need to do that!
No! You're going to break it!
I can't pay, I can't!
I can't believe you're doing this! Stop!
Did you hear? The Hall of the Dead is closed. Brother Verulus isn't the most popular man in town right now.
Thanks again for saving my life. I think I'm done with venturing outside of Markarth, though.
Namira watch over you, champion.
Names no longer matter. You may refer to me as The Caller.
Now, do you have a reason for making such a mess?
Oh, now we're all please and thank you, are we?
I'm afraid we're well beyond pleasantries.
I'll allow you the opportunity to turn around, walk out that door, and never come back. I suggest you move quickly.
So you're just one of Aren's lackeys? That's disappointing. You show real promise.
You come here, kill my assistants, disrupt my work... You've annoyed me, so I don't think I'll be giving you anything.
So, you're the one who barged into my home and laid waste to my projects.
How nice to meet you.
I'm afraid you don't have anything worth trading.
Now, you can go back to your College and leave me be, or I can kill you. Your choice.
Brother! Mara's mercy, it's good to see you!
Hod! Come here a minute. I need your help with something.
And who's this? One of your comrades?
Gerdur...
Is there somewhere we can talk? There's no telling when the news from Helgen will reach the Imperials...
Ah. I see.
Well, then I'll just stay behind you a little bit, and follow along quietly.
An arrangement? After you've destroyed so much?
Fine. Leave me the elf. You may go with your books.
How could you just leave me here?
I've got to get out of here.
Please! Help me!
Don't leave me here!
Yes, of course. I said I'd tell you, didn't I?
The Caller will have them. She was most interested in one of the volumes.
Although not interested enough to keep me from being locked up.
But is it safe for you to be here? We heard that Ulfric had been captured...
Ralof! What are you doing here? Ah... I'll be right down.
Gerdur!
Not a comrade yet, but a friend. I owe him my life, in fact.
Not a comrade yet, but a friend. I owe her my life, in fact.
Looks like nobody here knows what's happened yet. Come on. Gerdur's probably working in her lumber mill.
You're right. Follow me.
Gerdur, I'm fine. At least now I am.
No! Don't pity me. I deserve whatever fate the Dread Lord has in store. I betrayed you... and now Maro has betrayed me. Fitting...
I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong.
But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this.
Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
Don't you see? It was me. I set you up, wanted you dead. I betrayed you, the Night Mother... everything I hold dear. And now Maro has betrayed me.
I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong.
But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this.
Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
I'm saying you were right. The Night Mother was right. The old ways... they guided the Dark Brotherhood for centuries. I was a fool to oppose them.
And to prove my... sincerity, I have prayed for a contract. You lead this Family now. I give you the Blade of Woe, so that you can see it through.
You must kill...
Me.
Alive.... You're alive... Thank Sithis...
Yes. I set you up. I wanted you dead. I betrayed you, the Night Mother, and everything I hold dear. And now Maro has betrayed me.
I just wanted things... to stay the way they were. Before Cicero, before the Night Mother. Before... you. I thought I could save us. I was wrong.
But you're alive! So there's still a chance. A chance to start over, rebuild. That's why I did... this.
Don't you see? I prayed to the Night Mother! I am the Black Sacrament.
Ssshhh.... Please. There is much... I have to say. And... not much time...
I'm sorry. So very sorry. The Penitus Oculatus... Maro... He said that by giving you to them, he would leave the Dark Brotherhood alone. Forever.
By Sithis, I was such a fool. All of this... it's all my fault. You are the best of us, and I nearly killed you... as I've killed everyone else...
Are you hurt? What's happened?
The number of graves in this cemetery never decreases.
War is not new to Falkreath. You have only to stand in our cemetery to see that.
Dengeir deserves more respect than his nephew, Jarl Siddgeir, affords him.
Without birth there is no life, without death there is only stasis. Such is the truth of Arkay.
Are you all right? Can you walk?
I need you on your feet. We're in trouble here.
Ancano! Stop this at once! I command you!
I don't care what it is, I want it down now! I want to know what he's doing in there!
Excellent. I'd suggest that we go retrieve it immediately, but right now we have more pressing matters.
Ancano has somehow locked himself in the hall. He's up to something, and I intend to put a stop to it.
Help us get through this, will you? We're throwing everything we have at it.
Ancano is doing something with that thing... the Eye. We can't stop him!
I haven't seen Savos since the explosion. He must've been blown clear, and he may be injured.
I need you to find the Arch-Mage, and I need you to do it quickly. Get moving!
Don't go near him!
What's going on?!
Ancano. He's in there, doing... something. We don't know what. We're trying to get in now.
I will have his head for this, I assure you.
Help take this down, will you? We're throwing everything we can at it.
Then you are weak and inconsequential.
You are nothing.
She is here, among us.
Ahhhhgh. Wearing flesh is so... distasteful.
You have caught my attention, mortal. That is most unwise.
Shhh! Be still. Beware her anger!
Our Lord! She has come!
My favor?
My good pleasure is not some trophy for mortal sport!
I should slay you for such insolence!
Were you certain in your purpose, you would have no need to apologize.
You have much to learn... But you have proven your will is strong and your heart is devoid of pity.
So, perhaps you are not entirely useless... We shall soon find out.
The Lord of Plots, Deceiver of Nations, Devourer of Trinimac. The Queen of Shadows, Goddess of Destruction, He-Who-Destroys and She-Who-Erases...
Many are our lord's lofty names, but they befit only us mortals. Intone her mighty names from now until the end of time - it is for naught.
Names mean nothing to our Lord. She only cares for those who care for themselves, whose hearts are full of purpose, whose lives are full of deeds.
(Gasps)
We are forged by Boethiah's example. We carve our will upon the world through our tongues and our blades.
You? You are nothing. An empty vapor, dispersed by the slightest breeze.
A well placed word, or a well placed dagger. Both can achieve equal purposes.
Though we live in shadow, we carve indelible signs of our passing into the world.
We are Lord Boethiah's faithful. Have you come to test your mettle?
Good. Perhaps I've misjudged.
Hear me!
If you desire a glimpse of our Dark Mistress, then this is what you must do.
First, prove your tongue can wield a lie. Find someone. Gain his trust. Lead him to the shrine above.
Next, instruct your thrall to touch the Pillar of Sacrifice. Its magic will ensnare him, rendering him helpless.
Then, girded with certitude, plant the ceremonial blade deeply, so that the waters of his heart wash over you.
If your will is strong enough, it will stir Boethiah in her Dark Mansion, and she will appear before us.
That remains to be seen. But if it is so, and if Boethiah appears, then we will welcome you as one of us.
Don't leave me here!
I, uhh... wait, what?
He gets the books, I get you. It's very simple.
Do we have a deal?
You wouldn't do that, would you? You wouldn't leave me here with her!
Fine. Take your books and leave at once.
If you ever come back, I won't be so gracious.
I'm waiting.
I can promise you if it comes to that, and my back's to the wall, I won't hesitate.
She's the mistress of night and darkness and the patron of every thief in Tamriel.
Again, you have to think differently. Haven't you ever noticed how our luck behaves?
Like a novice picking an impossible lock or a blind man suddenly turning to face you as you reach for his pocket?
It's through these subtle means that Nocturnal influences us.
Nocturnal isn't one for worship and reverence. There are no priests and no sermons, no services and no alms.
She influences our luck and in return demands payment.
You're closer to understanding than you realize.
The only difference is she doesn't demand payment in the traditional sense and sometimes the cost can be quite high.
Whether you know it or not, Nocturnal dictates how well we perform as rogues.
Nocturnal's whim is the greatest mystery to everyone. There have been volumes written on the subject.
Does she exact payment when we die? When we suffer does she revel in our misery? No one knows.
The return certainly seems worth the risk though.
He was a scholar, a master thief and a natural leader. Everyone respected him and followed him without question.
It was Gallus who inducted me into the Nightingales and honed my skills to a razor sharp point. I owe everything to him.
We were... very close.
Gallus once said he felt comfortable around me; able to let his guard down. I can't help but think that I'm responsible for what happened to him.
Mercer lied to the Guild, branded me a murderer and slandered my name across his network of contacts.
For twenty-five years I ran, never sleeping in the same place twice and carefully covering my tracks.
Mercer doesn't need to die... he needs to feel the cold sting of fate as his life crumbles in front of him and he's hunted by the Guild.
Strangers like you bring war. I've seen enough war.
Show respect while you're here and you and I will be fine.
You have the look of a killer.
Arkay helps us find peace with death.
Indara and I like to keep to ourselves and run our farm.
My wife, Indara, has a gift with animals.  It makes her a better farmer than anyone I've ever seen.
This war isn't needed. Imperials like my wife can be hard to handle but we Nords could work with them. I should know.
Runil has been a true friend to my wife and I in our time of need.
I don't know if the war is Arkay's will or man's, but it's none of my doings.
My husband Mathias and I tend Corpselight Farm.
Falkreath is a hard place and tending a farm here is thankless work.
Mathies is a kind and wise man. The Nords could use more like him. So could the Imperials.
How could people see all the graves in Falkreath and still want to make war?
Are you attempting to threaten me? After I've been so hospitable?
Well, then you won't be leaving here at all.
Zu'u ni tahrodiis. It was you that lured me here and took me prisoner... vobalaan grahmindol. I have done nothing to earn your distrust.
Hin aar, orin nu. And yet here I am, still your prisoner.
Indeed. Orin brit ro. I cannot leave here until you defeat Alduin, which you cannot do without my help.
Ahraan. You wound me, Dovahkiin. I may not tell the whole truth, but I am no liar.
Go and see for yourself. Zu'u ni bo nol het. I will be here... unless Alduin returns before you do.
You have reconsidered my offer, hmm? Onikaan kron?
Hey, when you make up your mind, just let me know. I'll be here.
Saraan uth - I await your command, as promised. Are you ready to see the world as only a dovah can?
Your funeral. Someone else is gonna have to help you get him back in there again.
Zok brit uth! I warn you, once you've flown the skies of Keizaal, your envy of the dov will only increase.
Only this. You have the Thu'um of a dovah, but without the wings of one, you will never set foot in Skuldafn.
Of course... I could fly you there. But not while imprisoned like this.
Ah. Well. Hmm... krosis. There is one... detail about Skuldafn I neglected to mention.
This seems like a really bad idea to me.
Onikaan koraav gein miraad. It is wise to recognize when you only have one choice.
And you can trust me. Zu'u ni tahrodiis. Alduin has proven himself unworthy to rule. I go my own way now.
Free me, and I will carry you to Skuldafn.
You sure about that? You want to let that dragon loose after all the trouble to catch him in there?
Kreh zini! The freedom of the sky beckons! Yet I stay here, nau gol, as promised.
Steel's good, but loyalty's better. I'm loyal to Dengeir first and the Empire second.
The war bleeds Skyrim. It bleeds her dry and I think none of us are the better for it.
Someone new, perhaps someone looking for me to mix something up for them?
If either these Nords or Imperials had some red in their blood this war would be over.
Falkreath's warriors always return, one way or another.
A hearty welcome to Falkreath!  All of Skyrim's true sons are welcome here!
Stay out of my way, boot.
I can't believe we let provincials like you wander Skyrim.
You'll find my brother Solaf and I are the only true Nords in Falkreath.
Well met. Unlike my brother, I've no dislike of strangers. Met lots of 'em while I was a Stormcloak.
Don't mind Bolund. He's young and gets riled easily.
Listen. All of you!
I have little need for mindless thralls.
My servants serve their own purposes. But, if their will is insufficient, nor suitably aligned with mine, I simply destroy them.
Which brings me to my present concern.
You have earned the right to call yourself my Champion.
And now you must perform the task for which I created you. You have already proven to possess the needed skills.
Go to Solitude. Stalk the Jarl and slay her.
I have spread rumors of an assassin. It will make your job harder, but will allow me to place blame where I desire it.
You will need stealth and lies to get past her guards. You will assassinate her swiftly and quietly. And if combat finds you, survive the escape.
Succeed, and you may keep your title and my armor, and I shall write your name upon the Tablet of Absolute Blackness.
Failure is not an option.
Well done. You have proven the strength of your will and your tongue's gift for lies. You have shown ferocity and prowess in combat.
Now the time has come for a final proving. Are you able to cast aside your honor and strike with the hidden blade?
I am loath to utter his name... one cannot erase a thing if it has a name to be remembered.
You have done well, my Champion. You have earned my respect, a feat few manage and live to tell about it.
Now go. I have strings to pull that require my full attention. You may pursue your own course wherever it leads you.
Remember always this: You need answer to no authority, neither mortal, nor divine... As you will it, so it shall be.
We better get moving. Come here. Let me see if I can get those bindings off.
We'll meet again in Sovngarde, brother.
Here we are, found a key. Let's see if it opens that door.
This one's locked. Let's see about that gate.
There you go. You may as well take Gunjar's gear. He won't be needing it any more.
That's it! Come on, let's get out of here before the dragon brings the whole tower down on our heads.
Maybe one of these Imperials had the key.
Looks like we're the only ones who made it.
I'm going to see if I can find some way out of here.
Damn. No way to open this from our side.
Looking for something?
What do you need, stranger?
Shor's bones! A handsome man in Falkreath.
You're going to have the men around here wrapped around your finger in no time.
Anything I can get you?
Can I get you a drink?
Welcome to Dead Man's Drink.
I wouldn't complain if we could string together a few sunny days now and then.
I escaped fighting in Cyrodiil only to have it track me down again in Skyrim.
Try not to judge Falkreath too harshly, visitor - the war has been hard on us.
I'm sorry, I should really go now.
I can't really help you, I'm just Dengeir's maid.
I was Jarl once, but I was encouraged to step down when I took sides with the Stormcloaks. It was an imperial plot, I tell you!
If you're sympathetic to the Empire, you'll be wise to put Falkreath far behind you.
Stendarr protect Falkreath until Skyrim's Sons rise.
Enemies and ears. Both are everywhere.
The war has changed Falkreath, even our neighbors can be our enemies.
I protect the Jarl, whoever that might be.
Falkreath's cemetery reminds of us that war is not new here.
Being Housecarl of Falkreath is an honor.
Treat the Jarl with respect, and you'll be welcome here.
I'm the Steward here.  I serve under Jarl Siddgeir and Jarl Dengeir before him.
Siddgeir can sometimes act too quickly but he does listen to Helvard and I.
For Falkreath and I this is just one more war in a long line.
Yes? What is it that you want?
Boland speaks much about the war and the Stormcloaks but he knows who provides.
The Stormcloaks want freedom but don't understand its price.
There are opportunities everywhere.  Why don't you go find them.
No time, I have business to conduct.
Maven Black-Briar's gone out of her way to try and have me shut down, but fortunately I have a good relationship with Whiterun's Jarl.
Every year, I sell more Honningbrew and Maven loses customers. Know why? My mead tastes better.
Faith? I don't have faith in anyone.
All I care about is cause and effect. Did the job get done and was it done correctly. There's no gray area.
Exactly. With Sabjorn in prison, his meadery will be forced to close.
Then I swoop in and take over the place. No more competition.
This is exactly what I'm talking about.
Once again Brynjolf sends me someone with no backbone, no determination.
I bought some poison. I was going to have my lazy, good-for-nothing assistant Mallus handle it, but he seems to have vanished.
If you plant this in the vermin's nest, it should stop them from ever coming back.
Word on the street is that poor Sabjorn has found himself in Whiterun's prison. How unfortunate for him.
Not a day goes by that I don't regret letting Sabjorn get as far as he did.
In only a few short years, he's taken that bile he calls mead to market and a chunk of my profits with it!
I can't imagine where he found the gold to take it to market so quickly.
So, you're the one. Hmm. You don't look so impressive.
The only reason we're having this conversation is due to Brynjolf's assurance you won't botch another assignment.
He claims you possess some sort of uncanny aptitude for your line of work. Quite frankly I find that hard to believe.
I hope not. This is an important job.
I have a competitor called Honningbrew Meadery that I want to put out of business.
I also want to know how they managed to get the place up and running so quickly.
Is that confidence I hear... or is it arrogance? Strange how often they're confused.
You have to understand, it's been a long time since Brynjolf's sent me anyone I can rely on.
But a single candle can banish the darkness of the entire Void. If not you, then someone else.
My beacon is sure to attract a worthy soul.
But if you are wise, you will heed my bidding.
It is time for my splendor to return to Skyrim.
But the token of my truth lies buried in the ruins of my once great temple, now tainted by a profane darkness skittering within.
Can you not hear me? Are your ears full of hate?
The defiler waxes!
Go now, with haste. Take my beacon to Mount Kilkreath.
A new hand touches the beacon.
It matters not. The plant cares nothing for the rays that bring it the warmth of the sun. As you carry Dawnbreaker, so will my light touch the world.
Of course you will. I have commanded it!
The Necromancer Malkoran defiles my shrine with vile corruptions, trapping lost souls left in the wake of this war to do his bidding.
Worse still, he uses the power stored within my own token to fuel his foul deeds.
I have brought you here, mortal, to be my champion. You will enter my temple, retrieve my artifact, and destroy the defiler.
Guide my light through the temple to open the inner sanctum and destroy the defiler.
Malkoran is vanquished. Skyrim's dead shall remain at rest. This is as it should be. This is because of you.
A new day is dawning. And you shall be its herald.
Take the mighty Dawnbreaker and with it purge corruption from the dark corners of the world. Wield it in my name, that my influence may grow.
May the light of certitude guide your efforts.
Malkoran has forced the doors shut. But this is my temple, and it responds to my decree.
I will send down a ray of light. Guide this light through my temple and its doors will open.
Go now, the artifact must be reclaimed and Malkoran destroyed.
What is it? I said I didn't wish to... be disturbed.
I think perhaps it might be best if you lead the way. My eyes aren't what they used to be.
Don't worry, I'll stay right with you.
This is all very strange. I've never seen anything like this.
You mess with the Silver-Blood family, you end up here.
I catch you stealing anything, I'll break your neck.
Now this is real guard duty. Just throw them in the mine and be done with it.
The World-Eater waits within the mist! Bring word to Shor's hall of our hard fate!
Turn back, traveler! Terror waits within this mist.
Many have braved the shadowed vale but vain is all courage against the peril that guards the way.
I'll try to hold to your hopeful purpose. Quickly, before this encompassing fog once more snares me in the World-Eater's net.
Near Giants' Gap, in the gloom before dawn, we marched, unsuspecting into the Imperials' trap.
Then we stood and fought, our shield-wall defending until by dawn's light the Legion's ranks wavered.
But I never knew if nights-end brought victory - a swift-flying arrow to Sovngarde carried me.
I do not know - but none have passed through.
Alduin, his hunger insatiable, hunts the lost souls snared within this shadowed valley.
Can you lead the way to where Shor's hall waits, beckoning us on to welcome long sought?
I saw it fair when first I trod this long-sought path. The pain and fear vanished, dreamlike, and a vision beckoned -
Shor's hall, shimmering across the clouded vale. But quenched was hope by the shrouding mist - my mind is darkened.
I've lost the way and wander blindly.
Hurry! Before Alduin your life devours bring word to Shor's hall of our hard fate!
I want to be an adventurer like you, but my father says I can't.
He says that he needs me to stay here and work the farm, and even if he did let me be an adventurer, we couldn't afford to buy armor.
His name's Mralki, and he's the innkeeper here. I hope you can change his mind.
I'll take your challenge, but if I win I get to start a new life as an adventurer, and with no objections from you.
Thank you. I can't stand the thought of being trapped in this village for the rest of my days.
Strong, yes, but how clever are you? I'll tell you what, if you can open the lock on my chest, you can have everything in it.
Some folk here will tell you I'm sheltering my boy too much, but I ask you, isn't it a father's duty to keep his children safe?
I was a different man, once -- strong, commanding, ah, and young. But the Great War was a long time ago.
You look like you've seen your share of adventure. I envy you that. In fact, maybe you can help me with something. Will you hear me out?
I'll make the preparations immediately!
Hmmm. I don't think you've got enough to cover my expenses with Silver-Bloods, much less convince me give my words away.
This is insane! I didn't steal anything! I never saw this ring before in my entire life!
All right, Brand-Shei. Turn out your pockets, we know you have it.
I'm telling you, I don't... wait, what's this ring? This isn't mine!
We can do this one of two ways. You can walk with me up to the keep, or I can drag your lifeless body. Your choice.
Have what? What in blazes are you talking about?
But... I... very well.
That's right. It isn't yours. You're under arrest, Brand-Shei.
Don't play stupid. I said turn out your pockets... now!
Wait a second. Looks like there's something in this cage.
Come on!
A storeroom. See if you can find any potions. We'll need them.
No, I haven't seen him since the dragon showed up.
Look out!
Hear that?
Damn, that dragon doesn't give up easy.
Was Jarl Ulfric with you?
Shor's bones. We were too late. Damn the Empire.
Don't you know? What drew you here? Surely your dreams showed you the way?
The Hall of Valor, where heroes wait to follow Shor to the final battle.
Astrid is dead. It is as it should be. May she find redemption in the Void.
But while you live, the Dark Brotherhood lives. We must fulfill our contract. Emperor Titus Mede II must be eliminated.
Speak with Amaund Motierre at the Bannered Mare in Whiterun. He will know the true Emperor's location.
But first, inform Nazir of your plans. For you are the Listener, and must bind this Family together.
I hope your stay in Markarth treats you better.
Underneath the College. It's not the nicest place, so if you go down there, please do be careful.
The what of what? No. No idea.
If it's important, maybe Tolfdir knows. He seems to know a lot.
Very well. It's not something often discussed, as it might be... misunderstood by the locals.
The Augur is down in the Midden, below the College. I don't recommend going down there unless it's absolutely necessary.
What brings you, wayfarer grim, to wander here, in Sovngarde, souls-end, Shor's gift to honored dead?
Ah! It's been too long since last I faced a doom-driven hero of the dragon blood.
Do not mistake the night-shrouded thief's stealthily-taken spoils, stolen and unearned, for a warrior's plunder, won in honorable battle.
Your doom already binds you to your dark mistress, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
You trespass here, shadow-walker. Shor does not know you.
Perhaps before the end you will earn the right to pass this way.
Welcome I do not offer, but your errand I will not hinder, if my wrath you can withstand.
I welcome the chance to challenge the blade of Ysgramor's heir, honored shield-brother to Kodlak Whitemane, whom I've watched for in vain.
Well met, mage of Skyrim.
The Nords may have forgotten their forefathers' respect for the Clever Craft, but your comrades throng this hall.
Here in Shor's house we honor it still.
There is no help for you here.
There is no solace in knowing what is to come.
Your perseverance will only lead you to disappointment.
Still you persist? Very well, you may enter.
Never done an honest day's work in your life for all that coin you're carrying, eh lad?
Running a little light in the pockets, lass?
Your pockets... they're a little low on coin. I can tell.
Indeed. And so you have come looking, though you do not know why.
Like others before you, you blindly follow a path to your own destruction.
The Thalmor came seeking answers as well, unaware they will be his undoing.
Your path now follows his, though you will arrive too late.
You seek that which all who wield magic seek. Knowledge.
You shall find this: Knowledge will corrupt. It will destroy. It will consume.
You seek meaning, shelter in Knowledge. You will not find it.
The Thalmor sought the same thing, and it shall lead to his end as it has so many others.
You will hear me out, then? Good.
You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition.
So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion.
This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery.
Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
Well, I don't know.
It's in the Solitude Inlet - that's the channel just north of the docks.
I suppose you should go to the docks and... swim?
Oh, you can save your sinister bravado. I'll not go to my grave whimpering like an infant.
I know why you're here. I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Tsk. How unfortunate. I had hoped you would honor an old man's dying wish. Well, let's be done with it then.
Thank you.
Now, on to the business at hand I suppose, hmm?
Hmph. Pity. I had hoped to obtain even a modicum of justice.
Well, on to the business at hand then, hmm?
By the gods. You... you're alive! But I had heard... your Sanctuary...
Please! You mustn't think I had anything to do with that! I wanted the Emperor dead! The true Emperor! I still do! It was Maro! He...
You mean, after all that's transpired, the Dark Brotherhood will still... honor the contract?
Why, this is astounding news! Wonderful news!
The Emperor is still in Skyrim, but not for long. He's onboard his ship, the Katariah, moored offshore in the Solitude Inlet. But you must hurry!
If you can get onboard that ship. Kill Titus Mede II, as contracted... I will reveal the location of the dead drop that holds your payment.
Well? I won't fight you, so we may as well get this over with.
I thank you for your courtesy.
You will kill me, and I have accepted that fate. But regardless of your path through life, I sense in you a certain... ambition.
So I ask of you a favor. An old man's dying wish. While there are many who would see me dead, there is one who set the machine in motion.
This person, whomever he or she may be, must be punished for their treachery.
Once you have been rewarded for my assassination, I want you to kill the very person who ordered it. Would you do me this kindness?
You and I have a date with destiny, it would seem. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm?
Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Well. Perhaps you'll at least consider it, hmm?
Now, on to the business at hand...
But of course. You and I have a date with destiny. But so it is with assassins and emperors, hmm?
Yes, I must die. And you must deliver the blow. It is simply the way it is.
But I wonder... would you suffer an old man a few more words before the deed is done?
Come now, don't be shy. You haven't come this far just to stand there gawking.
All right. Had I known the deal would bring me this much trouble, I never would have accepted the gold.
Ha! Well, I certainly do feel that way.
Now, if, um... you'll get going?
I know! I know! I received the news not moments ago! Ha ha! This is glorious!
My friend, you may not realize it, but you have served the Empire, indeed all of Tamriel, in ways you cannot possibly imagine.
Ah, but you care little for politics, am I right? You want money! And money you shall have!
Your payment waits for you at a dead drop. It is inside an urn, in the very chamber where we first met, in Volunruud.
Now please, go. Collect your money, and let us never look upon one another again. Our business, thank the gods, is concluded.
And what might that be?
You assassins and your riddles. It really does get very annoying, you know. Now if you'll excuse me...
Aha! You're back!
Your payment awaits. And don't worry, I'm not stupid enough to betray you.
Yes? No? Nothing?
Very well, then. If you'll just... get going now...
Surely you're joking?
This is the true Emperor, not some half-Septim lookalike. He'll be surrounded by elite bodyguards, I'm sure. You'll have your work cut out for you.
Still... everyone assumes the Dark Brotherhood has been completely destroyed. They may be a bit more lax than usual.
Look, that's all I know. I never promised you I'd have all the answers.
Now, since our transaction is done, I'll be on my way.
I was approached by a woman who wanted me to act as the broker for something big.
She flashed a bag of gold in my face and said all I had to do was pay Aringoth for the estate.
I brought him the coin and walked away with her copy of the deed.
Not at all. I tend not to ask too many questions when I'm on the job. I'm sure you understand.
However, I did notice she was quite angry and it was being directed at Mercer Frey.
In this business we rarely deal in names; our identity comes from how much coin we carry.
It is done. Emperor Titus Mede II lies dead, and the Dark Brotherhood yet lives.
Your time runs short.
Welcome to the Midden.
Oh, no. No, that won't do at all.
Not my problem. Not even a little. Take it to Tolfdir, he's supposed to be looking after you lot.
That's nothing you need to concern yourself with.
Huh? That sounds ominous. Are you getting yourself into even more trouble?
Did you ask Mirabelle? She runs things around here, after all.
Clear Skies - combine our Shouts!
Shor's favor has found you, Dragonborn. The Hall of Valor, heart's-ease, awaits.
We cannot fight the foe in this mist!
You fought well. I find you worthy.
It is long since one of the living has entered here. May Shor's favor follow you and your errand.
It's a little gift from the dwarves. Who knew they would end up helping our cause?
We have to move. We'll talk more once we see the sky.
I say you've earned an early pardon. Let's go.
Into the tunnel. Let's go.
My brothers, we have been here long enough. It's time to leave Cidhna Mine and continue our fight against the Nords.
Through this gate, just beside my quarters, is a tunnel. A tunnel that leads right through the old Dwarven ruins of Markarth, into the city.
Well, what do you say, my brothers?
Get moving.
Good. I can always count on you, can't I?
I can always count on you, can't I?
This will require stealth and discretion. The Jarl's guards won't take kindly to anyone rummaging through her Steward's private quarters.
You'll need to be careful with this one. The Jarl's men won't look kindly on you rummaging through the steward's quarters.
You're headed to Markarth.
Get over to Markarth.
You're headed to Morthal.
Get over to Morthal.
You're headed to Whiterun.
Get over to Whiterun.
You're headed to Falkreath.
Get over to Falkreath.
You're headed to Dawnstar.
Get over to Dawnstar.
You're headed to Winterhold.
Get over to Winterhold.
You're going to Riften.
Get over to Riften.
You're headed to the capital.
Get over to the capital.
First on my list, is a problem with our supply caravans being constantly harassed by a giant. Need to deal with that hairy brute. And soon.
The last few supply caravans have been attacked... by a giant. As if I didn't have enough to deal with. Need that damn hairy brute straightened out.
And then there's a tiny problem with a giant that's been raiding our supply caravans... You heard me right. A giant. Need to put a stop to that.
And then there's a tiny problem with a giant attacking our supply caravans. A giant! As if I didn't have enough to deal with.
I can't believe we're finally getting out.
No turning back now.
Get going.
Come on, move it. We have Nords to kill.
Ha! I knew it, you rascal! By Sithis, the old man was worth the trouble.
Well? What word of the Emperor?
Ha! Clearly, we should have killed that old bastard years ago!
Truly?
Could you have brought us more wondrous news? Recent events notwithstanding, this is a happy day for us, my friend.
Despite your misfortunes, you stayed true to the Dark Brotherhood. You've saved us all, and for this you have my eternal thanks.
Now, of course I must ask... Killing the Emperor... How much did Motierre pay for such a thing?
Ha! Remarkable! Well, the old bastard certainly made it worth your while, didn't he?
Oh. Is that all? Really?
Come now! You're joking with old Nazir! Surely the Emperor's life was worth more than that.
Pfft. You'd think an emperor would fetch at least twice that. Damn that motherless cheapskate, Motierre.
In any event, here's my advice. Go to Riften's Ratway, find Delvin Mallory. I believe Astrid had you visit him once before, no?
Mallory is an expert "obtainer of goods." We can use the money to repair and refit this Sanctuary. Make a true home for us, eh?
Watch it!
Oh.
I'll be fine. I just need a minute to catch my breath. Find Savos...
What if I told you about a large shipment of gold and weapons?
So. You're an Imperial spy... Interesting...
I have no strong love for the Stormcloaks but I can't say I'm all that fond of the Empire, either.
This war of yours makes it difficult for a resourceful woman to advance her interests.
Perhaps we can find a way for both of us to prosper from this unfortunate discovery?
So. You're one of Ulfric's spies...
I can't deny the man is right about a few things...
But I've seen first hand what Ulfric is capable of, given the chance. Suffice it to say, he is no friend to Markarth, and no friend of mine.
My first and only loyalties are to my nephew, and to this city.
Good work. I'll send some men with a wagon to collect the prize. We can use the weapons here, and I'll pass on the coin to Solitude.
Well done! I'll send men with a wagon to collect our prize. We'll put the weapons to use here, and I'll send the coin back to Windhelm.
Good job! I knew I gave the mission to the right person.
Good job! Ulfric was right about you. I'm glad to have you with us!
I suppose you're here to extort something from me? Is that it?
Well, what is it you want?
Stand fast! His strength is failing! Once more, and his might will be broken!
Ven Mul Riik!
Again, together! Alduin is strong!
Again, as one! The World-Eater fears us!
We can shatter his power if we Shout together!
One more time, and the World-Eater must face us!
His power crumbles - do not pause for breath!
Again, and we break his grim hold over Shor's realm, and redeem it for eternity!
Again!
Does his strength have no end? Is our struggle in vain?
We must clear this confounding mist.
Alduin will hide in this lightless mist until we Shout together, and shatter his power.
We must Shout together these shadows to dispel.
We're ready, Dragonborn, our wrath to unleash.
By the gods...
Dragon!
What's going on? What happened in there?
It 'may be' in danger? Take a look. I don't think there's much question.
Let's get down there. We have to do something.
We'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
I'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
Look, in case you hadn't noticed, these aren't the glory days of the Guild.
We've lost more clients in the last decade than I'd care to count, but Maven's stuck with us through throughout the entire ordeal.
I'll be damned if I'm going to lose her to... well, to whatever they are.
If you were to bring me the deed to Goldenglow Estate, so I was the full owner, that would be a step in the right direction.
Goldenglow Estate provided a huge payout for us before Aringoth locked us out.
Maven looked the other way as long as the honey kept flowing and we kept him in line.
Since I know losing the farm's payments will harm the Guild, I intend to make a substantial contribution for compensation.
I take care of my friends.
Let's not kid ourselves. Black-Briar Mead is swill. The only thing keeping it successful is Maven.
Word on the street was that Honningbrew tasted better, cost less and Sabjorn didn't shove it down everyone's throat.
If Sabjorn ever managed to push Maven off her pedestal, we'd all be in trouble.
You're going to have to buy him off; it's the only way to get his attention.
If that fails, follow him and see what he's up to. If I know Gulum-Ei, he's in way over his head and you'll be able to use it as leverage.
It would seem our adversary is attempting to take us apart indirectly by angering Maven Black-Briar. Very clever.
Yes, we spoke at length while you were in Whiterun dealing with our meadery situation.
I hope for all your sakes you find out who's behind this supposed "plot."
Having Goldenglow kept in line by the Thieves Guild has kept the honey flowing with minimal cost.
Now, mysteriously, some sort of anonymous adversary shows up and takes the Estate out of my hands.
When you've been doing business like I have for decades, you quickly discover that the only person you can trust is yourself.
Everyone, inside. Now!
Must be my imagination.
Damn ears... playing tricks on me again. Can't stand this sea air!
You're trailing me? I knew I heard something.
Ha! I knew it was you all along.
Following me, huh? You're not very good at this are you?
You may as well give up, I see you there.
Who's there?
Is someone following me?
Hey... is someone watching me?
I swear, if I'm being followed...
I swear I feel like I'm being watched.
If this keeps up, I'm headed back to the Winking Skeever.
I don't have time to chase shadows, I've got better things to do.
She got sick, last winter, when the snows came. And she just... she never got better. Not all year. One night she fell asleep and...  never woke up.
So now I'm all alone. And the Jarl said I had to go to Honorhall Orphanage. It's not fair!
Folk been hearing some strange stuff coming from the Aretino place. Chanting. Talk is, little Aventus is trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood...
A little boy, Aventus Aretino, got sent to the orphanage. Now there's talk he's trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. No good can come of that...
I was walking by the old Aretino Residence, heard the little boy, Aventus? He was chanting. It was the Black Sacrament, I tell ya.
Hear about the little Imperial boy, Aventus Aretino? The stupid whelp is trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood...
I've never been more sure about anything in my entire life. Someone like Grelod doesn't deserve to live one more day. She's a monster.
Your corpse will feed my spiders for weeks. Now die, you fool!
What? Who are you? How did you get this far?
It's really dead, then?
I never would have believed it, if I hadn't seen it with my own eyes...
It may be dead now, but... where did it come from?
In all my years, I've never seen such a thing...
Something's happening...
What manner of magic is this?
Look out! Don't get too close!
It's like the ancient legends. Dragonborn...
It can't be... You... You took its very soul...
By the gods... I don't even know what to say...
Please hurry. To be honest, I'm kind of lonely here. As much as I hated getting sent to Honorhall, I really miss my friends there...
Have you heard about Aventus Aretino? He's a little boy, lives in Windhelm. They say he's been trying to get in touch with the Dark Brotherhood...
Been talk of a little boy in Windhelm, name of Aventus Aretino. Get this - he's been trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. Foolish lad...
I heard tell of a lad in Windhelm. Aventus Aretino. They say he's been doing the Black Sacrament, trying to contact the Dark Brotherhood. Damn fool.
Goodbye, assassin.
Which one was the rat?
Arcturus took some men deeper in, wanted to be sure. We should maybe... assist them.
Suppose not. But what's taking the others so long? The sooner we get out of here, the better. Smoke's getting bad. This place'll be raging soon.
Dunno. One of these corpses. Does it matter?
You assist all you like. And get roasted for your trouble. I'll guard the exit.
First, I need to get a handle on rebel operations. Need to discretely liberate some documents from their headquarters.
First, I need to know what the Imperials are planning. Need to slip into their headquarters, grab plans, maps, something useful like that.
I also would like to get a handle on what the rebels are planning - slip into their headquarters and steal their war plans.
Also, I need a peek inside Imperial headquarters, ideally lifting some documents detailing their plans. I need to know what to concentrate on.
What's happening? What did you people do?
You brought this on us! You!
Please, whatever you did, just make it stop!
There's a cave here. Perfect hideout for bandits or worse.
It's a Nordic ruin. Thousands of years old.
What's going on out there? What happened?
Did you make something bad happen?
I'm not seeing things again, am I?
Whatever it is you're up to at the College, you need to fix this at once!
What do you want? You have no business being in here.
You did? Really?
This is the best news I've ever gotten!
I mean, I knew the Dark Brotherhood was good... just not that good! You killed the old hag before I even asked!

Thank you again for resolving my... problem.
You've taken up enough of my time. Now leave.
Think you can intimidate me? In my own orphanage? Ha! Not bloody likely.
We'll go with you, but when this is all over I want an explanation.
I'll go with you, but when things are back to normal I want an explanation.
Well? Is everything out there all right?
What's going on? What happened in there?
That's certainly an understatement. I've never seen anything like this!
Well, I suppose we can't just stand here. We'd better do something.
Do you want me to hold your hand as well?
You're going to have to sneak over to Madesi's stall and use your lockpick on the strongbox.
Then when you have the ring, you pickpocket Brand-Shei and leave it behind.
Constance!
All right. I'll take care of it.
I'll handle it. No need to blow things out of proportion.
Please, just let me handle it. It's not a problem.
Okay, okay. I'll deal with it.
Yes, Grelod?
Samuel is getting to be a problem. Fancies himself something of a "leader" to the other children. Put an end to the nonsense, or I will.
Hroar's crying is keeping me up at night. I'll give you one chance to talk the tears out of him, or he's getting the belt.
I can't abide Samuel's constant talk of his parents. "They're coming for me." "This is only temporary." The boy must accept his fate.
Seven septims are missing from my purse. I know it was Runa. You get those coins back, or by Stendarr, I'll see her rot in the dungeon.
This place is filthy. I've a mind to cancel all town privileges unless those brats start pulling their weight.
The stores are running low. We'll need to water down the milk again. Besides, don't want the little darlings getting fat.
We haven't had an adoption in months. At this rate, we'll be stuck with these brats forever. We need to get the word out. A sale, if need be.
This was a mighty deed! The doom of Alduin encompassed at last, and cleansed is Sovngarde of his evil snare.
They will sing of this battle in Shor's hall forever. But your fate lies elsewhere.
When you have completed your count of days, I may welcome you again, with glad friendship, and bid you join the blessed feasting.
I am Tsun, shield-thane to Shor.
The Whalebone Bridge he bade me guard and winnow all those souls whose heroic end sent them here, to Shor's lofty hall
where welcome, well-earned, awaits those I judge fit to join that fellowship of honor.
It's got to be somewhere around here! Now where did I put it?
Oh, and I wasn't entirely truthful before. This is actually going to hurt a lot. You see, you have to be dead in order for the spell to take hold.
Don't worry though. It'll be relatively painless...relatively.
Hhhmmm...maybe I put it over here.
Ooooh...yes...yes. You've fallen for my trap! Greed makes a great lure. Odd you didn't die from the fall though. Most do.
Oh, and don't mind my assistant here. He may have seen better days, but he's still more help to me dead than he was alive.
Ah...Here it is. Now, if you'd be so kind as to stand still this will be much easier for me to do.
Come on! You know you want what's in that chest. Just walk toward it!
Just walk toward the chest! Gravity will do the rest.
I know you're up there. Go get the treasure. Doesn't it look pretty?
Alright, let's see if we have something here to end this as quickly as possible.
Nope. Not even close.
Call it what you want. You don't pay, then you're not getting in.
All right, calm down, no need to for that. I'll let you in right away, just need to get the gate unlocked.
Are you excited to see what's in there? This is important to you Nords, isn't it?
Don't get any ideas. We're here on official business, remember?
Well, this is highly unusual. And very interesting.
Like anyone would even notice, in all this mess.
As some of you may know, Saarthal was one of the earliest Nord settlements in Skyrim. It was also the largest.
I think, given the state of Skyrim, some might disagree.
There's no gold, nothing shiny at all.
That doesn't begin to describe it.
I just don't know that it's right for us to be in here like this, picking through the ruins .
I'm betting bodies. Lots and lots of bodies. And maybe some valuables...
It's thousands of years old. Who knows what we might find?
You found what?
Hmm. Well now. Yes, this might actually be something interesting. You'd better show that to Tolfdir right away.
This is a little creepy.
I'm not sure what I was expecting, but this isn't it.
Sacked by the elves in the infamous "Night of Tears," not much is known about what happened to Saarthal.
Don't sound so disappointed. You wouldn't be allowed to take it even if there was.
Not excited, no. Some very bad things happened here.
You seem uncomfortable being here.
Oh, right. That whole thing with the elves. Well, it all worked out in the end, didn't it?
What do you think we'll find in there?
Why in the world would this be sealed off? What is this place?
What... What was that? Everything seemed to shimmer for a moment.
What happened?
You know, there might be gold somewhere in here.
This is exciting, isn't it?
I find it hard to believe this excavation was approved.
Don't touch anything!
It's going to take forever to sift through all this.
I'll be amazed if we find anything useful here.
Well, certainly none of this will benefit my research...
This is an exciting opportunity for us. To be able to study such an early civilization, and the magics they used...
I think you'd be a little smarter than that.
Don't be sorry for me. I just - if I would have known it would come to this. Mother...
I've thought this through, and it's the only way. She's too far gone, too corrupted by the Hags. At least in death she can be free again.
Enough. Let's go.
All right. Mother is just outside this door. I have a plan.
Keep Mother's staff. Think of it as payment. I want to leave everything from this cursed place behind. Goodbye. And thank you.
I'll present you as the sacrifice. Just play along. While mother begins the ritual, I'll strike. Then, together we can... we can kill her.
You've decided to help?
Do I make myself clear?
Don't know. Creepy fella, black robe. Couldn't see his face. Paid me a pretty sum to get that into your hands, though.
Yeah, got this note.
You beasts are the worst.
Adelaisa, I'm sorry.
Our secret ingredient, if you will.
A battle-mage of fearsome cunning.
Runs the Blood Horkers, and makes sure the battles end right-side up.
Come now, dearie, surely we aren't all that bad.
Sorry isn't good enough, Orthus. There's a lot of gold at stake here, and sniveling isn't going to get it back.
Finally, someone around here that can get things done.
Since we're short on soldiers you'll be coming with us, too. No arguments.
Anything else you want to say?
Just... leave me alone! I've brought your mead, now drink it and shut your filthy mouths.
Well, here we are. We think.
Aye, the war keeps the Empire and the locals too busy to worry about us. Haldyn calls it "our golden age."
Haldyn will take my hide for this, but Skyrim's been feeling a bit confining lately.
We draw council at Japhet's Folly. The fools in Windhelm won't go near it, which is fine by us.
If you'll pardon me now, my crew needs gathering. Should be in Hammerfell before the other Horkers know who let slip their secret.
Oh, we've got other things in mind for our mouths, sweetie.
Haldyn? I've heard of the man, but never guess he'd join up with some pirates.
This could be even more dangerous than I thought. Head to the ships. We'll plan the assault on the way.
Know about them? Lass, you're looking at them.
It was a truly terrifying scene. Beautiful, in some respects.
I knew he wasn't gone, I just knew it!
Please, get that to Avulstein right away.
If only you could help bring my son back to me.
Thorald's disappearance has left our family in shambles.
I never thought I'd see another friendly face again.
We need to get to safety as soon as possible. Let's move.
Thorald's disappearance has been hard on all of us, but Avulstein has taken it badly.
He's afraid of being taken as well, so he hasn't left the house. All he talks about is Thorald.
Please, help Avulstein if you can. Something has to be done, or we're all going to lose our minds.
Are you joking?
Even if we had a prisoner by that name, the only way he'd be released is by order of the Imperial Legion.
Now I suggest you turn around and go back the way you came.
Are you joking?
Even if we had a prisoner by that name, the only way he'd be released is by order of the Imperial Legion.
Now I suggest you turn around and go back the way you came.
The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking?
I'm sorry, that's just not possible. It would cause far too many problems.
The Thalmor? Do you have any idea what you're asking?
I can only imagine the headaches this is going to cause...
Here, take this. Assuming they even honor it, you should be able to get your prisoner out.
What?
Well... This seems to be in order. Very well, follow me.
Does it matter? You're warm, dry... and still very much alive. That's more than can be said for old Grelod. Hmm?
Sleep well?
We're right behind you.
Lead on, we're ready.
We're with you.
Will do. Don't stray too far away or else we'll come after you.
Almost ever since he became leader of the tribe.
He killed Shagrol in combat, which none of us thought possible. But the winner is made Chief. That is how it is done.
They threw me in here until they were ready to use me in one of their experiments.
This wasn't supposed to happen. I thought they wanted my help, not to use me as a test subject!
No, it's the levers in the center there. Just make sure you don't pull the wrong one. Please, hurry. I don't like being in here!
Let's just get this over with.
Curses aren't enough, now must I suffer you too? What is it?
Why are you here, outsider?
See? I knew Aren wouldn't leave me in here!
Hurry and get me out of this cage before they come for me!
No problem, but if you get too far away, we're going to catch up.
All right, but don't wander ahead too far or else we'll be forced to come find you.
My previous champion displeases me.
It is time he is replaced - in the traditional fashion.
An astute observation.
Skyrim is a beautiful and harsh mistress, but her people cling to such a petty notion of honor.
No, no! This can't be happening!
The sealed letter will explain everything that needs to be done. The amulet is quite valuable - you can use it to pay for any and all expenses.
Just head north - you can't miss it. It's the capital of Whiterun Hold.
Jarl Balgruuf still hasn't declared for one side or the other, so at least you won't run into any Imperials along the way.
Cross the river and then head north. You'll see Whiterun on its hill as you pass the falls.
Of course. Here's a key to the house. Stay as long as you like. If there's anything else you need, just let me know.
But what's this all about? What are you two doing here?
Ralof? How do you know him?
Like I said, I'm glad to help in any way I can. Help yourself to whatever you need, within reason.
You did? We heard that Ulfric was captured...
... no, sorry, but in times like these I can't just take the word of a stranger who wanders into town claiming to be my brother's friend.
I'm afraid not. There are preparations to make and Gallus's remains to lay to rest.
I promise to join you there as soon as I can.
Besides the fact that there are only a handful of people in Tamriel that even recognize the language?
I'm fairly certain he was planning some sort of a heist that involved a deep understanding of the Falmer language.
Sadly, we never had the opportunity to speak about the details.
Well, for the thrill of course.
He was quite clear that he felt more in his element climbing through a window rather than hunched over a dusty tome.
Ironically, I pointed him in the same direction I pointed you. To Markarth and Calcelmo.
I'm only hoping whatever means he used to learn the language will still be available to you.
Please, there isn't much time. What is it?
Please, make it brief. We have much to do.
Back, eh? And how was our friend Calcelmo?
Can I help you with anything else?
Ah yes, my matchmaker. What is it I can do for you?
Remember, speak only to Enthir. Trust no one else.
If you have further need of me, you can find me in the College.
Be careful.
He was a dear friend of mine and a surprisingly astute pupil of academia... I was devastated when he was killed.
I suppose that risk always coexisted with his line of work, I just never thought his luck would run out.
Wish I could say the same for us.
Tolfdir and I can try and keep this contained. You need to get your hands on the Staff of Magnus. Now.
I certainly hope so. I don't know how long we can keep this up.
At least tell me where you're going, so we can send someone after you if you don't come back.
I've got to stay here, to see if we can get to Ancano and stop this. I need someone I can trust to do it.
Besides, you're the one having all of these visions, aren't you?
It's coming loose. I can feel it.
That's it. Keep hacking at it.
Don't stop. Keep cutting.
We must hasten to Riften before Mercer can do any more damage to the Guild.
I'll make for Riften and scout the situation; see if I can discover what Mercer's up to. When you're ready, meet me at the Ragged Flagon.
In the meantime, I wanted you to have this. It belonged to Gallus, but given the circumstances I think he'd approve.
I felt the same way when Gallus first revealed these things to me.
I think given time, you'll understand what I mean.
Everything that represents Nocturnal's influence is contained within the walls of the Sepulcher.
Now it seems Mercer's broken his oath with Nocturnal and defiled the very thing he swore to protect.
You've come this far, so I see no harm in concealing it any longer.
The Twilight Sepulcher is the temple to Nocturnal. It's what the Nightingales are sworn to protect with their lives.
Ah yes, excellent samples. Thank you. This coin should compensate you for all the bother.
I need someone to take this small keg of Black-Briar Mead to Wilhelm at the Vilemyr Inn in Ivarstead.
Drop it off and he'll trade you something for the delivery. Keep it as payment for the legwork.
Oh, and if you bump into Indaryn from the meadery, keep your mouth shut. He'd rat us out in a second.
No, I'm sorry. You must have me mistaken for someone else. I've never even heard that name before.
Sibbi and I were to be married. We were supposed to be happy together forever.
Then I found that wretched poetry from Svana. When I confronted Sibbi about it, he threatened to kill me.
I was scared so I told my brother Wulfur. He went to talk to Sibbi for me and... and... Sibbi killed him. My brother wasn't even armed!
When I heard what Sibbi did, I fled. Now you've found me, and you're going to tell him where I am.
No, no friend. You've got it all wrong. I'm not selling cheap mead. I'm selling good mead for cheap... Black-Briar Mead.
I sell cases of it for half of what the inns and taverns pay through the Meadery.
All I need is someone to deliver it for me. If anyone saw me leave Riften, they'd get suspicious.
Oh, splendid! Just splendid! Mara smiles upon us this day.
Allow me to give you her gift... a true blessing to aid you in times of need. Despair not, her warmth will protect you always!
When you told me of your intent to join the Thieves Guild, I honestly didn't think we'd have much to speak about ever again.
Yet here you are with my weapon that you could have easily sold or kept for yourself.
Forgive me for my hasty opinion of you, I see that I was wrong and that there's much I can learn from you.
If you'd permit me, I'd be honored to accompany you in your travels for a time.
Exactly! And now I have to stare at these bars for eight months. Can you believe it?
I was about to let that wench marry into the richest family in Riften, and this is how she repays me.
I'd do anything to have that whore's head on a platter. All I need to know is where she is.
Look, we all have our flaws; mine is that women can't get enough of me.
So I had this little affair going on while I was betrothed to a beautiful girl named Svidi.
Well, she finds out and she tells her brother, Wulfur. Then her brother attacks me with a knife... I mean, I had to defend myself!
Aye, I suspected as much but I've never been able to catch that little sneak with proof.
So, the whore thinks the Black-Briar reach ends at the border of Skyrim, eh? She'll soon discover her mistake.
That fetcher! I gave him the same keg just this morning and told him to store it in the cellars.
Oh, he'll rot in prison for this one I promise you. As soon as Maven finds out, it's all over for him.
Here, take this as a bit of a reward for nabbing our little thief.
Smithing's been in my blood for generations. I owe my success to my forefathers and their recipe for flame.
The secret is my forge. It consumes fire salts, a strange mineral that burns as hot as Red Mountain lava.
Well, it was. Sadly, this forge is dying and I've used the last of my fire salts. If I can't feed it soon, it may grow cold.
Good, good. Just point me in the right direction and I'll do the rest.
All right, pipe down. You want the whole blessed place to hear you?
The arrangement called for a trade. I hope you'll find this suitable.
Oh my, how wonderful! Lady Mara will be so pleased!
Here you are. Now make sure you give out every single one, we can't afford to let even one lost soul stray from Her warmth.
Good, my experiments are at a critical stage and these are just what I need to finish. Here's some coin for all the trouble.
Ivarstead? So close to Riften? She's an even bigger fool than I thought.
Riften is drowning in a sea of sin and wickedness and it's my personal mission to let everyone know that the warmth of Mara can see them through.
All I need is a messenger to bring Her words to these poor souls by distributing these missives.
I met her when I wandered into Riften a few years ago. Been smitten with her ever since. In fact, I'm thinking of asking her to marry me.
In Black Marsh, tradition dictates we present a potential mate a unique wedding ring to represent our future bond.
I already have the gold band, but all I need are three flawless amethysts to complete the setting.
Perfect. This is just what I needed. Here's some gold for your time.
Mara only helps those who can afford her tithes. This is nonsense.
Oh, how kind! Thank you.
Excellent, I needed some more kindling for my kitchen hearth.
Whatever you're peddling, I'm not buying.
Are you serious? You're as crazy as Maramal.
Look, the only religion I believe in is the good old septim. Everything else is just a waste.
Ugh. Get this awful paper away from me, by Talos!
Oh, a new pamphlet from Dinya? I'll be glad to read it. Thanks.
Huh? Oh. Sure, whatever. I prefer Dibella's teachings myself.
Thank you. Umm, blessings upon you too.
I wouldn't even clean my tail with this trash.
Unless Mara wishes to come down and help me with these urchins, I don't really give a damn about her blessings.
Thank you. I'll share this with Aerin.
Hmmm. Never read anything from the temple before. I suppose there wouldn't be any harm in reading it.
What's this nonsense? Bah.
I'll read it. Thank you.
I don't have time for this nonsense.
Seems like a noble cause, I'll look it over.
If only more people would read this, things might be better in Riften.
I don't understand how paper will help the dregs of this city, but suit yourself.
Do you take me for a fool? Get this drivel away from me!
Thank you. Blessings of Mara upon you as well.
Interesting. I'll look it over. Thanks.
I hold my own beliefs, but thanks anyway.
Mara this and Mara that. Where is she when hundreds of Stormcloaks are dying for no good reason, eh?
Not really my usual read, but I'll give it a try.
Mara can't help me. No one can.
Oh, interesting. I'll read it this evening.
I have no desire to seek religion.
I visit the temple sometimes, but perhaps not often enough. Thanks for bringing this to me.
Thank you for the blessing, I'll put this to good use.
Oh, well thank you. I'll try and read this if I can.
Are you out of your mind?
I think I have some more of these padding my mattress.
I have no time for Marla or Mara or whatever her name is... there's work to be done!
Had only Mara saved that poor animal...
I'll give it a try, thanks very much.
You look like you could use a drink. What do you say to some cheap mead?
Ah finally, someone besides the stupid guards to talk to.
My experiments continue to go well thanks to you. How can I return the favor?
Lady Mara's messenger returns. What can I help you with, my child?
Oh, it's you. Come to extort more from me?
Come to see Balimund perform miracles with steel, eh?
Oh, it's you. Not here to rob me are you?
Enthusiasm. Good. Here's the key to the dig site.
I'll let you in if you agree to something for me. There's a giant spider in Nchuand-Zel. My workers call her "Nimhe," the poisoned one.
If you deal with Nimhe, I'll let you into both the excavation site and my Dwemer Museum. What do you say?
Head on in. Keep your nose clean, and you won't have any problems with us.
Correct. It's a private place where I can catalog my research. Occasionally, the Jarl will come in and have questions, but that's thankfully rare.
Persistent aren't you? Oh, very well. Who am I to stand in the way of curiosity?
You're our favorite kind of traveler, then. The market area is just inside in the gate.
It means "Dwarven Museum." Lost race of people that built the city.
You want to know more? Ask Calcelmo. He's usually in front of the ruins, just down the tunnel.
Jarl Igmund sits on the Mournful Throne. You can find him in the keep, in the back end of the city inside the mountain.
Calcelmo. He's the Jarl's magical advisor, but he spends most of his time studying the ruins.
If you want in, Calcelmo has to give his say-so. He's usually in front of Nchuand-Zel. Just down the tunnel.
See, there you go. Getting involved. Don't ask too many questions in Markarth. Safer for everyone that way.
Because Calcelmo doesn't want anyone to go in. He runs the museum, he makes the rules.
You have a problem with that? Go talk to him. You'll find him in front of the ruins during the day. Just down the tunnel.
You're working with the Daedra? Right. Now tell me the one about the Argonian Maid and the lusty baron.
First time in Markarth, traveler? Take my advice. You see anything, don't get involved. The city guard will take care of it.
You have my gratitude. That spider had been interfering with my excavations for months.
As promised, here's the key to the Dwemer Museum. Mind the displays. Some of them are quite fragile.
Halt. The Dwemer Museum isn't open to anyone without the court wizard's permission.
Death to the Nords!
Glory to the Forsworn!
Things are so much quieter with my darling Frabbi dead.
Oh, very well. Probably safest that way.
Margret!
Guards! Forsworn!
Gods, not again....
I bleed for the people of the Reach!
It seems that a predator has invaded their home, and someone needs to clear the beast out.
A citizen of the Reach has asked for our help.
A citizen of Haafingar has asked for our help.
A citizen of Hjaalmarch has asked for our help.
A citizen of Falkreath has asked for our help.
A citizen of Whiterun has asked for our help.
A citizen of the Pale has asked for our help.
A citizen of the Rift has asked for our help.
A citizen of Eastmarch has asked for our help.
A citizen of Winterhold has asked for our help.
I really must get back to my duties. This fort isn't going to clean itself.
Well, I'm not blind to the fact that you've been instrumental in improving my affairs of the heart.
If you wish, you may enter my tower to view the museum section, but I'm afraid the laboratories are still off limits.
That's the best I can offer.
Oh, be certain to purchase my Falmer translation tome when it's completed!
Preposterous! That research represents years of personal toil in some of the most dangerous Dwemer ruins in Skyrim!
You must be mad to think I'd allow anyone to see it before it's completed.
Look, I'm very busy, so this better be important.
I've already provided you with the key to my museum, what else could you possibly want?
This better be important.
Ha! All right, I'm writing that in. I'm not sure about the court but I find that much more probable than Olaf capturing a dragon.
Elisif has forbidden the Burning of King Olaf, a Festival put on by the Bards College. We need to change her mind.
To convince her I want to read King Olaf's Verse. A part of the Poetic Edda, the living history of Skyrim. Unfortunately the verse was lost long ago.
It means I can't read it to the court. Without the verse I won't be able to convince Elisif of the importance of The Burning of King Olaf Festival.
If she isn't convinced of the festival's importance then she won't reverse her decision to stop the effigy burning.
It means that the Burning of King Olaf, which the Bards College has held for time immemorial, won't be happening.
Welcome to the Bards College. I am the headmaster here. How may I help you?
"O, Olaf, our subjugator, the one-eyed betrayer;"
"death-dealing demon and dragon-killing King."
"Your legend is lies, lurid and false;"
"your cunning capture of Numinex, a con for the ages."
"Olaf gave orders, Winterhold disguises. An attack on Solitude total destruction to follow."
"His men dressed up and then went out to fight, but they reversed Olaf's orders much to Winterhold's sorrow."
Thank you yet again. I will make sure our applicant, who was instrumental in... recovering the poem will be well rewarded.
I have to admit I didn't think it would actually be there.  Now let's take a look at this...
Oh. Oh-no. This won't do at all.
The copy is incomplete, it's aged to the point that parts are unreadable.
And the parts that are readable... well... bardic verse has come a long way since ancient times.
Soon, soon. These things must be done properly, you will be inducted as part of the Festival itself.
I need you to go speak to Jorn. He was preparing the Effigy of King Olaf.
Tell him to finish the preparations, the Festival is back on.
I'm a bard, trained at the Bards College in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.
I'm a bard, from the Bards College here in Solitude. Wanderers like you should think about applying.
I thank you and the college thanks you, Jarl.
"Olaf grabbed power, by promise and threat;"
"From Falkreath to Winterhold, they fell to their knees;"
"But Solitude stood strong, Skyrim's truest protectors."
"Olaf's vengeance was instant, inspired and wicked."
"No shouting match between dragon and man, no fire or fury did this battle entail."
"Olaf captured a fast-sleeping dragon, a mighty feat which the meek would not fail."
I find that highly unlikely... but the court will love it. I'm writing it in.
Make it up? That doesn't seem appropriate...
I suppose I could copy his style based on what you brought me but I have no idea what happened in between these verses.
Hmmm, Olaf did become king and Numinex escaped later. It's entirely possible... and I'm sure the court will like that, I'm writing it in.
It's not the most exciting answer but the most likely. I'll write it in.
It has a few final lines but that's all we needed to add. I need to head to court immediately and present this. You should come.
I think they like you.
Unbelievable! You have done us a great service here. I can't begin to thank you enough.
Oh that is exciting! I'm sure the court and the Jarl will love it. I'm writing it in now.
Always a pleasure to meet a prospective bard.
You should be aware that many apply but we accept very few people.
When possible, we ask applicants to perform tasks the college needs completed. In this case, I do have a task befitting an aspiring bard...
That sounds good. I'm not sure the court will believe that Olaf had wizard powers but we shall see.
You have proven your point, Viarmo.  The festival is truly a celebration of Solitude and a condemnation of false kings.
As you may be aware Elisif's husband High King Torygg was recently killed.
Elisif mourns her husband deeply and she feels that a festival that burns a King in effigy is... distasteful.
I've tried to convince her the festival is many centuries old and celebrates Solitude but I need proof.
I believe King Olaf's verse will provide that proof.
"So ends the story of Olaf the liar, a thief and a scoundrel we of Solitude commit to the fire."
"In Solitude bards train for their service, they also gather each year and burn a King who deserves it."
Look, I'm getting out of here. Normally, spiders make my skin crawl, but magic spiders? Blech.
Furthermore, I believe that such a fine poem deserves some payment of Patronage. The college will be moderately rewarded.
Rexus will now give you two items which must be passed along to your superior.
Is there... something else you need?
Climb down that ledge and see if you can...
Karliah, when will you learn you can't get the drop on me?
He's here and he hasn't seen us yet. Brynjolf, watch the door.
Aye, lass. Nothing's getting by me.
The old gods give me strength!
I'm in the mood to ssssiiiiinnnggg! What about you, friend? Care to join me?
Please, just... just leave me be!
The power of Mzinchaleft is mine! Begone from here!
By the love of the Nine, what is it with you sycophants and hangers on? The jarl is busy! And so am I, for that matter.
By the gods! Somebody help me!
You do realize there's only one way this can end.
Go away! This is my wreck! Mine!
I'm busy, and you're bothering me. So go away.
I find your presence... unnerving. Please depart.
Purchase something or leave. Those are your options.
You really don't want to be loitering here, stranger.
Good work. A simple job, but it keeps the coin rolling in. Here's your share.
I'm sure that the owner of that place is relieved. They certainly came through with the coin.
Well done. Here's your gold.
A citizen of Skyrim has asked for our help.
Go on, now.
Good luck. And try not to get yourself killed.
Happy hunting.
Will there ever come a time when the people of Skyrim no longer need our services? I think not.
You reek of death, my friend. I salute you.
You're going to happen by and lend poor old Sabjorn a helping hand.
He's going to give you the poison to use on the pests, but you're also going to dump it into the brewing vat.
Go. Out. Before I change my mind.
What? Why? But... you must have said something to Erikur! You lied to him and now here I am! You're a heartless monster!
No... please, you have to help me! You don't understand what they do to people here!
You must be new. We don't talk to each other here.
You here to see Rulindil, too, eh? None of my business of course.
Push off, will you?
Ah! You made it! Good to see you, my friend. I'll never forget how you saved my life from those Thalmor maniacs.
So you made it out. Good for you. Don't expect me to thank you. I still remember what you did to me.
Oh... thank you. I don't understand what's happening. I can't stay here. What will I do now?
Ah. You must mean that one over there. He's not one of us.
Thalmor filth, eh? Giving all Khajiit a bad name.
What did you tell him? Why did you do this to me?
You've got to help me! Please!
I don't know where I'll go. Somewhere far away from here.
Maybe this is a way out?
Maybe one of the guards had a key?
There's got to be some way out of here!
It seems that predators have taken up residence and have been attacking farmers and travelers.
Someone's got to head out there and take care of the beasts.
The Jarl of the Reach has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Haafingar has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Hjaalmarch has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Falkreath has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Whiterun has asked for our help.
The Jarl of the Pale has asked for our help.
The Jarl of the Rift has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Eastmarch has asked for our help.
The Jarl of Winterhold has asked for our help.
This is going to be terrible for business...
Both of the buildings are connected by tunnels made by the pests infesting the meadery.
There's an entrance to it in the basement storeroom of the warehouse that used to be boarded over.
I've already removed the boards so the meadery would get infested. That's where you should start.
Are you kidding me? Look at this place.
I'm supposed to be holding a tasting of the new Honningbrew Reserve for the Captain of the Guard.
If he sees the meadery in this state, I'll be ruined.
Mallus is the best deal I've ever made. Lent him a bit of gold some time ago I knew he'd never be able to pay back.
Nothing like free labor to make operating costs cheaper.
I made the mistake of borrowing coin from Sabjorn. He's allowing me to pay it back, but he's working my fingers to the bone!
He treats me like a slave... I have to do every nasty, dirty job in the meadery.
Once Sabjorn is out of the way, Maven has plans for this place. One way or another, we don't want the pests coming back.
Consider it just more of the dirty work. I did my part getting them in there, now you need to clear them out.
Remember, Sabjorn will be needing a helping hand. Make it look good.
Good luck. I'll be here if you need me.
Suit yourself.
Well... yes. I mean, all of the gold didn't come from me. I have a silent partner that helped with the finances.
One of the Jarls has asked for our help.
Good work. The mindless cretins didn't stand a chance.
Welcome to Riften.
You want in, you need to cooperate.
Keep at it. We need to work twice as hard now that fat-headed Leigelf has declared war!
That was a close one!
I don't know, but this place is full of them. Every one I've encountered has been enchanted or the like.
Tell Cicero the time has come. Tell him the words he has been waiting for, all these years: "Darkness rises when silence dies."
What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's tomb! Explain yourself!
Poor Cicero has failed you. Poor Cicero is sorry, sweet mother. I've tried, so very hard. But I just can't find the Listener.
These look like good quality, too. I know it's not the glamorous, heroic work, but don't be surprised if I ask you to do it again.
Shame. But, I'm sure one of the whelps around here will take it up.
Thank the Divines! Let's move!
Unfortunate. Let's be more careful next time.
I'm ever so grateful. Please, give my regard to the rest of the Companions.
And I have already received word that the client is satisfied. You have done well for yourself, and for the Companions.
Another job well done. Your payment is secure, and you have my thanks.
I expected no less of you. And once again, you have not disappointed.
We've got some traders coming in, and are short on Cave Bear pelts.
I knew you could be counted on. Do what must be done.
Excellent. Show them no mercy.
Good. Do not spare them your blade.
Nothing but a bunch of lowly bandits. Cowards, who will take little effort.
